title: Automated Invoice Processing & Data Extraction
description: Automatically captures, validates, and processes vendor invoices from multiple channels (email, EDI, portal, paper) using AI-powered OCR and data extraction, eliminating manual data entry and accelerating invoice processing cycles.

layers.name: Multi-Channel Invoice Capture
layers.description: Receives invoices from email attachments, EDI feeds, vendor portals, scanned paper documents, and mobile uploads. Automatically classifies document type and routes to processing queue.

layers.name: Intelligent Data Extraction
layers.description: Extracts key fields using AI-powered OCR including vendor name, invoice number, date, due date, line items, amounts, tax codes, and payment terms. Handles various invoice formats and languages.

layers.name: PO Matching & Validation
layers.description: Automatically matches invoices to purchase orders, validates quantities, prices, and terms. Identifies two-way matches (PO-Invoice) and three-way matches (PO-GRN-Invoice).

layers.name: Exception Detection & Routing
layers.description: Flags exceptions such as missing POs, price variances, quantity discrepancies, duplicate invoices, and invalid vendor details. Routes exceptions to appropriate resolvers based on exception type.

layers.name: Approval Workflow Automation
layers.description: Routes invoices through approval workflows based on amount thresholds, cost centers, and authorization matrices. Tracks approval status and sends reminders for pending approvals.

flow.description: Invoice matched to PO and GRN
Invoices routed for approval or exception handling
Approved invoices posted to accounts payable

title: Duplicate Invoice Detection & Prevention
description: Identifies and prevents duplicate invoice payments through intelligent matching algorithms that detect duplicates despite variations in invoice presentation, vendor naming, or data entry errors.

layers.name: Multi-Factor Duplicate Detection
layers.description: Compares invoices across multiple dimensions including invoice number, vendor, amount, date, PO number, and line item details. Uses fuzzy matching to handle data entry variations.

layers.name: Historical Payment Analysis
layers.description: Checks against historical payment records to identify invoices that may have been paid under different invoice numbers or through different channels.

layers.name: Vendor Pattern Recognition
layers.description: Learns vendor-specific invoice numbering patterns and identifies anomalies such as sequential duplicates, zero-padded numbers, or suffix variations.

layers.name: Cross-Entity Detection
layers.description: Identifies potential duplicates across multiple legal entities, especially relevant for shared service centers processing invoices for multiple companies.

layers.name: Confidence Scoring & Review
layers.description: Assigns confidence scores to potential duplicates, routes high-confidence matches for automatic blocking, and medium-confidence matches for manual review.

title: Vendor Master Data Management
description: Manages vendor master data including vendor creation, modification, deactivation, and duplicate prevention, ensuring data quality, compliance with vendor onboarding policies, and prevention of fraudulent vendors.

layers.name: Vendor Onboarding Workflow
layers.description: Manages vendor registration process including vendor information collection, tax form validation (W-9/W-8), bank account verification, and compliance screening.

layers.name: Duplicate Vendor Detection
layers.description: Identifies potential duplicate vendors using name matching, tax ID comparison, address similarity, and bank account analysis before creating new vendor records.

layers.name: Vendor Data Enrichment
layers.description: Enriches vendor records with external data including business registration verification, credit ratings, beneficial ownership information, and sanctions screening.

layers.name: Change Management Control
layers.description: Requires dual approval for critical vendor changes such as bank account modifications, payment terms updates, and address changes to prevent fraud.

layers.name: Vendor Performance Tracking
layers.description: Maintains vendor performance metrics including on-time delivery, quality scores, pricing competitiveness, and payment dispute history.

title: Payment Processing & Cash Disbursement
description: Automates payment processing including payment batch creation, payment file generation, bank integration, and payment confirmation, ensuring timely payments while maintaining strong financial controls.

layers.name: Payment Selection & Optimization
layers.description: Selects invoices for payment based on due dates, early payment discounts, vendor terms, and cash availability. Optimizes payment timing to maximize working capital.

layers.name: Payment Method Determination
layers.description: Determines optimal payment method (ACH, wire, check, virtual card) based on vendor preferences, payment amount, urgency, and cost considerations.

layers.name: Payment Authorization Control
layers.description: Validates payment authorization based on amount thresholds and authority matrices. Ensures segregation of duties between payment preparation and approval.

layers.name: Bank File Generation
layers.description: Creates payment files in bank-required formats (ACH, SWIFT, local formats), encrypts sensitive data, and transmits securely to banking partners.

layers.name: Payment Status Tracking
layers.description: Tracks payment status from initiation through clearing, identifies failed payments, manages payment returns, and provides payment confirmation to vendors and internal stakeholders.

title: Accruals & Period-End Processing
description: Manages period-end accruals for goods received but not invoiced (GRNI), services rendered but not billed, and recurring accruals, ensuring accurate period-end financial statements.

layers.name: GRNI Identification
layers.description: Identifies all goods receipts without corresponding invoices, calculates accrual amounts based on PO prices, and generates accrual journal entries for period-end closing.

layers.name: Service Accrual Management
layers.description: Tracks service contracts and recurring services, calculates unbilled service amounts based on contract terms and service period, and creates appropriate accruals.

layers.name: Recurring Accrual Processing
layers.description: Manages recurring accruals such as rent, insurance, utilities, and subscriptions. Automates monthly accrual calculations and validates against contracts.

layers.name: Accrual Reversal Control
layers.description: Automatically reverses accruals in the subsequent period, tracks invoice receipt against accruals, and identifies accruals requiring write-off or adjustment.

layers.name: Accrual Aging & Analysis
layers.description: Ages outstanding accruals, identifies long-outstanding items, investigates accruals without corresponding invoices, and ensures accrual accuracy.

title: Expense Report Processing & Policy Compliance
description: Automates employee expense report processing including receipt capture, policy validation, approval routing, and reimbursement processing while ensuring compliance with expense policies and tax regulations.

layers.name: Mobile Receipt Capture
layers.description: Enables employees to capture receipts via mobile app using OCR technology. Extracts transaction details including merchant, date, amount, and category.

layers.name: Policy Compliance Validation
layers.description: Validates expenses against company policies including per diem rates, mileage rates, category spending limits, approval requirements, and documentation standards.

layers.name: Mileage & Per Diem Calculation
layers.description: Automatically calculates mileage reimbursements based on GPS data or entered routes, applies appropriate rates, and calculates per diem allowances for business travel.

layers.name: Corporate Card Reconciliation
layers.description: Matches expense reports to corporate card transactions, identifies unsubmitted expenses, flags personal charges, and ensures complete expense reporting.

layers.name: Tax Compliance & Reporting
layers.description: Identifies taxable benefits, calculates imputed income for personal use, maintains records for tax reporting, and generates required tax forms.

title: Automated Invoice Generation & Delivery
description: Automatically generates customer invoices based on shipments, service delivery, contracts, or subscriptions, formats invoices per customer requirements, and delivers through preferred channels (email, EDI, portal).

layers.name: Trigger-Based Invoice Creation
layers.description: Monitors shipment confirmations, service completion records, milestone achievements, and subscription periods to automatically trigger invoice generation at the appropriate time.

layers.name: Pricing & Revenue Recognition
layers.description: Applies contract pricing, volume discounts, promotional pricing, and special terms. Validates revenue recognition criteria before invoicing.

layers.name: Invoice Formatting & Customization
layers.description: Formats invoices according to customer specifications including layout, data fields, branding, language, currency, and supporting documentation requirements.

layers.name: Multi-Channel Delivery
layers.description: Delivers invoices through customer-preferred channels including email (PDF), EDI, customer portals, printed mail, and API integrations with customer systems.

layers.name: Delivery Confirmation & Tracking
layers.description: Tracks invoice delivery status, monitors email open rates, confirms EDI transmission, and maintains audit trail of all invoice delivery attempts.

title: Intelligent Cash Application & Reconciliation
description: Automatically matches customer payments to outstanding invoices using AI-powered matching algorithms, handles complex payment scenarios, and minimizes manual cash application effort.

layers.name: Payment Receipt Capture
layers.description: Captures payments from bank lockbox files, electronic payment notifications, credit card processors, and manual entry. Extracts payment amount, date, reference, and payer information.

layers.name: Intelligent Invoice Matching
layers.description: Matches payments to invoices using remittance information, invoice numbers, amounts, customer IDs, and historical payment patterns. Handles partial payments, overpayments, and multi-invoice payments.

layers.name: Exception Handling
layers.description: Manages payment exceptions including unapplied cash (payments without invoice reference), short payments, overpayments, duplicate payments, and wrong account payments.

layers.name: Discount & Deduction Management
layers.description: Validates early payment discounts, processes authorized deductions, identifies unauthorized deductions, and routes for resolution.

layers.name: Bank Reconciliation Integration
layers.description: Integrates cash application with bank reconciliation, ensures all receipts are accounted for, and maintains complete audit trail from bank to general ledger.

title: Collections Management & Dunning Automation
description: Automates collections activities including dunning letter generation, payment reminder calls, escalation workflows, and collector work queue prioritization to accelerate cash collection and reduce DSO.

layers.name: Aging Analysis & Prioritization
layers.description: Analyzes receivables aging, segments customers by risk level, payment history, and outstanding amounts. Prioritizes collection efforts based on likelihood of recovery and dollar impact.

layers.name: Automated Dunning Process
layers.description: Generates and sends payment reminders via email, SMS, and letters based on aging thresholds. Escalates communication frequency and tone as invoices age.

layers.name: Collector Work Queue Management
layers.description: Creates optimized work queues for collectors based on customer assignments, account risk, aging, and collector capacity. Suggests next best actions.

layers.name: Payment Promise Tracking
layers.description: Records payment commitments from customers, sets follow-up dates, tracks promise fulfillment, and escalates broken promises.

layers.name: Dispute Management
layers.description: Captures customer payment disputes, routes to appropriate resolution teams, tracks dispute status, and ensures disputes are resolved before collection escalation.

title: Credit Management & Risk Assessment
description: Manages customer credit limits, performs ongoing credit risk assessment, enforces credit policies, and prevents shipments to customers exceeding credit limits or with poor payment history.

layers.name: Credit Application Processing
layers.description: Processes new customer credit applications, validates trade references, obtains credit reports, analyzes financial statements, and assigns initial credit limits.

layers.name: Dynamic Credit Scoring
layers.description: Continuously updates customer credit scores based on payment behavior, aging trends, credit utilization, external credit ratings, and financial health indicators.

layers.name: Credit Limit Enforcement
layers.description: Validates every order against available credit before acceptance. Places credit holds on orders exceeding limits and routes for credit manager approval.

layers.name: Periodic Credit Review
layers.description: Performs scheduled reviews of credit limits based on customer payment performance, order volume changes, and external credit report updates.

layers.name: Credit Insurance Integration
layers.description: Integrates with credit insurance providers, validates coverage limits, files claims for non-payment, and ensures coverage is maintained for high-risk customers.

title: Customer Master Data Quality Management
description: Maintains accurate and complete customer master data including customer creation, modification, deactivation, duplicate prevention, and data enrichment to ensure billing accuracy and compliance.

layers.name: Customer Onboarding Workflow
layers.description: Manages new customer setup including information collection, credit application processing, tax exemption validation, and system account creation.

layers.name: Duplicate Customer Detection
layers.description: Identifies potential duplicate customers using name matching, address similarity, tax ID comparison, and contact information analysis before creating new records.

layers.name: Data Quality Validation
layers.description: Validates customer data for completeness and accuracy including address standardization, tax ID format validation, contact information verification, and required field completion.

layers.name: Change Management Control
layers.description: Tracks all customer master data changes, requires appropriate approvals for critical changes (billing address, payment terms, tax status), and maintains complete audit trail.

layers.name: Customer Data Enrichment
layers.description: Enriches customer records with external data including business registration information, industry classification, company size, credit ratings, and parent-subsidiary relationships.

title: Bad Debt Provisioning & Write-Off Management
description: Manages bad debt provisions based on aging analysis and historical loss rates, processes write-offs for uncollectible accounts, and tracks recovery efforts for written-off balances.

layers.name: Provision Calculation
layers.description: Calculates bad debt provisions using aging-based percentages, historical loss rates, and forward-looking risk factors. Applies collective and specific provisions per accounting standards.

layers.name: Write-Off Identification
layers.description: Identifies accounts requiring write-off based on age, collection efforts exhausted, bankruptcy filings, business closures, or management determination of uncollectibility.

layers.name: Write-Off Approval Process
layers.description: Routes write-off requests through approval workflow based on amount thresholds, validates documentation requirements, and ensures proper authorization.

layers.name: Recovery Tracking
layers.description: Continues tracking written-off accounts for potential recovery, maintains separate recovery account, and applies recoveries to restore previously written-off amounts.

layers.name: Bad Debt Analytics
layers.description: Analyzes bad debt trends by customer segment, geography, product line, and sales channel. Identifies root causes and recommends credit policy improvements.

title: Journal Entry Automation & Validation
description: Automates recurring journal entries, validates manual entries for accuracy and completeness, enforces approval workflows, and maintains complete audit trail of all general ledger postings.

layers.name: Recurring Entry Automation
layers.description: Identifies recurring journal entries (rent, depreciation, amortization, allocations), automates monthly posting, validates amounts against contracts or calculations, and adjusts for period-specific factors.

layers.name: Template Management
layers.description: Maintains library of journal entry templates for common transactions, pre-populates account codes and descriptions, enforces data validation rules, and ensures consistency.

layers.name: Manual Entry Validation
layers.description: Validates manual journal entries for balanced debits and credits, valid account codes, required supporting documentation, appropriate period, and policy compliance.

layers.name: Approval Workflow
layers.description: Routes journal entries through approval workflow based on amount, account type, entry type (standard vs. adjusting), and approver authority. Tracks approval status and maintains approval history.

layers.name: Supporting Documentation
layers.description: Ensures all manual journal entries have attached supporting documentation, validates document relevance, maintains document repository, and provides audit trail linkage.

title: Automated Account Reconciliation
description: Automates balance sheet account reconciliations by matching transactions between GL and sub-ledgers or external statements, identifies reconciling items, and tracks resolution to ensure all accounts reconcile.

layers.name: Reconciliation Scope Management
layers.description: Maintains inventory of all accounts requiring reconciliation, defines reconciliation frequency (daily, weekly, monthly), assigns preparer and reviewer, and tracks reconciliation requirements.

layers.name: Automated Transaction Matching
layers.description: Automatically matches transactions between GL and source systems (bank statements, sub-ledgers, third-party systems) using transaction IDs, amounts, dates, and reference numbers.

layers.name: Reconciling Item Identification
layers.description: Identifies unmatched items on both sides of reconciliation, categorizes reconciling items (timing differences, errors, adjustments needed), and calculates net reconciling amount.

layers.name: Aging & Resolution Tracking
layers.description: Ages reconciling items, tracks resolution progress, escalates long-outstanding items, validates item clearance, and ensures timely resolution.

layers.name: Reconciliation Review & Sign-Off
layers.description: Routes completed reconciliations to independent reviewers, validates review completion, tracks reviewer comments, and maintains sign-off documentation.

title: Financial Close Process Management
description: Orchestrates the entire financial close process including task management, dependency tracking, bottleneck identification, and timeline monitoring to ensure timely and accurate period-end closing.

layers.name: Close Task Library
layers.description: Maintains comprehensive library of close tasks including standard monthly tasks, quarter-end tasks, year-end tasks, and ad-hoc tasks. Defines task dependencies and sequence.

layers.name: Dependency Management
layers.description: Tracks task dependencies, ensures tasks start only after prerequisites complete, identifies critical path, and highlights tasks on critical path requiring priority attention.

layers.name: Real-Time Progress Tracking
layers.description: Monitors task completion in real-time, calculates percentage complete, compares actual vs. planned timeline, and identifies tasks at risk of missing deadlines.

layers.name: Bottleneck Detection
layers.description: Identifies bottlenecks where multiple tasks are waiting, analyzes resource constraints, suggests task re-prioritization, and escalates risks to close timeline.

layers.name: Close Calendar Management
layers.description: Maintains close calendar with key dates, communicates close schedule to stakeholders, tracks schedule changes, and manages early close initiatives.

title: Intercompany Transaction Processing & Elimination
description: Manages intercompany transactions including automated intercompany invoicing, receivables/payables matching, transaction elimination, and profit elimination for consolidated financial statements.

layers.name: Intercompany Transaction Capture
layers.description: Captures all intercompany transactions including sales, purchases, loans, services, royalties, and management fees. Tags transactions with intercompany codes for tracking.

layers.name: Automated IC Invoicing
layers.description: Generates intercompany invoices automatically based on transfer pricing policies, service level agreements, and cost allocation rules. Ensures both sides record transaction.

layers.name: IC Reconciliation & Matching
layers.description: Matches intercompany receivables to payables across legal entities, identifies timing differences and amount mismatches, and facilitates resolution.

layers.name: Elimination Entry Generation
layers.description: Automatically generates consolidation elimination entries for IC sales/COGS, IC receivables/payables, IC profits in inventory, and IC loans/interest.

layers.name: Transfer Pricing Compliance
layers.description: Validates intercompany pricing against transfer pricing policies, maintains transfer pricing documentation, and ensures arm's length pricing for tax compliance.

title: Multi-Entity Consolidation & Currency Translation
description: Consolidates financial results from multiple legal entities, performs foreign currency translation, applies consolidation adjustments, and produces consolidated financial statements.

layers.name: Entity Hierarchy Management
layers.description: Maintains corporate structure including parent-subsidiary relationships, ownership percentages, consolidation methods (full, proportionate, equity), and effective dates of ownership changes.

layers.name: Trial Balance Collection
layers.description: Collects trial balances from all entities, validates completeness, ensures consistent chart of accounts mapping, and identifies missing or late submissions.

layers.name: Currency Translation
layers.description: Translates foreign entity financial statements using appropriate rates (current rate for balance sheet, average rate for income statement), applies translation adjustments, and tracks cumulative translation adjustments.

layers.name: Consolidation Adjustments
layers.description: Applies consolidation adjustments including intercompany eliminations, equity method adjustments, purchase accounting, goodwill impairment, and minority interest calculations.

layers.name: Consolidated Statement Preparation
layers.description: Aggregates entity trial balances, applies all adjustments, produces consolidated trial balance, and generates consolidated financial statements.

title: Financial Statement Preparation & Review
description: Automates financial statement preparation including balance sheet, income statement, cash flow statement, and notes to financials, performs analytical review, and ensures compliance with accounting standards.

layers.name: Statement Generation
layers.description: Automatically generates financial statements from trial balance using predefined mapping rules, formats statements per accounting standards (GAAP/IFRS), and applies presentation requirements.

layers.name: Comparative Reporting
layers.description: Produces comparative financial statements showing current period vs. prior period, calculates variances and percentages, and highlights significant changes.

layers.name: Analytical Review Procedures
layers.description: Performs automated analytical procedures including ratio analysis, trend analysis, reasonableness tests, and benchmarking against budget and prior periods.

layers.name: Disclosure Checklist
layers.description: Validates disclosure completeness against accounting standards checklist, identifies missing disclosures, and ensures required notes are included.

layers.name: Statement Review & Approval
layers.description: Routes statements through review workflow, tracks reviewer comments and adjustments, maintains version control, and obtains final approval.

title: Fixed Asset Acquisition & Capitalization
description: Manages fixed asset acquisitions including capitalization threshold validation, asset classification, useful life determination, and initial recognition ensuring accurate asset accounting.

layers.name: Capitalization Threshold Validation
layers.description: Validates that asset acquisitions meet capitalization criteria including minimum cost threshold, useful life exceeding one year, and intended use for business operations.

layers.name: Asset Classification
layers.description: Classifies assets into appropriate categories (land, buildings, machinery, vehicles, IT equipment, furniture) based on asset characteristics, determines appropriate depreciation method for category.

layers.name: Cost Accumulation
layers.description: Accumulates all costs properly capitalizable including purchase price, freight, installation, testing, and asset retirement obligations. Excludes operating costs and maintenance.

layers.name: Useful Life & Residual Value
layers.description: Determines useful life based on asset class standards, historical experience, and management estimates. Establishes residual value for depreciation calculation.

layers.name: Asset Master Record Creation
layers.description: Creates asset master record with complete details including description, location, custodian, acquisition date, cost, useful life, depreciation method, and tax attributes.

flow.description: Useful life and residual value assigned

title: Automated Depreciation Calculation & Posting
description: Calculates and posts depreciation expense automatically using appropriate methods (straight-line, declining balance, units of production), handles mid-period acquisitions, and manages tax vs. book depreciation.

layers.name: Depreciation Method Application
layers.description: Applies appropriate depreciation method based on asset class and accounting policy including straight-line, declining balance, sum-of-years-digits, and units of production.

layers.name: Mid-Period Convention
layers.description: Handles mid-period acquisitions and disposals using appropriate conventions (half-year, mid-quarter, full-month, daily) per company policy.

layers.name: Book vs. Tax Depreciation
layers.description: Calculates separate depreciation for book and tax purposes, applies accelerated tax depreciation (MACRS, Section 179, bonus depreciation), and tracks book-tax differences.

layers.name: Fully Depreciated Asset Management
layers.description: Identifies fully depreciated assets still in use, stops depreciation when net book value reaches residual value, and flags for disposal consideration.

layers.name: Depreciation Posting Automation
layers.description: Automatically generates and posts depreciation journal entries monthly, allocates depreciation to appropriate cost centers, and updates asset register.

title: Asset Transfer & Location Tracking
description: Manages asset transfers between locations, departments, or cost centers, updates custodian assignments, maintains complete transfer history, and ensures accurate asset tracking and cost allocation.

layers.name: Transfer Request Management
layers.description: Captures asset transfer requests including reason, new location, new custodian, effective date, and approvals required based on asset value and transfer type.

layers.name: Transfer Approval Workflow
layers.description: Routes transfer requests through approval workflow based on asset value, transfer type (location, department, legal entity), and organizational policy.

layers.name: Asset Location Update
layers.description: Updates asset master records with new location, department, cost center, and custodian. Maintains transfer history with effective dates and reasons.

layers.name: Depreciation Allocation Adjustment
layers.description: Adjusts depreciation expense allocation from transfer date forward, posts catch-up entries if needed, and ensures proper cost center charging.

layers.name: Intercompany Transfer Handling
layers.description: For transfers between legal entities, generates intercompany sale/purchase transactions, calculates gain/loss on transfer, and handles transfer pricing.

title: Asset Disposal & Retirement Management
description: Manages asset disposals including sale, scrapping, or trade-in, calculates gain or loss on disposal, posts disposal entries, and removes retired assets from active register.

layers.name: Disposal Identification
layers.description: Identifies assets for disposal through disposal requests, fully depreciated asset review, asset condition assessments, and operational obsolescence determination.

layers.name: Disposal Method Processing
layers.description: Processes different disposal methods including sale (external, internal, intercompany), scrapping (zero proceeds), trade-in (part-exchange), and donation (charitable contribution).

layers.name: Gain/Loss Calculation
layers.description: Calculates gain or loss on disposal by comparing proceeds to net book value, determines tax impact of disposal, and identifies ordinary vs. capital gains for tax purposes.

layers.name: Disposal Entry Generation
layers.description: Generates disposal journal entries removing asset cost and accumulated depreciation, recording proceeds if applicable, and recognizing gain or loss on disposal.

layers.name: Asset Retirement Obligation
layers.description: Settles asset retirement obligations if applicable, calculates actual vs. estimated retirement costs, and adjusts ARO liability and related accounts.

title: Asset Impairment Testing & Recognition
description: Performs periodic asset impairment testing, identifies indicators of impairment, calculates recoverable amounts, recognizes impairment losses, and tracks impairment reversals per accounting standards.

layers.name: Impairment Indicator Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors for impairment indicators including significant market decline, physical damage, technological obsolescence, adverse business changes, and underperformance vs. expectations.

layers.name: Recoverable Amount Determination
layers.description: Calculates recoverable amount as higher of fair value less costs to sell or value in use (present value of future cash flows from asset).

layers.name: Impairment Loss Calculation
layers.description: Compares carrying amount to recoverable amount, calculates impairment loss as excess of carrying amount over recoverable amount, and determines if loss is material.

layers.name: Impairment Recognition
layers.description: Recognizes impairment loss in income statement, reduces asset carrying amount (or revaluation reserve if applicable), and adjusts depreciation for reduced carrying amount going forward.

layers.name: Impairment Reversal Tracking
layers.description: Monitors for indicators of impairment reversal, calculates reversal amount (limited to original carrying amount less depreciation), and recognizes reversal in income.

title: Physical Asset Verification & Reconciliation
description: Manages periodic physical verification of fixed assets, reconciles physical counts to asset register, identifies missing or unrecorded assets, and ensures asset register accuracy.

layers.name: Verification Planning
layers.description: Plans physical verification cycles (annual, rotating), assigns verification teams, generates asset count lists, and schedules verification activities.

layers.name: Mobile Asset Tagging
layers.description: Enables mobile asset verification using barcode or RFID scanning, captures asset location and condition, records verification date and verifier, and flags discrepancies.

layers.name: Asset Condition Assessment
layers.description: Assesses physical condition of assets during verification, identifies damaged or non-functional assets, recommends maintenance or disposal, and updates asset records.

layers.name: Reconciliation Process
layers.description: Reconciles physical verification results to asset register, identifies assets in register not found physically, identifies physical assets not in register, and categorizes discrepancies.

layers.name: Discrepancy Resolution
layers.description: Investigates verification discrepancies, determines root causes (theft, unrecorded transfer, disposal not recorded), processes adjustments, and updates asset register.

title: Perpetual Inventory Valuation & Cost Tracking
description: Maintains perpetual inventory records with real-time valuation using appropriate costing methods (FIFO, weighted average, specific identification), ensuring accurate inventory balance and COGS calculation.

layers.name: Real-Time Inventory Tracking
layers.description: Updates inventory quantities and values in real-time for all transactions including receipts, issues, transfers, adjustments, and returns.

layers.name: Costing Method Application
layers.description: Applies appropriate inventory costing method (FIFO, weighted average, moving average, standard cost, specific identification) based on inventory category and accounting policy.

layers.name: Cost Layer Management
layers.description: For FIFO, maintains cost layers with receipt date and cost per unit, consumes oldest layers first on issuance, and tracks remaining balance in each layer.

layers.name: Standard Cost Variance Tracking
layers.description: For standard cost systems, tracks variances between actual costs and standard costs, analyzes variances by type (price, usage, volume), and adjusts inventory value.

layers.name: Inventory Value Reconciliation
layers.description: Reconciles inventory value per perpetual records to general ledger, identifies and resolves discrepancies, and ensures GL accuracy.

title: Lower of Cost or Market (LCM) Valuation
description: Performs lower of cost or market valuation by comparing inventory carrying cost to market value, identifies write-downs required, and recognizes inventory reserves to comply with accounting standards.

layers.name: Market Value Determination
layers.description: Determines market value for inventory items using replacement cost, net realizable value, or current selling price less selling costs depending on inventory category and accounting standards.

layers.name: Cost vs. Market Comparison
layers.description: Compares carrying cost to market value for each inventory item, identifies items where market is lower than cost, and calculates required write-down.

layers.name: Reserve Calculation
layers.description: Calculates inventory reserve as the aggregate excess of cost over market, applies materiality thresholds, and determines period-end reserve requirement.

layers.name: Reserve Movement Analysis
layers.description: Analyzes changes in inventory reserves between periods, identifies reserve increases (additional write-downs), reserve decreases (prior write-downs recovered), and validates reasonableness.

layers.name: Reserve Recognition
layers.description: Records inventory reserve through cost of goods sold or separate line item, maintains reserve as contra-asset account, and reverses reserve when inventory is sold or scrapped.

title: Inventory Reserve Management (Obsolescence & Slow-Moving)
description: Identifies obsolete and slow-moving inventory, calculates appropriate reserves based on aging, turnover, and management assessment, and maintains inventory reserve to reflect realizable value.

layers.name: Inventory Aging Analysis
layers.description: Ages inventory based on last movement date, receipt date, or manufacture date. Categorizes inventory by age buckets (0-90 days, 91-180 days, 181-365 days, >365 days).

layers.name: Slow-Moving Identification
layers.description: Identifies slow-moving items based on turnover ratio, demand forecast, months of supply on hand, and historical usage patterns.

layers.name: Obsolescence Detection
layers.description: Flags obsolete inventory based on product discontinuation, technology obsolescence, expiration dates, customer specification changes, or quality hold status.

layers.name: Reserve Percentage Application
layers.description: Applies reserve percentages to inventory based on age, turnover, and obsolescence status. Uses sliding scale (e.g., 25% for 180+ days, 50% for 365+ days, 100% for obsolete).

layers.name: Management Review & Override
layers.description: Routes reserve calculations to management for review, allows management override with justification, and maintains documentation of reserve decisions.

title: Cycle Counting & Perpetual Inventory Accuracy
description: Implements cycle counting program to verify physical inventory on a rotating basis, maintains perpetual inventory accuracy, identifies root causes of variances, and minimizes disruption from annual physical counts.

layers.name: Cycle Count Planning
layers.description: Plans cycle count schedule using ABC classification (high-value items counted more frequently), random sampling, and exception-based counting (negative balances, high variance items).

layers.name: Count Assignment & Execution
layers.description: Assigns count tasks to warehouse staff, generates count sheets with expected quantities (or blind counts), and captures actual counts via mobile devices or scanners.

layers.name: Variance Analysis
layers.description: Compares actual counts to perpetual records, calculates count accuracy percentage, identifies material variances, and categorizes variance types (damaged, lost, location error, transaction error).

layers.name: Root Cause Investigation
layers.description: Investigates significant variances to determine root causes such as timing differences, unrecorded transactions, system errors, theft, or process breakdowns.

layers.name: Adjustment Processing
layers.description: Processes inventory adjustments for verified variances, obtains appropriate approvals based on adjustment amount, posts to inventory and GL, and maintains adjustment history.

title: Physical Inventory Count Management
description: Manages annual or periodic physical inventory counts including count planning, count execution, variance reconciliation, and financial statement adjustment to ensure accurate period-end inventory balances.

layers.name: Pre-Count Preparation
layers.description: Prepares for physical count including count date selection, warehouse organization, inventory cutoff procedures, count team training, and count instruction documentation.

layers.name: Count Sheet Generation
layers.description: Generates count sheets organized by location, provides item descriptions and expected quantities (or blind), assigns count zones to count teams, and implements multi-count procedures for high-value items.

layers.name: Count Execution Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors count progress in real-time, tracks count sheet completion by zone, identifies uncounted areas, and ensures complete physical coverage.

layers.name: Count Reconciliation
layers.description: Reconciles physical count results to perpetual records, identifies count discrepancies, investigates significant variances, and validates recount results for major differences.

layers.name: Financial Statement Impact
layers.description: Calculates inventory adjustment amount, evaluates materiality, obtains management approval for significant adjustments, and posts adjustment to inventory and COGS.

title: Consignment & Third-Party Inventory Management
description: Manages inventory held on consignment at customer locations or received on consignment from suppliers, ensuring proper ownership recognition, timing of revenue/COGS, and accurate inventory balances.

layers.name: Consignment Out Tracking
layers.description: Tracks inventory shipped to customers on consignment, maintains separate consignment inventory account, monitors inventory at customer sites, and recognizes revenue only upon customer consumption.

layers.name: Consignment In Recognition
layers.description: Tracks inventory received on consignment from suppliers, excludes from company's inventory balance until used or purchased, and recognizes payable only upon consumption.

layers.name: Consumption Reporting
layers.description: Receives and validates consumption reports from customers (for consignment out) or generates reports for suppliers (for consignment in), reconciles consumption to shipments/receipts.

layers.name: Periodic Reconciliation
layers.description: Reconciles consignment inventory balances with customers and suppliers, investigates discrepancies, tracks returns of unused consignment inventory, and validates ownership transfer timing.

layers.name: Revenue & COGS Recognition
layers.description: Recognizes revenue and COGS for consignment out upon receiving customer consumption report, validates pricing, and ensures proper period recognition.

title: Automated Sales Tax Calculation & Remittance
description: Calculates sales tax, use tax, and VAT accurately for all transactions based on jurisdiction rules, manages exemption certificates, files returns, and remits tax to authorities.

layers.name: Tax Jurisdiction Determination
layers.description: Determines applicable tax jurisdictions based on ship-to location, bill-to location, product type, and transaction characteristics. Handles multi-jurisdiction scenarios.

layers.name: Tax Rate Application
layers.description: Applies correct tax rates based on jurisdiction, product taxability, customer tax status, and effective dates. Handles rate changes automatically.

layers.name: Exemption Certificate Management
layers.description: Validates customer tax exemption certificates, tracks certificate expiration dates, ensures certificates are current before applying exemption, and maintains certificate repository.

layers.name: Tax Accrual & Collection
layers.description: Accrues tax liability on sales transactions, collects tax from customers, tracks tax collected by jurisdiction, and reconciles tax collected to tax accrued.

layers.name: Return Preparation & Filing
layers.description: Prepares sales tax returns by jurisdiction, calculates tax due/refund, files returns electronically where required, and remits payment to tax authorities.

title: Income Tax Provision & Current Tax Calculation
description: Calculates quarterly and annual income tax provision including current tax expense, manages tax payments and estimates, and ensures accurate tax accrual on financial statements.

layers.name: Taxable Income Calculation
layers.description: Calculates taxable income starting from pre-tax book income, applying permanent differences (non-deductible expenses, tax-exempt income) and temporary differences.

layers.name: Multi-Jurisdiction Tax Allocation
layers.description: Allocates income to tax jurisdictions (federal, state, foreign), applies jurisdiction-specific tax rates, and calculates tax liability by jurisdiction.

layers.name: Tax Credit Management
layers.description: Identifies and applies available tax credits including R&D credits, foreign tax credits, investment credits, and renewable energy credits.

layers.name: Estimated Tax Payment Tracking
layers.description: Tracks estimated tax payments made during the year, applies payments against tax liability, calculates underpayment penalties if applicable.

layers.name: True-Up Adjustment
layers.description: Compares quarterly provision estimates to annual actual tax liability, calculates true-up adjustment in Q4 or year-end, and adjusts tax provision accordingly.

title: Deferred Tax Asset & Liability Management
description: Calculates and tracks deferred tax assets and liabilities arising from temporary differences between book and tax basis, assesses valuation allowance, and maintains detailed deferred tax roll-forward.

layers.name: Temporary Difference Identification
layers.description: Identifies all temporary differences including depreciation timing, inventory reserves, bad debt reserves, accruals, prepaid expenses, and revenue recognition differences.

layers.name: Deferred Tax Calculation
layers.description: Calculates deferred tax assets and liabilities by applying enacted tax rates to temporary differences, tracks by jurisdiction, and separates current vs. non-current.

layers.name: Valuation Allowance Assessment
layers.description: Assesses whether deferred tax assets are more-likely-than-not to be realized based on projected future taxable income, tax planning strategies, and reversal patterns.

layers.name: Deferred Tax Roll-Forward
layers.description: Maintains detailed roll-forward of deferred tax balances including beginning balance, provision-to-return adjustment, current year changes, and ending balance.

layers.name: Rate Change Impact
layers.description: Monitors tax law changes affecting future tax rates, recalculates deferred taxes using new rates when enacted, and recognizes rate change impact in provision.

title: Transfer Pricing Documentation & Compliance
description: Manages transfer pricing for intercompany transactions, maintains transfer pricing documentation, ensures arm's length pricing, and supports transfer pricing positions for tax audits.

layers.name: Intercompany Transaction Identification
layers.description: Identifies all intercompany transactions requiring transfer pricing including product sales, service charges, royalties, interest on loans, and cost allocations.

layers.name: Transfer Pricing Method Selection
layers.description: Selects appropriate transfer pricing method (comparable uncontrolled price, resale price, cost plus, transactional net margin, profit split) based on transaction type and available data.

layers.name: Arm's Length Pricing Validation
layers.description: Validates that intercompany pricing is at arm's length by comparing to comparable third-party transactions, industry benchmarks, or economic analyses.

layers.name: Documentation Maintenance
layers.description: Maintains contemporaneous transfer pricing documentation including functional analysis, economic analysis, comparable company analysis, and pricing policy documentation.

layers.name: True-Up Adjustment Calculation
layers.description: Calculates year-end true-up adjustments to ensure intercompany transactions achieve target margins or comply with advance pricing agreements.

flow.description: TP method selected per transaction type

title: Tax Compliance & Return Preparation
description: Manages tax compliance calendar, prepares tax returns (income, sales, property, payroll), ensures timely filing, tracks extensions, and maintains complete tax filing documentation.

layers.name: Compliance Calendar Management
layers.description: Maintains comprehensive tax compliance calendar including federal, state, local, and foreign filing deadlines for all tax types (income, sales, property, payroll, excise).

layers.name: Return Data Collection
layers.description: Collects all data required for tax return preparation including financial statements, tax adjustments, credits, apportionment factors, and supporting schedules.

layers.name: Return Preparation Workflow
layers.description: Manages return preparation process including preparer assignment, review cycles, tax manager review, and final approval before filing.

layers.name: Extension Management
layers.description: Files extensions when needed, tracks extension deadlines, monitors extended return completion, and ensures timely filing within extension period.

layers.name: Filing & Documentation
layers.description: Files returns electronically where required, obtains filing confirmations, makes associated tax payments, and maintains complete return documentation.

title: Tax Audit Management & Controversy Resolution
description: Manages tax audits and examinations, coordinates information requests, evaluates tax positions, manages uncertain tax positions (FIN 48/IAS 12), and resolves tax controversies.

layers.name: Audit Notification Management
layers.description: Receives and logs tax audit notifications, assesses audit scope and risk, assigns audit team, and develops audit response strategy.

layers.name: Information Request Response
layers.description: Manages tax authority information requests, coordinates with business units to gather requested documents, reviews for privilege, and submits timely responses.

layers.name: Tax Position Evaluation
layers.description: Evaluates tax positions under audit for technical merits, assesses likelihood of sustaining position, quantifies potential exposure, and determines settlement strategy.

layers.name: Uncertain Tax Position Accounting
layers.description: Accounts for uncertain tax positions per FIN 48/IAS 12 including recognition threshold (more-likely-than-not), measurement, and periodic reassessment.

layers.name: Settlement & Appeals
layers.description: Negotiates settlements with tax authorities, evaluates settlement offers, manages appeals process, and tracks resolution to closure.

title: Management Reporting & Dashboard Automation
description: Automates management reporting including income statement by segment, balance sheet, cash flow, KPIs, and custom reports, delivering real-time insights to management for decision-making.

layers.name: Report Definition & Templates
layers.description: Maintains library of management reports including P&L by business unit, product line, geography, customer; cash flow waterfall; working capital analysis; and custom KPI dashboards.

layers.name: Data Aggregation & Transformation
layers.description: Aggregates data from multiple sources (GL, sub-ledgers, operational systems), applies transformations and calculations, and formats for reporting.

layers.name: Automated Report Generation
layers.description: Generates management reports on schedule (daily, weekly, monthly), applies current period data, includes comparative periods, and calculates variances.

layers.name: Interactive Dashboards
layers.description: Provides interactive dashboards with drill-down capability from summary to detail, filtering by dimensions (time, entity, product), and real-time data refresh.

layers.name: Distribution & Access Control
layers.description: Distributes reports to appropriate stakeholders via email, portal, or mobile app. Controls access based on user roles and data security requirements.

title: Variance Analysis & Explanation
description: Performs automated variance analysis comparing actual results to budget, forecast, and prior periods, identifies significant variances, and generates variance explanations for management review.

layers.name: Multi-Dimensional Variance Calculation
layers.description: Calculates variances across multiple dimensions including actual vs. budget, actual vs. forecast, actual vs. prior year, current month vs. YTD run rate.

layers.name: Materiality Threshold Application
layers.description: Applies materiality thresholds (both absolute dollar and percentage) to identify variances requiring explanation, filters immaterial variances to focus management attention.

layers.name: Driver-Based Variance Analysis
layers.description: Decomposes variances into drivers such as volume, price, mix, and rate to understand root causes. Quantifies contribution of each driver to total variance.

layers.name: Automated Explanation Generation
layers.description: Generates initial variance explanations using AI analysis of transaction details, operational metrics, and historical patterns. Routes to business owners for validation and enhancement.

layers.name: Variance Commentary Workflow
layers.description: Manages workflow for variance commentary including business owner input, finance review, management approval, and inclusion in management reporting package.

title: Budget vs. Actual Tracking & Performance Management
description: Tracks actual performance against approved budget, monitors budget utilization, enforces budget controls, and provides real-time visibility into budget performance by department and project.

layers.name: Budget Loading & Allocation
layers.description: Loads approved budget into system, allocates budget by period (monthly, quarterly), department, cost center, account, and project.

layers.name: Budget Utilization Tracking
layers.description: Tracks budget consumption in real-time as transactions are posted, calculates remaining budget, projects budget run rate, and identifies areas at risk of overrun.

layers.name: Budget Control Enforcement
layers.description: Enforces budget controls by preventing or flagging transactions that would exceed budget, requires budget override approval, and maintains override audit trail.

layers.name: Commitment Tracking
layers.description: Tracks commitments (open purchase orders, contracts) that will consume budget in future periods, includes commitments in available budget calculation.

layers.name: Budget Performance Metrics
layers.description: Calculates budget performance KPIs including budget variance percentage, forecast to budget, budget utilization rate, and budget accuracy.

title: Financial Forecasting & Projection
description: Automates financial forecasting using historical trends, driver-based modeling, and machine learning to project future financial performance and support business planning decisions.

layers.name: Historical Trend Analysis
layers.description: Analyzes historical financial data to identify trends, seasonality patterns, growth rates, and cyclical behaviors that inform future projections.

layers.name: Driver-Based Modeling
layers.description: Links financial forecasts to business drivers such as headcount for salary expense, production volume for COGS, sales for revenue, and constructs driver-based forecast models.

layers.name: Scenario Planning
layers.description: Creates multiple forecast scenarios (base case, upside, downside) based on different assumptions, quantifies scenario impacts, and supports sensitivity analysis.

layers.name: Machine Learning Forecasting
layers.description: Applies machine learning algorithms to historical data to predict future outcomes, improves forecast accuracy over time through model refinement.

layers.name: Rolling Forecast Management
layers.description: Maintains rolling forecasts (e.g., 12-month rolling) that update monthly, compares forecasts to actuals, and continuously refines forecast methodology.

title: Regulatory & Statutory Reporting
description: Manages regulatory and statutory reporting requirements including SEC filings (10-K, 10-Q), local statutory reports, industry-specific reports, and ensures timely compliance with all reporting obligations.

layers.name: Regulatory Calendar Management
layers.description: Maintains comprehensive regulatory reporting calendar including SEC deadlines, local statutory deadlines, industry regulator deadlines, and stock exchange requirements.

layers.name: Report Mapping & Data Collection
layers.description: Maps financial data to regulatory report formats, collects all required data elements, validates data completeness, and ensures consistency with financial statements.

layers.name: XBRL Tagging & Validation
layers.description: Tags financial data with XBRL taxonomy for SEC filings, validates tagging accuracy and completeness, and ensures compliance with SEC XBRL requirements.

layers.name: Multi-GAAP Reporting
layers.description: Manages reporting under multiple accounting frameworks (US GAAP, IFRS, local GAAP), maintains reconciliation between frameworks, and prepares required reconciliation disclosures.

layers.name: Filing Management
layers.description: Manages filing process including report preparation, review cycles, management certification, board approval, electronic filing, and confirmation receipt.

title: Financial Statement Disclosure Management
description: Manages financial statement disclosures including notes to financials, disclosure checklists, disclosure review, and ensures compliance with all required accounting standard disclosures.

layers.name: Disclosure Requirement Tracking
layers.description: Maintains complete disclosure requirement library based on applicable accounting standards (ASC, IFRS), tracks requirement changes, and maps to financial statement areas.

layers.name: Disclosure Checklist Management
layers.description: Manages disclosure checklists with all required disclosures, tracks completion status, identifies missing disclosures, and ensures comprehensive coverage.

layers.name: Automated Disclosure Generation
layers.description: Auto-generates certain disclosures from transactional data including significant accounting policies, debt footnote, lease footnote, and revenue disaggregation.

layers.name: Disclosure Review Workflow
layers.description: Routes draft disclosures through review workflow including technical accounting review, management review, legal review, and audit committee review.

layers.name: Disclosure Quality Control
layers.description: Validates disclosure accuracy, ensures consistency between disclosures and financial statements, checks cross-references, and maintains disclosure version control.

title: Omnichannel Contact Intake & Unified Customer View
description:
Captures every inbound customer interaction — phone, email, chat, WhatsApp, social media, web form, and in-app — and consolidates it into a single, unified customer view before any agent or system responds.
  Eliminates the fragmented experience where customers repeat themselves on every channel, and gives every agent instant 360-degree context on who the customer is and what they have experienced.

layers.name: Channel Detection & Normalisation
layers.description: Identifies the inbound channel (voice, email, chat, WhatsApp, Twitter/X, Instagram, Facebook, in-app, web form, SMS) and normalises the message into a structured format — channel, timestamp, customer identifier, raw message.

layers.name: Customer Identity Resolution
layers.description: Matches the inbound contact to the customer master — using phone number, email, order ID, registered account, or device fingerprint. Links the customer to their complete interaction history, open tickets, orders, and account status.

layers.name: New vs. Returning Contact Detection
layers.description: Determines whether this is a new issue or a follow-up to an existing ticket. If an existing ticket is found for the same topic within the last 30 days, links the contact automatically rather than creating a duplicate.

layers.name: Sentiment & Urgency Pre-Scoring
layers.description: Analyses the opening message for sentiment (positive / neutral / frustrated / angry / distressed) and urgency signals (words like 'urgent', 'emergency', 'threatening', 'legal action'). Tags for downstream routing.

layers.name: Unified Context Assembly
layers.description: Assembles the complete customer context card — account details, recent orders, open tickets, prior interaction summary, sentiment tag, and channel — ready for the resolving agent or AI.

flow.description: Inbound message detected on all channels
New ticket created or existing updated

title: Intelligent Ticket Classification & Prioritisation
description:
Classifies every incoming ticket by issue type, product, and priority — applying consistent, objective rules that eliminate agent subjectivity in queue management and ensure the highest-impact issues are handled first.
  Poor classification is the root cause of most SLA breaches — AI classification is faster and more consistent than manual triage.

layers.name: Issue Type Classification
layers.description: Reads the customer message and classifies the issue into a multi-level taxonomy — Level 1 (e.g., Order), Level 2 (e.g., Delivery), Level 3 (e.g., Late Delivery vs. Missing Item vs. Wrong Item). Applies multi-label classification where a ticket covers multiple issues.

layers.name: Product/Service Identification
layers.description: Identifies which product, service, or SKU the customer is contacting about — from order reference, product name mention, or account-linked recent purchase. Links to the product's known issue list.

layers.name: Priority Scoring
layers.description: Scores each ticket on a 1-5 priority scale — combining issue severity (billing error > password reset), customer value (VIP vs. standard), SLA breach risk (time elapsed since contact), and channel urgency (social media public post > private email).

layers.name: Team & Skill Routing
layers.description: Based on classification, determines the correct team (billing, technical, logistics, returns, escalation) and required agent skill level. Avoids routing technical issues to general agents.

layers.name: SLA Assignment
layers.description: Assigns the SLA for the ticket based on priority tier, customer segment, and channel. Sets first response target, resolution target, and breach alert threshold.

title: AI-Powered First Response & Acknowledgement
description:
Sends an intelligent, personalised first response to every customer within seconds of contact — acknowledging their issue, setting expectations, and in many cases resolving the query immediately without agent involvement.
  First response time is the single highest-impact metric for customer satisfaction — sub-60-second AI response transforms the experience.

layers.name: Intent & Resolvability Assessment
layers.description: Reads the classified ticket and assesses whether it can be resolved immediately (FAQ-level, self-service, or simple action) vs. requires human or back-office investigation.

layers.name: Immediate Resolution Attempt
layers.description: For resolvable issues, executes the resolution in the first response — order status lookup and delivery ETA, password reset link generation, invoice resend, balance query response, or cancellation confirmation.

layers.name: Personalised Acknowledgement
layers.description: For non-immediately-resolvable issues, sends a personalised acknowledgement — using the customer's name, referencing their specific issue (not a generic template), providing the case ID, and stating a specific resolution timeline.

layers.name: Tone Calibration
layers.description: Calibrates the tone of the response to match the customer's sentiment — empathetic for frustrated customers, reassuring for distressed ones, efficient and direct for neutral informational queries.

layers.name: Channel-Appropriate Formatting
layers.description: Formats the response for the delivery channel — concise for SMS/WhatsApp, more detailed for email, conversational for chat, and structured for in-app.

title: Intelligent Queue Management & Workforce Routing
description:
Manages the CS team's workload in real time — routing tickets to the right agent at the right time, balancing queues across channels, predicting volume surges, and ensuring SLAs are met across all queues simultaneously.
  Queue mismanagement causes SLA breaches even when the team has sufficient capacity — intelligent routing maximises throughput.

layers.name: Real-Time Queue Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors all queues in real time — volume, age of tickets, SLA breach risk for each open ticket, and channel distribution. Identifies which queues are at risk of SLA breach in the next 30 minutes.

layers.name: Agent Availability & Skill Mapping
layers.description: Reads agent availability (logged-in, on-call, in wrap-up), current workload, and skill profile. Matches tickets to the best-fit available agent — considering skill, language, channel proficiency, and current load.

layers.name: Dynamic Priority Re-ranking
layers.description: Continuously re-ranks the queue as time passes and SLA breach risk increases. A ticket at risk of breaching gets promoted in the queue even if newer, higher-severity tickets arrive.

layers.name: Volume Surge Prediction
layers.description: Uses historical patterns (day of week, time of day, post-event spikes) to predict volume surges 2-4 hours ahead. Triggers pre-emptive staffing adjustments and AI bot scaling.

layers.name: Cross-Queue Rebalancing
layers.description: When one queue is overloaded and another is underloaded, automatically reassigns cross-trained agents to maintain balance and protect overall SLA performance.

flow.description: All queues monitored in real time
Best-fit agent identified for each ticket

title: Proactive Customer Communication
description:
Proactively communicates with customers about events that affect them — delivery updates, outages, known issues, or proactive apologies — before they need to contact support, dramatically reducing inbound contact volume.
  Every inbound contact that proactive communication prevents is a better customer experience at lower cost.

layers.name: Event Detection
layers.description: Monitors operational systems for events that will trigger customer contacts — delayed deliveries, system outages, failed payments, subscription renewals, appointment reminders, and product recalls.

layers.name: Affected Customer Identification
layers.description: For each event, identifies the affected customer segment — which customers have orders on the delayed shipping batch, which accounts are on the affected server, which subscriptions are renewing this week.

layers.name: Communication Personalisation
layers.description: Drafts personalised proactive communications — specific to the customer's situation, with their order reference, their specific delivery window, and a concrete resolution action or timeline.

layers.name: Channel Selection
layers.description: Selects the optimal channel for each proactive communication — WhatsApp / SMS for time-sensitive delivery updates, email for subscription and account matters, in-app for system notifications.

layers.name: Contact Deflection Measurement
layers.description: Tracks whether each proactive communication successfully deflected an inbound contact — comparing inbound rate from notified vs. unnotified customers for the same event.

flow.description: Customer response to proactive contact tracked

title: AI Chatbot & Conversational Resolution
description:
Operates an AI-powered conversational chatbot that resolves 55-65% of all customer contacts without human involvement — handling FAQs, account queries, order status, basic troubleshooting, and self-service transactions 24/7/365.
  The chatbot is not a call deflector — it is a resolution engine that customers actually want to use.

layers.name: Conversational Understanding
layers.description: Reads each customer message using NLU (Natural Language Understanding) — identifying intent, entities (order number, product, date), and context from the conversation history. Handles multi-turn conversations naturally.

layers.name: Knowledge Base Retrieval
layers.description: Retrieves the most relevant answer from the structured knowledge base — FAQs, product manuals, policy documents, and process guides. Uses semantic search to find answers even when the customer's words don't match the KB exactly.

layers.name: Transaction Execution
layers.description: For transactional requests (order status, cancellation, payment status, appointment booking, password reset), directly queries or updates the relevant system. Returns a real-time, personalised answer rather than a generic response.

layers.name: Confidence Threshold Management
layers.description: If the AI's confidence in the answer is below 85%, it asks one clarifying question rather than guessing. If still below threshold, it escalates to a human agent with full conversation context.

layers.name: Resolution Confirmation
layers.description: After providing an answer, asks the customer if their issue is resolved. Tracks confirmation rate. If not resolved, collects what was still missing and either retries or escalates.

title: Knowledge Base Management
description:
Maintains the customer-facing and agent-facing knowledge bases — keeping content current, accurate, and findable — so both AI and human agents always give the right answer on the first attempt.
  Outdated or missing knowledge base content is the root cause of wrong answers, long handle times, and low first-contact resolution rates.

layers.name: Content Gap Detection
layers.description: Analyses bot escalations, agent queries, and search-with-no-result events to identify topics where customers are asking questions the KB cannot answer. Flags gaps to the KB team with frequency and volume.

layers.name: Content Staleness Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors KB articles for staleness signals — articles not updated in >90 days, articles where the linked product/policy has been changed, and articles generating low satisfaction ratings.

layers.name: Automated Draft Generation
layers.description: When a gap is identified, drafts a new KB article using available internal sources — product documentation, policy documents, prior agent responses, and SME input. Routes to KB owner for review.

layers.name: Content Quality Scoring
layers.description: Scores each article on quality indicators — readability score, completeness, accuracy (last-reviewed date), and customer satisfaction rating when article is surfaced.

layers.name: Version Control & Approval
layers.description: Manages the KB content lifecycle — draft, review, approval, publish, archive. Ensures no unapproved content is published. Maintains version history.

title: Self-Service Portal & Customer Account Management
description:
Powers a comprehensive self-service portal where customers can resolve their own issues — tracking orders, managing subscriptions, raising returns, updating account details, and downloading invoices — without contacting support.
  Customers who resolve issues through self-service have higher satisfaction than those who must contact support — and at a fraction of the cost.

layers.name: Authentication & Account Access
layers.description: Manages secure customer authentication — password reset, OTP, biometric app login. Detects suspicious login attempts (new device, unusual geography) and triggers step-up authentication.

layers.name: Order & Transaction Visibility
layers.description: Provides real-time order tracking, delivery status, invoice downloads, and payment history. Integrates with logistics partners for live GPS-based delivery tracking where applicable.

layers.name: Self-Service Transactions
layers.description: Enables customers to execute transactions independently — cancel order (within policy), request return, schedule callback, update delivery address (before dispatch), manage subscription, and update payment method.

layers.name: Guided Troubleshooting
layers.description: For technical products, provides interactive troubleshooting guides — step-by-step, with decision trees adapted to the customer's specific product variant and reported symptom.

layers.name: Self-Service Analytics
layers.description: Tracks self-service usage — which features are used, where customers abandon the portal and escalate to contact, and which journeys have the highest completion rates.

title: Agent-Assisted Resolution & Co-Pilot
description:
Operates an AI co-pilot alongside every human agent — surfacing real-time answer suggestions, relevant KB articles, customer history context, and next-best-action recommendations — so agents resolve issues faster and more accurately.
  AI co-pilot transforms average agents into expert agents by giving them the right information at the right moment.

layers.name: Real-Time Transcription & Analysis
layers.description: Listens to or reads the live agent-customer conversation. Extracts the issue being discussed in real time. Updates the suggested resolution as the conversation evolves.

layers.name: Suggested Response Generation
layers.description: Generates suggested responses the agent can use or adapt — pulling from the KB, prior similar cases, and the customer's specific context. Reduces typing time and improves accuracy.

layers.name: Relevant Article Surfacing
layers.description: Automatically surfaces the 3 most relevant KB articles as the issue is being discussed — without the agent needing to search. Articles are ranked by relevance to the specific conversation, not just keywords.

layers.name: Policy & Process Guidance
layers.description: When the agent is discussing a policy decision (refund eligibility, exception, escalation), the co-pilot surfaces the exact policy clause and the approved decision matrix.

layers.name: After-Call Work Automation
layers.description: After the conversation, automatically drafts the case summary, updates the ticket with the resolution, and suggests the post-call disposition code — reducing after-call work (ACW) from 5 minutes to 30 seconds.

title: FAQ & Tier-0 Deflection Management
description:
Identifies the company's highest-volume, most-repetitive customer questions and fully automates their resolution — ensuring no agent ever has to manually answer 'What is your return policy?' for the ten-thousandth time.
  Tier-0 deflection is the highest ROI activity in customer service — systematically automating the top 50 FAQs can reduce contact volume by 30-40%.

layers.name: FAQ Volume Analysis
layers.description: Analyses all resolved tickets to identify the top 50 most frequently asked questions by volume — categorised by product, channel, and customer segment. Ranks by deflection opportunity (volume × cost-to-serve).

layers.name: Deflection Path Design
layers.description: For each top-volume FAQ, designs the optimal deflection path — self-service portal feature, bot flow, IVR option, proactive communication, or website content update.

layers.name: Automation Implementation
layers.description: Implements the deflection path — publishing the answer clearly on the website/app, configuring the bot to handle the question, or enabling a self-service transaction that eliminates the need to ask.

layers.name: Deflection Measurement
layers.description: Tracks whether each implemented deflection is working — has the contact volume for that question type decreased? Are customers finding the answer before contacting?

layers.name: Continuous Refresh
layers.description: Updates the deflection programme monthly — retiring questions whose volume has already been reduced, and adding newly emerging high-volume questions.

title: Email & Async Channel Management
description:
Manages all asynchronous customer communications — email, web forms, and messaging app threads — with AI triage, intelligent suggested responses, and automated resolution for routine requests.
  Email remains the highest-volume CS channel for complex issues — AI management dramatically improves response quality and speed.

layers.name: Email Parsing & Intent Extraction
layers.description: Reads inbound emails — extracts the primary issue, any attachments (photos, invoices, error screenshots), customer details, and urgency signals. Handles multi-issue emails and email thread context.

layers.name: Attachment Analysis
layers.description: Analyses email attachments — reads invoices, extracts order numbers from photos, reads error screenshots, and processes any supporting evidence the customer has attached.

layers.name: Response Drafting
layers.description: Drafts a complete, personalised email response for agent review — with the specific answer, relevant policy, and any required next steps. Agent reviews, modifies if needed, and sends in one click.

layers.name: Thread Management
layers.description: Tracks email thread continuity — ensures replies go to the same agent handling the thread, links new emails in a thread to the open ticket, and detects when a new email has started a new topic requiring re-classification.

layers.name: SLA Compliance
layers.description: Tracks email response SLAs — first response within 4 hours, resolution within 24 hours. Flags emails approaching breach. Enables auto-acknowledgement when agent response is not yet ready.

title: Formal Complaint Intake & Classification
description:
Distinguishes formal complaints from general queries — applies the formal complaint process, regulator-compliant acknowledgement, and root-cause-focused investigation — protecting the company from regulatory and reputational risk.
  Mishandling a complaint as a general query is a regulatory and legal risk — AI classification ensures every complaint gets the right process.

layers.name: Complaint vs. Query Detection
layers.description: Applies a multi-signal classifier to distinguish formal complaints (customer is dissatisfied and seeks remedy) from general service queries. Uses linguistic markers — 'complaint', 'unacceptable', 'never again', 'demand', 'disgusted' — and contextual signals.

layers.name: Complaint Taxonomy
layers.description: Classifies the complaint into the formal taxonomy — Product Quality, Service Failure, Billing & Payment, Staff Behaviour, Delivery/Logistics, Policy Unfairness, Discrimination, and Safety. Enables systematic root cause tracking.

layers.name: Severity Assessment
layers.description: Rates complaint severity — Critical (safety risk, regulatory, legal threat, media mention, VIP), High (significant financial impact, repeat customer, social media public post), Medium (standard complaint), Low (minor service miss).

layers.name: Regulatory Identification
layers.description: Identifies complaints that may trigger regulatory reporting obligations — consumer forum filings, sector regulator complaints (IRDAI, RBI, SEBI, FSSAI), or CCPA matters. Flags for legal review.

layers.name: Complaint Acknowledgement
layers.description: Sends a formal, regulator-compliant acknowledgement — within 24 hours, with case reference, assigned owner, and committed resolution timeline. Tone and content appropriate to severity.

title: Complaint Investigation & Root Cause Analysis
description:
Investigates every formal complaint — gathering evidence, identifying root cause, determining fault, and computing the appropriate remedy — so that the resolution is fair, defensible, and prevents recurrence.
  Complaints that are resolved without root cause analysis tend to recur — AI-powered investigation ensures systemic fixes, not just case closures.

layers.name: Evidence Gathering
layers.description: Collects all relevant evidence for the complaint — transaction records, delivery logs, call recordings or chat transcripts, agent notes, product batch records, and any customer-provided evidence (photos, videos, receipts).

layers.name: Timeline Reconstruction
layers.description: Reconstructs the full customer journey relevant to the complaint — what happened, in what sequence, and at what point things went wrong. Maps the failure point precisely.

layers.name: Root Cause Classification
layers.description: Classifies the root cause using the standard taxonomy — Process Failure (the process was not followed), Product Defect (product did not perform as specified), System Error (technology failed), Third-Party Failure (courier, partner), or Policy Failure (policy caused the poor outcome).
