layers.name: Fault Determination
layers.description: Determines fault allocation — Company Fault (full), Shared Fault (contributory), Customer Fault, or Third-Party Fault. This drives the remedy quantum and recovery options.

layers.name: Systemic Pattern Detection
layers.description: Checks whether the same root cause is appearing across multiple complaints — if so, flags a systemic issue to operations, product, or process improvement team. Prevents the same failure from causing thousands of complaints.

title: Complaint Resolution & Remedy Management
description:
Determines and executes the appropriate remedy for every formal complaint — whether refund, replacement, repair, compensation, or apology — within the company's policy framework and the handler's delegated authority.
  Inconsistent remedy decisions are a major driver of complaint escalation and regulatory action — AI-assisted decisions ensure fairness and policy compliance.

layers.name: Remedy Policy Engine
layers.description: Reads the company's remedy policy — what remedies are available for which complaint types, what the standard and maximum remedy values are, and what requires manager approval.

layers.name: Remedy Recommendation
layers.description: Based on the investigation findings and fault determination, recommends the specific remedy — full refund, partial refund, replacement product, repair, voucher, goodwill gesture, or formal apology.

layers.name: Proportionality Check
layers.description: Validates that the remedy is proportionate — neither excessively generous (creating precedent risk) nor inadequate (risking escalation). Flags outliers for manager review.

layers.name: Remedy Execution
layers.description: Once approved, executes the remedy — processes the refund through the payment gateway, raises a replacement order, initiates the repair job, or sends the compensation voucher. Confirms execution to the customer.

layers.name: Closure Communication
layers.description: Sends the formal complaint resolution communication — what the investigation found, what remedy has been provided, and what steps the company is taking to prevent recurrence. Tone calibrated to severity and fault.

title: Escalation & Executive Complaint Management
description:
Manages high-severity complaints that escalate beyond the frontline — including social media viral complaints, consumer forum filings, media enquiries, regulatory complaints, and executive complaints — with dedicated, senior-level handling.
  A complaint that goes viral or reaches the regulator is a crisis — having a dedicated, AI-assisted escalation process contains damage and improves outcomes.

layers.name: Escalation Trigger Detection
layers.description: Detects all escalation triggers — customer requests manager, consumer forum filing received, social media post gaining traction (>100 engagements), media enquiry, regulator forwarding a complaint, or repeat contact after inadequate resolution.

layers.name: Social Media Escalation Management
layers.description: For social media complaints gaining traction, activates the crisis response protocol — response within 30 minutes, move to private channel for resolution, and prevent public back-and-forth.

layers.name: Regulatory Complaint Response
layers.description: For complaints forwarded by regulators (consumer forum, sector regulator), prepares a structured, legally reviewed response within the regulatory prescribed period.

layers.name: VIP & High-Profile Escalation
layers.description: For VIP customers, media personalities, or high-profile individuals, assigns a named senior CS manager as dedicated single point of contact. White-glove resolution process.

layers.name: Learning from Escalations
layers.description: After every escalation is resolved, conducts a structured post-mortem — could it have been resolved earlier? What was the trigger? What systemic fix would prevent future similar escalations?

title: Consumer Forum & Regulatory Complaint Management
description:
Manages all consumer forum cases (District Forum, State Commission, NCDRC), CCPA complaints, and sector regulator complaints — filing timely responses, managing hearing dates, and pursuing early settlement where appropriate.
  Consumer forum defaults result in ex-parte orders and penalties — systematic management eliminates this risk while improving settlement rates.

layers.name: Forum Notice Intake
layers.description: Receives and registers all consumer forum and regulatory notices. Classifies by forum level, claim amount, and subject matter. Sets response deadlines per the Consumer Protection Act and regulatory rules.

layers.name: Case Merit Assessment
layers.description: Assesses the merits of the complaint — factual basis, policy compliance, prior resolution attempts, and likelihood of adverse order. Determines the optimal strategy: contest, settle, or settle partially.

layers.name: Written Statement Preparation
layers.description: Prepares the written statement / reply — factual background, company's position, evidence (order records, communications, resolution offered), and legal grounds.

layers.name: Hearing Date Management
layers.description: Tracks all hearing dates across forums. Ensures company representative or external counsel is available. Manages adjournment requests where needed. Never allows a default.

layers.name: Settlement Analysis
layers.description: For cases where settlement is preferred, computes the settlement range — claim amount, legal costs to defend, time value, and precedent risk. Authorises settlement within defined parameters.

title: Return Request Processing & Policy Enforcement
description:
Processes all return requests — validating eligibility against policy, authorising approved returns, rejecting ineligible ones with clear explanation, and initiating pickup logistics — in real time without agent involvement for standard cases.
  Returns are high-volume, policy-intensive interactions — automation reduces cost while ensuring consistent policy application.

layers.name: Return Eligibility Check
layers.description: Validates the return request against all eligibility criteria — return window (e.g., 7/10/30 days from delivery), product condition (new, unopened, or damaged), reason code (defective, not as described, change of mind), and item category (non-returnable items).

layers.name: Evidence Collection
layers.description: For damage or defect claims, requests photographic evidence. Validates uploaded photos — checks that they show the correct product, that the damage is clearly visible, and that the product matches the order. Uses CV (computer vision) analysis.

layers.name: Fraud Risk Scoring
layers.description: Scores each return request for fraud risk — customer's return history (frequent returner), account age, product category (high-value electronics), and consistency between claimed reason and evidence.

layers.name: Return Authorisation
layers.description: For approved returns, generates a Return Merchandise Authorisation (RMA) with barcode, pickup scheduling (for physical products), and refund/replacement commitment with timeline.

layers.name: Rejection Communication
layers.description: For rejected returns, sends a clear, empathetic explanation — which policy criterion was not met, what options remain (e.g., warranty claim instead of return), and how to appeal.

title: Refund Processing & Payment Reconciliation
description:
Processes refunds across all payment methods — UPI, card, netbanking, wallet, BNPL — computing the correct refund amount, initiating the payment reversal, tracking completion, and reconciling against the order and returns ledger.
  Refund delays are one of the top drivers of customer frustration and social media complaints — fast, accurate processing is a competitive differentiator.

layers.name: Refund Amount Computation
layers.description: Computes the correct refund amount — original payment less any applicable deductions (restocking fee if policy allows, partial refund for used product, or delivery charge non-refund). Applies payment method-specific rules.

layers.name: Payment Method Routing
layers.description: Routes the refund to the correct payment channel — UPI refund to original VPA, card refund to original card, wallet credit, BNPL reversal, or bank transfer for cash-on-delivery refunds.

layers.name: Refund Initiation & Confirmation
layers.description: Initiates the refund through the payment gateway / banking API. Captures the refund reference ID. Sends the customer an immediate notification with the refund amount, method, and expected credit timeline.

layers.name: Refund Status Tracking
layers.description: Monitors the refund journey — from initiation through processing to credit confirmation. Detects failed or stuck refunds (e.g., expired card, closed bank account) and triggers resolution.

layers.name: Finance Reconciliation
layers.description: Reconciles all refunds against the refund ledger — total refunds by day, payment method, and product category. Flags unreconciled refunds to Finance for investigation.

title: Warranty Claim Management
description:
Manages the complete warranty claim lifecycle — eligibility validation, defect assessment, repair vs. replacement decision, service centre routing, and claim closure — providing a seamless warranty experience for customers.
  Warranty claims are a trust-building moment — handled well, they create loyal customers; handled poorly, they generate viral complaints.

layers.name: Warranty Eligibility Validation
layers.description: Validates the warranty claim — product purchase date vs. warranty period, product registration status (if required), and whether the defect is covered (manufacturing defect vs. accidental damage vs. wear and tear).

layers.name: Defect Assessment
layers.description: Collects evidence of the defect — customer description, photos or videos. Classifies the defect type and severity. Determines whether the defect is consistent with a manufacturing issue (triggering full warranty) or user damage (out-of-warranty).

layers.name: Repair vs. Replacement Decision
layers.description: Based on defect type, product value, repair cost estimate, and customer preference, determines the optimal resolution — on-site repair, send-in repair, advance replacement, or refund.

layers.name: Service Network Routing
layers.description: Routes the claim to the appropriate service resource — nearest authorised service centre, in-home service technician, or pick-and-drop repair service. Books the appointment and confirms with the customer.

layers.name: Claim Tracking & Closure
layers.description: Tracks the repair or replacement through to delivery or service completion. Sends status updates to the customer. Confirms resolution satisfaction and captures post-warranty-claim CSAT.

title: Replacement & Exchange Order Management
description:
Manages all replacement and exchange orders — creating the replacement order, coordinating with warehousing for product availability, arranging simultaneous pickup of the defective item, and tracking to delivery.
  Replacement management is operationally complex — without AI coordination, replacements get stuck between CS, warehouse, and logistics.

layers.name: Replacement Order Creation
layers.description: Creates the replacement order in the OMS (Order Management System) — same SKU, size, and variant as original, or upgraded variant if the original is out of stock. Links to the original order for tracking.

layers.name: Inventory Availability Check
layers.description: Checks replacement product availability — nearest fulfillment centre, expected replenishment date if out of stock. Offers the customer alternatives (different colour, upgraded model) if original is unavailable.

layers.name: Simultaneous Return Coordination
layers.description: Coordinates the replacement dispatch with defective product pickup — either simultaneous (advance replacement) or sequential (return first, then ship). Selects based on product value and customer history.

layers.name: Priority Fulfilment
layers.description: Flags replacement orders for expedited fulfilment — next-day or 2-day delivery rather than standard timelines. Ensures the replacement experience is better than the original order experience.

layers.name: Replacement Delivery Confirmation
layers.description: Tracks the replacement to delivery. Confirms receipt with the customer. Verifies the replacement product is satisfactory before closing the original complaint.

title: Returns Fraud Detection & Policy Abuse Prevention
description:
Identifies and prevents returns fraud and policy abuse — serial returners, wardrobing, return of counterfeit items, and organised return fraud rings — while maintaining the experience for legitimate customers.
  Returns fraud costs Indian e-commerce companies an estimated 0.5-1% of GMV — AI detection provides significant financial protection.

layers.name: Customer Return History Analysis
layers.description: Builds a return behaviour profile for each customer — return rate vs. cohort baseline, return reason patterns, product categories returned, and time-since-purchase patterns.

layers.name: Return Pattern Detection
layers.description: Identifies suspicious return patterns — wardrobing (returning after use event), buy-return cycling (same item multiple times), systematic size abuse, and bulk return fraud.

layers.name: Product Authenticity Check
layers.description: Uses computer vision on return photos to identify return of counterfeit products — different product than ordered, tampered packaging, missing serial numbers, or visible quality differences.

layers.name: Network Analysis
layers.description: Detects organised fraud rings — multiple accounts with the same delivery address, phone, or payment method; accounts created just before high-value purchases; and coordinated bulk return activity.

layers.name: Policy Enforcement Actions
layers.description: Based on fraud risk score, applies graduated policy responses — enhanced verification for medium-risk, manual review for high-risk, account suspension for confirmed fraud, and policy exception denial for serial abusers.

title: Field Service Scheduling & Dispatch
description:
Optimises field service scheduling — matching technician skills to job requirements, minimising travel time, predicting job duration, and dynamically re-routing when situations change — maximising first-visit resolution rate.
  Inefficient field service scheduling costs 30-40% in productivity — AI optimisation dramatically improves technician utilisation and customer satisfaction.

layers.name: Job Requirement Analysis
layers.description: Reads the service request — product type, reported fault, warranty or paid service, customer location, and any special requirements (requires senior technician, specific tools). Determines skill and time requirements.

layers.name: Technician Skill Matching
layers.description: Matches job requirements to available technicians — certified for the product, carries required tools and parts, language match for customer location, and within feasible travel distance.

layers.name: Route & Time Optimisation
layers.description: Optimises the daily schedule for each technician — sequencing jobs to minimise travel time, accounting for realistic job durations, traffic, and customer availability windows. Uses live traffic data.

layers.name: Parts Pre-Staging
layers.description: Based on the reported fault, predicts likely required spare parts. Checks technician van stock. Triggers parts collection from depot if van stock is insufficient — ensuring first-visit fix rate.

layers.name: Dynamic Re-routing
layers.description: Re-routes technicians in real time when situations change — job overrun, emergency priority job, cancelled appointment, or traffic disruption. Updates customer ETAs automatically.

title: High-Value Customer & VIP Service Management
description:
Provides premium, differentiated service to the company's highest-value customers — identifying VIPs from the customer base, routing them to dedicated senior agents, applying enhanced SLAs, and proactively managing their service experience.
  VIP customers represent a disproportionate share of revenue — losing one due to a poor service experience is far more costly than the investment in premium service.

layers.name: VIP Identification
layers.description: Identifies VIP customers from the customer master — based on lifetime value (top 5%), spend in last 12 months, strategic account designation, referral volume, or manual VIP flag. Segments into Gold, Platinum, and Diamond tiers.

layers.name: Dedicated Routing
layers.description: Routes all VIP contacts to a dedicated VIP service team — bypassing standard queues, with SLA targets of < 2 minutes first response and < 4 hours resolution for all tiers.

layers.name: Named Relationship Manager
layers.description: For Diamond tier customers, assigns a named relationship manager — the customer always knows who to contact. The RM receives a daily brief on the customer's recent interactions and account status.

layers.name: Proactive Account Health Monitoring
layers.description: Proactively monitors VIP customer account health — recent orders, delivery performance, contact history, NPS score trend, and any open issues. Triggers proactive outreach when health indicators decline.

layers.name: VIP Recovery Programme
layers.description: When a VIP customer has had a poor experience, activates the VIP recovery programme — senior management outreach, enhanced remedy, and a structured win-back plan.

title: Escalation Path Management
description:
Manages the complete escalation hierarchy — from frontline agent to team lead to CS manager to CS Director — ensuring every escalation follows a clear process, is handled at the right level, and is resolved without unnecessary delay.
  Poorly managed escalations damage customer trust and create avoidable costs — structured escalation paths improve outcomes at every level.

layers.name: Escalation Trigger Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors live conversations for escalation triggers — customer explicitly requests manager, agent marks case as stuck, issue exceeds agent authority, customer mentions regulator or legal action, or conversation sentiment hits critical threshold.

layers.name: Level Determination
layers.description: Determines the appropriate escalation level — not every escalation needs the CS Director. Level 1 (team lead): authority issues. Level 2 (CS Manager): policy exceptions, high-value remedies. Level 3 (CS Director): legal threat, media, VIP. Level 4 (C-suite): strategic accounts, regulatory.

layers.name: Context Handoff
layers.description: Prepares a complete escalation brief for the receiving manager — customer history, issue summary, what was tried and failed, what the customer wants, and recommended resolution.

layers.name: Escalation SLA
layers.description: Applies a strict SLA to each escalation level — L1: acknowledge within 15 min, resolve within 4 hrs. L2: acknowledge within 30 min, resolve within 24 hrs. L3: acknowledge within 15 min, resolve within 4 hrs (priority).

layers.name: Escalation Prevention Learning
layers.description: Analyses all escalations to identify whether they could have been resolved at frontline level with better tools, training, or authority. Feeds recommendations to training and policy teams.

title: Third-Party Service Partner Management
description:
Manages the performance of third-party service partners — delivery partners, installation contractors, repair network, and outsourced CS teams — ensuring they deliver the experience the company has promised customers.
  Third parties cause ~40% of customer complaints but companies take 100% of the customer relationship risk — structured performance management is essential.

layers.name: Partner Performance Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks key performance metrics for each third-party partner — delivery success rate, damage in transit rate, installation first-visit success, repair turnaround time, and outsourced CS CSAT score.

layers.name: Customer Complaint Attribution
layers.description: Attributes complaints to the responsible party — company fault vs. third-party fault. For third-party-attributed complaints, automatically triggers a claim against the partner's SLA.

layers.name: SLA Penalty Computation
layers.description: Computes SLA penalties against underperforming partners — as per the service agreement. Prepares the penalty deduction claim and submits it to Finance and the partner.

layers.name: Partner Performance Reviews
layers.description: Prepares quarterly performance review packs for each key partner — trends, root cause analysis, and improvement requirements. Escalates chronic underperformers for contract review.

layers.name: Partner Risk Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors partner financial health, compliance status, and capacity adequacy. Flags partners at risk of service disruption — enabling early contingency planning.

title: CSAT, NPS & CES Survey Management
description:
Deploys and analyses customer satisfaction, Net Promoter Score, and Customer Effort Score surveys across all touchpoints — providing real-time insight into customer experience quality and trends.
  Real-time CSAT/NPS data allows the CS team to catch declining satisfaction before it becomes churn — it is the early warning system for customer experience.

layers.name: Survey Trigger & Deployment
layers.description: Triggers CSAT surveys at the right moments — immediately after contact resolution (CSAT), 7 days after purchase (transactional NPS), and quarterly for relationship NPS. Selects channel (WhatsApp, email, in-app) based on customer preference.

layers.name: Response Collection & Validation
layers.description: Collects survey responses. Validates for completeness and detects fraudulent or incentivised responses (same IP, batch responses). Applies response rate optimisation — reminder at 48 hours for non-respondents.

layers.name: Score Computation
layers.description: Computes CSAT (% satisfied), NPS (Promoters% - Detractors%), and CES (average effort score). Segments by channel, product, agent, team, and customer tier. Tracks trend over time.

layers.name: Verbatim Analysis
layers.description: Applies NLP to open-text survey responses — extracts themes, sentiment, and specific improvement suggestions. Identifies the top 5 drivers of high scores and low scores.

layers.name: Closed-Loop Follow-Up
layers.description: For Detractors (NPS 0-6) and low CSAT (1-2 stars), triggers an automatic follow-up — acknowledges the negative experience, escalates to a senior agent, and offers to resolve the underlying issue.

title: Customer Effort Score & Journey Friction Analysis
description:
Analyses the customer effort required across every service journey — identifying high-friction points where customers have to work too hard to get resolution — and recommends specific changes to reduce effort.
  Customer Effort is the single best predictor of loyalty and churn — systematically reducing effort is the highest-ROI CS investment.

layers.name: Journey Mapping
layers.description: Maps all customer service journeys — from initial contact through resolution and closure. For each journey, identifies the steps, channels, and handoffs involved.

layers.name: Effort Signal Collection
layers.description: Collects effort signals at every touchpoint — CES survey scores, number of contacts to resolve an issue, number of transfers, time waiting, number of times customer had to re-explain, and self-service abandonment events.

layers.name: High-Effort Journey Identification
layers.description: Identifies the journeys with highest customer effort — computed from CES score, repeat contact rate, escalation rate, and complaint rate. Ranks by impact (volume × effort score).

layers.name: Root Cause of Friction
layers.description: For each high-effort journey, identifies the friction root cause — policy too complex, information unavailable, multiple handoffs required, or process not customer-centric.

layers.name: Improvement Recommendation
layers.description: Generates specific, actionable improvement recommendations for each high-effort journey — process change, policy simplification, KB article addition, or self-service feature.

title: Social Media Listening & Reputation Management
description:
Monitors all social media channels for brand mentions, product discussions, and emerging complaints — detecting issues before they go viral and enabling real-time brand reputation management.
  A social media crisis can damage brand value worth hundreds of crores in days — real-time listening and rapid response is the defence.

layers.name: Social Listening
layers.description: Monitors Twitter/X, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, LinkedIn, Reddit, Google Reviews, and App Store reviews for all brand mentions — direct tags, indirect mentions, hashtags, and competitor comparisons.

layers.name: Sentiment & Virality Scoring
layers.description: Scores each mention for sentiment (positive/neutral/negative) and virality risk (engagement rate, follower count of poster, trending hashtag association). Prioritises by combined risk score.

layers.name: Issue Theme Clustering
layers.description: Clusters related mentions by theme — product defect mentions, pricing complaints, service failures, or PR crises. Identifies when a theme is reaching critical mass (potential viral event).

layers.name: Response Routing & Drafting
layers.description: Routes mentions to the appropriate team — CS team for individual complaints, PR for media mentions, Legal for defamatory content. Drafts platform-appropriate responses for CS team to review and post.

layers.name: Crisis Detection & Escalation
layers.description: Detects crisis signals — sudden spike in negative mentions, influencer post, news article pickup. Activates the crisis communication protocol and alerts senior management.

flow.description: Mentions scored for sentiment and virality

title: Customer Feedback Analytics & Product Intelligence
description:
Synthesises all customer feedback signals — surveys, complaint themes, chat transcripts, social media, and app reviews — into structured product and service intelligence that drives improvement decisions.
  Customer feedback is the richest source of product and service improvement data — but only if it is systematically captured, analysed, and acted upon.

layers.name: Multi-Source Feedback Aggregation
layers.description: Aggregates customer feedback from all sources — CSAT verbatim, complaint root causes, bot escalation reasons, social media comments, app store reviews, and support chat transcripts.

layers.name: Theme Extraction & Quantification
layers.description: Applies NLP to extract themes from all text sources. Quantifies each theme by frequency and sentiment severity. Identifies themes that are increasing in frequency (emerging issues).

layers.name: Product vs. Service Attribution
layers.description: Classifies each feedback theme as a Product issue (design, quality, features), Service issue (CS process, agent behaviour, policy), or Operational issue (delivery, packaging, logistics).

layers.name: Product Intelligence Reporting
layers.description: Packages product-attributed feedback into structured product intelligence reports — top 10 product complaints, feature gap requests, quality issues by batch or supplier, and competitive comparisons from reviews.

layers.name: Feedback Loop Closure
layers.description: Tracks which feedback themes have been actioned by Product, Operations, or CS — and whether the action has resulted in a measurable improvement in feedback scores for that theme.

flow.description: Product vs. service vs. ops attribution

title: CS Performance Analytics & Reporting
description:
Measures, analyses, and reports on all CS function performance metrics — agent productivity, team KPIs, SLA performance, channel metrics, and cost-to-serve — enabling data-driven management of the CS operation.
  CS leaders who manage by intuition manage badly — comprehensive analytics transforms CS into a measurable, improvable operation.

layers.name: Real-Time Operations Dashboard
layers.description: Provides a live view of CS operations — contacts in queue, agent availability, SLA compliance rate, AHT by channel, bot resolution rate, and CSAT rolling 7-day. Enables intraday management decisions.

layers.name: Agent Performance Analytics
layers.description: Measures individual agent performance — AHT, FCR rate, CSAT score, adherence to schedule, and quality score. Identifies top performers and underperformers for coaching.

layers.name: SLA Performance Analysis
layers.description: Analyses SLA performance — by channel, tier, team, and issue type. Identifies which segments are consistently breaching and root causes (volume spike, understaffing, or routing issue).

layers.name: Cost-to-Serve Analysis
layers.description: Computes cost per contact by channel and issue type — and cost per resolved ticket. Tracks trend. Identifies the most expensive contact types and the opportunities to reduce cost through automation or prevention.

layers.name: Executive & Board Reporting
layers.description: Prepares the monthly CS performance report for the CCO and CEO — contact volume trend, CSAT/NPS, SLA performance, cost-to-serve, and key improvements. Includes forward-looking volume forecast.

title: Quality Assurance & Interaction Monitoring
description:
Evaluates the quality of every agent interaction — using automated AI scoring supplemented by targeted human review — identifying coaching needs, compliance issues, and best practices.
  Quality monitoring at scale is impossible manually — AI QA covers 100% of interactions vs. the 2-5% manual sample, giving a true picture.

layers.name: 100% Interaction Coverage
layers.description: Reads or listens to every agent interaction — chat transcripts, email responses, call recordings (post-speech-to-text). Applies the standard QA scorecard to each interaction automatically.

layers.name: QA Scorecard Evaluation
layers.description: Scores each interaction against the defined scorecard — greeting, problem identification, knowledge accuracy, empathy, solution quality, resolution confirmation, and compliance disclosures. Scores 0-100.

layers.name: Compliance Monitoring
layers.description: Checks every interaction for mandatory compliance elements — regulatory disclosures (for financial/insurance), data privacy statements, escalation rights disclosure, and complaint recording obligation.

layers.name: Agent Coaching Identification
layers.description: Identifies specific coaching needs per agent — not just a score, but the specific skill gap (e.g., Agent X consistently scores low on empathy, Agent Y has knowledge accuracy issues on Product Z).

layers.name: Best Practice Extraction
layers.description: Identifies interactions with scores above 95 — extracts what made them excellent (specific phrases, resolution approaches, de-escalation techniques) and packages them for team training.

flow.description: AI scores vs. human scores calibrated
QA report by agent and team

title: Agent Training & Knowledge Development
description:
Identifies CS team training needs, designs and delivers targeted learning — product knowledge, process skills, and soft skills — and tracks competency development to ensure every agent is equipped to resolve issues correctly.
  Undertrained agents are the root cause of poor FCR, low CSAT, and high escalation rates — systematic training directly improves all CS metrics.

layers.name: Training Need Identification
layers.description: Analyses QA scores, escalation drivers, and FCR data by agent to identify specific knowledge and skill gaps. Generates a prioritised training agenda per agent — not generic training, but gap-specific development.

layers.name: Learning Content Curation
layers.description: Curates training content for each identified gap — product knowledge modules, process walkthroughs, policy guides, and soft skills (empathy, de-escalation, active listening). Links to real interaction examples.

layers.name: Personalised Learning Paths
layers.description: Creates a personalised learning path for each agent — mixing mandatory content (product updates, compliance) with gap-specific content. Schedules micro-learning sessions during natural workflow gaps.

layers.name: Onboarding Programme
layers.description: Manages the new agent onboarding programme — structured 4-week curriculum combining product knowledge, process training, shadowing, buddy calls, and progressive live contact handling with QA monitoring.

layers.name: Competency Assessment
layers.description: Runs regular knowledge checks — product quizzes, scenario-based assessments, and simulation calls. Tracks competency development over time. Identifies agents who are not progressing despite training.

title: Workforce Management & Capacity Planning
description:
Forecasts contact volumes, plans staffing levels, manages schedules, and optimises agent utilisation — ensuring the CS team is the right size, in the right place, at the right time, at the right cost.
  Workforce planning errors cost both ways — understaffing destroys customer experience, overstaffing wastes budget.

layers.name: Volume Forecasting
layers.description: Forecasts contact volume by channel, day, and half-hour interval — using historical patterns, known upcoming events (promotions, product launches, seasonal peaks), and leading indicators (order volume, delivery batches).

layers.name: Staffing Requirement Computation
layers.description: Converts volume forecast into staffing requirements — accounting for AHT by channel, occupancy targets, shrinkage (breaks, training, absence), and service level targets.

layers.name: Schedule Building
layers.description: Builds optimal agent schedules — matching staffing to the intraday demand curve, respecting legal requirements (working hours, breaks), agent preferences, and part-time/flexible arrangements.

layers.name: Real-Time Adherence
layers.description: Monitors agents' adherence to their scheduled activities in real time — are they on time, on the right channel, taking breaks at the right time? Alerts supervisors to adherence issues.

layers.name: Intraday Reforecasting
layers.description: Continuously updates the volume forecast through the day — if actual volume is higher or lower than forecast, triggers intraday staffing adjustments: overtime, voluntary early departure, or cross-skilling.

title: CS Technology & Tools Management
description:
Manages the CS technology stack — CRM, ticketing system, telephony/ACD, chatbot platform, QA tools, and WFM software — ensuring all systems are integrated, performant, and delivering the capabilities the team needs.
  CS technology failures directly impact customer experience and agent productivity — proactive management prevents avoidable incidents.

layers.name: Systems Health Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors the health of all CS technology systems in real time — uptime, response time, API error rates, and integration point health. Detects degradation before it becomes a customer-visible failure.

layers.name: Integration Management
layers.description: Manages all CS system integrations — CRM to OMS, telephony to CRM, chatbot to knowledge base. Monitors data flow, detects sync failures, and ensures customer data is consistent across systems.

layers.name: Agent Experience Optimisation
layers.description: Identifies friction in the agent desktop — multiple system switches per contact, slow search results, missing data fields. Recommends UX improvements that reduce AHT.

layers.name: Technology Roadmap
layers.description: Maintains the CS technology roadmap — mapping capability gaps to available technology solutions, prioritising investments by ROI, and managing the procurement and implementation pipeline.

layers.name: Vendor & Licence Management
layers.description: Manages technology vendor relationships — SLAs, licence costs, renewal dates, and performance reviews. Identifies opportunities for licence optimisation and vendor consolidation.

title: Complaint Root Cause & Process Improvement
description:
Analyses complaint and contact data to identify systemic process failures, product issues, and policy gaps — and drives cross-functional improvement projects that eliminate the root causes of customer dissatisfaction.
  The best CS team is the one that uses its contact data to eliminate the need for contacts — root cause improvement is the highest-leverage CS activity.

layers.name: Contact Root Cause Analysis
layers.description: Analyses all contact and complaint data monthly — what are customers contacting about, what is the root cause, which product/process/policy is responsible, and is the frequency increasing?

layers.name: Systemic Issue Identification
layers.description: Identifies issues that are systemic rather than one-off — defined as the same root cause appearing in 5+ contacts in a month, or a 20% increase in a contact type over two consecutive months.

layers.name: Cross-Functional Impact Assessment
layers.description: Quantifies the impact of each systemic issue — contacts generated, customer satisfaction impact, cost-to-serve, and downstream revenue risk (churn likelihood for affected customers).

layers.name: Improvement Project Initiation
layers.description: Raises formal improvement projects for each high-impact systemic issue — with business case, responsible owner (Operations, Product, Policy), timeline, and success metric.

layers.name: Impact Measurement
layers.description: Tracks whether each improvement project has delivered the expected reduction in contact volume or improvement in CSAT for the targeted issue. Closes the loop between insight and action.

flow.description: Monthly contact root cause analysis run

title: CS Budget Management & ROI Reporting
description:
Plans and manages the CS department budget — headcount costs, technology, training, outsourcing, and facility costs — while measuring and communicating the ROI of CS investments to the CFO and CEO.
  CS departments that cannot demonstrate ROI are always the first cut — data-driven value measurement protects the function and justifies investment.

layers.name: Budget Planning
layers.description: Builds the annual CS budget — headcount (internal + outsourced), technology licences, training, quality tools, and facility costs. Benchmarks unit cost metrics against industry (cost per contact, cost per resolution).

layers.name: Cost-Per-Contact Tracking
layers.description: Tracks cost per contact by channel in real time — phone (most expensive), email (moderate), chat (lower), and bot (lowest). Identifies channel shift opportunities that reduce cost without sacrificing experience.

layers.name: Technology ROI Measurement
layers.description: Measures the ROI of each CS technology investment — bot platform (contacts deflected × cost per contact), QA tool (training cost reduction + CSAT improvement), and WFM (staffing efficiency gain).

layers.name: Revenue Impact of CS
layers.description: Quantifies the revenue impact of CS quality — retention value from customer recovery, upsell during service contacts, and NPS improvement correlated with revenue growth.

layers.name: CFO & CEO Reporting
layers.description: Prepares the monthly CS financial report — costs vs. budget, cost per contact trend, automation savings, revenue impact, and forward-looking investment case for planned technology.

title: Invoice Classification & Routing
description:
Reads incoming invoice from any channel (email, portal, EDI, scan) and classifies it by type, assigns the right approval workflow, and routes without human involvement.
  Handles PO-Based, Non-PO, Utility, SES, Credit Notes, Debit Notes, Proforma, and Advance invoices.
  Determines urgency (due date proximity), routes to the correct approver hierarchy, and tracks SLA.

layers.name: Document Reading
layers.description: OCR extracts sender, subject, attachment type, invoice number, date, and amount. NLP classifies document as invoice vs. statement vs. remittance vs. other.

layers.name: PO Reference Detection
layers.description: Searches for PO number in document body. If found and valid in ERP — classified PO-Based. If absent — triggers Non-PO routing.

layers.name: Vendor Category Lookup
layers.description: Queries vendor master for spend category (utilities, services, goods, labour). Maps to invoice type (Utility, SES, Goods, Professional Services).

layers.name: Urgency Scoring
layers.description: Reads due date, payment terms, and any discount windows. Scores urgency (High / Medium / Low) to prioritize queue position.

layers.name: Workflow Assignment
layers.description: Matches invoice type + amount + cost center to the approval matrix. Assigns approver hierarchy, SLA timer, and escalation path.

flow.description: ERP API call for PO reference
Due date and discount window analysis

title: Duplicate Invoice Detection
description:
Detects duplicate invoices before payment — including near-duplicates that exact-match rules miss (same amount, slightly different date or invoice number).
  Operates across all invoice types and channels. Catches intentional vendor fraud as well as inadvertent re-submissions.

layers.name: Fingerprint Generation
layers.description: Creates a multi-dimensional fingerprint per invoice: Vendor ID + Invoice Number + Amount + Invoice Date + PO Number. Stores fingerprint hash in deduplication index.

layers.name: Exact Match Scan
layers.description: Queries dedup index for identical fingerprint. Instant flag if found — highest confidence duplicate.

layers.name: Fuzzy Match Analysis
layers.description: For near-duplicates, computes similarity scores across fields. Invoice numbers differing by one character, amounts within 1% tolerance, dates within 7-day window — all flagged as suspicious.

layers.name: Historical Pattern Analysis
layers.description: Checks if this vendor has a history of re-submissions. Assigns a vendor fraud risk score. High-risk vendors require manual verification even for low-similarity matches.

layers.name: Context Validation
layers.description: Validates against GRN and delivery records. If goods/services were received only once, a second invoice for the same PO is definitively duplicate regardless of superficial differences.

title: TDS Category & Rate Determination
description:
Autonomously assigns the correct TDS section (194C, 194J, 194I, 194H, 194D, etc.), rate, and threshold status for every vendor payment — eliminating manual lookup and computation.
  Handles PAN-linked rate variations, higher deduction for non-PAN vendors, and lower deduction certificates.

layers.name: Payment Nature Classification
layers.description: Reads invoice description and vendor category to classify the nature of payment — Works Contract, Professional Service, Rent, Commission, Insurance, Interest, etc.

layers.name: Section Mapping
layers.description: Maps payment nature to the correct TDS section using the Income Tax Act schedule. Handles overlapping categories (e.g., technical services can be 194C or 194J depending on context).

layers.name: Vendor PAN Validation
layers.description: Checks vendor PAN status in master data. Valid PAN = standard rate. Invalid/missing PAN = 20% higher deduction rate per Section 206AA.

layers.name: Threshold Accumulation Check
layers.description: Aggregates all payments to this vendor in the current FY. Checks against annual threshold (e.g., 194C: Rs.30,000 per transaction or Rs.1,00,000 cumulative). TDS triggered only when threshold crossed.

layers.name: Lower Deduction Certificate
layers.description: Checks if vendor has submitted Form 15G/15H or a lower deduction certificate under Section 197. Applies reduced rate or nil deduction accordingly.

flow.description: Invoice nature and vendor category extracted
TDS section lookup against payment nature
YTD payment accumulation computed for vendor

title: Vendor Statement Reconciliation
description:
Matches vendor ledger balance in company books against the vendor's own statement — line by line — identifies timing differences vs. genuine discrepancies, and resolves or escalates.
  Runs automatically at period end or on demand.

layers.name: Statement Ingestion
layers.description: Reads vendor statement (email attachment, PDF, Excel) using OCR and structured extraction. Parses each line: invoice number, date, amount, running balance.

layers.name: Ledger Pull
layers.description: Fetches all vendor transactions from company AP ledger for the reconciliation period. Structures data in matching format.

layers.name: Matching Logic
layers.description: Performs waterfall matching — exact match first (invoice number + amount), then fuzzy match (amount within tolerance, date within window), then unmatched residuals.

layers.name: Discrepancy Classification
layers.description: Classifies each unmatched item — timing difference (invoice in transit), credit note not applied, payment not acknowledged, duplicate charge, or genuine dispute.

layers.name: Resolution Routing
layers.description: Timing differences auto-resolved. Genuine discrepancies trigger vendor communication draft. Disputes above threshold escalated to AP Manager.

flow.description: Vendor query draft prepared for disputes

title: Early Payment Discount Identification
description: Proactively identifies invoices eligible for early payment discounts, validates positive NPV, and triggers accelerated payment — capturing value that is routinely missed in manual AP.

layers.name: Payment Terms Extraction
layers.description: Reads discount terms from invoice (e.g., 2/10 net 30 = 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Parses structured terms and free-text descriptions.

layers.name: NPV Computation
layers.description: Calculates annualised return of taking the discount. Formula: (Discount% / (1 - Discount%)) x (365 / (Net Days - Discount Days)). Compares against company WACC or benchmark rate.

layers.name: Cash Position Check
layers.description: Queries treasury system for available cash balance and 7-day cash forecast. Confirms payment will not create a liquidity shortfall.

layers.name: Vendor Relationship Factor
layers.description: Checks if vendor is strategic (preferred supplier list). Strategic vendors may warrant accelerated payment even for marginal NPV.

layers.name: Approval & Payment Trigger
layers.description: For positive-NPV discounts with available cash, raises payment proposal to CFO/Treasurer. On approval, triggers accelerated payment in ERP.

flow.description: Accelerated payment instruction sent to bank

title: GSTR-2B Reconciliation
description: Matches the company purchase register against GSTR-2B data from the GST portal, identifies mismatches, quantifies ITC at risk, and communicates with vendors for missing/incorrect filings.

layers.name: GSTR-2B Download
layers.description: Fetches GSTR-2B JSON/Excel from GSTN portal via API for the relevant period. Parses all supplier-wise credit entries.

layers.name: Purchase Register Pull
layers.description: Extracts all inward supply entries from company books for the same period — invoice-wise with GSTIN, amount, and GST components.

layers.name: Multi-Level Matching
layers.description: Matches GSTR-2B entries to purchase register — exact match (GSTIN + Invoice No + IGST/CGST/SGST amounts), then amount-only match for date/number variations.

layers.name: Mismatch Classification
layers.description: Classifies each mismatch — missing in GSTR-2B (vendor has not filed), amount difference (vendor filed incorrect amount), reversed in next period, GSTIN mismatch.

layers.name: ITC Risk Quantification
layers.description: Computes total ITC at risk from mismatches. Prioritises vendors by ITC value. Generates vendor-wise correction request with evidence.

flow.description: GSTR-2B data fetched from GSTN API

title: Cash Application & Receipt Allocation
description: Maps incoming bank receipts to open customer invoices autonomously — even when remittance advice is partial, missing, or contains short payments and deductions.

layers.name: Bank Feed Ingestion
layers.description: Reads bank statement (MT940, CSV, API feed) in real time. Extracts each credit entry: amount, value date, UTR, narration text.

layers.name: Remittance Parsing
layers.description: Checks for accompanying remittance advice (email, portal, EDI). Extracts invoice references if present. Falls back to amount-matching if remittance is absent.

layers.name: Customer Identification
layers.description: Maps receipt to customer using bank account number, UTR narration, or historical payment pattern. Handles multiple entities paying on behalf of one customer.

layers.name: Invoice Matching
layers.description: Matches receipt amount to open invoice combinations — exact match, FIFO allocation, or partial payment. Handles multi-invoice payments and short payments.

layers.name: Deduction Classification
layers.description: Short payments classified as valid deductions (agreed discount, claim) or unauthorized. Valid deductions auto-processed; unauthorized flagged for dispute.

title: Customer Deduction Classification
description: When a customer pays less than the invoice, the agent determines whether the short payment is a valid deduction or unauthorized — and routes to the correct resolution path.

layers.name: Deduction Detection
layers.description: Identifies short payment by comparing remittance / payment amount against open invoice total. Computes deduction amount per invoice.

layers.name: Remittance Code Classification
layers.description: Reads customer-provided deduction codes (pricing dispute, quality claim, freight allowance, early payment discount, promotional allowance) from remittance or email.

layers.name: Contract & Policy Lookup
layers.description: Checks customer contract for agreed deduction types and limits. Validates claimed deduction against contractual entitlements.

layers.name: Historical Deduction Pattern
layers.description: Reviews customer's past deduction history. Flags customers with unusual frequency or deduction types not previously claimed.

layers.name: Validity Decision & Routing
layers.description: Valid deductions auto-processed and credit note raised. Invalid deductions routed to sales / key account manager with evidence pack.

title: Dunning & Collections Prioritisation
description: Scores overdue invoices by collection risk, autonomously drafts and sends right-tone communications per customer segment, and tracks response to escalate intelligently.

layers.name: Aging Analysis
layers.description: Scans AR ledger for all overdue invoices. Segments by aging bucket (0-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days). Computes total exposure per customer.

layers.name: Risk Scoring
layers.description: Scores each customer on payment behaviour (historical DSO, broken promises, dispute frequency), amount at risk, credit limit utilisation, and industry sector risk.

layers.name: Relationship Context
layers.description: Checks customer strategic importance (top 20 customer list), ongoing sales opportunities, and recent complaint history before determining communication tone.

layers.name: Communication Drafting
layers.description: Generates dunning message calibrated to risk score and relationship context — from gentle reminder (low risk, valued customer) to formal demand (high risk, transactional).

layers.name: Response Tracking & Escalation
layers.description: Monitors responses. No reply within SLA triggers next-level escalation. Promises to pay logged and tracked against follow-up dates.

flow.description: AR ledger scanned for overdue items
Senior contact or legal notice triggered

title: Credit Limit Assessment
description: Reviews new customer credit applications or periodic reviews by analysing financials, payment behaviour, and market data — recommends credit limits without manual underwriting.

layers.name: Application Data Extraction
layers.description: Reads credit application form, company registration documents, audited financials, and bank references. Extracts key financial metrics: turnover, net worth, current ratio, debt levels.

layers.name: Payment History Analysis
layers.description: For existing customers, retrieves internal payment history — average DPD, dispute frequency, broken promises. For new customers, requests trade references.

layers.name: External Credit Bureau Data
layers.description: Queries CIBIL Commercial, Dun & Bradstreet, or Experian Business for credit score, payment rating, and any adverse filings.

layers.name: Industry & Sector Risk
layers.description: Applies sector risk overlay — distressed industries (real estate, telecom in certain periods) get haircut on credit limit recommendation.

layers.name: Credit Limit Computation
layers.description: Applies credit limit model — typically a multiple of monthly purchase volume constrained by customer net worth and sector risk. Recommends limit band with rationale.

flow.description: Approved limit set in customer master

title: Dispute Resolution Agent
description: Receives customer dispute communications, classifies dispute type, retrieves all supporting documents, resolves straightforward cases autonomously, and routes complex disputes with a complete evidence pack.

layers.name: Dispute Intake
layers.description: Reads customer email, portal submission, or phone call transcript. Classifies dispute type: pricing error, quantity discrepancy, quality claim, duplicate billing, goods not received.

layers.name: Evidence Retrieval
layers.description: Pulls all relevant documents — original invoice, purchase order, delivery note, proof of delivery, quality inspection report, rate contract — automatically.

layers.name: Validity Assessment
layers.description: Compares customer claim against retrieved evidence. Pricing dispute: checks invoice rate vs. rate contract. Quantity dispute: checks delivery note vs. invoice quantity.

layers.name: Resolution Path
layers.description: Valid dispute — auto-raise credit note or corrected invoice. Invalid dispute — draft rebuttal with evidence. Ambiguous — route to KAM with full pack.

layers.name: Communication & Closure
layers.description: Drafts customer response — acknowledgement, resolution confirmation, or rebuttal. Tracks case to closure. Updates AR ledger on resolution.

title: GL Code Allocation
description: Multi-layer AI reasoning assigns the correct General Ledger account code to every transaction — vendor payments, expense claims, bank receipts, and journal entries — with context-aware decision making.

layers.name: Document Intelligence
layers.description: NLP reads invoice/transaction description to classify spend intent — service, goods, capital, subscription, fee, reimbursement.

layers.name: Vendor Master Intelligence
layers.description: Retrieves vendor spend category, commodity code, and complete GL posting history. High-consistency vendors get high confidence signal.

layers.name: Historical Learning Model
layers.description: Queries 2-3 years of journal entry history. Computes probability distribution over GL codes for similar transactions.

layers.name: HSN/SAC Cross-Validation
layers.description: Validates GL assignment against GST taxonomy to ensure fiscal consistency and correct ITC treatment.

layers.name: Policy Enforcement
layers.description: Applies capex threshold, chart of accounts validation, blocked GL rules, and budget availability check before final assignment.

flow.description: Post or route based on score

title: Journal Entry Narration & Audit Trail
description: Automatically writes plain-language narratives for every journal entry — describing the business event, supporting documents, and accounting rationale — making audit substantiation effortless.

layers.name: Journal Entry Reading
layers.description: Reads the draft or posted journal entry — GL accounts, amounts, cost centers, and any reference documents attached.

layers.name: Context Enrichment
layers.description: Fetches supporting context — original invoice, PO, contract, bank statement, or prior period entry that this entry relates to.

layers.name: Accounting Policy Lookup
layers.description: References the company's accounting policies for the relevant transaction type — recognition criteria, treatment rules, disclosure requirements.

layers.name: Narration Generation
layers.description: Generates a structured narrative: Business Event + Amount + Reference + Accounting Treatment + Period + Preparer Authority. Written in auditor-ready language.

layers.name: Immutable Logging
layers.description: Attaches narration to the journal entry with timestamp, user ID, and document links. Creates an immutable, searchable audit log.

title: Accrual Identification & Posting
description: At period end, scans open POs, service agreements, and delivery confirmations to identify expenses incurred but not yet invoiced — computes accrual amounts and posts reversal-ready entries.

layers.name: Open PO Scan
layers.description: Retrieves all open purchase orders where GRN has been received (or service entry created) but no invoice has been posted. These are definitively accrual candidates.

layers.name: Contract & SLA-Based Accruals
layers.description: Reads active service contracts. For recurring services (maintenance, security, cleaning), estimates the accrual based on contract value and period proportion.

layers.name: Utility Estimation
layers.description: For utility invoices not yet received, estimates consumption based on prior period actuals with seasonal adjustment. Posts estimated accrual.

layers.name: Payroll Accruals
layers.description: Identifies unpaid wages, bonuses, and variable pay for the period. Cross-references payroll data and HR records. Computes accrual per cost centre.

layers.name: Reversal Scheduling
layers.description: All accrual entries tagged with reversal date (typically first day of next period). Reversal entries auto-generated and scheduled.

flow.description: Open POs with GRN but no invoice identified

title: Prepaid Amortisation Scheduling
description: Identifies prepaid expenses from invoices, builds amortisation schedules, and posts monthly expense recognition entries automatically — eliminating the manual spreadsheet chase.

layers.name: Prepaid Identification
layers.description: Reads invoice and detects prepaid indicators — multi-period coverage, advance payment terms, subscription keywords (annual, upfront, renewal), insurance policy periods.

layers.name: Coverage Period Extraction
layers.description: Extracts or infers the service coverage period from invoice text, PO, or contract — start date, end date, and total months.

layers.name: Amortisation Computation
layers.description: Calculates monthly expense amount (straight-line: total amount / coverage months). Handles pro-rata for partial first/last months.

layers.name: GL Mapping
layers.description: Maps the prepaid asset to the correct prepaid GL account. Maps the monthly expense to the correct expense GL and cost centre.

layers.name: Automated Posting
layers.description: Posts initial prepaid asset debit. Schedules monthly amortisation entries for all future periods. Tracks balance and confirms zero on schedule completion.

flow.description: Prepaid and expense GL codes assigned

title: Intercompany Transaction Matching
description: Matches intercompany invoices, loans, dividends, and recharges across legal entities — identifies mismatches, proposes eliminations, and ensures consolidation is clean before group close.

layers.name: IC Transaction Identification
layers.description: Reads all transactions flagged with intercompany counterpart codes. Identifies pairs: Entity A charges Entity B = one receivable + one payable.

layers.name: Bilateral Matching
layers.description: Matches Entity A's IC receivable against Entity B's IC payable — amount, currency, period, and transaction reference. Flags mismatches.

layers.name: Currency Reconciliation
layers.description: For foreign currency IC transactions, applies the same agreed exchange rate to both entities. Flags where different rates were applied.

layers.name: Timing Difference Analysis
layers.description: Classifies mismatches — in-transit invoices, period differences, vs. genuine errors. In-transit items auto-resolved with expected clearing date.

layers.name: Elimination Entry Preparation
layers.description: Generates consolidation elimination journals — IC revenue vs. IC cost, IC receivable vs. IC payable. Validates that elimination is complete.

title: Misposting Detection & Correction
description: Scans all posted journal entries for anomalies — wrong GL, wrong cost centre, wrong period, unusual amounts — and raises correction journals with full audit trail.

layers.name: Pattern Baseline
layers.description: Builds expected posting patterns per GL account, cost centre, and vendor — typical amounts, counterpart GLs, and posting frequencies.

layers.name: Anomaly Detection
layers.description: Scans new postings against baseline. Flags statistical outliers — amounts 3x the norm, postings to unusual GL/cost centre combinations, postings in closed periods.

layers.name: Contextual Validation
layers.description: For flagged entries, retrieves the original invoice, PO, or supporting document. Validates whether the posting is consistent with the underlying transaction.

layers.name: Misposting Confirmation
layers.description: Classifies anomaly — confirmed misposting (GL wrong), confirmed cost centre error, timing error, or legitimate exception (large one-off transaction).

layers.name: Correction Journal
layers.description: Drafts the correction journal — reversal of wrong entry + new correct entry. Routes for approval before posting. Full audit trail preserved.

title: Period-End Checklist Orchestration
description: Manages the full month-end close checklist — tracking task completion, sending contextual reminders, escalating overdue items, and giving finance controllers a live close status dashboard.

layers.name: Checklist Configuration
layers.description: Loads the master close checklist — task names, owners, dependencies, deadlines, and escalation rules. Configures are entity-specific and period-specific.

layers.name: Dependency Mapping
layers.description: Understands task dependencies — Trial Balance extract cannot run until all subledgers are closed. Sequences tasks and notifies owners in dependency order.

layers.name: Progress Monitoring
layers.description: Checks ERP for completion signals (sub-ledger close status, journal posting completeness, approval queue clearing). Updates task status in real time.

layers.name: Intelligent Reminders
layers.description: Sends reminders calibrated to urgency — T-3 days gentle reminder, T-1 day firm reminder, T-0 escalation to manager. Personalised per task and owner.

layers.name: CFO Dashboard
layers.description: Maintains live close status dashboard — tasks complete, at risk, and overdue. Predicts close completion time based on current progress velocity.

title: Trial Balance Review & Flux Analysis
description: Compares current period trial balance against prior periods and budget, identifies material variances, and drafts explanatory commentary — cutting MIS commentary cycle from days to hours.

layers.name: Trial Balance Extraction
layers.description: Pulls the period trial balance from ERP at entity and consolidated level. Structures by GL account, cost centre, and profit centre.

layers.name: Comparative Data Assembly
layers.description: Fetches prior period actuals (MoM, YoY), budget for current period, and year-to-date actuals. Aligns all data on a common GL structure.

layers.name: Variance Computation
layers.description: Computes absolute and percentage variances — actual vs. prior month, actual vs. budget, actual vs. prior year. Flags material variances above threshold.

layers.name: Root Cause Analysis
layers.description: For each material variance, retrieves the underlying transactions — which invoices, which cost centres, which projects drove the movement.

layers.name: Commentary Generation
layers.description: Drafts plain-English variance commentary per P&L line and balance sheet account. Commentary includes the driver, amount, and qualitative context.

flow.description: Prior period and budget data assembled
Final commentary inserted into MIS template

title: Balance Sheet Substantiation
description: For every balance sheet account, retrieves sub-ledger detail, confirms the balance is supported and appropriately aged, and flags items requiring write-off or reclassification.

layers.name: Account Listing Pull
layers.description: Retrieves itemised listing for each BS account from sub-ledger — AR aging, AP aging, prepaid schedule, fixed asset register, loan statements.

layers.name: Balance Reconciliation
layers.description: Validates that the sub-ledger sum matches the GL balance. Flags any differences for investigation.

layers.name: Aging & Staleness Analysis
layers.description: Identifies items that are old — long-outstanding AR, old advances, aged prepayments, stale creditors. Applies company aging policy to classify action required.

layers.name: Provision Assessment
layers.description: Evaluates whether provisions are adequate — doubtful debt provision vs. AR aging, inventory provision vs. slow-moving stock, warranty provision vs. claims trend.

layers.name: Substantiation Pack
layers.description: Assembles the account substantiation pack — opening balance, movements, closing balance, supporting schedule, and sign-off status. Ready for auditor.

title: Fixed Asset Capitalisation Assessment
description: Reads purchase invoices, POs, and project completion certificates to determine whether expenditure qualifies for capitalisation — applying policy and useful life criteria autonomously.

layers.name: Expenditure Review
layers.description: Reads invoice description, PO purpose field, and project code. Identifies potential capital expenditure items — new assets, significant improvements, installations.

layers.name: Capitalisation Criteria Test
layers.description: Applies company capitalisation policy — minimum value threshold, asset life > 1 year criterion, economic benefit test. Items failing criteria = expense.

layers.name: Asset Category Classification
layers.description: Classifies capital expenditure by asset category — Plant & Machinery, IT Hardware, Furniture, Vehicles, Intangibles, Capital WIP — for correct depreciation rate.

layers.name: Useful Life & Depreciation Rate
layers.description: Assigns standard useful life per Ind AS / Companies Act Schedule II for the asset category. Computes depreciation method and rate.

layers.name: Asset Record Creation
layers.description: Creates asset record in fixed asset register — asset description, location, cost centre, capitalisation date, and depreciation start date.

flow.description: Capital WIP entry created if asset not yet commissioned
On receipt of commissioning certificate, asset capitalised

title: Depreciation Review & Anomaly Detection
description: Monitors depreciation runs for errors — assets depreciating after full amortisation, wrong rates, assets without commissioning — and alerts before financial statements are finalised.

layers.name: Depreciation Run Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors the monthly depreciation run in the fixed asset module. Captures total depreciation by asset category, cost centre, and entity.

layers.name: Fully Depreciated Asset Check
layers.description: Identifies assets where accumulated depreciation equals or exceeds gross book value but depreciation is still running. Flags for immediate stop.

layers.name: Rate Consistency Check
layers.description: Validates that depreciation rates applied match the rate schedule for each asset category. Flags rate changes not supported by reclassification journal.

layers.name: Uncommissioned Asset Check
layers.description: Identifies assets in the FA register with a capitalisation date but no commissioning certificate — depreciation should not have started.

layers.name: Comparative Flux Check
layers.description: Compares total depreciation charge this month vs. prior month. Material unexplained increase/decrease flagged for review.

title: GST ITC Eligibility Assessment
description: For every purchase invoice, determines whether Input Tax Credit is fully eligible, partially eligible (proportionate basis), or blocked — based on nature of purchase and end-use.

layers.name: Invoice Classification
layers.description: Reads invoice description and HSN/SAC code to classify the nature of purchase — input goods, input services, capital goods.

layers.name: Blocked Credit Check
layers.description: Applies Section 17(5) of CGST Act — checks for blocked categories: motor vehicles (exceptions apply), food/beverages, club memberships, personal expenses, works contracts for immovable property.

layers.name: Proportionate Credit (Rule 42/43)
layers.description: For purchases used for both taxable and exempt supplies, computes proportionate ITC using the applicable formula. Monitors cumulative D1/D2 ratios.

layers.name: Vendor Compliance Check
layers.description: Verifies vendor GSTIN is active and not suspended. Checks vendor GSTR-1 filing compliance — ITC claim is at risk if vendor has not filed.

layers.name: ITC Claim Entry
layers.description: Creates the eligible ITC claim entry in the purchase register. Flags partially eligible and blocked amounts separately for reporting.

title: TDS Return Preparation
description: Aggregates all TDS deductions by section, challan, and vendor PAN — validates completeness, identifies mismatches with 26AS, and prepares Form 26Q / 27Q data for filing.

layers.name: TDS Ledger Aggregation
layers.description: Pulls all TDS deduction entries from AP and payroll ledgers for the quarter. Structures by section, deductee PAN, and payment date.

layers.name: Challan Matching
layers.description: Matches TDS deducted against challans deposited with the government. Confirms all deductions have been remitted on time.

layers.name: 26AS Reconciliation
layers.description: Downloads 26AS data for the company as deductor. Matches against internal TDS records. Flags entries in 26AS not in books and vice versa.

layers.name: PAN Validation
layers.description: Validates each deductee PAN against NSDL database. Flags invalid or missing PANs (higher rate applied; must be disclosed in return).

layers.name: Return File Generation
layers.description: Generates Form 26Q / 27Q FVU file in the required NSDL format. Validates file using NSDL File Validation Utility before filing.

flow.description: TDS ledger queried for the quarter

title: Transfer Pricing Documentation
description: Monitors intercompany transactions against arm's-length benchmarks, flags transactions approaching documentation thresholds, and triggers the TP study update workflow.

layers.name: IC Transaction Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks all intercompany transactions — sales, purchases, loans, management fees, royalties — against TP documentation thresholds (INR 20 Cr for domestic, INR 1 Cr for international).

layers.name: Comparability Analysis
layers.description: Retrieves external benchmark data (TNMM, CUP, RPM comparables) and compares intercompany transaction margins against the benchmark range.

layers.name: Arm's Length Test
layers.description: Determines whether the transaction price falls within the arm's-length price range. Flags transactions outside the range for documentation and adjustment.

layers.name: Documentation Trigger
layers.description: When transactions exceed threshold or approach year-end documentation deadline, triggers TP study update workflow — alerts TP team and external advisors.

layers.name: Form 3CEB Data Preparation
layers.description: Compiles all international transaction data in the format required for Form 3CEB (Accountant's Report) — transaction-wise, entity-wise summary.

title: GST Liability Computation
description: Calculates output GST liability by supply type, HSN/SAC, and place of supply — cross-validates with the sales register and identifies unreported supplies before GSTR-1 filing.

layers.name: Sales Register Extraction
layers.description: Pulls all outward supply transactions for the month from ERP — invoice-wise with customer GSTIN, supply type, HSN/SAC, and taxable value.

layers.name: Supply Classification
layers.description: Classifies each supply — taxable, exempt, nil-rated, zero-rated (export), non-GST supply. Identifies B2B, B2C large, B2C others, exports, and deemed exports.

layers.name: Place of Supply Determination
layers.description: Applies IGST Act rules to determine place of supply for goods (destination-based) and services (various rules by service type). Determines IGST vs. CGST+SGST applicability.

layers.name: Tax Rate Application
layers.description: Applies the correct GST rate for each HSN/SAC based on current rate notifications. Flags rate changes effective during the period.

layers.name: Output Liability Computation
layers.description: Computes total output IGST, CGST, SGST, and cess liability. Cross-validates against sales accounting entries. Identifies any supplies missing from the sales ledger.

flow.description: Output liability vs. accounting entries cross-checked

title: Daily Cash Position Forecasting
description: Aggregates bank balances, expected receipts, scheduled payments, and payroll to build an autonomous 7-day and 30-day cash forecast — giving treasury a real-time liquidity view.

layers.name: Bank Balance Aggregation
layers.description: Fetches real-time bank balances across all accounts, entities, and banks via bank API or MT940 feed. Converts multi-currency balances to reporting currency.

layers.name: Expected Receipts Forecast
layers.description: Reads AR aging and customer payment patterns. Applies historical payment probability per customer segment to forecast receipts by day.

layers.name: Scheduled Payments Pull
layers.description: Extracts all scheduled AP payments, payroll run dates, tax payment dates, loan repayments, and dividend obligations from ERP and payroll.

layers.name: Gap Identification
layers.description: Projects net daily cash position = Opening Balance + Expected Receipts - Scheduled Payments. Identifies days where balance falls below minimum buffer.

layers.name: Action Recommendations
layers.description: For projected shortfalls, recommends actions — draw on overdraft facility, delay non-critical AP payments, accelerate collection calls on large overdue invoices.

flow.description: Payroll and statutory payment dates loaded

title: Bank Reconciliation
description: Matches bank statement entries against the GL cash book line by line — identifies reconciling items, classifies them, and posts required entries — autonomously.

layers.name: Bank Statement Ingestion
layers.description: Reads bank statement via MT940 feed, CSV, or bank portal API. Parses each entry: date, amount, transaction type, narration, UTR/reference.

layers.name: GL Cash Book Pull
layers.description: Fetches all cash and bank GL postings for the same period. Structures by value date, amount, and payment reference.

layers.name: Exact Matching
layers.description: Matches bank entries to GL entries by amount + date + reference. High-confidence matches cleared immediately.

layers.name: Fuzzy Matching
layers.description: For timing differences and reference mismatches, applies tolerance-based matching — same amount, date within 3 days, partial reference match.

layers.name: Unmatched Classification
layers.description: Classifies residuals — bank charges not in books, interest income, errors, cheques in transit, deposits in transit. Drafts required GL entries.

title: Idle Cash Deployment
description: Monitors surplus cash in current accounts against thresholds — autonomously moves funds to overnight deposits or liquid mutual funds, and optimises idle cash cost.

layers.name: Surplus Identification
layers.description: Reads current account balances across all banks. Compares against minimum operating balance + 7-day payment buffer. Identifies surplus available for deployment.

layers.name: Investment Universe Check
layers.description: Reads the approved investment policy — permitted instruments (overnight deposits, liquid MF, T-Bills), counterparty limits, and tenor restrictions.

layers.name: Rate Comparison
layers.description: Fetches current overnight/liquid rates from approved banks and fund houses. Selects the highest-yielding option within policy constraints.

layers.name: Placement Instruction
layers.description: Generates placement instruction — amount, instrument, counterparty, tenor — and routes for Treasurer approval (or auto-executes within pre-approved limits).

layers.name: Maturity Tracking
layers.description: Tracks maturity dates of all placements. Initiates rollover or recall based on upcoming payment obligations in the cash forecast.

flow.description: Placement instruction sent to bank/fund house

title: Covenant Compliance Monitoring
description: Tracks banking covenants (DSCR, leverage ratio, current ratio) in real time against financial data — alerts treasury and CFO when ratios approach breach thresholds.

layers.name: Covenant Register
layers.description: Loads all active loan agreements and their covenant conditions — ratio thresholds, testing frequency, cure periods, and reporting obligations.

layers.name: Financial Data Pull
layers.description: Fetches required financial metrics from ERP — EBITDA, net debt, current assets, current liabilities, debt service payments — on a monthly or quarterly basis.

layers.name: Ratio Computation
layers.description: Computes each covenant ratio for the current period. Compares against threshold and defines headroom.

layers.name: Trend Projection
layers.description: Projects ratio trajectory for next 2-3 quarters based on current run rate and known upcoming events (large capex, maturing debt). Flags if breach is projected.

layers.name: Lender Reporting
layers.description: Prepares covenant compliance certificate in lender-required format. Routes for CFO sign-off and lender submission on due date.

title: Expense Policy Compliance Check
description: Reviews every employee expense claim against policy in real time — before it reaches a manager — flagging violations, requesting corrections, and auto-approving compliant claims.

layers.name: Claim Ingestion
layers.description: Employee submits expense claim via mobile app or portal. Agent reads all claim lines: date, merchant, amount, category, receipt image.

layers.name: Receipt Validation
layers.description: OCR reads receipt image. Validates merchant, amount, date, and GST details match the claim form. Flags mismatches or missing receipts above threshold.

layers.name: Per Diem & Limit Check
layers.description: Applies grade-based and city-tier-based per diem limits. Flags any line item exceeding the applicable limit. Applies meal, hotel, and entertainment caps.

layers.name: Category Eligibility
layers.description: Checks whether claimed category is eligible for reimbursement (e.g., alcohol, personal grooming, and club fees are typically ineligible). Applies purpose-based eligibility rules.

layers.name: Advance Netting
layers.description: Checks outstanding travel advances for the employee. Nets advances against claim. Triggers advance recovery or additional payment as applicable.

flow.description: Claim routed based on compliance score

title: Payroll Variance Analysis
