layers.description: Filters the enormous volume of information reaching the CEO — identifying the 20% of signals that require CEO-level attention and enabling the CEO to delegate or ignore the remaining 80% with confidence.

layers.name: Pattern Recognition and Foresight
layers.description: Identifies non-obvious patterns across the enterprise — where are multiple weak signals converging into a material future event? What emerging issues would a human analyst miss in the noise of day-to-day management?

layers.name: CEO Decision Support
layers.description: For the highest-stakes decisions — strategy, capital allocation, leadership, M&A — provides a structured decision framework: alternatives, evidence for each, risks, and second-order consequences. Not the decision — the framework that makes the decision better.

flow.description: All enterprise intelligence unified in one CEO view
CEO decision queue managed and prioritised
Structured decision framework prepared for major decisions

title: Job Requisition & Headcount Approval
description:
Manages the end-to-end headcount approval process — from business unit request through budget validation, grade alignment, and CHRO sign-off — before any recruitment begins.
  Ensures every open role is properly justified, budgeted, and approved before resources are committed to recruitment.

layers.name: Requisition Intake
layers.description: Reads the manager's headcount request — role title, level, department, reporting line, start date, and business justification. Validates that the role exists in the approved organisation chart.

layers.name: Budget Validation
layers.description: Cross-checks the requested role against the approved headcount budget for the cost centre and the current financial year. Flags requests that exceed approved headcount.

layers.name: Grade & Compensation Range
layers.description: Determines the appropriate grade band for the role based on job family, level, and geography. Pulls the approved salary range for budget provisioning.

layers.name: Approval Routing
layers.description: Routes the requisition through the correct approval chain — line manager → BU head → Finance (budget confirmation) → CHRO. Applies escalation logic if any approver is non-responsive within 48 hours.

layers.name: JD Generation
layers.description: Once approved, generates a structured job description from the role profile template — responsibilities, required qualifications, preferred skills, and reporting structure. Posts to internal and external job boards.

title: Candidate Sourcing & CV Screening
description:
Sources candidates from multiple channels — job boards, LinkedIn, employee referrals, and talent pools — screens CVs against the job scorecard, and ranks the pipeline for recruiter review.
  Reduces recruiter time on manual CV screening by 80% while improving quality of shortlist through consistent, objective scoring.

layers.name: Multi-Channel Sourcing
layers.description: Monitors job boards (Naukri, LinkedIn, Indeed), campus portals, employee referral submissions, and the internal talent pool for the approved requisition. Aggregates all applications in one pipeline.

layers.name: CV Parsing
layers.description: Extracts structured data from CVs — education, companies, roles, durations, skills, certifications, and achievements. Handles varied formats (PDF, Word, scanned) using OCR + NLP.

layers.name: Scorecard Matching
layers.description: Scores each candidate against the job scorecard — mandatory qualifications (pass/fail), experience match (0-40 pts), skills match (0-30 pts), and achievement signals (0-30 pts).

layers.name: Duplicate & Internal Candidate Check
layers.description: Detects duplicate applications across channels. Checks if applicant is a current employee or recently rejected candidate (cooling-off period).

layers.name: Ranked Shortlist
layers.description: Delivers a ranked shortlist to the recruiter with score breakdown, profile summary, and source channel. Flags candidates with referral bonus eligibility.

flow.description: Ranked list of top candidates compiled

title: Interview Scheduling & Coordination
description:
Orchestrates the full interview process — scheduling rounds, coordinating panel calendars, sending briefs, collecting feedback, and advancing candidates — without recruiter manual coordination.
  Eliminates the back-and-forth scheduling overhead that typically delays hiring by 5-10 days.

layers.name: Panel Identification
layers.description: Reads the interview process template for the role level — number of rounds, interviewers required per round (technical, functional, HRBP, leadership), and format (video/in-person).

layers.name: Calendar Integration
layers.description: Reads interviewer calendars via calendar API. Identifies common availability windows across all required interviewers that also align with candidate's stated availability.

layers.name: Scheduling Optimisation
layers.description: Proposes optimal interview slots — minimising time between rounds, avoiding back-to-back on same day, and respecting interviewer load limits (max 2 interviews/day).

layers.name: Pre-Interview Brief
layers.description: Sends each interviewer a personalised brief — candidate profile, assigned evaluation dimensions, question bank for their dimension, and scoring rubric.

layers.name: Feedback Collection
layers.description: Sends structured feedback form to each interviewer immediately post-interview. Chases non-respondents at 4-hour and 24-hour intervals. Aggregates feedback across rounds.

title: Background Verification & Pre-Employment Checks
description:
Initiates, tracks, and adjudicates background verification for all selected candidates — employment history, education, criminal record, reference checks, and CIBIL — ensuring all checks complete before Day 1.
  Eliminates manual follow-up with BGV agencies and prevents compliance gaps from incomplete verifications.

layers.name: BGV Scope Determination
layers.description: Determines the BGV scope for the role — based on level (senior roles get deeper checks), function (Finance/IT get CIBIL), and any regulatory requirements (SEBI-registered entities require SEBI debarment check).

layers.name: Consent & Document Collection
layers.description: Collects candidate consent (DPDP compliant) and required documents — employment proof for last 3 companies, degree certificates, ID proof, and address proof. Validates completeness.

layers.name: Agency Submission
layers.description: Submits verified documents to the empanelled BGV agency via their API. Monitors submission acknowledgement and tracks each check category independently.

layers.name: Discrepancy Detection
layers.description: As reports come in, reads each check result. Classifies discrepancies — Minor (e.g., employment gap < 3 months), Moderate (designation mismatch), Major (employment fraud, criminal record), Critical (degree fabrication).

layers.name: Adjudication & Offer Decision
layers.description: For clean checks, confirms offer. For discrepancies, routes to HRBP with classification and recommendation. Tracks joining confirmation against BGV completion.

title: Offer Management & Candidate Experience
description:
Generates, routes, and tracks offer letters — computing the exact compensation package, managing approvals, and handling candidate queries — while maintaining a positive candidate experience throughout.
  Reduces offer-to-acceptance time and prevents offer rejections through proactive engagement.

layers.name: Compensation Modelling
layers.description: Reads candidate's current CTC (from BGV or declaration), the approved grade band, budget approved for the role, and the hiring manager's recommendation. Computes the offer within band.

layers.name: Offer Letter Generation
layers.description: Populates the approved offer letter template — CTC break-up (fixed, variable, retiral benefits, joining bonus if any), reporting structure, role, location, and joining date.

layers.name: Approval Routing
layers.description: Routes the offer for approval — hiring manager → HRBP → Finance (for above-band or joining bonus) → CHRO (for senior roles). Tracks approvals with timestamps.

layers.name: Delivery & Acceptance Tracking
layers.description: Delivers the offer letter digitally. Tracks candidate activity — opened, read, signed, or expired. Sends reminders at 48 hours and 5 days if no action.

layers.name: Decline Analysis
layers.description: If offer is declined, conducts an automated exit interview. Captures reason — compensation, role, location, counter-offer. Flags competitive insights to HRBP.

title: Onboarding Automation
description:
Automates the complete new employee onboarding journey from Day -30 (pre-joining) through Day 90 — covering documentation, IT provisioning, policy acknowledgements, induction scheduling, and milestone check-ins.
  Ensures every new joiner reaches productivity faster with a structured, consistent, and engaging first experience.

layers.name: Pre-Joining Workflow (Day -30 to 0)
layers.description: Sends welcome email, pre-joining document checklist (PAN, Aadhaar, bank details, relieving letter, qualifications), and portal access for form completion. Validates submissions.

layers.name: IT & Infra Provisioning
layers.description: Triggers IT requests — laptop, email ID, system accesses, software licences, access cards — based on role and department. Monitors provisioning status; flags delays.

layers.name: Induction Scheduling
layers.description: Books the new joiner into mandatory induction sessions — company overview, HR policies, IT security, compliance, and function-specific orientation. Assigns a buddy from Day 1.

layers.name: Policy Acknowledgement Tracking
layers.description: Sends all mandatory policy documents (Code of Conduct, POSH, Data Privacy, IT usage). Tracks e-signature completion. Escalates unsigned policies at Day 7 and Day 14.

layers.name: Milestone Check-ins
layers.description: Sends structured check-in surveys at Day 7, Day 30, and Day 90. Analyses responses for early attrition signals. Triggers HRBP intervention if satisfaction drops below threshold.

flow.description: Welcome email and doc checklist sent

title: Employee Data & HRMS Management
description:
Maintains 100% accuracy of employee master data across the HRMS — managing all lifecycle events (promotions, transfers, grade changes, cost centre moves) and ensuring downstream system synchronisation.
  Prevents payroll errors, compliance failures, and reporting inaccuracies that stem from stale or incorrect employee data.

layers.name: Lifecycle Event Capture
layers.description: Reads all HR lifecycle events from approvals — promotions, lateral transfers, location changes, reporting line changes, grade revisions, and separation notices.

layers.name: HRMS Update & Validation
layers.description: Updates the HRMS in real time for each approved event. Validates that the change is internally consistent — grade must match job family, reporting manager must be active, cost centre must be valid.

layers.name: Downstream Synchronisation
layers.description: Propagates HRMS changes to dependent systems — payroll (for compensation changes), IT (for access rights based on new role), Finance (for cost centre reallocations), and identity management.

layers.name: Data Quality Monitoring
layers.description: Runs daily data quality checks — missing fields, expired documents (visa, work permits), inactive manager assignments, duplicate records, and phantom employees (on rolls but no activity).

layers.name: Audit Trail
layers.description: Maintains a complete, immutable audit trail of every HRMS change — who changed what, when, and based on which approval. Required for statutory compliance and internal audit.

title: Separation & Offboarding
description:
Manages the complete employee separation process — resignation, retirement, or termination — including notice period management, exit interviews, asset recovery, full and final settlement computation, and system deprovisioning.
  Ensures clean separation for every employee, protecting the company from legal risk and data security exposure.

layers.name: Separation Trigger Classification
layers.description: Reads the separation notice — voluntary (resignation), involuntary (termination), retirement, or end of contract. Classifies and applies the appropriate process and notice period.

layers.name: Notice Period Management
layers.description: Validates notice period per contract and company policy. Computes notice pay recovery (if early exit) or pay in lieu (if served waived). Manages notice period waiver requests.

layers.name: Exit Interview & Knowledge Capture
layers.description: Sends structured exit interview to departing employee. Schedules HRBP exit conversation for senior employees. Captures reasons for attrition and key knowledge transfer requirements.

layers.name: Asset & Access Recovery
layers.description: Generates an asset recovery checklist — laptop, phone, access card, company documents. Triggers IT deprovisioning for all accesses on the last working day. Tracks recovery status.

layers.name: Full & Final Settlement
layers.description: Computes F&F — pending salary, leave encashment (earned leave balance × daily rate), notice period adjustment, deductions (pending advances, loan recovery), and gratuity (if eligible).

title: Leave & Attendance Management
description:
Automates leave application processing, balance computation, attendance regularisation, and compliance with leave policy — including policy rules for carry-forward, encashment, and statutory leave entitlements.
  Eliminates manual leave management errors and provides real-time leave position to managers, Finance, and payroll.

layers.name: Leave Application Processing
layers.description: Reads leave requests from the HR portal or mobile app. Validates leave type, balance availability, minimum notice period (for planned leave), and manager delegation coverage.

layers.name: Policy Rule Engine
layers.description: Applies complex leave policy rules — sandwich leave (weekends counted between two leaves), minimum balance requirements, maximum continuous leave limits, and blackout dates.

layers.name: Manager Approval
layers.description: Routes leave request to line manager with team calendar view (who else is on leave). Applies auto-approval for short leaves (1-2 days) if balance is available and no team conflict.

layers.name: Attendance Reconciliation
layers.description: Reads biometric / HRMS attendance data. Identifies discrepancies — present without leave (auto-approved), absent without leave (LOP risk), and late logins. Sends regularisation requests.

layers.name: Year-End Processing
layers.description: Computes year-end leave balance — applies carry-forward rules (max N days), encashment eligibility, and lapse of non-encashable leaves. Updates HRMS and triggers payroll for encashment.

title: Internal Mobility & Transfers
description:
Manages internal job postings, employee applications, and approved transfers — ensuring internal talent is considered before external hiring, and all transfers are executed with full HRMS and payroll updates.
  Reduces attrition by creating visible career paths and fills roles faster with pre-verified internal talent.

layers.name: Internal Job Posting
layers.description: For every approved requisition, posts the role on the internal job board for 5 business days before opening to external candidates. Ensures all eligible employees can apply.

layers.name: Internal Candidate Eligibility
layers.description: Checks applicant eligibility — minimum tenure in current role (typically 18 months), no PIP or disciplinary action, and manager confirmation that business continuity is manageable.

layers.name: Transfer Process
layers.description: For approved internal moves, manages the transition — handover plan, notice period within company (typically 30-60 days), and new role start date. Coordinates between losing and gaining managers.

layers.name: Compensation Adjustment
layers.description: Computes any compensation adjustment on transfer — promotion-linked increase, lateral transfer (usually no change), or skills-premium for critical role. Routes for approval.

layers.name: HRMS Execution
layers.description: Updates all HRMS fields — department, reporting manager, cost centre, grade, location, and compensation. Triggers IT access changes, payroll updates, and new manager onboarding checklist.

title: Goal Setting & OKR Cascading
description:
Automates the annual and quarterly goal-setting process — cascading company OKRs to department and individual level, validating SMART goal quality, and ensuring 100% goal coverage across all employees.
  Eliminates goal-setting procrastination and misalignment by making the process structured, time-bound, and visible.

layers.name: Company OKR Input
layers.description: Reads the board-approved company-level OKRs for the year — objectives and key results. Maps each objective to the responsible department and function.

layers.name: Department Goal Cascading
layers.description: For each department, prompts the department head to define department-level OKRs that contribute to at least one company OKR. Validates linkage.

layers.name: Individual Goal Setting
layers.description: Opens the goal-setting portal for each employee. Guides them to set goals linked to their department OKRs. Validates SMART criteria — Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound.

layers.name: Manager Review & Approval
layers.description: Routes individual goals to line manager for review and approval. Flags goals that lack measurable KPIs or are misaligned with department priorities.

layers.name: Completion Tracking
layers.description: Monitors goal-setting completion rates by department. Sends escalating reminders. Reports completion status to CHRO with names of non-compliant managers.

title: Continuous Performance Feedback
description:
Enables and tracks continuous 360-degree feedback — prompting check-ins, collecting structured peer and manager feedback, identifying high performers and underperformers, and alerting HRBP to flight risks.
  Replaces the 'surprise' annual review with real-time performance visibility for managers and employees.

layers.name: Check-in Prompts
layers.description: Sends monthly manager-employee check-in prompts. Tracks whether 1:1s are happening. Flags managers whose direct reports haven't had a check-in in 30+ days.

layers.name: 360 Feedback Collection
layers.description: Triggers quarterly 360 feedback cycles. Identifies feedback respondents (peers, cross-functional partners, direct reports). Sends structured feedback forms with competency-based rating scales.

layers.name: Sentiment Analysis
layers.description: Applies NLP to qualitative feedback comments. Identifies positive patterns (emerging leader, great collaborator), neutral, and concerning patterns (missed deadlines, poor communication).

layers.name: High Performer Flagging
layers.description: Identifies employees receiving consistently high feedback scores across multiple dimensions over two+ quarters. Flags to HRBP for succession planning and retention action.

layers.name: Flight Risk & PIP Triggers
layers.description: Identifies early warning signals — declining feedback scores, manager check-in avoidance, or negative sentiment patterns. Triggers HRBP alert for proactive intervention.

title: Annual Performance Review & Ratings
description:
Manages the annual performance review cycle — self-appraisal, manager rating, normalisation / calibration, final rating assignment, and communication — with transparency and audit trail.
  Ensures performance ratings are fair, calibrated, and consistent across departments.

layers.name: Self-Appraisal Initiation
layers.description: Opens self-appraisal forms for all employees. Populates with their approved goals, year-long feedback summary, and learning completions. Sets completion deadline with escalation.

layers.name: Manager Assessment
layers.description: After self-appraisal, routes to manager for rating against each goal and competency. Pre-fills year-long feedback data to support the manager's assessment.

layers.name: Calibration Support
layers.description: Compiles department-level rating distribution. Flags statistical anomalies — leniency bias (all Exceeds Expectations), severity bias (all Below Expectations), or central tendency. Prepares calibration data pack.

layers.name: Normalisation
layers.description: Applies the approved bell curve / forced ranking within departments. Flags employees where proposed rating deviates significantly from bell curve — requires additional justification.

layers.name: Rating Finalisation & Communication
layers.description: After calibration approval, locks ratings. Generates employee performance summary with final rating, key achievements, and development areas. Routes for employee acknowledgement.

title: Learning & Development Management
description:
Identifies learning needs, curates personalised learning paths, manages course completions, tracks mandatory training compliance, and measures learning effectiveness for every employee.
  Connects learning to performance gaps and career aspirations, making L&D investment measurable.

layers.name: Learning Need Identification
layers.description: Reads performance review outcomes, 360 feedback themes, and career aspiration data. Maps gaps to available learning content — internal, external, and curated online courses.

layers.name: Personalised Learning Path
layers.description: Generates a personalised learning plan per employee — prioritised by role criticality, career goal alignment, and manager-identified gaps. Suggests format (self-paced, instructor-led, mentoring).

layers.name: Mandatory Training Compliance
layers.description: Tracks all mandatory training completions — POSH (annual), Information Security (annual), Anti-Bribery (annual), Fire Safety, and role-specific compliance training. Sends escalating reminders.

layers.name: Learning Engagement Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks course engagement — enrolment, progress, completion rates, assessment scores, and feedback ratings. Flags employees with low engagement for manager awareness.

layers.name: Effectiveness Measurement
layers.description: At 30 and 90 days post-training, sends follow-up survey — behavioural change observed, skill application on the job. Links completion to performance improvement trend.

flow.description: Performance gaps mapped to learning needs

title: Succession Planning & Critical Role Coverage
description:
Identifies critical roles, maps succession pipelines, assesses readiness of potential successors, and tracks development actions to close readiness gaps — ensuring business continuity for key positions.
  Prevents the company from being caught without leadership bench strength at critical moments.

layers.name: Critical Role Identification
layers.description: Analyses all roles and flags those that are critical — based on strategic importance, high difficulty to replace externally (time + cost), single-point-of-failure risk, and succession vacancy.

layers.name: Successor Mapping
layers.description: For each critical role, identifies 1-3 potential successors from the internal talent pool — using performance ratings, potential assessments, and competency match scores.

layers.name: Readiness Assessment
layers.description: Evaluates each potential successor's readiness — Now (can step in within 3 months), 1-2 years (with development), 3-5 years (early pipeline). Identifies readiness gaps.

layers.name: Development Action Tracking
layers.description: Defines specific development actions for each successor — stretch assignments, mentoring, external programmes. Tracks completion and progress against readiness milestones.

layers.name: Retention Risk Overlay
layers.description: Overlays flight risk data on the succession map. Flags critical roles where the current incumbent or the top successor is a retention risk — triggers emergency retention action.

flow.description: Retention risk on succession map overlaid
Retention action triggered for at-risk successors

title: Probation & Confirmation Management
description:
Manages the probation period assessment process — triggering assessments at the right time, collecting structured manager and peer feedback, and recommending confirmation, extension, or separation.
  Ensures probation decisions are data-driven, fair, and legally documented.

layers.name: Probation Calendar
layers.description: Tracks all employees in probation (typically 3-6 months). Sends assessment alerts to the manager at Day 60 (early warning) and Day 90 (formal assessment).

layers.name: Probation Assessment Form
layers.description: Sends a structured probation assessment form to the line manager — performance against role KPIs, culture fit, attendance, and learning agility. Includes supporting evidence from onboarding check-ins.

layers.name: Peer Input
layers.description: Collects brief input from the buddy and key cross-functional partners on collaboration and integration. Aggregates with manager assessment.

layers.name: HRBP Review
layers.description: Routes the completed assessment to HRBP for review. HRBP checks for consistency with onboarding feedback and flags any assessments that seem inconsistent with earlier positive check-ins.

layers.name: Decision & Communication
layers.description: Based on the assessment, routes to one of three outcomes — Confirm (auto-confirm letter generated), Extend (30-day extension with specific targets), or Separate (HR manages the exit with legal review).

title: Payroll Processing & Reconciliation
description:
Processes the full monthly payroll — computing gross-to-net for every employee including all earnings, deductions, statutory contributions, TDS, and off-cycle changes — and reconciles against the prior month and HRMS.
  Ensures 100% accurate, on-time payroll with complete statutory compliance and zero surprises.

layers.name: Input Collection
layers.description: Aggregates all payroll inputs — HRMS active employee list, approved compensation changes, leave and attendance data (LOP), expense reimbursements, variable pay, and statutory contribution rates.

layers.name: Gross-to-Net Computation
layers.description: Computes gross pay (basic + HRA + allowances + variable). Applies deductions — PF (employee 12% + employer 12%), ESI, professional tax, income tax (Section 192 TDS), and loan/advance recoveries.

layers.name: Statutory Contribution Computation
layers.description: Computes employer statutory contributions — PF (12% of basic), ESI (employer 3.25%), gratuity provision (4.81% of basic), and LWF. Generates ECR (Electronic Challan cum Return) for PF and ESI.

layers.name: Variance Analysis
layers.description: Compares payroll output against previous month. Flags variances above threshold — new joiners, exits, increments, LOP changes. Requires explanation for unexpected variances before processing.

layers.name: Bank File & Reconciliation
layers.description: Generates bank transfer file (NEFT/RTGS format) per employee. Reconciles payroll liability with Finance journal entry. Checks that net pay in bank file equals payroll register.

flow.description: Payroll register vs. bank file reconciled

title: Annual Increment & Compensation Review
description:
Manages the annual merit increase cycle — computing increment budgets, generating individual recommendations within approved bands, managing approvals, and implementing changes in payroll and HRMS.
  Ensures increment decisions are fair, defensible, differentiated by performance, and within budget.

layers.name: Budget Computation
layers.description: Reads the board-approved overall increment budget (% of total payroll). Distributes the budget by department and performance rating band — higher budgets for Exceeds Expectations, minimal for Below Expectations.

layers.name: Individual Recommendation
layers.description: Generates a recommended increment for each employee — combining performance rating weight, time since last increment, compa-ratio (current CTC vs. midpoint of grade band), and market benchmarks.

layers.name: Manager Adjustment Window
layers.description: Provides managers a window to adjust recommendations within their department budget — can increase one employee's increment but must reduce another's to stay within the approved envelope.

layers.name: Budget Reconciliation
layers.description: Continuously checks that manager adjustments stay within the approved department budget. Flags and blocks adjustments that would breach budget — manager must rebalance.

layers.name: Implementation & Communication
layers.description: After CHRO approval, updates all compensation records in HRMS and payroll (effective date). Generates increment letters per employee. Notifies managers to have increment conversations.

title: Benefits Administration
description:
Manages the full employee benefits lifecycle — enrolment, claims, renewals, and vendor management for medical insurance, group life, accident cover, meal cards, and wellness benefits.
  Ensures employees maximise their benefit entitlements while the company minimises cost leakage and compliance gaps.

layers.name: Enrolment Management
layers.description: Triggers benefit enrolment at joining and during the annual open enrolment window. Communicates each benefit, its value, and enrolment deadline. Tracks enrolment completion rates.

layers.name: Life Event Updates
layers.description: Processes mid-year benefit changes triggered by life events — marriage (spouse added to Mediclaim), newborn (child added), divorce (removal), and employee death (nominee claim initiated).

layers.name: Claims Assistance
layers.description: Assists employees in filing insurance claims — collects documents, validates claim completeness, submits to insurer, and tracks claim status. Escalates delayed or rejected claims.

layers.name: Utilisation Analytics
layers.description: Tracks benefit utilisation rates per employee cohort — which benefits are used, which are not. Identifies benefits with < 20% utilisation for cost optimisation at renewal.

layers.name: Renewal & Vendor Management
layers.description: At policy renewal (typically annual), analyses claims data, market benchmarking, and utilisation rates. Prepares renewal brief for broker / HR — whether to renew, renegotiate, or switch.

title: Variable Pay & Incentive Management
description:
Computes, approves, and processes all variable pay — annual bonuses, quarterly incentives, sales commissions, and retention bonuses — linked to company performance, business unit results, and individual ratings.
  Ensures pay-for-performance discipline with transparent, auditable, and on-time variable pay disbursement.

layers.name: Plan Reading & Entitlement
layers.description: Reads each employee's variable pay plan — target % of CTC, payout schedule (annual/quarterly), performance metrics, and minimum performance threshold for any payout.

layers.name: Performance Multiplier
layers.description: Applies company performance multiplier (e.g., 0.8x if company achieves 80% of target), business unit multiplier, and individual performance rating multiplier. Computes final payout formula.

layers.name: Pro-Ration for Joiners/Exiters
layers.description: Pro-rates variable pay for employees who joined or resigned mid-year. Applies policy rules — minimum months for eligibility, exit clause (resigned employees typically not eligible).

layers.name: Approval Hierarchy
layers.description: Routes variable pay computation to Finance for budget validation, then to CHRO for final approval. Flags cases where payout deviates significantly from target.

layers.name: Disbursement & TDS
layers.description: After approval, processes variable pay through payroll. Computes TDS on the additional income for the month. Generates payslip showing variable pay break-up and TDS impact.

flow.description: Variable pay explanation sent to employees

title: Statutory Compliance — PF, ESI, Gratuity, LWF
description:
Ensures 100% compliance with all statutory employment obligations — Provident Fund, ESI, Gratuity, Labour Welfare Fund, PT, Maternity Benefit, and Shops & Establishments — across all states of operation.
  Eliminates the significant penalty and prosecution risk from statutory non-compliance.

layers.name: Applicability Matrix
layers.description: Maps each establishment to all applicable labour laws — PF (all employees), ESI (employees earning ≤ ₹21,000/month), Gratuity (5+ years employees), and state-specific laws (PT, LWF rates vary by state).

layers.name: Monthly ECR Filing (PF)
layers.description: Computes ECR — employer 12% + employee 12% of basic wage per member. Generates the ECR file. Monitors EPFO portal for challan generation and payment confirmation.

layers.name: ESI Filing
layers.description: Computes ESI contributions — employer 3.25% + employee 0.75% of gross wages for eligible employees. Generates half-yearly returns. Monitors ESIC portal.

layers.name: Gratuity Provision & Payment
layers.description: Computes gratuity entitlement for all eligible employees (15/26 × last drawn monthly salary × years of service). Maintains provision. Processes payment upon eligibility trigger (resignation, retirement, death).

layers.name: Compliance Calendar
layers.description: Maintains a statutory compliance calendar with all due dates. Sends 15-day advance reminders. Generates compliance score dashboard for CHRO and board.

flow.description: All applicable laws per establishment identified

title: POSH Compliance & Investigation Management
description:
Manages the full POSH (Prevention of Sexual Harassment) compliance framework — ICC constitution, annual awareness training, complaint intake, investigation coordination, and report filing.
  Protects the company from legal liability while creating a safe, fair, and compliant workplace.

layers.name: ICC Compliance
layers.description: Monitors ICC constitution — whether the Internal Committee is constituted as required by law (50% women, external member, 3-year terms). Triggers renewal alerts 60 days before term expiry.

layers.name: Mandatory Training Tracking
layers.description: Tracks POSH awareness training completion for all employees annually. Sends escalating reminders. Reports non-compliance to CHRO and flags for board reporting.

layers.name: Complaint Intake
layers.description: Provides a safe, confidential complaint intake channel — email, portal, or HR hotline. Acknowledges receipt within 24 hours. Assigns a complaint number and activates the ICC process.

layers.name: Investigation Support
layers.description: Creates an investigation file — complaint, respondent details, witness list, and hearing schedule. Tracks all investigation milestones against the 90-day statutory timeline.

layers.name: Annual Report
layers.description: Compiles the mandatory annual report to the District Officer — number of complaints received, disposed, pending, and details of each case. Generates the report in statutory format.

title: HR Policy Management & Compliance
description:
Maintains the company's HR policy framework — ensuring policies are current, communicated, acknowledged, and consistently applied — and identifies compliance gaps across the workforce.
  Prevents HR-related legal exposure from outdated, unapplied, or unacknowledged policies.

layers.name: Policy Currency Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors all HR policies for relevance against current labour laws. Flags policies that need review due to legislative changes, court judgements, or business changes.

layers.name: Review & Approval Workflow
layers.description: For flagged policies, initiates the review — legal review, CHRO approval, and where required, works council / union consultation. Tracks the review to completion.

layers.name: Policy Communication
layers.description: Publishes updated policies on the HR portal. Sends mandatory read-and-acknowledge emails to all employees. Tracks acknowledgement rates. Sends reminders for non-acknowledgement.

layers.name: Query Resolution
layers.description: Handles employee queries on policy via an AI-powered HR chatbot. For complex or sensitive queries, routes to HRBP. Tracks query patterns to identify areas of policy confusion.

layers.name: Compliance Audit
layers.description: Periodically checks that policies are being consistently applied — reviewing leave approvals, disciplinary actions, and accommodation requests for policy adherence. Reports anomalies.

title: Grievance Management
description:
Manages the formal employee grievance process — intake, classification, investigation, resolution, and escalation — ensuring every grievance is addressed fairly, confidentially, and within the prescribed timeline.
  Builds employee trust and prevents grievances from escalating to labour disputes or legal action.

layers.name: Grievance Intake
layers.description: Provides a confidential grievance submission channel. Receives grievance, acknowledges within 24 hours, assigns a tracking number, and classifies by type — compensation, promotion, harassment, workplace, manager behaviour.

layers.name: Severity Classification
layers.description: Assesses severity — Minor (policy query, process concern), Moderate (discrimination claim, manager bias), Major (harassment, wage theft, retaliation). Routes to appropriate level for resolution.

layers.name: Investigation Coordination
layers.description: For Moderate and Major grievances, assigns an HRBP investigator. Tracks investigation milestones, document collection, and both-party hearings against the 30-day resolution SLA.

layers.name: Resolution & Communication
layers.description: Documents the resolution decision with rationale. Communicates to the grievance-raiser with full explanation. Tracks whether the employee is satisfied with resolution or wishes to escalate.

layers.name: Pattern Analytics
layers.description: Analyses grievance patterns — which managers receive repeated grievances, which departments have high grievance rates, which issues are systemic. Reports to CHRO for proactive intervention.

title: Disciplinary Action Management
description:
Manages disciplinary proceedings — from misconduct reporting through show cause, inquiry, and outcome — ensuring natural justice principles, policy consistency, and complete documentation.
  Protects the company from wrongful termination claims and ensures disciplinary actions are legally watertight.

layers.name: Misconduct Report Intake
layers.description: Reads a disciplinary report from a manager, HR, or compliance team. Classifies misconduct severity — Minor (absenteeism, late submission), Major (data theft, policy violation), Gross (fraud, harassment, violence).

layers.name: Process Determination
layers.description: Applies the appropriate process per severity — Minor (verbal warning → written warning), Major (show cause → inquiry committee), Gross (suspension → domestic inquiry → termination).

layers.name: Show Cause Notice
layers.description: Drafts and issues the Show Cause Notice (SCN) — specific allegations, evidence summary, and opportunity to respond within 7 working days. Tracks response receipt.

layers.name: Inquiry Coordination
layers.description: For Major/Gross misconduct, constitutes an inquiry committee. Manages the inquiry process — evidence submission, cross-examination, and final report. Tracks against statutory timelines.

layers.name: Outcome & Documentation
layers.description: Based on inquiry findings, supports the disciplinary authority in determining the outcome. Generates the outcome letter. Archives the complete file for statutory compliance and legal defence.

title: Labour Law & Regulatory Compliance
description:
Monitors all applicable labour laws — the four Labour Codes (Wages, Industrial Relations, Social Security, Occupational Safety), state amendments, and registration requirements — across all establishments.
  Prevents prosecution, penalties, and reputational damage from labour law violations.

layers.name: Establishment Mapping
layers.description: Maps all company establishments (factories, offices, warehouses) to applicable labour laws — based on number of employees, nature of work, and state location.

layers.name: Registration & Licence Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks all labour law registrations and licences — Shops & Establishments, Factory Licence, Contract Labour Registration, Principal Employer registration. Monitors expiry and renewal.

layers.name: Compliance Calendar
layers.description: Maintains a comprehensive compliance calendar — all periodic returns (annual, half-yearly, quarterly, monthly) under applicable acts — by establishment and due date.

layers.name: Return Preparation
layers.description: Prepares periodic returns — Form A/B under Shops & Establishments, Annual Return under Factories Act, EPF/ESI returns. Validates data completeness before filing.

layers.name: Labour Code Transition Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks implementation notifications for the four Labour Codes. As codes come into force state by state, assesses the operational and policy changes required.

flow.description: All establishments and applicable laws mapped
Due dates for all returns monitored

title: HR Audit & Compliance Reporting
description:
Conducts periodic HR compliance audits — statutory registers, employment contracts, policy implementation, and process adherence — and prepares the HR section of the board compliance report.
  Provides the CHRO and board with confidence that HR operations are legally compliant across all dimensions.

layers.name: Statutory Register Maintenance
layers.description: Checks that all mandatory statutory registers are maintained correctly — Register of Employees, Register of Wages, Muster Roll, Register of Leaves, Accidents Register, and Register of Contractors.

layers.name: Employment Contract Audit
layers.description: Audits a sample of employment contracts — correct designation and CTC, appropriate notice period, IP assignment clauses, and GDPR / DPDP data processing consent clauses.

layers.name: Process Adherence Audit
layers.description: Reviews a sample of HR transactions — hiring approvals followed the correct process, increments within approved bands, disciplinary actions properly documented.

layers.name: Policy Implementation Audit
layers.description: Checks whether policies are being applied consistently — leave approvals within policy, accommodation requests treated equally, no systematic policy bypass by specific managers.

layers.name: Board Compliance Report
layers.description: Compiles the HR compliance section of the quarterly board report — statutory compliance status, open grievances, POSH matters, pending disciplinary cases, and headcount against plan.

title: HR Analytics & Workforce Planning
description:
Provides the CHRO and leadership with data-driven workforce insights — headcount, attrition, diversity, cost, and productivity analytics — and models future workforce needs against strategic business plans.
  Transforms HR from reactive to predictive — enabling talent decisions based on evidence rather than intuition.

layers.name: Headcount Analytics
layers.description: Tracks live headcount against plan — total employees, by department, by grade, by location, and by employment type (permanent, contract, intern). Flags variance against approved plan.

layers.name: Attrition Analytics
layers.description: Computes attrition rate (voluntary, involuntary, total) by department, grade, tenure band, and manager. Identifies attrition hotspots and trends (improving vs. deteriorating).

layers.name: Predictive Attrition Modelling
layers.description: Applies machine learning to predict employees with high flight risk in the next 90 days — using features: tenure, last salary increase, recent feedback scores, manager change, and peer departures.

layers.name: Diversity Analytics
layers.description: Tracks workforce diversity — gender ratio by level, gender pay gap by grade, age diversity, and PwD representation. Compares against company targets and industry benchmarks.

layers.name: Strategic Workforce Planning
layers.description: Models future headcount needs — based on business growth plans, productivity benchmarks, and automation impact. Identifies future skill gaps and recommends build/buy/borrow strategy.

flow.description: Current vs. future skill requirements compared

title: HR Technology & HRMS Administration
description:
Manages the HR technology stack — HRMS configuration, integrations, data governance, user access, and system health — ensuring HR technology supports all HR processes without downtime or data quality issues.
  Prevents the costly operational failures and compliance risks that result from poorly maintained HR systems.

layers.name: HRMS Configuration Management
layers.description: Monitors HRMS configuration for completeness — workflows, grade structures, compensation bands, leave policies, and organisation hierarchies. Flags configuration drift as business changes.

layers.name: Integration Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors all HRMS integrations — payroll, IT (Active Directory), Finance (cost centre), and attendance systems. Detects data sync failures and reconciliation breaks.

layers.name: User Access Management
layers.description: Manages HRMS user roles and access rights. Reviews access quarterly — removes access for separated employees, adjusts rights for transfers, and ensures no excessive access accumulation.

layers.name: Data Quality Monitoring
layers.description: Daily data quality checks — employee records with missing mandatory fields, organisation hierarchy breaks, duplicate employee codes, and cost centre mismatches.

layers.name: Release & Change Management
layers.description: Manages HRMS vendor updates and custom configurations — testing new releases in UAT, impact assessment, rollout coordination, and employee communication for interface changes.

title: HR Shared Services & Employee Self-Service
description:
Operates HR Shared Services through an AI-powered service desk — handling employee queries, document requests, process guidance, and complaint routing — reducing HR team workload by 70%.
  Delivers consumer-grade HR service experience to all employees regardless of location or time zone.

layers.name: Query Classification
layers.description: Reads employee queries from chatbot, email, and HR portal. Classifies by topic — payroll (leave balance, payslip, TDS), benefits (Mediclaim, expense), processes (onboarding, transfer, increment), and policy.

layers.name: FAQ Resolution
layers.description: Resolves common, well-defined queries from the HR knowledge base — payslip download guidance, leave balance query, PF statement access, Form 16 availability, and policy interpretation.

layers.name: Document Generation
layers.description: Processes self-service requests — experience letter, salary certificate, employment certificate, relieving letter, Form 16, and No Objection Certificate — generating the document and delivering within 24 hours.

layers.name: Complex Query Routing
layers.description: For queries that require HR judgment — compensation concerns, manager complaints, policy exceptions — routes to the appropriate HRBP with full context. Tracks resolution and SLA.

layers.name: Service Quality Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks HR service desk metrics — query volume by category, first-contact resolution rate, average resolution time, and employee satisfaction. Identifies knowledge base gaps.

title: HR Budget Planning & Cost Management
description:
Plans, tracks, and controls the HR budget — manpower costs (salaries, benefits, increments, variable pay), HR operational expenses, and talent acquisition costs — providing real-time HR cost intelligence to the CFO.
  Ensures HR costs are within plan and gives the CFO advance warning of budget risks.

layers.name: Manpower Cost Projection
layers.description: Projects total manpower cost — current payroll run rate plus approved increments, variable pay provisions, new hires in pipeline, and backfills in progress. Compares against annual budget.

layers.name: Recruitment Cost Tracking
layers.description: Tracks all talent acquisition costs — agency fees, job board subscriptions, background verification, relocation costs, and referral bonuses. Computes cost-per-hire by channel and level.

layers.name: L&D Budget Tracking
layers.description: Monitors learning and development spend against budget — external training programmes, e-learning licences, and conference attendance. Tracks cost-per-learning-hour.

layers.name: Variance Analysis
layers.description: Compares HR actual spend against plan monthly. Identifies overspends — reasons could be unplanned hiring, above-band increments, or higher-than-expected attrition driving higher recruitment costs.

layers.name: CFO Reporting
layers.description: Prepares the monthly HR cost report for the CFO — total manpower cost, headcount reconciliation, cost-per-employee trend, and projected full-year HR cost vs. budget.

title: Diversity, Equity & Inclusion (DEI) Monitoring
description:
Tracks and reports on the company's DEI commitments — gender ratio, pay equity, inclusive hiring metrics, and representation at senior levels — and identifies actions to close equity gaps.
  Ensures DEI is measured, reported, and acted upon — not just aspirational.

layers.name: DEI Metrics Computation
layers.description: Computes DEI metrics across the employee lifecycle — hiring (gender and diversity of shortlists and offers), promotions (women promoted at same rate as men), attrition (is there disparate attrition among minorities), pay equity.

layers.name: Gender Pay Gap Analysis
layers.description: Computes like-for-like gender pay gap — controlling for grade, function, and location. Identifies roles or departments with statistically significant pay differentials not explained by these factors.

layers.name: Representation Tracking
layers.description: Tracks representation vs. targets — gender in senior leadership (VP and above), PwD as % of workforce, and regional diversity. Compares against BRSR and ESG commitments.

layers.name: Hiring Pipeline Diversity
layers.description: Monitors diversity at each stage of the hiring funnel — sourcing (% diverse), shortlist (% diverse), interview (% diverse), offer (% diverse). Identifies where drop-off occurs.

layers.name: Action Recommendation
layers.description: Identifies specific, data-backed actions to close DEI gaps — diverse sourcing partnerships, blind resume screening, structured interview training, sponsorship programmes.

flow.description: DEI metrics vs. industry and BRSR targets
DEI section of BRSR / ESG report prepared

title: Employee Engagement Surveys & Action Planning
description:
Designs, deploys, and analyses employee engagement surveys — computing engagement scores, identifying drivers, benchmarking against industry, and generating manager-level action plans from the results.
  Turns survey data into specific, manager-driven actions that actually improve engagement.

layers.name: Survey Design
layers.description: Designs the engagement survey — validated questions across drivers (leadership, growth, recognition, work environment, belonging). Customises for the company context while maintaining benchmarkability.

layers.name: Deployment & Participation
layers.description: Deploys survey via email and mobile. Tracks participation rates by department and sends personalised reminders to non-respondents. Targets 80%+ participation for statistical validity.

layers.name: Engagement Score Computation
layers.description: Computes engagement scores — overall Engagement Index (eNPS), driver scores, and department / level / tenure cohort breakdowns. Applies statistical significance tests.

layers.name: Insight Generation
layers.description: Analyses verbatim comments using NLP — identifies top positive themes and top areas of concern. Correlates driver scores with attrition data to identify the engagement drivers that most predict retention.

layers.name: Manager Action Planning
layers.description: Generates a personalised action report for each manager — team engagement score, team's top 3 strengths and 3 improvement areas, and 3 specific recommended actions.

title: Recognition & Rewards Programme Management
description:
Manages the employee recognition programme — peer recognition, manager recognition, long service awards, and spot bonuses — ensuring recognition is frequent, genuine, timely, and financially controlled.
  Creates a culture of appreciation that improves engagement and retention without budget overruns.

layers.name: Recognition Programme Design
layers.description: Configures the recognition programme parameters — peer nomination rules, manager discretionary budget, recognition categories (innovation, collaboration, customer focus), and point/reward values.

layers.name: Nomination Processing
layers.description: Receives recognition nominations from peers and managers. Validates that nominations are specific, evidence-based, and linked to company values. Auto-approves peer recognitions within point budget.

layers.name: Fairness Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors recognition distribution for bias — are certain departments over- or under-nominated? Is recognition gender-balanced? Flags statistical anomalies to HRBP.

layers.name: Awards & Communication
layers.description: Processes approved recognitions — points awarded, certificate generated, shared on internal social wall (with employee consent), and integrated with payroll for taxable awards above threshold.

layers.name: Budget Control
layers.description: Tracks recognition spend against the quarterly budget per department. Alerts managers approaching budget limit. Reconciles recognition points redeemed vs. issued.

title: Wellness & Employee Assistance Programme
description:
Manages employee wellness initiatives — mental health resources, EAP (Employee Assistance Programme), physical wellness benefits, and work-life balance monitoring — identifying at-risk employees and facilitating support.
  Builds a psychologically safe, healthy workplace that reduces absenteeism and improves productivity.

layers.name: Wellness Programme Management
layers.description: Coordinates wellness calendar — physical health camps, mental health webinars, nutrition workshops, and step challenges. Tracks participation and engagement.

layers.name: EAP Case Management
layers.description: Manages employee referrals to the EAP provider — work stress, relationship issues, financial counselling, and mental health support. Maintains complete confidentiality; only aggregate data visible to HR.

layers.name: Absence & Burnout Detection
layers.description: Monitors absence patterns, overtime hours, and leave utilisation. Identifies employees showing burnout indicators — high overtime, declining leave utilisation, or frequent short sick leave.

layers.name: Manager Wellness Enablement
layers.description: Sends managers personalised dashboards showing their team's aggregate wellness indicators (not individual data). Provides managers with scripts for wellness conversations.

layers.name: Wellness Analytics
layers.description: Measures wellness programme ROI — participation rates, sick leave trends, EAP utilisation, and absenteeism cost reduction. Reports to CHRO for programme investment decisions.

title: HR Knowledge Management & Chatbot
description:
Maintains and continuously improves the HR knowledge base — policies, FAQs, process guides, and forms — powering an AI chatbot that handles 70% of employee HR queries without human intervention.
  Democratises HR knowledge access across all employees at any time, reducing HR team load and improving employee experience.

layers.name: Knowledge Base Curation
layers.description: Maintains the HR knowledge base — policy documents, process guides, FAQs, forms, and how-to videos. Auto-detects when policies are updated and refreshes corresponding KB articles.

layers.name: Query Understanding
layers.description: Uses NLP to understand the intent behind employee questions — even when poorly phrased or multi-part. Maps intent to the most relevant knowledge base article or process.

layers.name: Personalised Response
layers.description: Tailors responses to the employee's context — their leave balance, benefits plan, grade level, and location. A query about 'my maternity leave entitlement' returns the employee's specific entitlement, not a generic policy.

layers.name: Escalation Intelligence
layers.description: Identifies when an employee needs human support — emotional distress signals, sensitive HR matters, or queries outside the knowledge base. Escalates to HRBP with full conversation context.

layers.name: Continuous Improvement
layers.description: Analyses queries that were escalated, poorly answered, or highly rated — to improve KB articles and chatbot responses. Identifies emerging query trends signalling policy confusion or employee concerns.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Control Design & Effectiveness Assessment
description: Continuously evaluates the design and operating effectiveness of internal controls across the organization, ensuring controls are properly designed to mitigate identified risks and are functioning as intended in day-to-day operations.

layers.name: Control Inventory & Mapping
layers.description: Maintains a complete inventory of all financial controls across processes (revenue, procurement, inventory, payroll, treasury, financial reporting). Maps each control to specific risks, process steps, and responsible owners.

layers.name: Design Effectiveness Analysis
layers.description: Evaluates whether controls are properly designed to address identified risks. Assesses control frequency, nature (preventive vs. detective), automation level, and coverage gaps.

layers.name: Operating Effectiveness Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks control execution in real-time through system logs, approvals, reconciliations, and exception reports. Identifies missed controls, delayed executions, or pattern deviations.

layers.name: Control Deficiency Detection
layers.description: Flags design deficiencies (controls that cannot effectively mitigate the risk) and operating deficiencies (controls not executed as designed). Categorizes severity as significant, material, or minor.

layers.name: Remediation Tracking
layers.description: Monitors management action plans for identified deficiencies, tracks implementation status, validates corrective actions, and updates control documentation.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Authority Matrix & Approval Rights Validation
description: Validates that all financial transactions and decisions are approved by individuals with appropriate authority levels as defined in the delegation of authority matrix, preventing unauthorized transactions and ensuring proper segregation of duties.

layers.name: Authority Matrix Maintenance
layers.description: Maintains the current state of approval authorities across all financial processes including purchase requisitions, purchase orders, vendor payments, journal entries, payroll changes, and bank transactions.

layers.name: Transaction Authorization Validation
layers.description: Checks every financial transaction against the authority matrix before processing. Validates approver's designation, approval amount limits, and transaction type permissions.

layers.name: Segregation of Duties Enforcement
layers.description: Ensures that individuals initiating transactions cannot approve them, those approving cannot record, and those with custody cannot reconcile. Flags violations in real-time.

layers.name: Exception Handling & Override Detection
layers.description: Identifies transactions processed without proper approval, emergency overrides, or circumvention of approval workflows. Flags for management review.

layers.name: Authority Change Management
layers.description: Tracks changes to the authority matrix (new joiners, role changes, resignations, temporary delegations) and ensures system access rights are updated immediately.

flow.description: System access aligned with authority matrix

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Delegation of Authority Compliance Monitoring
description: Ensures that all delegated authorities are properly documented, time-bound, within approved limits, and revoked when no longer needed, maintaining a clear chain of accountability for all financial decisions.

layers.name: Delegation Registry
layers.description: Maintains a complete record of all active delegations including temporary approvals during leave, project-specific authorities, and emergency delegation scenarios.

layers.name: Delegation Validity Check
layers.description: Validates that delegations are properly approved by authorized individuals, documented with clear scope and limits, and time-bound with expiry dates.

layers.name: Auto-Revocation Management
layers.description: Automatically revokes delegations when the specified period expires, when the delegating officer returns, or when the delegated individual changes roles.

layers.name: Delegation Limit Enforcement
layers.description: Ensures delegated authorities do not exceed the delegating officer's own limits and validates that delegation is within permissible scope.

layers.name: Abuse & Pattern Detection
layers.description: Identifies patterns of frequent delegation, delegations just before year-end, or delegation chains that circumvent proper oversight.

flow.description: Expiry alerts sent before delegation ends

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Policy & Procedure Enforcement Automation
description: Automatically enforces financial policies and accounting procedures across all transactions and processes, ensuring consistent application of company standards and regulatory requirements without relying on manual oversight.

layers.name: Policy Rules Engine
layers.description: Maintains all financial policies as executable rules including expense limits, vendor selection criteria, payment terms, credit approval criteria, capitalization thresholds, and revenue recognition rules.

layers.name: Real-Time Policy Validation
layers.description: Validates every transaction against applicable policies before processing. Blocks transactions that violate mandatory policies and flags those requiring management exception.

layers.name: Exception Management Workflow
layers.description: Routes policy violations to appropriate exception approvers, documents justification, tracks exception patterns, and maintains exception register for audit review.

layers.name: Policy Version Control
layers.description: Maintains complete history of policy changes, tracks effective dates, manages transition periods, and ensures all transactions are validated against the correct policy version.

layers.name: Compliance Gap Analysis
layers.description: Identifies processes, systems, or user groups with high policy violation rates, analyzes root causes, and recommends policy updates or training interventions.

flow.description: All policies loaded as validation rules
Policy changes tracked with effective dates

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Organizational Structure & Reporting Line Validation
description: Validates that the organizational structure supports proper financial controls, ensures reporting lines maintain segregation of duties, and prevents conflicts of interest in approval chains and control oversight.

layers.name: Org Structure Mapping
layers.description: Maintains complete organizational hierarchy including reporting lines, dotted-line relationships, matrix structures, and temporary assignments.

layers.name: Control Assignment Validation
layers.description: Ensures control owners and reviewers are not in the same reporting line, approval chains do not have gaps, and no single individual controls end-to-end processes.

layers.name: Conflict of Interest Detection
layers.description: Identifies situations where family members, related parties, or individuals with financial interests are in approval chains or oversight roles.

layers.name: Reporting Line Segregation
layers.description: Validates that finance, operations, and audit functions maintain independent reporting lines and that control testing is performed by individuals outside the process ownership.

layers.name: Structural Risk Assessment
layers.description: Flags organizational changes (mergers, restructuring, downsizing) that may create control gaps or concentration of authority.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Financial Misstatement Risk Identification
description: Continuously identifies and assesses risks of material misstatement in financial statements arising from errors, fraud, management override, or control failures across all significant account balances and transaction streams.

layers.name: Risk Universe Mapping
layers.description: Maintains a complete inventory of financial statement risks across revenue recognition, inventory valuation, accounts receivable, fixed assets, liabilities, accruals, and disclosures.

layers.name: Inherent Risk Assessment
layers.description: Evaluates the likelihood and magnitude of misstatement before considering controls based on transaction volume, complexity, judgment involved, and prior history.

layers.name: Control Effectiveness Evaluation
layers.description: Assesses how existing controls mitigate identified risks, calculates residual risk exposure, and identifies high-risk areas requiring additional controls.

layers.name: Fraud Risk Triangulation
layers.description: Analyzes pressure (financial targets, incentives), opportunity (weak controls, override ability), and rationalization indicators to flag high fraud risk scenarios.

layers.name: Risk Heat Mapping
layers.description: Creates dynamic risk heat maps showing areas of highest financial statement risk, prioritizes testing and monitoring resources, and triggers enhanced controls.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Fraud Risk Detection & Prevention
description: Detects potential fraud in financial transactions through pattern analysis, anomaly detection, and behavioral monitoring, enabling proactive fraud prevention rather than reactive detection.

layers.name: Fraud Scheme Library
layers.description: Maintains patterns of known fraud schemes including invoice manipulation, vendor fraud, payroll fraud, expense fraud, asset misappropriation, and financial statement fraud.

layers.name: Behavioral Analytics
layers.description: Analyzes user behavior patterns to detect unusual activities such as after-hours access, excessive transaction reversals, frequent policy exceptions, or transaction pattern changes.

layers.name: Vendor & Customer Analysis
layers.description: Identifies red flags in vendor/customer master data including duplicate addresses, suspicious bank accounts, shell companies, related parties, and circular transactions.

layers.name: Management Override Detection
layers.description: Monitors for signs of management override including manual journal entries near period-end, unusual account combinations, approvals outside normal authority, and audit adjustment patterns.

layers.name: Collusion Pattern Recognition
layers.description: Identifies potential collusion through analysis of communication patterns, shared IP addresses, sequential transaction timing, and coordinated approval behaviors.

flow.description: Fraud schemes mapped to detection rules

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Third-Party & Vendor Risk Assessment
description: Assesses and continuously monitors financial risks associated with third-party vendors, service providers, and business partners to ensure they do not introduce control weaknesses or financial reporting risks.

layers.name: Vendor Risk Profiling
layers.description: Categorizes vendors based on financial materiality, access to sensitive data, critical service dependency, geographic risk, and industry-specific risks.

layers.name: Due Diligence Validation
layers.description: Validates vendor financial stability, business licenses, tax compliance, beneficial ownership, sanctions screening, and adverse media checks before onboarding.

layers.name: Ongoing Risk Monitoring
layers.description: Continuously monitors vendor performance, financial health indicators, compliance violations, cybersecurity incidents, and adverse news for early warning signals.

layers.name: Control Reliance Assessment
layers.description: Evaluates whether the organization can rely on vendor controls for financial reporting (e.g., service organization SOC reports, data center controls, payroll processing).

layers.name: Vendor Concentration Analysis
layers.description: Identifies over-reliance on single vendors, assesses business continuity risks, and evaluates financial impact of vendor failures.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: IT General Controls (ITGC) Monitoring
description: Ensures that IT general controls supporting financial systems are operating effectively, including access controls, change management, backup and recovery, and IT operations controls that form the foundation for application controls.

layers.name: Access Control Validation
layers.description: Monitors user access to financial systems, validates segregation of duties in IT environment, tracks privileged access usage, and ensures timely access revocation.

layers.name: Change Management Oversight
layers.description: Tracks all changes to financial systems including patches, upgrades, configuration changes, and custom code modifications. Validates change approval, testing, and emergency change protocols.

layers.name: Backup & Recovery Testing
layers.description: Validates that backup procedures are executed as scheduled, tests restore capabilities periodically, monitors backup success rates, and validates offsite storage.

layers.name: IT Operations Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks system availability, batch job completion, interface processing, database integrity, and infrastructure controls supporting financial applications.

layers.name: ITGC Deficiency Impact Analysis
layers.description: Assesses how ITGC deficiencies impact application controls and financial statement risks, prioritizes remediation based on financial reporting impact.

flow.description: All system changes tracked and validated
IT control gaps tracked to closure

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Change Management Risk Assessment
description: Assesses financial reporting risks introduced by business changes including system implementations, process changes, organizational restructuring, mergers and acquisitions, and regulatory changes.

layers.name: Change Catalog Management
layers.description: Maintains inventory of all planned and ongoing changes affecting financial processes, systems, policies, or organizational structure.

layers.name: Pre-Implementation Risk Assessment
layers.description: Evaluates financial reporting impact of proposed changes including new control requirements, data migration risks, interface disruptions, and reporting gaps.

layers.name: Parallel Run Validation
layers.description: Validates that parallel runs between old and new systems are performed successfully, reconciled completely, and discrepancies resolved before cutover.

layers.name: Post-Implementation Control Validation
layers.description: Ensures that new controls are operating effectively after implementation, data integrity is maintained, and no unintended consequences emerged.

layers.name: Transition Period Monitoring
layers.description: Provides enhanced monitoring during transition periods when new and old processes coexist, identifies control gaps, and prevents period-end reporting failures.

flow.description: All changes to finance processes tracked

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Purchase-to-Pay (P2P) Control Execution
description: Executes and validates all controls in the purchase-to-pay cycle including requisition approval, PO creation, goods receipt, invoice matching, payment approval, and vendor master management.

layers.name: Requisition Control Validation
layers.description: Validates budget availability, approval authority, vendor selection compliance, and segregation between requestor and approver before creating purchase requisitions.

layers.name: Three-Way Match Automation
layers.description: Automatically matches purchase order, goods receipt note, and vendor invoice for quantity, price, and terms. Flags discrepancies for resolution before payment processing.

layers.name: Payment Authorization Control
layers.description: Validates payment approval authority, duplicate payment prevention, vendor bank account verification, and segregation between payment approval and execution.

layers.name: Vendor Master Control
layers.description: Ensures vendor creation requires dual approval, bank account changes are validated, duplicate vendors are prevented, and related party vendors are flagged.

layers.name: Period-End Accruals
layers.description: Identifies goods received but not invoiced (GRNI), monitors outstanding purchase orders, validates accrual completeness, and ensures proper expense period allocation.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Order-to-Cash (O2C) Control Execution
description: Executes and validates all controls in the order-to-cash cycle including customer credit approval, sales order processing, shipment validation, invoicing, revenue recognition, and cash collection.

layers.name: Credit Approval Control
layers.description: Validates customer credit limits before order acceptance, monitors credit exposure in real-time, flags overdue customers, and enforces credit hold procedures.

layers.name: Revenue Recognition Validation
layers.description: Ensures revenue is recognized in accordance with accounting standards (ASC 606 / IFRS 15), validates performance obligation completion, and prevents premature revenue recognition.

layers.name: Pricing & Discount Control
layers.description: Validates that pricing follows approved price lists, discount approvals are within authority, contract pricing is correctly applied, and bill-and-hold transactions are properly controlled.

layers.name: Shipment Matching Control
layers.description: Matches sales orders to shipment documents and invoices, prevents revenue recognition without shipment, and identifies unbilled shipments.

layers.name: Cash Application & Reconciliation
layers.description: Automatically matches customer payments to outstanding invoices, handles short payments and overpayments, and reconciles cash receipts to bank deposits.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Record-to-Report (R2R) Control Execution
description: Executes and validates all controls in the record-to-report cycle including journal entry approval, account reconciliations, financial close procedures, consolidation controls, and financial statement preparation.

layers.name: Journal Entry Control
layers.description: Validates that all journal entries are supported by appropriate documentation, approved by authorized individuals, and comply with accounting policies. Flags high-risk journal entries for additional review.

layers.name: Account Reconciliation Control
layers.description: Ensures all balance sheet accounts are reconciled within defined timelines, reconciling items are aged and resolved, and reconciliation reviews are completed by independent reviewers.

layers.name: Financial Close Checklist
layers.description: Executes financial close procedures in sequence, tracks completion of required tasks, identifies bottlenecks, and ensures close is completed within target timeline.

layers.name: Consolidation Control
layers.description: Validates intercompany eliminations, ensures consistent accounting policies across entities, identifies consolidation adjustments, and validates currency translations.

layers.name: Financial Statement Review
layers.description: Performs automated analytical review procedures, compares current period to prior period and budget, identifies unusual fluctuations, and validates disclosure completeness.

flow.description: All JE entries validated for approval

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Payroll Processing & Controls
description: Executes and validates all payroll controls including new hire approval, timesheet validation, payroll calculation accuracy, payment authorization, payroll tax compliance, and termination processing.

layers.name: Employee Master Control
layers.description: Validates that all new hires are properly approved, employee data changes require HR and finance approval, and terminated employees are removed from payroll immediately.

layers.name: Time & Attendance Validation
layers.description: Validates timesheet approvals, identifies unusual overtime patterns, ensures compliance with labor laws, and validates commission and bonus calculations.

layers.name: Payroll Calculation Control
layers.description: Validates payroll calculations against approved pay rates, identifies calculation anomalies, ensures proper tax withholdings, and validates net pay against historical patterns.

layers.name: Payment Authorization
layers.description: Ensures payroll is approved before payment execution, validates bank account changes, prevents duplicate payments, and segregates payroll preparation from approval.

layers.name: Payroll Reconciliation
layers.description: Reconciles payroll register to general ledger, validates tax deposits against returns, monitors payroll-related liabilities, and ensures compliance with statutory reporting.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Treasury & Cash Management Controls
description: Executes and validates treasury controls including bank account management, cash forecasting, investment authorization, debt covenant compliance, foreign exchange management, and daily cash reconciliation.

layers.name: Bank Account Control
layers.description: Validates that bank account openings require treasury and CFO approval, authorized signatories are current, account closures are processed timely, and dormant accounts are identified.

layers.name: Cash Position Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks cash positions across all entities and currencies in real-time, forecasts cash requirements, identifies potential shortfalls, and optimizes cash utilization.

layers.name: Investment & Borrowing Authorization
layers.description: Ensures investments and borrowings are within board-approved limits, counterparty risk is assessed, investment policy compliance is maintained, and debt covenants are monitored.

layers.name: Daily Cash Reconciliation
layers.description: Reconciles bank statements daily, identifies and investigates reconciling items, monitors uncleared checks, and validates intercompany cash transfers.

layers.name: Foreign Exchange Control
layers.description: Monitors FX exposure, validates hedge effectiveness, ensures timely revaluation of foreign currency balances, and tracks derivative positions.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Financial Data Quality & Integrity Validation
