description: Validates the accuracy, completeness, and consistency of financial data across all systems and processes, ensuring that financial reports are based on reliable data that meets quality standards.

layers.name: Data Validation Rules
layers.description: Maintains comprehensive data quality rules including format validation, range checks, referential integrity, mandatory field completion, and logical consistency checks.

layers.name: Duplicate Detection
layers.description: Identifies duplicate transactions, customers, vendors, and general ledger entries. Prevents duplicate processing and flags for resolution.

layers.name: Completeness Checks
layers.description: Validates that all required data elements are captured, sequence gaps in transaction numbers are identified, and all source system data is transferred to target systems.

layers.name: Consistency Validation
layers.description: Ensures data consistency across systems (e.g., customer balance in AR matches GL, inventory quantity matches warehouse system), identifies and resolves discrepancies.

layers.name: Historical Data Integrity
layers.description: Monitors for unauthorized changes to historical transactions, validates audit trail completeness, and ensures data cannot be manipulated without detection.

flow.description: Data quality issues tracked to resolution

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: System Integration & Interface Control Monitoring
description: Monitors the completeness and accuracy of data transfers between financial systems and source systems, ensuring interfaces operate reliably and data integrity is maintained during transmission.

layers.name: Interface Inventory Management
layers.description: Maintains complete inventory of all interfaces feeding financial systems including real-time APIs, batch file transfers, database replication, and manual uploads.

layers.name: Interface Execution Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks scheduled interface runs, identifies failed or delayed interfaces, monitors processing times, and validates interface completion before dependent processes start.

layers.name: Data Reconciliation Controls
layers.description: Reconciles record counts and totals between source and target systems, identifies missing or duplicate records, and validates data transformation logic.

layers.name: Error Handling & Retry Logic
layers.description: Monitors interface error logs, validates automatic retry mechanisms, tracks manual interventions, and ensures failed records are processed or resolved.

layers.name: Interface Change Management
layers.description: Ensures interface changes are properly tested, validated, and approved before production deployment, and parallel runs are performed for critical interfaces.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Management & External Reporting Controls
description: Validates the accuracy and completeness of financial reports distributed to management, board, investors, and regulators, ensuring reports are derived from accurate data and comply with reporting requirements.

layers.name: Report Source Validation
layers.description: Validates that reports are generated from approved data sources, formula and calculation logic is correct, and report parameters match reporting requirements.

layers.name: Reconciliation to General Ledger
layers.description: Ensures all external reports (investor presentations, regulatory filings, press releases) reconcile to the general ledger and supporting schedules.

layers.name: Disclosure Review Controls
layers.description: Validates disclosure completeness against checklists, ensures consistency between financial statements and MD&A, and validates regulatory compliance.

layers.name: Version Control & Approval
layers.description: Maintains version control on all distributed reports, tracks review and approval workflows, and ensures only approved versions are distributed.

layers.name: Distribution Control
layers.description: Validates that reports are distributed to the correct recipients, sensitive information is protected, and distribution timing complies with regulatory requirements.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: External Financial Communication Monitoring
description: Monitors and validates all external financial communications including earnings releases, investor presentations, press releases, and regulatory filings to ensure accuracy and consistency with audited financial statements.

layers.name: Communication Inventory
layers.description: Maintains inventory of all external financial communications, tracks disclosure obligations, and ensures timely communication of material events.

layers.name: Content Validation
layers.description: Validates that financial metrics in external communications match approved sources, non-GAAP measures are properly reconciled, and forward-looking statements include required disclaimers.

layers.name: Consistency Review
layers.description: Ensures consistency across all external communications, identifies conflicting information, and validates alignment with financial statements.

layers.name: Approval Workflow
layers.description: Enforces multi-level approval for external communications (CFO, legal, investor relations, CEO), tracks review completion, and maintains approval evidence.

layers.name: Regulatory Compliance Tracking
layers.description: Validates compliance with disclosure regulations (Reg FD, SOX, stock exchange rules), tracks filing deadlines, and monitors selective disclosure risks.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Internal Control Communication & Training
description: Ensures that employees understand their control responsibilities, are trained on control procedures, and that control changes and deficiencies are communicated effectively to all relevant stakeholders.

layers.name: Control Owner Communication
layers.description: Ensures control owners understand their responsibilities, receive updates on control changes, and are alerted to control failures or testing results.

layers.name: Training Needs Analysis
layers.description: Identifies training requirements based on role, control ownership, deficiency patterns, and regulatory changes. Tracks training completion and effectiveness.

layers.name: Control Change Communication
layers.description: Communicates control design changes, policy updates, and system enhancements to affected users with adequate lead time for adaptation.

layers.name: Deficiency Communication
layers.description: Ensures control deficiencies are communicated to appropriate levels of management, action plans are tracked, and escalation occurs if remediation is delayed.

layers.name: Continuous Awareness Programs
layers.description: Maintains ongoing internal control awareness through newsletters, workshops, refresher training, and control effectiveness scorecards.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Automated Transaction Testing & Validation
description: Performs continuous automated testing of financial transactions against control criteria, identifying exceptions and control failures in real-time rather than through periodic testing cycles.

layers.name: Test Script Library
layers.description: Maintains library of automated test scripts for each control including authorization checks, calculation validations, segregation of duties tests, and policy compliance tests.

layers.name: Continuous Testing Execution
layers.description: Executes test scripts on 100% of transactions in real-time or near-real-time, eliminating the need for sampling and providing complete population coverage.

layers.name: Exception Detection & Categorization
layers.description: Identifies control exceptions, categorizes by severity (critical, high, medium, low), determines root cause, and routes for investigation.

layers.name: Test Results Analytics
layers.description: Analyzes test results to identify control weakness patterns, high-failure controls, process areas requiring remediation, and emerging risks.

layers.name: Evidence Compilation
layers.description: Automatically compiles test evidence, maintains audit trail of testing performed, documents exceptions and resolutions, and provides audit-ready documentation.

flow.description: Control tests maintained as executable scripts

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Statistical Sampling & Extrapolation
description: Performs statistical sampling for controls that cannot be tested at 100% population, ensures sample sizes are adequate, and extrapolates results to the entire population with appropriate confidence levels.

layers.name: Sample Size Determination
layers.description: Calculates appropriate sample sizes based on population size, expected error rate, confidence level, and precision requirements using statistical sampling methodologies.

layers.name: Random Sample Selection
layers.description: Generates random samples or stratified samples ensuring all population segments are represented, eliminates selection bias, and maintains audit trail of selection.

layers.name: Sample Testing Execution
layers.description: Executes control tests on selected samples, documents test results, identifies exceptions, and validates that sample characteristics match population.

layers.name: Error Rate Calculation
layers.description: Calculates sample error rate, determines if error rate is within acceptable tolerance, and flags when error rates exceed thresholds.

layers.name: Population Extrapolation
layers.description: Extrapolates sample results to entire population, calculates upper error limits with specified confidence levels, and determines population control effectiveness.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: High-Risk Transaction Review
description: Identifies and subjects high-risk transactions to enhanced scrutiny and additional validation procedures beyond standard controls, preventing material errors and fraud.

layers.name: Risk Scoring Model
layers.description: Scores every transaction based on risk factors including amount, transaction type, approver, timing (period-end), account combinations, and historical patterns.

layers.name: Threshold-Based Flagging
layers.description: Flags transactions exceeding materiality thresholds, involving sensitive accounts, processed outside normal hours, or matching known fraud patterns.

layers.name: Enhanced Review Procedures
layers.description: Routes high-risk transactions for additional documentation review, secondary approval, independent verification, or management review before posting.

layers.name: Pattern Anomaly Detection
layers.description: Identifies unusual transaction patterns such as round amounts, sequential entries, off-cycle processing, or transactions just below approval thresholds.

layers.name: Investigative Workflow
layers.description: Initiates investigation workflow for highest risk transactions, compiles evidence, documents resolution, and maintains case management.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Continuous Audit Monitoring
description: Provides continuous audit coverage through automated monitoring of key controls, risk indicators, and compliance requirements, enabling real-time assurance and reducing reliance on periodic audits.

layers.name: Audit Universe Mapping
layers.description: Maps all auditable processes, controls, and compliance requirements to automated monitoring routines, ensuring comprehensive audit coverage.

layers.name: Real-Time Audit Tests
layers.description: Executes audit tests continuously on transaction streams, master data changes, access logs, and reconciliation processes rather than at period-end.

layers.name: Risk Indicator Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks leading risk indicators such as increasing error rates, rising exception volumes, control override frequency, and unusual activity patterns.

layers.name: Compliance Monitoring
layers.description: Validates ongoing compliance with policies, regulations, and industry standards through continuous rule-based testing rather than periodic audits.

layers.name: Audit Issue Tracking
layers.description: Maintains register of all audit issues identified through continuous monitoring, tracks remediation actions, and validates issue closure.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Exception & Variance Testing
description: Identifies and investigates exceptions and variances from expected patterns, norms, or thresholds across financial transactions and account balances, detecting errors and irregularities.

layers.name: Baseline Establishment
layers.description: Establishes baseline patterns for transaction volumes, amounts, account balances, ratios, and trends based on historical data and business drivers.

layers.name: Variance Detection
layers.description: Compares actual results against baselines, identifies statistically significant variances, and distinguishes between expected business variations and control failures.

layers.name: Exception Categorization
layers.description: Categorizes exceptions by type (timing, amount, frequency, authorization), assigns severity based on financial impact, and prioritizes investigation.

layers.name: Root Cause Analysis
layers.description: Investigates exceptions to determine root cause (system error, manual error, control failure, fraud, business change), documents findings, and recommends corrective action.

layers.name: Exception Resolution Tracking
layers.description: Tracks exception investigation and resolution, validates corrective actions, and monitors for recurrence of similar exceptions.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Journal Entry Review & Approval
description: Reviews and approves all journal entries to ensure they are supported by appropriate documentation, comply with accounting policies, are approved by authorized individuals, and are accurately recorded.

layers.name: Entry Classification
layers.description: Classifies journal entries by type (standard, recurring, manual, adjusting, reclassification), risk level, and review requirements.

layers.name: Supporting Documentation Validation
layers.description: Ensures all journal entries have adequate supporting documentation attached, validates calculations, and confirms business rationale.

layers.name: Accounting Policy Compliance
layers.description: Validates that journal entries comply with accounting policies, GAAP/IFRS requirements, and company-specific guidelines.

layers.name: Approval Authority Validation
layers.description: Ensures journal entries are approved by individuals with appropriate authority based on entry type, amount, and account impact.

layers.name: Posting Period Control
layers.description: Validates that entries are posted to the correct accounting period, prevents backdating, and ensures period-end adjustments are properly authorized.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Manual Journal Entry Monitoring
description: Monitors manual journal entries for unusual patterns, high-risk characteristics, and potential fraud indicators, providing enhanced oversight over the highest risk area of financial reporting.

layers.name: Manual Entry Identification
layers.description: Identifies all manual journal entries (entries not automatically generated by systems), tracks volume trends, and monitors increase in manual entries.

layers.name: High-Risk Pattern Detection
layers.description: Flags entries with fraud risk indicators including round amounts, unusual account combinations, period-end timing, reversals in subsequent period, and off-hours posting.

layers.name: User Behavior Analytics
layers.description: Monitors user patterns to identify unusual behavior such as first-time entries to sensitive accounts, entries outside normal work hours, or entries by users with excess access.

layers.name: Top-Side Entry Review
layers.description: Subjects corporate-level consolidation entries and top-side adjustments to enhanced review given their materiality and potential for management override.

layers.name: Trend Analysis
layers.description: Analyzes manual entry trends over time, identifies increases in manual interventions, and assesses whether increases indicate control deterioration or process automation opportunities.

flow.description: Corporate entries subjected to enhanced review

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Period-End Adjustment Control
description: Controls and monitors period-end adjusting entries to ensure they are appropriate, properly supported, and do not manipulate financial results to meet targets or manage earnings.

layers.name: Adjustment Type Validation
layers.description: Validates that period-end adjustments are for legitimate accruals, deferrals, reclassifications, or corrections rather than earnings management.

layers.name: Magnitude & Trend Analysis
layers.description: Compares period-end adjustment amounts to historical patterns, identifies unusual increases, and flags adjustments just sufficient to meet targets.

layers.name: Reversal Pattern Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks adjustments that reverse in subsequent period, identifies patterns of recurring reversals, and assesses whether adjustments were appropriate.

layers.name: Account Sensitivity Analysis
layers.description: Subjects adjustments to sensitive accounts (revenue, COGS, reserves, provisions) to enhanced scrutiny given their income statement impact.

layers.name: Management Pressure Indicators
layers.description: Identifies periods with high pressure to meet targets (quarter-end, year-end, covenant compliance) and applies enhanced review to adjustments made during these periods.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Account Reconciliation Management
description: Manages the account reconciliation process to ensure all balance sheet accounts are reconciled timely, reconciling items are investigated and resolved, and reconciliation reviews are completed independently.

layers.name: Reconciliation Universe
layers.description: Maintains inventory of all accounts requiring reconciliation, defines reconciliation frequency (daily, weekly, monthly), and assigns reconciliation owners.

layers.name: Reconciliation Completion Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks reconciliation completion against schedule, identifies late or missing reconciliations, and escalates to management.

layers.name: Reconciling Item Aging
layers.description: Ages all reconciling items, tracks resolution progress, flags long-outstanding items, and ensures timely resolution.

layers.name: Review Completion Validation
layers.description: Ensures reconciliations are reviewed by independent reviewers, validates review sign-off, and tracks reviewer workload to prevent review bottlenecks.

layers.name: Reconciliation Quality Assessment
layers.description: Assesses reconciliation quality through metrics such as first-time accuracy, number of reconciling items, and time to resolution.

flow.description: All accounts mapped to reconciliation schedule

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Intercompany Reconciliation & Elimination
description: Reconciles and eliminates intercompany transactions and balances to ensure consolidated financial statements are free from internal transactions and that intercompany accounts balance to zero.

layers.name: Intercompany Transaction Matching
layers.description: Matches intercompany sales to purchases, receivables to payables, and loans to borrowings across all legal entities within the group.

layers.name: Timing Difference Identification
layers.description: Identifies timing differences in intercompany transaction recording (e.g., goods shipped but not received), tracks in-transit items, and ensures resolution before consolidation.

layers.name: Foreign Currency Impact
layers.description: Validates that intercompany transactions in foreign currencies are translated consistently across entities and eliminates translation differences in consolidation.

layers.name: Elimination Entry Validation
layers.description: Validates that intercompany elimination entries are complete, accurate, and properly offset all internal transactions and balances.

layers.name: Unreconciled Balance Resolution
layers.description: Tracks and resolves unreconciled intercompany balances, investigates root causes, and ensures timely resolution to prevent consolidation delays.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: SOX Compliance Management
description: Manages Sarbanes-Oxley compliance requirements including control documentation, testing, deficiency tracking, certifications, and quarterly/annual assessments to ensure continuous SOX compliance.

layers.name: Control Documentation Management
layers.description: Maintains complete documentation of all key controls including control objectives, control activities, frequency, responsible parties, and testing procedures.

layers.name: Testing Cycle Management
layers.description: Manages control testing cycles (quarterly, annual), assigns testing responsibilities, tracks testing completion, and validates testing adequacy.

layers.name: Deficiency Management
layers.description: Tracks control deficiencies from identification through remediation, categorizes severity (material weakness, significant deficiency, deficiency), and monitors management action plans.

layers.name: Certification Process
layers.description: Manages CEO/CFO certification process, compiles supporting evidence, tracks sub-certifications from business unit leaders, and maintains certification documentation.

layers.name: Scoping & Prioritization
layers.description: Performs annual SOX scoping to identify significant accounts and processes, determines key controls, and prioritizes testing resources.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Audit Evidence & Workpaper Management
description: Manages audit evidence and workpapers to ensure all control testing, exception investigations, and audit requests are properly documented, organized, and readily accessible for internal and external auditors.

layers.name: Evidence Repository
layers.description: Maintains centralized repository of all audit evidence including test results, exception investigations, management reviews, approvals, and supporting documentation.

layers.name: Automated Evidence Collection
layers.description: Automatically captures audit evidence from systems including approval workflows, system logs, reconciliation files, and transaction records rather than manual collection.

layers.name: Workpaper Organization
layers.description: Organizes evidence by audit area, control, testing period, and auditor requests, enabling rapid retrieval and preventing duplicate requests.

layers.name: Version Control & Retention
layers.description: Maintains version control on all workpapers, ensures retention policies are followed, and provides secure access to historical audit evidence.

layers.name: Audit Request Tracking
layers.description: Tracks all internal and external audit requests, monitors response deadlines, validates completeness of responses, and maintains request history.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Control Documentation Maintenance
description: Maintains current and complete documentation of all internal controls including narratives, process flows, control matrices, and risk-control mappings, ensuring documentation accurately reflects actual control design.

layers.name: Documentation Completeness
layers.description: Ensures all controls have complete documentation including control objective, detailed control description, frequency, responsible party, and evidence of execution.

layers.name: Process Flow Accuracy
layers.description: Maintains process flowcharts showing where controls operate within processes, validates flows match actual operations, and updates flows when processes change.

layers.name: Risk-Control Mapping
layers.description: Maps each control to specific risks it mitigates, ensures all significant risks have controls, and identifies control gaps.

layers.name: Documentation Version Control
layers.description: Tracks changes to control documentation, maintains historical versions, and ensures all stakeholders work from current versions.

layers.name: Documentation Review Cycles
layers.description: Performs periodic reviews of control documentation to ensure accuracy, identify outdated content, and validate consistency across the organization.

flow.description: Process flows validated against actual operations

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Regulatory Compliance Monitoring
description: Monitors compliance with financial reporting regulations, accounting standards, tax regulations, and industry-specific requirements, ensuring the organization remains compliant and avoids penalties.

layers.name: Regulatory Universe Mapping
layers.description: Maintains inventory of all applicable regulations including GAAP/IFRS, SEC rules, tax regulations, industry regulations, and local statutory requirements.

layers.name: Requirement Tracking
layers.description: Tracks specific compliance requirements from each regulation, maps requirements to responsible parties, and monitors compliance status.

layers.name: Regulatory Change Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors regulatory updates and new pronouncements, assesses impact on the organization, and ensures timely implementation of new requirements.

layers.name: Compliance Gap Assessment
layers.description: Identifies gaps between current state and regulatory requirements, prioritizes gap remediation based on risk, and tracks remediation to completion.

layers.name: Filing & Deadline Management
layers.description: Tracks all regulatory filing deadlines, monitors preparation progress, validates filing completeness, and ensures timely submission.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Change Documentation & Traceability
description: Documents all changes to financial processes, systems, controls, and organizational structure, ensuring complete traceability, impact assessment, and audit trail of all changes affecting financial reporting.

layers.name: Change Request Management
layers.description: Captures all change requests affecting financial processes, validates business justification, and ensures proper approval before implementation.

layers.name: Impact Assessment Documentation
layers.description: Documents impact of changes on controls, processes, systems, risks, and financial reporting, identifies affected stakeholders, and validates readiness.

layers.name: Implementation Documentation
layers.description: Maintains complete record of implementation activities including testing results, parallel runs, data migration validation, and go-live verification.

layers.name: Before/After Comparison
layers.description: Documents pre-change and post-change state for all significant changes, enabling rollback if needed and providing audit trail of evolution.

layers.name: Lessons Learned Capture
layers.description: Captures lessons learned from each change, identifies opportunities for improvement, and incorporates insights into future change management.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Financial Performance Analytics
description: Provides comprehensive financial analytics to management including variance analysis, trend analysis, ratio analysis, and predictive insights to support decision-making and identify potential issues.

layers.name: Variance Analysis Automation
layers.description: Automatically calculates variances between actual and budget, actual and prior period, and actual and forecast across all financial statement line items.

layers.name: Ratio & Trend Analysis
layers.description: Calculates key financial ratios (liquidity, profitability, efficiency, leverage), tracks trends over time, and flags deteriorating trends.

layers.name: Segment & Product Analytics
layers.description: Provides profitability analytics by business segment, product line, customer, and geography, enabling informed portfolio decisions.

layers.name: Driver-Based Analysis
layers.description: Links financial results to business drivers (volume, price, mix, cost), quantifies driver impacts, and validates financial results against operational metrics.

layers.name: Scenario Modeling
layers.description: Enables what-if scenario analysis for management decisions, quantifies financial impact of strategic options, and supports business case development.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Control Effectiveness Analytics
description: Analyzes control effectiveness metrics to identify control weaknesses, high-failure controls, emerging risks, and opportunities for control optimization and automation.

layers.name: Control Performance Metrics
layers.description: Tracks metrics for each control including execution rate, exception rate, timeliness, and override frequency.

layers.name: Failure Pattern Analysis
layers.description: Identifies patterns in control failures including high-failure controls, repeat failures, failure clustering by period or user, and root cause trends.

layers.name: Control Coverage Analysis
layers.description: Assesses whether control coverage is adequate for identified risks, identifies redundant controls, and highlights control gaps.

layers.name: Automation Opportunity Identification
layers.description: Identifies manual controls with high execution burden, frequent failures, or automation potential to improve efficiency and effectiveness.

layers.name: Control Maturity Assessment
layers.description: Assesses overall control maturity using frameworks (COSO, COBIT), benchmarks against industry standards, and identifies maturity improvement opportunities.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Predictive Risk Analytics
description: Uses predictive analytics and machine learning to forecast future control failures, identify emerging risks before they materialize, and enable proactive risk mitigation rather than reactive response.

layers.name: Leading Indicator Modeling
layers.description: Identifies leading indicators that predict control failures or financial statement risks before they occur based on historical patterns.

layers.name: Risk Score Forecasting
layers.description: Develops predictive models to forecast risk scores for accounts, processes, and entities based on historical risk factors and current conditions.

layers.name: Anomaly Prediction
layers.description: Uses machine learning to predict unusual patterns, outliers, and potential fraud scenarios before they fully develop.

layers.name: Workload & Resource Forecasting
layers.description: Predicts future workload for reconciliations, reviews, and approvals to enable proactive resource allocation and prevent bottlenecks.

layers.name: Scenario-Based Risk Modeling
layers.description: Forecasts risk exposure under different business scenarios (growth, downturn, M&A, regulatory change) to enable forward-looking risk management.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Executive Dashboard & Reporting
description: Provides real-time executive dashboards with comprehensive visibility into financial performance, control effectiveness, compliance status, and risk exposure for informed decision-making.

layers.name: KPI Definition & Tracking
layers.description: Defines key performance indicators for financial reporting, internal controls, and compliance, tracks against targets, and highlights variances.

layers.name: Role-Based Dashboards
layers.description: Provides customized dashboards for different stakeholders (CFO, Controller, CAE, Board) with relevant metrics and drill-down capabilities.

layers.name: Real-Time Data Refresh
layers.description: Updates dashboards in real-time as data changes, ensuring management decisions are based on current information rather than stale data.

layers.name: Exception-Based Reporting
layers.description: Highlights only items requiring management attention, eliminating information overload and enabling focus on critical issues.

layers.name: Mobile Accessibility
layers.description: Provides mobile-optimized dashboards enabling executives to monitor key metrics and respond to alerts from anywhere.

function: ICOFR - Internal Control on Financial Reporting
title: Continuous Improvement & Optimization
description: Drives continuous improvement in financial processes and controls through systematic analysis of inefficiencies, automation opportunities, best practice adoption, and innovation in financial operations.

layers.name: Process Efficiency Analysis
layers.description: Analyzes cycle times, manual touchpoints, rework rates, and bottlenecks across all financial processes to identify improvement opportunities.

layers.name: Automation ROI Analysis
layers.description: Quantifies the cost-benefit of automating manual processes, prioritizes automation opportunities, and tracks realization of automation benefits.

layers.name: Best Practice Benchmarking
layers.description: Benchmarks internal practices against industry best practices, identifies adoption gaps, and develops implementation plans for leading practices.

layers.name: Innovation Scouting
layers.description: Monitors emerging technologies (AI, RPA, blockchain) relevant to financial operations, assesses applicability, and pilots innovative solutions.

layers.name: Continuous Improvement Tracking
layers.description: Tracks all improvement initiatives from ideation through implementation, measures realized benefits, and maintains improvement backlog.

title: Annual Audit Universe & Risk Assessment
description:
Builds the complete audit universe — every auditable entity, process, and system across the organisation — and scores each by inherent risk to produce a prioritised, risk-ranked audit plan for the year.
  Replaces the subjective, relationship-driven audit planning of the past with a data-driven, defensible risk-prioritisation framework approved by the Audit Committee.

layers.name: Universe Construction
layers.description: Reads the organisation structure, ERP process catalogue, subsidiary list, and prior audit register to build the complete audit universe — entities, business processes, IT systems, and third-party relationships.

layers.name: Inherent Risk Scoring
layers.description: Scores each universe item across risk dimensions — financial materiality (balance sheet and P&L exposure), transaction volume, regulatory sensitivity, prior audit findings, management complexity, and rate of change.

layers.name: Control Environment Assessment
layers.description: Overlays the control environment score — maturity of internal controls, frequency of prior findings, management self-assessment scores, and time since last audit.

layers.name: Residual Risk Ranking
layers.description: Computes residual risk = Inherent Risk minus Control Effectiveness. Ranks all universe items from Critical to Low. Allocates audit days proportionally to residual risk.

layers.name: Plan Optimisation
layers.description: Optimises the plan against available audit resources and mandatory audits. Ensures coverage of all Critical and High items. Identifies opportunities for continuous auditing on low-risk, high-volume items.

flow.description: Inherent risk scored per universe item

title: Continuous Risk Monitoring
description:
Continuously monitors the risk landscape between planned audits — scanning transactions, KPIs, news, and regulatory changes — to detect emerging risks that require an unscheduled audit or control enhancement.
  Shifts internal audit from periodic snapshot to near-real-time risk sentinel.

layers.name: Financial Risk Signals
layers.description: Monitors key financial metrics for anomalies — sudden revenue drops, cost spikes, margin deterioration, unusual accruals, and balance sheet inflections that could signal control failures or fraud.

layers.name: Operational Risk Signals
layers.description: Tracks operational KPIs — supplier concentration, customer concentration, inventory write-offs, unusual returns, and production downtime — for patterns inconsistent with the business plan.

layers.name: External Risk Signals
layers.description: Monitors external risk intelligence — regulatory changes, industry enforcement actions, supplier adverse media, and macroeconomic shifts that could affect the company's risk profile.

layers.name: Control Failure Indicators
layers.description: Reads exception reports from all key control systems — ERP segregation of duty violations, access control exceptions, policy bypass logs, and reconciliation failures.

layers.name: Risk Alert Generation
layers.description: Aggregates signals and generates risk alerts — with severity classification (Critical / High / Medium), supporting evidence, and recommended audit or management action.

flow.description: News, regulatory, and macro signals scanned

title: Audit Scoping & Fieldwork Planning
description:
Prepares the detailed scope, objectives, and fieldwork plan for each scheduled audit — including risk and control matrix, sampling plan, data analytics approach, and resource allocation.
  Ensures every audit starts with a clear, risk-focused scope and a realistic, resource-efficient fieldwork plan.

layers.name: Prior Audit Review
layers.description: Reads prior audit reports, management action plans, and follow-up status for the audit area. Identifies recurring findings and unresolved issues that must be covered.

layers.name: Process Mapping
layers.description: Reads process documentation (SOPs, flowcharts) for the audit area. Identifies key controls — preventive, detective, and corrective — and maps them to the relevant risks.

layers.name: Risk & Control Matrix
layers.description: Builds the Risk and Control Matrix (RCM) — risk ID, risk description, inherent risk, key control, control owner, control frequency, and test approach.

layers.name: Sampling Plan
layers.description: Determines the testing approach for each control — full population testing (for high-risk automated controls), statistical sampling, or judgmental sampling. Computes sample size based on population and confidence level.

layers.name: Fieldwork Schedule
layers.description: Builds the fieldwork schedule — auditor assignments, testing days per control area, management interviews, and data request deadlines. Shares with audit team and management.

flow.description: Prior findings and open items reviewed
Pre-fieldwork data requests sent to management

title: Audit Universe Maintenance
description:
Keeps the audit universe current — adding new processes, entities, and systems as the business evolves, retiring obsolete items, and re-scoring risks when business or regulatory conditions change.
  Prevents the audit plan from becoming stale and missing significant risks introduced by business change.

layers.name: Business Change Detection
layers.description: Monitors signals of audit universe change — new subsidiaries formed, M&A activity, new product launches, major IT system go-lives, regulatory expansion, and significant headcount additions.

layers.name: Universe Item Addition
layers.description: For each detected change, creates a new audit universe item — categorises it (entity/process/system/relationship), assigns an initial risk score, and recommends timing for first audit.

layers.name: Retirement of Obsolete Items
layers.description: Identifies universe items that no longer exist — discontinued businesses, decommissioned systems, and wound-up subsidiaries — and retires them from the active universe.

layers.name: Risk Re-Scoring
layers.description: Re-scores existing universe items when their risk profile changes — increased transaction volumes, regulatory changes, management turnover, or prior year findings.

layers.name: CAE Approval
layers.description: Presents universe changes to the CAE quarterly for review and approval. Any item moved from Low to High risk triggers an automatic plan review.

flow.description: Universe changes assessed against current plan

title: Audit Resource Management & Capacity Planning
description:
Manages the internal audit team's capacity — matching auditor skills to audit requirements, tracking utilisation, managing guest auditors and co-sourcing, and ensuring the annual plan is achievable.
  Prevents plan under-delivery from resource constraints and maximises the return on the audit team investment.

layers.name: Team Skill Profiling
layers.description: Maintains a skills inventory of all internal auditors — technical skills (IT audit, forensic, financial, operational), industry knowledge, certifications (CIA, CISA, CFE, CA), and language capabilities.

layers.name: Audit-to-Auditor Matching
layers.description: For each planned audit, matches the required skill profile (technical, domain, seniority) to available auditors. Flags audits where no suitable internal auditor is available — co-sourcing candidate.

layers.name: Utilisation Tracking
layers.description: Tracks auditor utilisation — audit days vs. available days, overtime, leave, and training. Identifies over-utilised auditors (burnout risk) and under-utilised capacity.

layers.name: Co-Sourcing & Guest Auditor Management
layers.description: Manages co-sourcing relationships — SOW preparation, quality oversight, knowledge transfer tracking, and cost vs. budget. Manages guest auditor onboarding and contribution.

layers.name: Plan Feasibility Check
layers.description: After any plan change or team change, recomputes plan feasibility — available audit days vs. plan days required. Flags infeasibility to CAE early for plan adjustment or resource add.

title: Accounts Payable Audit
description:
Performs a comprehensive audit of the accounts payable process — testing for duplicate payments, fictitious vendors, three-way match failures, payment terms violations, and segregation of duty weaknesses.
  Identifies AP leakage and control failures with near-100% transaction coverage through automated analytics.

layers.name: Full Population AP Data Pull
layers.description: Extracts the complete AP transaction file for the audit period — all invoices, payment runs, credit notes, advance payments, and vendor master changes. No sampling — full population.

layers.name: Duplicate Payment Testing
layers.description: Runs multi-dimensional duplicate detection — same vendor + same amount + same invoice number, same vendor + same amount + similar dates (fuzzy), same amount + same date + different vendors (ghost vendor signal).

layers.name: Three-Way Match Testing
layers.description: Tests every AP invoice for three-way match compliance — invoice vs. PO vs. GRN. Flags invoices processed without a PO, GRN, or within tolerance limit that exceeded the approved threshold.

layers.name: Vendor Master Change Analysis
layers.description: Analyses all vendor master changes — new vendor additions, bank account changes, address changes — during the audit period. Flags changes made by AP staff who also process payments (SOD violation).

layers.name: Payment Terms & Early Payment Analysis
layers.description: Tests compliance with contracted payment terms. Identifies early payments (potential preference fraud) and payments outside approved terms without documented exception.

title: Revenue & Receivables Audit
description:
Audits the revenue recognition process and accounts receivable balance — testing for premature recognition, unsupported adjustments, credit note abuse, and collection control weaknesses.
  Provides assurance on one of the highest-risk financial statement assertions — completeness and accuracy of revenue.

layers.name: Revenue Recognition Testing
layers.description: Tests that revenue is recognised in the correct period per Ind AS 115 — specifically for multi-element arrangements, milestone-based contracts, and subscription revenue. Flags revenue recognised before performance obligation is met.

layers.name: Credit Note & Discount Analysis
layers.description: Analyses all credit notes — total value, timing relative to year-end, distribution by salesperson, and concentration. Flags unusual credit note patterns near period-end (potential reversal of recognised revenue).

layers.name: Ageing & Provisioning Test
layers.description: Tests the adequacy of the bad debt provision — applies expected credit loss (ECL) model per Ind AS 109 to the AR ageing. Identifies under-provisioned balances.

layers.name: Collection Testing
layers.description: Tests that cash collected is applied to the correct invoices. Flags unapplied cash, long-outstanding unapplied balances, and collection agency payments without matching invoice closure.

layers.name: Channel & Customer Concentration
layers.description: Analyses revenue by channel, customer, and geography. Flags unusual concentration, customers with unusual payment patterns, and customers who accept large year-end volumes but return post-period.

title: Payroll Audit
description:
Audits the payroll process end-to-end — testing for ghost employees, duplicate payments, unsupported salary changes, and statutory compliance — using full population analytics rather than sampling.
  Payroll is often the largest single expense line and highest fraud risk area — continuous analytics provide assurance every month rather than once a year.

layers.name: Ghost Employee Detection
layers.description: Compares the payroll register against the HRMS active employee list, badge system access logs, and IT system active users. Employees on payroll but absent from all other systems are flagged as potential ghost employees.

layers.name: Salary Change Validation
layers.description: Tests every salary change in the period — increment, promotion, new joiner, and off-cycle adjustment — for supporting approval. Flags changes without a documented HRMS approval event.

layers.name: Duplicate Payment Testing
layers.description: Detects duplicate payroll payments — same employee + same period + same amount, same bank account as another employee, and terminated employees still on payroll.

layers.name: Statutory Deduction Compliance
layers.description: Tests that PF, ESI, TDS, and Professional Tax deductions are correctly computed and deposited. Compares ECR file against payroll register. Tests that deposited amounts match deducted amounts.

layers.name: High-Risk Employee Testing
layers.description: Analyses the distribution of payroll — employees in the top 1% by earnings, employees with large changes, and employees with compensation above their grade band ceiling.

flow.description: Payroll register and HRMS data extracted

title: Fixed Assets Audit
description:
Audits the fixed asset register — verifying physical existence, proper capitalisation vs. expensing, depreciation accuracy, impairment assessment, and disposal compliance.
  Fixed assets are a major balance sheet item and common source of both overstatement and misappropriation.

layers.name: Physical Existence Verification
layers.description: Selects a sample of fixed assets from the register and sends verification requests to asset custodians. Uses GPS-tagged assets and QR code scanning where available for automated verification.

layers.name: Completeness — Physical to Register
layers.description: Identifies assets physically present but not in the register (unrecorded assets). Tests whether significant capital expenditure from the period is fully reflected in the asset register.

layers.name: Capitalisation vs. Expense Testing
layers.description: Reviews items expensed in the period above the capitalisation threshold. Flags items that should have been capitalised — common form of expense manipulation to reduce reported profit.

layers.name: Depreciation Accuracy
layers.description: Tests depreciation computation — correct rate per asset class, no depreciation on fully depreciated assets, and correct depreciation from the month of addition. Reconciles net book value movement.

layers.name: Disposal & Impairment
layers.description: Tests that all asset disposals are approved and proceeds properly accounted for. Reviews idle or low-utilisation assets for impairment indicators per Ind AS 36.

flow.description: Disposals tested for approval and proceeds

title: Financial Closing & Reporting Audit
description:
Audits the financial closing process — journal entry review, account reconciliation quality, and financial statement accuracy — providing assurance that the numbers presented to management and the board are reliable.
  The financial close is where errors, manipulations, and omissions most commonly occur — AI-powered analytics cover 100% of journal entries.

layers.name: Journal Entry Analytics
layers.description: Extracts all journal entries for the period. Applies high-risk filters — manual journals (vs. automated), journals posted by IT or system admins, round-number journals, journals posted on holidays or outside business hours, and top-side adjustments.

layers.name: Unusual JE Pattern Detection
layers.description: Identifies patterns that suggest potential manipulation — entries that reverse in the next period, entries that bring an account balance exactly to zero, and entries with vague or missing narrations.

layers.name: Account Reconciliation Quality
layers.description: Reviews the quality of balance sheet account reconciliations — are all material balances reconciled? Are reconciling items old or unsupported? Is the reconciliation reviewed and approved?

layers.name: Cut-off Testing
layers.description: Tests financial period cut-off — revenue and expenses recorded in the correct period. Specifically tests goods-in-transit, accruals completeness, and deferred revenue appropriateness.

layers.name: Management Accounts Review
layers.description: Compares the management accounts against prior period, budget, and industry benchmarks. Flags unexplained variances for auditor follow-up.

flow.description: Financial accounts vs. budget and prior period

title: Procurement & Vendor Management Audit
description:
Audits the procurement process — vendor selection, competitive bidding compliance, purchase order controls, contract management, and related party transactions — identifying fraud and control gaps.
  Procurement is the single highest fraud risk process in most organisations. AI-powered analytics cover every transaction.

layers.name: Vendor Selection Compliance
layers.description: Tests whether competitive bidding requirements were followed — all purchases above threshold had minimum 3 quotes, no single-source without documented justification, and no split orders to avoid thresholds.

layers.name: Related Party Vendor Analysis
layers.description: Cross-references the vendor master against employee, director, and key management personnel databases. Flags vendors with ownership connections to company insiders.

layers.name: Purchase Order Compliance
layers.description: Tests PO compliance — all purchases with a valid PO before receipt, no after-the-fact POs, approval within authority matrix, and PO not raised after invoice.

layers.name: Contract Compliance
layers.description: Tests that purchases are within contracted prices, volumes, and terms. Identifies off-contract purchases, price exceeding contract, and purchases from non-approved vendors.

layers.name: Receiving & Approval Independence
layers.description: Tests that the person who approves the purchase is not the same person who receives the goods (segregation of duties) and that all receiving is physically confirmed.

title: Inventory & Supply Chain Audit
description:
Audits inventory management — physical count accuracy, valuation, obsolescence provisioning, movement controls, and supply chain resilience — ensuring inventory is accurately stated and theft/loss is minimised.
  Inventory is a major asset with significant exposure to theft, valuation error, and write-off manipulation.

layers.name: Stock Count Observation
layers.description: Plans and oversees the physical stock count — defining count teams, sample selection, count procedures, and variance investigation thresholds. Uses RFID / barcode scanning for accuracy.

layers.name: Book vs. Physical Reconciliation
layers.description: Reconciles the physical count against the book inventory. Classifies variances — count errors, unrecorded movements, theft, and system timing differences.

layers.name: Valuation Testing
layers.description: Tests inventory valuation — FIFO/WAC consistency, NRV comparison (for write-down requirement), and cut-off testing for goods in transit.

layers.name: Obsolescence & Write-Off Testing
layers.description: Analyses slow-moving and non-moving inventory. Tests that obsolescence provisions are adequate per policy and that write-offs are properly approved.

layers.name: Movement Control Testing
layers.description: Tests inventory movement controls — all movements documented, transfers approved, and FIFO picking sequence maintained. Analyses movement patterns for theft indicators.

title: Sales & Marketing Audit
description:
Audits sales and marketing processes — channel incentive compliance, marketing spend effectiveness, customer credit and discount controls, and sales commission accuracy.
  Sales and marketing controls are frequently weak, with high fraud exposure in commissions, channel payments, and marketing spend.

layers.name: Sales Commission Audit
layers.description: Tests that sales commissions are computed per the approved commission plan — correct tier, correct revenue base, correct approval of exceptions, and no commission on cancelled or returned orders.

layers.name: Channel Incentive Audit
layers.description: Tests channel partner incentive payments — scheme terms compliance, actual performance vs. claimed performance, and backdating of target achievement.

layers.name: Discount & Credit Note Controls
layers.description: Tests customer discounts — whether within approved authority, whether supported by commercial justification, and no unusual concentration around period-end.

layers.name: Marketing Spend Audit
layers.description: Reviews marketing expenditure — vendor selection, event costs, digital media spend, and agency fees. Tests against market rates and flags spend on related parties or unusual beneficiaries.

layers.name: Customer Credit Risk
layers.description: Tests customer credit limit compliance — whether orders were processed despite exceeding credit limits, and whether credit limit approvals are within authority.

flow.description: Sales commission and incentive data extracted

title: Capital Projects Audit
description:
Audits capital investment projects — procurement compliance, cost control, milestone validation, capitalisation timing, and benefit realisation — protecting major capital expenditure from waste and fraud.
  Capital projects have some of the highest fraud risk and weakest controls in many organisations.

layers.name: Project Approval & Scope Audit
layers.description: Tests that projects were approved through the correct capital investment process — business case quality, board/management approval within authority, and scope defined with clear success metrics.

layers.name: Procurement Compliance
layers.description: Tests procurement within the project — competitive tendering, contractor selection basis, contract terms adequacy, and variation order approval.

layers.name: Cost Control & Overrun Analysis
layers.description: Monitors project cost vs. approved budget. Tests that cost overruns have proper re-approval. Analyses contract variation orders for inflation or scope creep.

layers.name: Milestone & Quality Validation
layers.description: Tests milestone payments — whether milestone completion was independently validated before payment. Reviews quality certificates and completion certificates for authenticity.

layers.name: Benefit Realisation
layers.description: For completed projects, compares actual benefits achieved against the business case. Identifies over-optimistic business cases, scope reductions, or delayed benefit realisation.

title: HR & Payroll Process Audit
description:
Audits HR processes — recruitment compliance, employee data integrity, performance rating fairness, and separation controls — independently of the HR function to provide objective assurance.
  HR processes affect employee rights, regulatory compliance, and compensation integrity — independent audit is essential.

layers.name: Recruitment Process Compliance
layers.description: Tests that hiring followed the approved process — headcount approval obtained, JD posted internally first, selection based on documented criteria, and offer within approved grade band.

layers.name: Performance Rating Distribution
layers.description: Analyses rating distributions by department, manager, and level for statistical anomalies — systematic leniency, severity bias, and correlation between manager and team ratings.

layers.name: Increment Compliance
layers.description: Tests that increments are within approved bands, performance-based, and within the budget envelope. Flags above-band increments without documented CHRO approval.

layers.name: Separation Compliance
layers.description: Tests separation process — notice period served or waived per policy, F&F computation accuracy, and access revocation on last working day.

layers.name: Statutory Compliance Testing
layers.description: Independently tests statutory deductions and filings — PF, ESI, TDS, and Professional Tax — for a sample of employees. Cross-checks with TRACES and EPFO records.

title: Operations & Manufacturing Audit
description:
Audits core operational processes — production controls, quality management, safety compliance, maintenance records, and environmental compliance — ensuring operational integrity and regulatory adherence.
  Operational failures can result in safety incidents, regulatory penalties, product liability, and reputational damage.

layers.name: Production Control Testing
layers.description: Reviews production orders — material consumption vs. standard, yield variance, scrap rates, and production downtime. Identifies systematic over-reporting of scrap (potential theft disguise).

layers.name: Quality Management Audit
layers.description: Tests the QMS process — incoming inspection records, in-process inspection, final inspection, calibration records for equipment, and customer complaint handling.

layers.name: Safety & EHS Compliance
layers.description: Reviews safety inspection records, incident reports, near-miss logs, and statutory safety audits. Tests compliance with Factories Act, OSHA equivalent, and environmental regulations.

layers.name: Maintenance Compliance
layers.description: Audits preventive maintenance schedules — whether PMs are performed on time, maintenance work orders properly approved, and spare parts procurement through approved channels.

layers.name: Environmental Compliance
layers.description: Tests environmental compliance — effluent treatment records, emissions monitoring, hazardous waste disposal, and CPCB/SPCB regulatory filings.

title: IT General Controls (ITGC) Audit
description:
Audits the IT General Controls that underpin the reliability of all application controls — user access management, change management, data backup, and IT operations — providing the foundation for financial audit reliance on automated controls.
  Weak ITGCs mean that no automated application control can be relied upon — making ITGC the highest-leverage IT audit objective.

layers.name: User Access Review
layers.description: Extracts all system user accounts across key applications (ERP, banking, HR). Tests for terminated user accounts still active, users with excessive privileges, dormant accounts, and shared accounts.

layers.name: Privileged Access Review
layers.description: Reviews privileged accounts (admin, DBA, root) — are they justified by role, are activities logged, is access time-limited, and are privileged activities reviewed by a separate control owner?

layers.name: Change Management Testing
layers.description: Tests the IT change management process — all changes logged, tested in non-production before deployment, approved before promotion to production, and emergency changes reviewed post-implementation.

layers.name: Backup & Recovery
layers.description: Tests backup procedures — are backups performed per schedule, are test restores performed quarterly, and is the recovery time objective (RTO) achievable?

layers.name: IT Operations
layers.description: Reviews IT operations controls — incident management, capacity planning, availability monitoring, and patch management currency.

flow.description: User accounts extracted from all key systems
Terminated employees vs. active accounts compared

title: Cybersecurity & Data Privacy Audit
description:
Audits the cybersecurity control environment — network security, endpoint protection, incident response readiness, and DPDP / GDPR data privacy compliance — identifying vulnerabilities before adversaries do.
  Cybersecurity incidents represent existential risk to the organisation — independent audit provides assurance the board cannot get from management alone.

layers.name: Security Policy & Governance
layers.description: Reviews cybersecurity policies, governance structure, and alignment with ISO 27001 / NIST framework. Assesses whether the CISO has adequate authority, budget, and board access.

layers.name: Network & Perimeter Security
layers.description: Reviews firewall configurations, network segmentation, remote access controls (VPN, MFA), and intrusion detection coverage. Tests for open ports and misconfigured rules.

layers.name: Endpoint & Email Security
layers.description: Reviews endpoint protection coverage, email security controls (anti-phishing, DLP), and removable media controls. Tests patching currency for endpoints.

layers.name: Data Privacy Compliance
layers.description: Reviews DPDP Act compliance — personal data inventory, consent management, data retention schedules, breach notification procedures, and Data Protection Officer appointment.

layers.name: Incident Response Readiness
layers.description: Reviews the incident response plan — comprehensiveness, last test date (tabletop exercise), notification chain, and regulatory reporting procedures.

title: ERP Application Controls Audit
description:
Audits the controls embedded in the ERP system — automated input validation, segregation of duties in system roles, configuration settings, and interface controls — ensuring the ERP is configured to prevent rather than just detect errors.
  ERP application controls are the backbone of financial process integrity — misconfiguration creates systematic risk.

layers.name: Segregation of Duties (SOD) in ERP
layers.description: Extracts the full role-to-user matrix from the ERP. Runs SOD conflict analysis — identifies users with conflicting roles (e.g., can both create vendors and process payments) using a pre-defined SOD rule set.

layers.name: Configuration Review
layers.description: Reviews critical ERP configuration settings — payment approval thresholds, credit limit enforcement, bank account change controls, and period-close authorisations. Tests whether settings match the approved design.

layers.name: Interface Controls
layers.description: Tests all ERP interfaces — payroll to GL, HR to payroll, supply chain to finance — for completeness (all records transferred), accuracy (no transformation errors), and timeliness (no significant lag).

layers.name: Programme Change Controls
layers.description: Reviews ERP programme changes — all changes logged, tested, approved, and traced to a valid change request. No unauthorised changes to production environment.

layers.name: Automated Control Reliance Assessment
layers.description: Assesses which ERP automated controls are reliable enough for financial statement reliance — and communicates results to the external auditor to optimise the overall audit approach.

flow.description: Compensating controls for SOD conflicts assessed

title: Data Analytics & Continuous Auditing
description:
Builds and operates a continuous auditing engine — running automated tests on transactions 24/7 and alerting auditors to anomalies in near-real-time rather than waiting for the annual audit.
  Continuous auditing transforms internal audit from a periodic reviewer to a real-time control monitoring function.

layers.name: Analytics Use Case Library
layers.description: Maintains a library of audit analytics scripts covering all high-risk areas — AP duplicates, revenue recognition timing, payroll anomalies, inventory shrinkage, and expense policy violations.

layers.name: Data Pipeline Management
layers.description: Manages secure, automated data feeds from ERP, HRMS, expense systems, and other sources into the audit analytics environment. Monitors data quality and pipeline health.

layers.name: Automated Test Execution
layers.description: Runs analytics tests on a scheduled basis — some daily (high-risk transaction tests), some weekly (KPI monitoring), some monthly (period-end tests). Logs all results with timestamps.

layers.name: Exception Triage
layers.description: Applies a triage model to analytics results — separates true anomalies from false positives based on historical patterns. Routes only genuine exceptions to auditors for follow-up.

layers.name: Trend & Pattern Learning
layers.description: Learns from auditor follow-up decisions — which exceptions were genuine findings vs. false positives — to improve triage accuracy over time. Identifies new risk patterns for new analytics tests.

title: Third-Party & Vendor Risk Audit
description:
Audits the company's third-party risk management process — vendor onboarding due diligence, ongoing monitoring, contractual compliance, and exit management — particularly for vendors with access to company data and systems.
  Third-party breaches are the most common source of major cyber incidents and regulatory penalties.

layers.name: Vendor Risk Inventory
layers.description: Builds a complete inventory of third parties — vendors, service providers, cloud providers, and business partners — classified by risk tier based on data access, criticality, and contract value.

layers.name: Onboarding Due Diligence
layers.description: Reviews whether new high-risk vendors underwent adequate due diligence — security assessment, financial stability check, reference verification, and legal and compliance screening.

layers.name: Contractual Data Protection
layers.description: Reviews vendor contracts for adequate data protection provisions — NDA, data processing agreement (DPDP compliant), breach notification obligation, audit rights, and data return/destruction on exit.

layers.name: Ongoing Monitoring
layers.description: Reviews the ongoing monitoring process for high-risk vendors — annual security reassessment, financial health monitoring, adverse media monitoring, and SLA compliance.

layers.name: Concentration Risk
layers.description: Analyses vendor concentration — single vendors for critical processes, vendors in high-risk geographies, and substitute availability. Flags unacceptable concentration.

title: Regulatory Compliance Audit
description:
Audits compliance with all key regulatory requirements applicable to the company — SEBI, RBI, Companies Act, sector-specific regulations — providing independent assurance to the board and management.
  Regulatory non-compliance can result in licence revocation, prosecution, and reputational damage — independent audit provides the earliest warning.

layers.name: Regulatory Universe
layers.description: Builds and maintains a complete map of all applicable regulations — based on industry, geography, listing status, and business activities. Tracks compliance obligations per regulation.

layers.name: Key Obligation Testing
layers.description: For each material regulation, tests the key compliance obligations — SEBI insider trading policy compliance, LODR quarterly filings, Companies Act board meeting requirements, RBI reporting for NBFC.

layers.name: Regulatory Filing Completeness
layers.description: Tests that all required regulatory filings are made on time — ROC filings, SEBI filings, BSE/NSE disclosures, and sector regulator returns.

layers.name: Regulator Correspondence Review
layers.description: Reviews all regulator notices, queries, and inspection reports. Assesses management responses and tracks remediation to completion.

layers.name: Emerging Regulatory Risk
layers.description: Monitors upcoming regulatory changes that will affect the company's compliance obligations. Prepares readiness assessments for new regulations before their effective date.

title: Anti-Money Laundering (AML) Compliance Audit
description:
Audits the Anti-Money Laundering programme — KYC processes, transaction monitoring, STR filing, and PMLA compliance — for applicable businesses including NBFCs, fintech entities, and high-value goods dealers.
  AML failures attract severe regulatory penalties and reputational damage.

layers.name: KYC Programme Audit
layers.description: Tests the KYC process — completeness of KYC documentation, re-KYC frequency for existing customers, risk-based CDD (Customer Due Diligence) application, and PEP/sanctions screening at onboarding.

layers.name: Transaction Monitoring
layers.description: Reviews the transaction monitoring system — alert generation logic, threshold calibration, alert disposition quality, and escalation procedures. Tests for false negative rates.

layers.name: STR Filing Compliance
layers.description: Tests Suspicious Transaction Report filing — whether STRs are filed within the 7-day window after identifying suspicion, quality of STR narratives, and non-tipping-off compliance.

layers.name: AML Programme Governance
layers.description: Reviews AML governance — PMLO appointment, AML policy currency, staff training completion, and board oversight.

layers.name: High-Risk Customer Review
layers.description: Reviews the high-risk customer portfolio — are enhanced due diligence procedures applied, are relationships reviewed annually, and are any relationships that should be exited still active?

flow.description: STR filing timeliness and quality tested

title: SEBI & Corporate Governance Audit
description:
Audits SEBI LODR compliance, insider trading code adherence, related party transaction approval processes, and board governance practices — providing assurance on corporate governance quality.
  Corporate governance failures result in investor loss of confidence, regulatory action, and personal liability for directors.

layers.name: LODR Compliance Testing
layers.description: Tests compliance with SEBI LODR — quarterly financial result disclosures within 45/60 days, material event disclosures within 24 hours, corporate governance report accuracy, and XBRL filing timeliness.

layers.name: Insider Trading Code
layers.description: Reviews the Insider Trading Code implementation — UPSI identification process, trading window management, pre-clearance compliance for designated persons, and SEBI disclosure filings.

layers.name: Related Party Transactions
layers.description: Tests RPT approval process — all material RPTs approved by Audit Committee and shareholders (if required), disclosure in annual report, and arm's length pricing basis documented.

layers.name: Board & Committee Governance
layers.description: Reviews board meeting compliance — minimum frequency, quorum, independent director participation, agenda quality, and minutes accuracy.

layers.name: Director Disclosure Testing
layers.description: Tests completeness of director disclosures — Form DIR-8 (annual disqualification), interest declarations, RPT interest disclosures, and shareholding disclosure under LODR.

title: Anti-Bribery & Corruption Audit
description:
Audits the anti-bribery and corruption compliance programme — gift and hospitality controls, agent and intermediary payments, facilitation payment risk, and Prevention of Corruption Act compliance.
  Bribery and corruption risk is high in India's regulatory environment — proactive audit reduces legal, financial, and reputational exposure.

layers.name: Gift & Hospitality Register
layers.description: Reviews the gift and hospitality register — completeness (all items declared), value compliance (within approved limits), and no patterns suggesting improper influence.

layers.name: Agent & Intermediary Payments
layers.description: Reviews payments to agents, intermediaries, and consultants — especially in regulated sectors and government-related business. Tests due diligence conducted and proportionality of payments.

layers.name: Government Interaction Controls
layers.description: Reviews controls around government interactions — who is authorised to interact with government officials, documentation of interactions, and no cash payments to government-related parties.

layers.name: Procurement Red Flag Analysis
layers.description: Analyses procurement transactions for ABC red flags — single-source contracts with government-connected vendors, unusual price premiums, and contracts won without competitive process.

layers.name: Culture & Training
layers.description: Reviews ABC programme adequacy — policy communication, training completion rates, speak-up mechanism effectiveness, and management tone at the top.

title: Contract Compliance Audit
description:
Audits contractual compliance — whether the company is performing its obligations under key contracts and whether counterparties are complying with their obligations — protecting the company from liability and recovering entitlements.
  Contracts represent significant financial and legal commitments — systematic audit recovers value and prevents liability.

layers.name: Contract Obligation Extraction
layers.description: Uses NLP to extract all key obligations from material contracts — payment terms, delivery milestones, SLA commitments, exclusivity provisions, and audit rights. Creates an obligation schedule.

layers.name: Company Obligation Compliance
layers.description: Tests whether the company is meeting its contractual obligations — payment on time, data protection commitments, confidentiality, and delivery milestones.

layers.name: Counterparty Compliance
layers.description: Tests whether counterparties are meeting their obligations — vendors delivering on time, service providers meeting SLAs, and licensees paying royalties correctly.

layers.name: Financial Entitlement Recovery
layers.description: Identifies contractual entitlements not being claimed — SLA penalty credits, volume rebates, price reduction triggers, and warranty claims. Quantifies unclaimed value.

layers.name: Contract Renewal Risk
layers.description: Identifies contracts with unfavourable terms coming up for renewal — above-market pricing, one-sided termination rights, or liability caps insufficient for the risk. Prepares renegotiation brief.

title: Proactive Fraud Risk Assessment
description:
Conducts a comprehensive fraud risk assessment across the organisation — identifying fraud schemes most likely to occur based on the business model, controls environment, and behavioural signals — to focus detection efforts.
  Fraud risk assessment is the foundation of effective anti-fraud programmes.

layers.name: Fraud Scheme Identification
layers.description: Maps the organisation's processes against known fraud schemes — ACFE fraud tree applied to the company's specific activities. Identifies the most likely fraud scenarios by process.

layers.name: Red Flag Indicators
layers.description: For each fraud scheme, identifies the behavioural and transactional red flags that would indicate it is occurring. Builds a library of red flag indicators with data sources.

layers.name: Control Gap Analysis
layers.description: Assesses the adequacy of anti-fraud controls for each high-risk fraud scheme. Identifies controls that are absent, ineffective, or easily overridden.

layers.name: Inherent Fraud Risk Scoring
layers.description: Scores each process area for inherent fraud risk — considering financial exposure, ease of perpetration, and likelihood based on control environment.

layers.name: Detection Programme Prioritisation
layers.description: Prioritises detection analytics based on fraud risk scores — ensures the highest-risk fraud scenarios have active data analytics running against them.

title: Real-Time Fraud Detection Analytics
description:
Runs automated fraud detection analytics continuously across all high-risk transaction streams — AP, payroll, expenses, revenue, and procurement — alerting the audit team to anomalies in near-real time.
  Shifts fraud detection from post-incident forensics to in-process prevention and detection.

layers.name: Multi-Stream Transaction Monitoring
layers.description: Simultaneously monitors AP transactions, payroll runs, expense claims, revenue transactions, and procurement activities for fraud indicators — running hundreds of analytical tests continuously.

layers.name: Behavioural Pattern Analysis
layers.description: Establishes normal behavioural baselines for each process, department, and individual. Flags deviations — unusual approval activity outside business hours, sudden spike in expense claims, or unusual vendor payment patterns.

layers.name: Network Analysis
layers.description: Maps relationships between employees, vendors, customers, and transactions. Detects collusion patterns — employees connected to vendors via personal relationships, circular payment patterns, and kickback networks.

layers.name: Machine Learning Anomaly Detection
layers.description: Applies unsupervised ML (isolation forest, autoencoder) to detect novel fraud patterns not covered by rule-based tests. Continuously learns from confirmed fraud cases.

layers.name: Alert Prioritisation & Triage
layers.description: Scores each alert by fraud probability, financial exposure, and urgency. Delivers a prioritised investigation queue to the audit team — ensuring the highest-risk cases get immediate attention.

flow.description: Investigation outcome fed back to model

title: Fraud Investigation Management
description:
Manages fraud investigations from initial trigger through evidence collection, interview coordination, finding documentation, and recovery/remediation — ensuring investigations are legally sound, confidential, and conclusive.
  A poorly conducted investigation can be worse than no investigation — legal exposure from improper methods, tipping off suspects, or chain of custody failures.

layers.name: Investigation Scoping
layers.description: Upon receiving a fraud allegation or detection alert, defines the investigation scope — the alleged fraud scheme, affected period, potential perpetrators, systems to be reviewed, and evidence preservation plan.

layers.name: Evidence Preservation
layers.description: Immediately executes the evidence preservation plan — forensic capture of digital evidence, preservation of physical documents, and notification to IT to prevent deletion of relevant data.

layers.name: Data Analytics Investigation
layers.description: Runs forensic analytics on the preserved data — quantifying the fraud, identifying all transactions involved, mapping the timeline, and identifying all individuals who may have been involved.

layers.name: Interview Planning & Execution
