layers.description: Prepares structured interview guides — starting with witnesses and ending with suspects. Records interview notes accurately. Manages the sequencing to prevent tipping off suspects prematurely.

layers.name: Investigation Report & Recovery
layers.description: Produces a legally sound investigation report — findings, evidence, timeline, responsible parties, quantum of loss, and recommended remedial actions. Initiates recovery and disciplinary/legal action.

flow.description: External forensic counsel engaged if needed

title: Whistleblower Programme Management
description:
Manages the whistleblower/vigil mechanism — secure intake, triage, investigation, and protection for complainants — ensuring the programme is trustworthy, effective, and compliant with Companies Act requirements.
  An effective whistleblower programme is the single most effective fraud detection mechanism — more effective than internal audit, external audit, or management review combined.

layers.name: Secure Intake Channel
layers.description: Maintains a multi-channel whistleblower intake — web portal, email, phone hotline, and physical drop box. All channels guarantee anonymity. Acknowledges receipt within 48 hours.

layers.name: Complaint Classification
layers.description: Classifies each complaint — fraud and financial crime, workplace harassment, regulatory violation, safety concern, or conflict of interest. Assigns priority based on severity and credibility.

layers.name: Investigation Routing
layers.description: Routes complaints to the appropriate investigator — CAE for financial fraud, CHRO for HR matters, Audit Committee for allegations against senior management, and external investigators for independence.

layers.name: Non-Retaliation Protection
layers.description: Monitors for potential retaliation against identified complainants — unusual adverse employment actions, performance rating drops, or disciplinary actions that follow closely after a complaint.

layers.name: Programme Effectiveness Measurement
layers.description: Tracks programme metrics — complaint volume, category distribution, investigation completion rate, outcomes, and resolution time. Reports to Audit Committee on programme health.

flow.description: Outcome communicated to complainant (if not anonymous)

title: Audit Finding Documentation & Rating
description:
Standardises the documentation and rating of audit findings — ensuring every finding is clearly articulated, accurately rated, supported by evidence, and positioned for management action.
  Poorly documented findings are often dismissed by management or fail to drive action — quality documentation is as important as quality testing.

layers.name: Finding Structuring
layers.description: Structures every audit finding using the standard format — Condition (what we found), Criteria (what should happen), Cause (why it's happening), Consequence (what could go wrong), and Recommendation (what to do).

layers.name: Risk Rating
layers.description: Rates each finding by risk using a calibrated framework — Critical (potential financial loss > ₹1 Cr or regulatory licence risk), High (significant control failure), Medium (control gap with compensating control), Low (best practice improvement).

layers.name: Evidence Tagging
layers.description: Links each finding to the specific evidence that supports it — transaction IDs, screenshots, documents, interview notes, and analytical outputs. Evidence is preserved and accessible for management response.

layers.name: Management Response Collection
layers.description: Sends findings to management for response — specific action planned, owner, and due date. Reviews management response for adequacy — the action must address the root cause, not just the symptom.

layers.name: Finding Consistency Check
layers.description: Compares each finding against the historical findings database — is this a repeat finding? Is the risk rating consistent with similar findings? Are the recommendations consistent with prior practice?

title: Audit Report Production
description:
Produces high-quality, clear, and concise audit reports — converting the fieldwork findings into a board-ready document that communicates risk, assurance, and recommendations effectively.
  The audit report is internal audit's primary product — its quality directly determines whether the function is respected and its recommendations acted upon.

layers.name: Report Structure
layers.description: Assembles the report using the approved format — Executive Summary (for non-specialists), Audit Objectives & Scope, Overall Opinion, Findings (by risk rating), and Management Action Plan.

layers.name: Executive Summary Drafting
layers.description: Drafts an executive summary — key risks identified, overall control opinion, top 3 findings, and management commitment to remediate. Clear enough for a board member to act on without reading the full report.

layers.name: Overall Opinion Assignment
layers.description: Assigns the overall engagement opinion — Effective (no material findings), Partially Effective (some significant findings), Ineffective (material control failures). Validates that the opinion is consistent with the finding ratings.

layers.name: Language Quality
layers.description: Reviews report for clarity, tone, and factual accuracy. Flags passive voice, jargon, and ambiguous language. Ensures each finding is actionable and the recommendation is specific.

layers.name: Management Review Cycle
layers.description: Manages the report review cycle — draft to management, management response incorporation, CAE final review, and issued report distribution. Tracks to completion within the SLA.

title: Management Action Plan Tracking
description:
Tracks all open Management Action Plans (MAPs) from current and prior audits — monitoring completion, validating evidence, escalating overdue items, and reporting status to the Audit Committee.
  MAP tracking is where audit value is realised — findings without remediation are simply observations.

layers.name: MAP Register
layers.description: Maintains a live register of all open MAPs — audit area, finding, agreed action, responsible owner, agreed due date, and current status. Totals open by risk rating and age.

layers.name: Due Date Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors every MAP due date. Sends reminders to owners at 30, 14, and 7 days before due date. Automatically marks as Overdue the day after the due date.

layers.name: Evidence Validation
layers.description: When management marks a MAP as complete, collects and validates the supporting evidence — is the action actually done, or is it just claimed as done? Applies risk-based verification intensity.

layers.name: Escalation Logic
layers.description: For overdue Critical and High findings, escalates to department head (14 days overdue), CFO/CHRO (30 days overdue), and Audit Committee (60 days overdue without remediation).

layers.name: Trend Analysis
layers.description: Analyses MAP completion patterns — by department, business unit, and management owner. Identifies systemic low compliance departments for CAE and AC attention.

flow.description: Due date alerts sent to owners
MAP status report for AC prepared

title: Audit Committee Reporting
description:
Prepares and delivers the Audit Committee reporting package — plan vs. actual updates, key findings, fraud risk summary, MAP tracker, and Chief Audit Executive's assessment of the overall control environment.
  The Audit Committee reporting is internal audit's primary accountability moment — it must be insightful, balanced, and actionable.

layers.name: Plan vs. Actual Update
layers.description: Summarises audit plan delivery — audits completed vs. planned, explanations for any deviations, and resource utilisation. Includes upcoming planned audits for the next quarter.

layers.name: Key Findings Summary
layers.description: Compiles all findings issued since the last AC meeting — categorised by risk rating, business area, and finding type. Highlights any Critical or repeat findings requiring AC attention.

layers.name: MAP Status Dashboard
layers.description: Provides a visual summary of MAP completion status — total open by risk rating and age, overdue items requiring AC awareness, and trend in MAP resolution rate.

layers.name: Fraud & Whistleblower Summary
layers.description: Reports on fraud detection activity, investigations completed, and whistleblower programme metrics. Highlights any material fraud incidents or emerging fraud risks.

layers.name: CAE Control Opinion
layers.description: Provides the CAE's overall assessment of the control environment — based on audit results, MAP completion trends, and risk monitoring signals. Rated: Strong / Satisfactory / Needs Improvement / Weak.

title: Quality Assurance & Improvement Programme (QAIP)
description:
Manages Internal Audit's Quality Assurance and Improvement Programme — ongoing internal quality monitoring, periodic self-assessment, and periodic external quality assessment — in conformance with IIA Standards.
  QAIP is a mandatory IIA requirement and the mechanism by which internal audit holds itself to the same standards it applies to the rest of the organisation.

layers.name: Ongoing Monitoring
layers.description: Continuously monitors audit quality indicators — working paper completeness, finding quality scores, report timeliness, MAP closure rate, and AC satisfaction. Flags quality drops for CAE attention.

layers.name: Engagement-Level QA
layers.description: Reviews a sample of completed audits — are the work papers complete, is the evidence sufficient, do the findings support the opinion, and is the report clear? Provides feedback to auditors.

layers.name: Annual Self-Assessment
layers.description: Conducts the annual QAIP self-assessment against IIA Standards — for each standard, assesses conformance (full/partial/non-conformance), evidence, and improvement actions.

layers.name: External Quality Assessment
layers.description: Facilitates the periodic (at least every 5 years) external quality assessment — prepares materials, manages the assessor relationship, tracks recommendations.

layers.name: Improvement Tracking
layers.description: Tracks all QAIP improvement actions — from self-assessment findings, external assessment findings, and ongoing monitoring gaps. Reports progress to CAE and AC.

title: Internal Audit Annual Report & Charter
description:
Prepares the Internal Audit Annual Report — summarising the year's work, key findings, assurance coverage, and value delivered — and reviews the IA Charter annually for adequacy and board approval.
  The Annual Report and Charter are internal audit's accountability document to the board — demonstrating value and securing the mandate for the coming year.

layers.name: Annual Work Summary
layers.description: Compiles the year's audit activity — all audits completed, advisory engagements, investigations, and continuous auditing outputs. Compares against the approved annual plan.

layers.name: Key Findings & Value Report
layers.description: Summarises the year's key findings — by risk type, business area, and finding category. Quantifies value identified — fraud recovered, overpayments identified, compliance risks prevented, and process efficiencies recommended.

layers.name: Assurance Map
layers.description: Presents the assurance map — which processes and risks received audit coverage, at what level, and what remains in the audit universe for future coverage.

layers.name: IA Team Performance
layers.description: Reports on the IA team — headcount, qualifications, CPE completions, certifications acquired, and staff turnover. Assesses team capability against the risk profile.

layers.name: Charter Review
layers.description: Reviews the IA Charter against current IIA Standards, the company's current risk profile, and board governance requirements. Recommends updates to ensure the charter remains fit for purpose and submits for board approval.

title: Contract Request Intake & Triage
description:
Receives all contract requests from business units — new agreements, amendments, renewals, and terminations — classifies them by type, risk, and urgency, and routes them to the appropriate legal resource or standard template.
  Eliminates the unstructured email-based contract request process that leads to missed requests, incorrect templates, and legal team overload.

layers.name: Request Intake
layers.description: Reads contract requests from the legal intake portal, email, or ticketing system. Extracts key parameters — contract type, counterparty, estimated value, business purpose, and requestor details.

layers.name: Contract Type Classification
layers.description: Classifies the request — NDA, vendor agreement, customer contract, employment contract, JV agreement, licensing, lease, or loan agreement. Determines applicable template and standard terms.

layers.name: Risk Tier Assignment
layers.description: Assigns a risk tier to the contract — Tier 1 (high value/strategic, e.g., >₹1 Cr or data processing), Tier 2 (standard commercial, ₹10L–₹1 Cr), Tier 3 (low value, standard terms, <₹10L). Determines SLA and resource requirement.

layers.name: Standard vs. Bespoke Assessment
layers.description: Determines whether the request can be handled by a standard template (self-service) or requires lawyer involvement. Templates cover 70% of volume — NDA, standard vendor T&C, employment letters.

layers.name: Assignment & SLA
layers.description: Routes Tier 1 to a senior lawyer, Tier 2 to a junior lawyer or paralegal, and Tier 3 to self-service portal. Sets SLA targets — Tier 1: 5 days, Tier 2: 3 days, Tier 3: same day. Tracks against SLA.

flow.description: Standard template identified or gap flagged

title: Contract Drafting & Template Management
description:
Generates first-draft contracts using AI-powered drafting — drawing from approved clause libraries, standard templates, and playbooks — reducing drafting time by 70% while maintaining legal quality.
  Transforms lawyers from drafters to reviewers, dramatically increasing throughput without adding headcount.

layers.name: Template Selection
layers.description: Based on the contract type, jurisdiction, and counterparty profile, selects the most appropriate template from the approved template library. Applies jurisdiction-specific variations automatically.

layers.name: Parameter Extraction
layers.description: Reads the contract request parameters — parties, commercial terms (price, payment terms, duration), delivery obligations, SLAs, and special conditions — and populates the template with these specifics.

layers.name: Clause Library Application
layers.description: For each contract section, selects the appropriate clause variant from the approved clause library — standard, enhanced (more protective), or lightweight — based on the risk tier and counterparty type.

layers.name: Playbook Compliance
layers.description: Checks the draft against the legal playbook — minimum acceptable positions for each key clause (liability cap, indemnity, governing law, jurisdiction, IP ownership, data protection). Flags any draft clauses below the minimum.

layers.name: Template Currency Check
layers.description: Validates that the template being used reflects the latest approved version — incorporating recent legal changes (DPDP, Indian Contract Act amendments) and policy updates.

title: Contract Review & Redline Analysis
description:
Reviews counterparty-paper contracts — identifying deviations from the company's standard positions, flagging high-risk clauses, and generating a negotiation redline with recommended positions — in minutes rather than hours.
  Ensures consistent application of the legal playbook across all counterparty-paper contracts, regardless of which lawyer reviews them.

layers.name: Document Ingestion
layers.description: Reads the counterparty-provided contract (PDF or Word). Extracts the full text, identifies clause headings, and maps the contract structure to the standard clause taxonomy.

layers.name: Playbook Gap Analysis
layers.description: Compares each counterparty clause against the company's playbook position. Classifies deviations — Acceptable (within standard range), Negotiation Point (deviate from preferred but acceptable with modification), Deal Breaker (must be changed).

layers.name: High-Risk Clause Detection
layers.description: Flags specific high-risk clause types — uncapped liability, unilateral termination rights, IP assignment to counterparty, unfavourable data processing terms, and jurisdiction clauses pointing to unfavourable courts.

layers.name: Recommended Redline
layers.description: For each negotiation point, generates the recommended redline language from the clause library. Provides the justification and negotiation strategy — what to prioritise and what to concede.

layers.name: Risk Summary Report
layers.description: Produces a one-page risk summary — overall contract risk rating (Red/Amber/Green), top 5 risk issues, deal-breaker clauses, and commercial considerations for the business team.

title: Contract Negotiation & Execution Management
description:
Manages the negotiation workflow — tracking redline versions, monitoring negotiation progress, flagging concessions beyond approved limits, and managing the e-signature execution process to contract-signed status.
  Provides complete version control and negotiation audit trail while accelerating time-to-signature.

layers.name: Version Control
layers.description: Tracks every iteration of the contract — counterparty redlines, company responses, and agreed positions. Maintains a clean version history with timestamps and authors.

layers.name: Negotiation Progress Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks open issues across negotiation rounds. Identifies clauses that have been agreed, clauses under negotiation, and new issues raised by counterparty. Flags stalled negotiations.

layers.name: Concession Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors agreed deviations from the playbook during negotiation. When concessions exceed the approved authority level, triggers escalation to senior lawyer or GC before agreeing.

layers.name: Commercial-Legal Alignment
layers.description: Maintains visibility of commercial terms agreed between business teams separately from legal drafting. Flags when commercial agreements are not reflected in contract language.

layers.name: Execution Workflow
layers.description: Once all issues are resolved, manages the execution process — final clean version, execution authority check (within signing authority matrix), e-signature routing (DocuSign / SignDesk), and execution confirmation.

flow.description: Commercial terms vs. contract language checked

title: Contract Repository & Obligation Management
description:
Maintains the central contract repository — extracting key terms from all executed contracts, tracking obligations and milestones, and alerting responsible owners before deadlines and renewal dates.
  Turns the contract repository from an archive into a live obligation management system that protects the company from missing commitments.

layers.name: Contract Ingestion & Metadata Extraction
layers.description: Reads all executed contracts in the repository. Uses NLP to extract key metadata — parties, effective date, expiry date, auto-renewal clauses, value, governing law, key obligations, and notice period for termination.

layers.name: Obligation Tagging
layers.description: Identifies and tags every obligation in each contract — payment milestones, delivery timelines, reporting requirements, insurance maintenance, exclusivity obligations, and performance benchmarks.

layers.name: Owner Assignment
layers.description: Assigns each obligation to the responsible business owner — procurement for vendor delivery, finance for payment terms, IT for technical SLAs, and legal for legal notice requirements.

layers.name: Renewal & Expiry Tracking
layers.description: Monitors all contract expiry and auto-renewal dates. Sends alerts at 90, 60, and 30 days before expiry. Flags auto-renewal contracts where the cancellation window is approaching.

layers.name: Obligation Status Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks completion of obligations by responsible owners. Flags overdue obligations with risk impact (breach risk, SLA penalty exposure, or lost entitlement). Escalates to GC for critical misses.

flow.description: Key dates entered into compliance calendar
Breach risk from missed obligations assessed

title: Contract Analytics & Performance Reporting
description:
Analyses the contract portfolio — cycle times, compliance rates, risk exposure, counterparty performance, and legal team productivity — providing GC and CFO with data-driven insights on contract management performance.
  Makes the legal function measurable and accountable, enabling continuous improvement.

layers.name: Cycle Time Analytics
layers.description: Measures contract lifecycle times — intake to first draft, draft to execution, and overall cycle time. Benchmarks against targets and identifies bottlenecks.

layers.name: Risk Exposure Dashboard
layers.description: Aggregates the liability cap, indemnity exposure, and IP risk across all active contracts. Provides total contract risk exposure by business unit, counterparty, and contract type.

layers.name: Playbook Compliance Analytics
layers.description: Measures how often the legal playbook positions were maintained vs. conceded in negotiations. Identifies clause types where the company consistently loses negotiation ground.

layers.name: Counterparty Performance
layers.description: Tracks performance of key counterparties under their contracts — on-time delivery, SLA compliance, invoice accuracy, and dispute frequency.

layers.name: Legal Team Productivity
layers.description: Measures legal team output — contracts reviewed per lawyer, cycle times by lawyer, SLA compliance, and value of contracts managed. Benchmarks against industry.

title: Companies Act Compliance — Filings & Registers
description:
Manages all mandatory Companies Act filings with the Registrar of Companies (ROC) — annual returns, financial statement filings, director disclosures, charge registrations, and event-based filings — with zero missed deadlines.
  ROC filing defaults attract automatic penalties and director prosecution — AI-driven deadline management eliminates this risk entirely.

layers.name: Filing Calendar
layers.description: Maintains a comprehensive Companies Act filing calendar for every entity — ADT-1 (auditor appointment), MGT-7/7A (annual return), AOC-4 (financial statements), DPT-3 (deposits), MSME Form 1, BEN-2 (beneficial ownership), and all event-based forms.

layers.name: Trigger Event Detection
layers.description: Monitors for events that trigger MCA filings — board changes (DIR-12), change in registered office (INC-22), charge creation (CHG-1), share allotment (PAS-3), and amalgamation orders.

layers.name: Data Compilation
layers.description: Compiles required data for each filing from Finance (for financial statement filings), HR (for director and employee data), and legal (for event details). Validates completeness before form preparation.

layers.name: Form Preparation & Validation
layers.description: Prepares MCA forms in the prescribed format. Validates against MCA portal validation rules before filing. Manages DSC (Digital Signature Certificate) routing to authorised signatories.

layers.name: Statutory Register Maintenance
layers.description: Maintains all statutory registers — Register of Members, Register of Directors, Register of Charges, Register of Contracts, and Register of Beneficial Owners — up to date and audit-ready.

title: Board & Committee Meeting Management
description:
Manages the full board and committee meeting lifecycle — scheduling, agenda preparation, board pack distribution, minute-taking, resolution management, and action tracking — ensuring statutory compliance and governance quality.
  Board governance is a key regulatory obligation and investor trust signal — AI-powered management ensures zero compliance lapses.

layers.name: Meeting Scheduling
layers.description: Plans the annual board and committee meeting calendar — ensuring minimum statutory frequency (board: once per quarter, gap not exceeding 120 days; audit committee: at least 4 times per year). Manages director availability.

layers.name: Agenda & Board Pack
layers.description: Compiles the agenda from all functions — Finance (accounts), Legal (regulatory matters), HR (people matters), and Strategy (business updates). Assembles the board pack with all supporting papers. Distributes 7 days before meeting.

layers.name: Statutory Compliance Checks
layers.description: Before each meeting, verifies quorum requirements, checks whether independent director participation meets the threshold, validates that agenda items comply with disclosure and related party transaction requirements.

layers.name: Minutes Drafting
layers.description: After the meeting, drafts minutes from the structured discussion notes — capturing resolutions, voting, dissents, and action items. Routes draft minutes to Chairman for review within 15 days.

layers.name: Resolution & Action Tracking
layers.description: Extracts all resolutions and action items from minutes. Routes resolutions for appropriate follow-up (e.g., board resolution for bank mandate sent to Finance). Tracks action item completion.

title: Director Compliance & KYC Management
description:
Manages all director compliance requirements — KYC updates, disqualification checks, disclosure of interest, DIR filings, and DIN maintenance — for all directors across all group entities.
  Director non-compliance can result in disqualification, company strike-off, and personal liability — proactive management is essential.

layers.name: Director KYC (DIR-3 KYC)
layers.description: Monitors annual DIR-3 KYC deadlines (September 30 every year) for all active DIN holders. Sends reminders from July 1. Manages the filing process including DSC validation.

layers.name: Disqualification Monitoring
layers.description: Checks all directors against MCA's disqualified directors list monthly. Alerts GC and board immediately if any director appears on the disqualification list.

layers.name: Director Disclosure Management
layers.description: Manages MBP-1 (director's interest declaration at each board meeting) and DIR-8 (annual disqualification declaration). Tracks submissions and follows up on non-submissions.

layers.name: Appointment & Resignation Filings
layers.description: Manages DIR-12 filings for all director changes — appointments, resignations, and changes in designation — within the 30-day prescribed period.

layers.name: Multi-Entity Director Register
layers.description: Maintains a consolidated director register across all group entities — tracking each director's positions, term lengths, rotation requirements, and maximum directorship limits (10 public companies).

flow.description: DIR-12 filing triggered on board change
Director rotation and maximum limits tracked

title: Shareholder & Securities Compliance
description:
Manages shareholder-related compliance — share allotments, transfers, transmission, buybacks, dividend payments, investor grievance redressal, and SEBI LODR disclosures — for listed and unlisted companies.
  Shareholder compliance failures attract SEBI enforcement action, investor litigation, and reputational damage.

layers.name: Share Register Maintenance
layers.description: Maintains the register of members — processing allotments (PAS-3), transfers (SH-4), transmission, and dematerialisation requests. Reconciles with NSDL/CDSL depository records.

layers.name: SEBI LODR Disclosures (Listed Companies)
layers.description: Manages continuous disclosure obligations — quarterly results (within 45 days), material events (within 24 hours), related party transactions, corporate governance report, and annual report.

layers.name: Dividend Processing
layers.description: Manages the complete dividend payment cycle — board and shareholder approval, record date and book closure, IEPF compliance for unpaid dividends (7-year rule), and Form 15G/H for eligible shareholders.

layers.name: Investor Grievance Redressal
layers.description: Manages SEBI SCORES complaints and investor correspondence — acknowledgement within 3 days, resolution within 30 days, and escalation for complex grievances.

layers.name: Insider Trading & Trading Window
layers.description: Manages the insider trading compliance framework — trading window opening and closing announcements, pre-clearance requests from designated persons, and SEBI filing of transactions.

flow.description: Share register updated for all events

title: Subsidiary & Group Entity Management
description:
Manages compliance across the entire group entity structure — tracking each subsidiary's statutory obligations, board compositions, dormant company filings, and cross-entity reporting requirements.
  Multi-entity compliance failures can cascade into group-level regulatory risk if not managed systematically.

layers.name: Entity Register
layers.description: Maintains a complete register of all group entities — CIN, jurisdiction, date of incorporation, authorised and paid-up capital, directors, registered office, activity status, and relationship to holding company.

layers.name: Entity-Level Compliance Matrix
layers.description: For each entity, maintains a compliance matrix — applicable acts, filing calendar, and status. Distinguishes dormant companies (Sec 455 compliance) from active subsidiaries.

layers.name: Dormant & Strike-Off Management
layers.description: For dormant entities, manages Form MSC-1 (dormant status) and MSC-3 (annual returns). For entities being wound up, manages the voluntary strike-off (STK-2) process.

layers.name: Cross-Entity Reporting
layers.description: Consolidates compliance status across all entities for the GC dashboard — which entities are at risk, which have overdue filings, and which have governance gaps.

layers.name: Foreign Subsidiary Coordination
layers.description: For foreign subsidiaries, coordinates with local counsel on equivalent local filings. Tracks global filing calendar. Ensures Indian holding company disclosures reflect subsidiary events correctly.

title: Litigation Portfolio Management
description:
Maintains a complete, live litigation register across all pending cases — tracking court dates, filing deadlines, lawyer assignments, provisions, and strategic status — giving GC and CFO real-time visibility of the company's entire legal exposure.
  Litigation that is not tracked is litigation that will be lost by default.

layers.name: Case Registry
layers.description: Maintains a comprehensive register of all litigation — civil suits, criminal complaints, labour court matters, consumer forum cases, arbitrations, and regulatory proceedings. Each case has a unique ID, parties, forum, subject matter, and risk rating.

layers.name: Deadline Tracking
layers.description: Tracks all court dates, filing deadlines, limitation periods, and appeal windows. Sends multi-level reminders to the responsible lawyer and external counsel — 30, 14, 7, and 1 day before each date.

layers.name: Financial Exposure Mapping
layers.description: For each case, maintains the claimed amount, the company's assessed probable exposure, and the legal provision required under Ind AS 37 (Provisions, Contingent Liabilities). Aggregates to total litigation exposure.

layers.name: Strategy Classification
layers.description: Classifies each case by strategy — Defend (strong grounds), Settle (cost of defence exceeds likely outcome), Accelerate (company has counterclaim), or Monitor (low risk, minimal action needed).

layers.name: External Counsel Management
layers.description: Tracks external law firm assignments, fee arrangements, billing against budget, and performance (win rate, turnaround). Manages conflict checks for new external counsel appointments.

flow.description: Court dates and filing deadlines tracked

title: Pre-Litigation Dispute Resolution
description:
Manages pre-litigation dispute resolution — demand notices, negotiations, mediation, and conciliation — to resolve disputes before court filing, saving time, cost, and relationship capital.
  70% of disputes can be resolved pre-litigation with the right process — but only if managed proactively and systematically.

layers.name: Dispute Identification
layers.description: Reads all inbound legal notices, disputes, and escalated complaints from commercial teams. Classifies by dispute type — payment, performance, IP, employment, and breach of contract.

layers.name: Merit Assessment
layers.description: Analyses the merits of the dispute — company's legal position, counterparty's position, contractual provisions (dispute resolution clause, governing law), and commercial context.

layers.name: Resolution Options Analysis
layers.description: Evaluates the optimal resolution path — direct negotiation, mediation, conciliation, expert determination, or arbitration. Considers cost, timeline, relationship, and precedent risk.

layers.name: Demand Notice Management
layers.description: Drafts and tracks all outbound legal notices — demand notices, notice under Section 138 (cheque bouncing), SARFAESI notices, and pre-arbitration notices. Tracks response and limitation periods.

layers.name: Negotiation & Settlement Tracking
layers.description: Supports the negotiation process — preparing position papers, computing settlement value ranges, tracking negotiations, and documenting settlement agreements with proper discharge language.

title: Arbitration Management
description:
Manages arbitration proceedings — from notice of arbitration through pleadings, evidence, hearing, award, and enforcement — as both claimant and respondent, under domestic and international arbitration rules.
  Arbitration is increasingly the primary dispute resolution forum for commercial disputes — dedicated management improves outcomes and controls costs.

layers.name: Arbitration Clause Analysis
layers.description: Reviews the dispute resolution clause in the underlying contract — seat of arbitration, governing rules (SIAC, ICC, LCIA, DIAC, or domestic), number of arbitrators, and applicable law.

layers.name: Arbitrator Selection
layers.description: Analyses potential arbitrators — background, prior awards in similar matters, availability, and any conflicts of interest. Prepares a ranked shortlist for the parties or appointing authority.

layers.name: Pleadings Management
layers.description: Manages the pleadings timeline — Statement of Claim / Defence, Counterclaim, Reply, and Rejoinder. Tracks all filing deadlines under the applicable rules and arbitral directions.

layers.name: Document Management
layers.description: Organises the complete case document file — correspondence, evidence, expert reports, and legal authorities. Creates an indexed hearing bundle. Manages the discovery and disclosure process.

layers.name: Award Analysis & Enforcement
layers.description: Reads the award and analyses it — quantum, reasoning, and costs. Computes the net recovery / liability. Initiates enforcement (Section 36 of Arbitration Act) or challenge (Section 34) depending on outcome.

title: Labour & Employment Dispute Management
description:
Manages all employment-related disputes — labour court matters, Industrial Tribunal proceedings, High Court writ petitions by employees, and employment arbitrations — coordinating with HR and external labour counsel.
  Labour disputes carry reputational, financial, and operational risk — systematic management prevents escalation and precedent-setting adverse decisions.

layers.name: Dispute Classification
layers.description: Classifies employment disputes by type — individual disputes (wrongful termination, wage claims), collective disputes (union matters, Section 9A notices under Industrial Disputes Act), and statutory claims (PF, ESI, gratuity disputes).

layers.name: Labour Court Calendar Management
layers.description: Tracks all dates across labour courts, Industrial Tribunals, and appellate courts. Coordinates with specialised labour law external counsel.

layers.name: HR-Legal Coordination
layers.description: Coordinates with HR on factual evidence — employment records, disciplinary files, termination orders, and service records. Ensures HR provides complete and accurate inputs to legal team.

layers.name: Conciliation Support
layers.description: Supports conciliation proceedings before the Labour Commissioner — preparing the company's statement of facts, position on workman's claims, and conciliation settlement terms if settlement is preferred.

layers.name: Compliance Risk Monitoring
layers.description: Analyses all labour disputes for systemic HR compliance gaps — if multiple similar claims are being filed, identifies the root cause and recommends policy or process change to prevent further claims.

title: Regulatory Enforcement & Show Cause Management
description:
Manages all regulatory enforcement actions — show cause notices (SCNs), adjudication orders, compounding applications, and prosecution proceedings from SEBI, RBI, MCA, FSSAI, BIS, and other regulators.
  Regulatory enforcement carries financial penalties, licence suspension, and personal director liability — structured management dramatically improves outcomes.

layers.name: SCN Intake & Classification
layers.description: Reads all incoming show cause notices. Classifies by regulator, nature of alleged violation, potential penalty range, and response deadline. Assigns severity — Critical (licence risk), High (significant penalty), Medium/Low.

layers.name: Violation Analysis
layers.description: Analyses the alleged violation — whether the facts are disputed, whether the regulatory interpretation is correct, what mitigating factors exist, and whether similar matters have been decided by the regulator or courts.

layers.name: Response Drafting
layers.description: Prepares the SCN response — statement of facts, legal grounds, supporting evidence, precedent analysis, and mitigating factors (past compliance record, voluntary disclosure, cooperation). Routes for expert review.

layers.name: Compounding Application
layers.description: Where compounding is available and strategic, prepares the compounding application — admission of default, computation of compounding fee, and undertaking for future compliance.

layers.name: Adjudication & Appeal
layers.description: Attends adjudication hearings. Analyses adjudication order. Initiates appeal (CCI, SEBI SAT, Securities Appellate Tribunal, or appropriate court) where the order is adverse and has strong appellate merit.

title: Regulatory Universe & Compliance Calendar
description:
Builds and maintains the complete regulatory compliance universe — every applicable law, regulation, and guideline — and creates a master compliance calendar with all filing, reporting, and procedural obligations.
  The foundation of all compliance activity — without a complete, current regulatory universe, compliance will always have gaps.

layers.name: Regulatory Universe Construction
layers.description: Builds the regulatory universe from the company's operations — industry (FSSAI, BIS, Drugs, Environment, Labour), geography (state-specific laws), listing status (SEBI), and sector regulation (RBI, IRDAI, PFRDA).

layers.name: Obligation Extraction
layers.description: For each applicable regulation, extracts all compliance obligations — periodic filings, annual returns, event-based reporting, registration renewals, and procedural requirements.

layers.name: Due Date Calendar
layers.description: Builds a master compliance calendar — all obligations with due dates, responsible owner, and regulatory authority. Incorporates state-specific variations and leap year adjustments.

layers.name: Legislative Change Monitoring
layers.description: Continuously monitors the regulatory environment for changes — new regulations, amendment notifications, circulars, and compliance guidance. Updates the universe and calendar within 48 hours of any change.

layers.name: Compliance Health Dashboard
layers.description: Provides real-time compliance health — % of obligations complied with on time, overdue items, and upcoming high-risk deadlines. Alerts GC to Critical and High-risk gaps.

title: Environmental, Health & Safety (EHS) Legal Compliance
description:
Manages legal compliance with environmental laws (Environment Protection Act, Water Act, Air Act, Hazardous Waste Rules), occupational safety laws (Factories Act, BOCW, POSH), and EHS regulatory filings.
  EHS non-compliance carries criminal prosecution of directors, factory closure, and severe reputational damage.

layers.name: EHS Regulatory Mapping
layers.description: Maps all applicable EHS regulations per establishment — Environment Act, Water Act, Air Act, Hazardous Waste Rules, Factories Act, Boilers Act, and state-specific pollution control board requirements.

layers.name: Consent & NOC Management
layers.description: Tracks all environmental consents — Consent to Establish (CTE), Consent to Operate (CTO), and Hazardous Waste Authorisation. Manages renewal 6 months before expiry.

layers.name: CPCB/SPCB Reporting
layers.description: Manages all periodic reports to the Central/State Pollution Control Board — annual environmental statement (Form V), half-yearly monitoring reports, and incident reporting.

layers.name: Factories Act Compliance
layers.description: Manages Factory Licence renewal, annual returns (Form No. 21), half-yearly returns (Form 22), and maintenance of statutory registers (Register of Accidents, Register of Dangerous Occurrences).

layers.name: EHS Incident Legal Response
layers.description: When an EHS incident occurs, manages the legal response — statutory notification requirements (CPCB, SPCB, factory inspector), preparation of incident report, and defence strategy if enforcement action follows.

flow.description: Applicable EHS laws per facility identified

title: Consumer Protection & Product Liability Compliance
description:
Manages compliance with consumer protection laws — Consumer Protection Act, Legal Metrology, BIS standards, and e-commerce rules — and handles consumer forum complaints and product liability claims.
  Consumer protection non-compliance attracts CCPA action, mandatory product recalls, and class-action risk.

layers.name: Consumer Law Compliance Audit
layers.description: Reviews product labelling (Legal Metrology), advertising claims (ASCI and CCPA guidelines), warranty terms, and return/refund policy for compliance with Consumer Protection Act and e-commerce rules.

layers.name: BIS & Standards Compliance
layers.description: Tracks BIS licence renewals, mandatory certification requirements (BIS hallmark, ISI mark), and statutory testing requirements. Manages FSSAI licence for food businesses.

layers.name: Consumer Forum Complaint Management
layers.description: Tracks all consumer forum complaints — District Forum, State Commission, NCDRC. Manages filing of written statements, evidence, and arguments within prescribed timelines.

layers.name: Product Recall Management (Legal)
layers.description: When a product recall is required, manages the legal aspects — CCPA / FSSAI notification, recall execution documentation, consumer communication, and disposal records.

layers.name: Advertising Compliance
layers.description: Reviews advertising content for compliance — no misleading claims, substantiation for health/performance claims, mandatory disclosures (paid partnership, side effects), and ASCI code compliance.

title: Competition Law Compliance
description:
Manages Competition Act compliance — cartel and anti-competitive conduct risk monitoring, merger control filings, dominance abuse assessment, and CCI investigation management.
  CCI penalties can reach 10% of average turnover — proactive compliance is essential for market-leading businesses.

layers.name: Competition Compliance Programme
layers.description: Manages the competition compliance programme — risk assessment for the company's industry position, staff training on prohibited conduct, and competitor interaction protocols.

layers.name: Pricing & Distribution Review
layers.description: Reviews pricing decisions, distribution policies, and dealer agreements for competition law risk — resale price maintenance, exclusive dealing, tying, and predatory pricing signals.

layers.name: Merger Control
layers.description: Monitors all M&A transactions for CCI filing thresholds — asset and turnover-based triggers. Prepares Form I or Form II filings within the prescribed 30-day pre-merger notification window.

layers.name: Industry Association Participation
layers.description: Reviews all industry association interactions — agenda, discussions, and outcomes — for cartel risk. Provides real-time guidance to executives before industry meetings.

layers.name: CCI Investigation Response
layers.description: Manages CCI investigations — Director General (DG) investigation cooperation, dawn raid protocol, response to information requests, and defence strategy for penalty proceedings.

title: Foreign Exchange & FEMA Compliance
description:
Manages FEMA compliance — FDI reporting (FCGPR/FCTRS), ECB filings (Form ECB), ODI compliance (Form ODI), and annual declarations (FLA return) — ensuring all cross-border transactions are compliant with RBI regulations.
  FEMA compounding penalties and RBI enforcement actions are increasingly common — systematic compliance management is essential.

layers.name: FEMA Transaction Classification
layers.description: Reviews every cross-border transaction — equity investment, loan, trade credit, guarantee, and remittance — and classifies under FEMA as current account or capital account. Determines applicable route (automatic vs. approval).

layers.name: FDI Reporting
layers.description: For inbound FDI, manages all RBI reporting — FCGPR (within 30 days of share allotment), FCTRS (within 60 days of transfer), and Annual Return on Foreign Liabilities and Assets (FLA — July 15).

layers.name: ECB Compliance
layers.description: For External Commercial Borrowings, manages Form ECB filings, monthly Form ECB 2 returns, and compliance with permitted end-use restrictions.

layers.name: ODI Compliance
layers.description: For Overseas Direct Investment, manages Form ODI filings, annual performance reports (APR) for foreign subsidiaries, and monitoring of ODI conditions.

layers.name: Compounding Management
layers.description: When FEMA violations are identified (often through RBI or bank flagging), manages the voluntary compounding application — computing the compounding amount and preparing the application.

title: Trademark Portfolio Management
description:
Manages the company's trademark portfolio — applications, renewals, watch notices, enforcement, and international registrations — ensuring brand assets are protected across all key markets.
  Trademarks are core brand assets — unmanaged, they lapse or get attacked by competitors and squatters.

layers.name: Trademark Register
layers.description: Maintains the complete trademark register — all marks by class and jurisdiction, application date, registration number, registration date, renewal date, and owner.

layers.name: Renewal Management
layers.description: Tracks renewal dates for all registered trademarks (10-year renewal cycle in India). Sends alerts at 12, 6, and 3 months before expiry. Manages renewal filing and fee payment.

layers.name: Trademark Watch
layers.description: Runs ongoing trademark watch services — monitoring TM Registry journal for new applications that are confusingly similar to the company's registered marks. Files oppositions within the 4-month window.

layers.name: Infringement Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors the market for trademark infringement — e-commerce platforms, social media, and physical goods. Generates cease-and-desist notices and manages takedown requests.

layers.name: International Portfolio
layers.description: Manages international trademark registrations via Madrid Protocol. Tracks country-specific renewal requirements and handles office actions from foreign trademark offices.

title: Patent & Design Portfolio Management
description:
Manages the company's patent and design portfolio — prosecution, annuity payments, commercialisation analysis, and enforcement — maximising the value of the company's innovation investment.
  Patent lapsal from missed annuities is an irreversible loss — systematic management prevents it entirely.

layers.name: Patent Register
layers.description: Maintains the patent register — all applications and granted patents by jurisdiction, filing date, publication date, grant date, annuity due dates, and legal status.

layers.name: Annuity Payment Management
layers.description: Tracks annuity payment dates for all granted patents (annual in India, varies internationally). Manages payment through patent agents. Never lets a patent lapse from non-payment.

layers.name: Prosecution Management
layers.description: Tracks examination reports and deadlines. Prepares responses to patent office objections in coordination with patent agents and R&D team. Manages the grant process.

layers.name: Prior Art Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors patent databases for competitor filings in the company's technology space. Alerts R&D to freedom-to-operate risks and competitive intelligence.

layers.name: Portfolio Valuation & Strategy
layers.description: Periodically assesses the portfolio for commercial value — which patents are being practised, which have licensing potential, and which are candidates for abandonment (saving annuity costs).

title: Trade Secret & Confidentiality Management
description:
Manages the legal protection of the company's trade secrets and confidential information — NDAs, employee confidentiality obligations, trade secret classification, and enforcement against misappropriation.
  Trade secrets are often more valuable than registered IP — but unmanaged, they are legally unprotectable.

layers.name: Trade Secret Identification
layers.description: Works with business units to identify and document the company's trade secrets — formulae, processes, customer lists, pricing models, and strategic plans. Creates a trade secret register.

layers.name: NDA Management
layers.description: Manages all NDAs — ensuring appropriate NDAs are in place before any confidential disclosure, tracking expiry dates, maintaining a counterparty NDA register, and following up on violations.

layers.name: Employee Confidentiality Obligations
layers.description: Ensures all employment contracts have adequate confidentiality and IP assignment clauses. Manages exit obligations — remind departing employees of post-employment restrictions.

layers.name: Misappropriation Detection
layers.description: Monitors for potential trade secret misappropriation — ex-employees joining competitors, unusual data downloads before resignation, and competitors launching suspiciously similar products.

layers.name: Enforcement
layers.description: When misappropriation is suspected, manages the evidence preservation and legal response — injunction application in civil court, complaint under IPC Section 408/420, and criminal remedies.

title: IP Licensing & Commercialisation
description:
Manages IP licensing — both licensing-out the company's IP to third parties and licensing-in third-party IP — negotiating terms, tracking royalty flows, and ensuring compliance with licence conditions.
  IP licensing is a significant revenue and cost item that is often poorly managed, leading to royalty leakage and licence term violations.

layers.name: IP Inventory Valuation
layers.description: Assesses the commercial value of licensable IP — which patents, trademarks, or know-how have third-party licensing potential. Prepares licensing strategy for GC and business.

layers.name: Outbound Licence Management
layers.description: Drafts and manages licence agreements — field of use, territory, exclusivity, royalty rate, sublicensing rights, quality control (for trademarks), and audit rights. Tracks licence compliance.

layers.name: Royalty Management
layers.description: Tracks all royalty receivables and payables — due dates, computation bases, minimum royalty obligations, and milestone payments. Reconciles royalty statements.

layers.name: Inbound Licence Compliance
layers.description: Monitors compliance with inbound technology licences — permitted use restrictions, territory limitations, sublicensing restrictions, and maintenance fee obligations.

layers.name: Royalty Audit Rights
layers.description: Exercises audit rights in licence agreements where royalty understatement is suspected. Coordinates royalty audit through external auditors. Recovers underpaid royalties.

title: DPDP Act Compliance Programme
description:
Implements and manages compliance with India's Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act 2023 — data inventory, consent management, privacy notices, data principal rights fulfilment, and breach notification.
  The DPDP Act introduces significant penalties (up to ₹250 Cr per instance) — structured compliance is essential for every data-processing business.

layers.name: Personal Data Inventory
layers.description: Maps all personal data processed — what data, from whom (data principals), for what purpose, where stored, how protected, who has access, and how long retained. Creates the Record of Processing Activities (ROPA).

layers.name: Consent Management
layers.description: Implements a consent framework — clear, specific, and freely given consent notices, purpose limitation, granular consent options, and easy withdrawal mechanism. Manages consent records.

layers.name: Data Principal Rights
layers.description: Sets up the mechanism for data principal rights — access request, correction, erasure, and grievance redressal. Manages requests within the prescribed timelines.

layers.name: Data Fiduciary Obligations
layers.description: Manages all Data Fiduciary obligations — Data Protection Officer appointment, data processing agreements with data processors, cross-border transfer restrictions, and Significant Data Fiduciary obligations where applicable.

layers.name: Breach Notification
layers.description: Manages the personal data breach response — detection, containment, impact assessment, and mandatory notification to the Data Protection Board within the prescribed period.

title: GDPR & International Privacy Compliance
description:
Manages compliance with GDPR and other international privacy laws (CCPA, PDPA, PIPL) for the company's cross-border operations — ensuring adequate protections for personal data transferred to and from foreign jurisdictions.
  International privacy violations attract massive fines and market access restrictions.

layers.name: Cross-Border Data Flow Mapping
layers.description: Maps all cross-border personal data flows — India to EU (GDPR), India to US (CCPA for California residents), India to Singapore (PDPA), and China (PIPL). Identifies transfer mechanisms in place.

layers.name: Transfer Mechanism Compliance
layers.description: Ensures appropriate transfer mechanisms for each jurisdiction — Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) for EU, certification schemes, and adequacy decisions. Updates for regulatory changes.

layers.name: GDPR Data Subject Rights
layers.description: Manages GDPR data subject requests from EU residents — SAR (Subject Access Request) within 30 days, erasure rights, portability requests, and objection to processing.

layers.name: Privacy by Design
layers.description: Reviews new products, processes, and technology changes for privacy implications. Conducts DPIA (Data Protection Impact Assessment) for high-risk processing. Advises on privacy-by-design architecture.

layers.name: Regulatory Response
layers.description: Manages responses to data protection authority investigations — DPC (Ireland), ICO (UK), and CNIL (France) — in coordination with local privacy counsel.

flow.description: New projects assessed for privacy risk
Cookie consent and privacy notice maintained

title: Cybersecurity Legal Response & Incident Management
description:
Manages the legal dimension of cybersecurity incidents — IT Act obligations, CERT-In reporting, RBI/SEBI cyber incident reporting, forensic investigation oversight, and stakeholder communication.
  Cybersecurity incidents have significant legal obligations — failure to manage them correctly compounds the harm.

layers.name: CERT-In Reporting Obligations
layers.description: Reads CERT-In guidelines on mandatory incident reporting — which incident types must be reported (within 6 hours for critical incidents), to which authority, and in what format.

layers.name: Sector-Specific Reporting
layers.description: For regulated entities, manages sector-specific cyber incident reporting — RBI for banks/NBFCs, SEBI for listed companies, IRDAI for insurance, and TRAI for telecom.

layers.name: Forensic Investigation Legal Oversight
layers.description: Manages the legal aspects of forensic investigation — chain of custody, external forensic firm instructions, scope limitation advice, and preservation of privilege.

layers.name: Stakeholder Communication
layers.description: Drafts legally reviewed communications to impacted customers, employees, and the board — balancing transparency obligations, defamation risk, and litigation posture.

layers.name: IT Act & Criminal Complaints
layers.description: Advises on IT Act (Section 43A, 66, 66C) remedies — when to file a complaint with cybercrime police, what evidence to preserve, and how to pursue civil and criminal remedies against attackers.

title: Digital Business Legal Compliance
description:
Manages legal compliance for digital business operations — IT Act, Intermediary Guidelines, e-commerce rules, digital payment regulations, and UCC (Unsolicited Commercial Communications) compliance.
  Digital businesses face a rapidly evolving compliance landscape — staying current is critical to avoiding enforcement action.

layers.name: Intermediary Compliance
layers.description: Manages compliance with the IT (Intermediary Guidelines) Rules 2021 — Grievance Officer appointment, monthly compliance reports for significant social media intermediaries, and content takedown processes.

layers.name: E-Commerce Compliance
layers.description: Ensures compliance with Consumer Protection (E-Commerce) Rules — seller information disclosure, returns policy, grievance mechanism, and dark pattern prohibitions.

layers.name: Digital Payment Compliance
layers.description: For fintech/payment businesses, manages PPI licence compliance, RBI payment aggregator guidelines, and NPCI rule compliance.

layers.name: UCC & Marketing Compliance
layers.description: Manages compliance with TRAI's TCCCPR (Telemarketing regulations) — DND scrubbing, sender registration, and template registration. Manages email marketing CAN-SPAM and DPDP compliance.

layers.name: Website & App Legal Requirements
layers.description: Ensures website and app legal requirements — privacy policy, terms of service, cookie consent, accessibility (WCAG), and app store compliance (Google Play / App Store policies).

title: External Counsel Management
description:
Manages all external law firm relationships — matter intake, counsel selection, fee arrangements, invoice review, performance measurement, and panel management — optimising legal spend and quality.
  External counsel costs are significant and poorly managed in most companies — structured management typically saves 20-30%.

layers.name: Panel Management
layers.description: Maintains the approved external counsel panel — firms empanelled by practice area and geography. Manages RFP process for panel additions and annual panel review. Conflict checks on new matters.

layers.name: Matter Assignment
layers.description: When a matter requires external counsel, matches the matter type and urgency to the appropriate panel firm — practice area expertise, conflict-free, and rate efficiency.

layers.name: Engagement Letter & Budget
layers.description: Ensures every matter has a signed engagement letter — scope, hourly rates or fixed fee, budget, billing guidelines, and reporting requirements. Tracks actuals against budget.

layers.name: Invoice Review
layers.description: Reviews all external counsel invoices — validates against billing guidelines (no block billing, no excessive paralegal work, no travel markup), checks against matter budget, and approves appropriate invoices.

layers.name: Performance Management
layers.description: Measures external counsel performance — matter outcomes (win/loss rate), adherence to timeline commitments, proactive advice quality, and client service.

title: Legal Budget Planning & Cost Management
description:
Plans and manages the legal department budget — internal costs (headcount, technology), external counsel spend, regulatory filing fees, and litigation provisions — providing GC and CFO with real-time legal cost visibility.
  Legal departments are often viewed as cost centres without visibility — budget management improves both control and stakeholder trust.

layers.name: Annual Budget Planning
layers.description: Builds the annual legal budget — internal team costs, anticipated external counsel spend by practice area, regulatory fees, IP maintenance costs, and litigation provisions. Benchmarks against industry.

layers.name: Matter-Level Cost Tracking
layers.description: Tracks legal costs at matter level — each litigation, transaction, or compliance project has its own budget and actual cost tracking. Enables cost attribution to business units.

layers.name: External Spend Analytics
layers.description: Analyses external counsel spend — by firm, practice area, matter type, and business unit. Identifies spend concentration and opportunities for alternative fee arrangements.

layers.name: Litigation Provision Management
layers.description: Tracks Ind AS 37 provisions for litigation — coordinates with Finance for quarterly provision updates, manages provision releases on favourable outcomes, and provision additions on adverse developments.

layers.name: Value Demonstration
layers.description: Quantifies legal department value delivered — litigation savings vs. claimed amount, regulatory penalty avoidance, contract value protected, and IP licensing revenue. Prepares value report for CEO and board.

title: Legal Knowledge Management
description:
Manages the legal team's knowledge base — precedent contracts, research memoranda, opinions, playbooks, and regulatory guidance — ensuring institutional knowledge is captured, searchable, and accessible.
  Knowledge management prevents reinventing the wheel on every matter and captures value from every lawyer's work.

layers.name: Precedent Library
layers.description: Maintains an organised, searchable library of all precedent contracts — organised by type, counterparty type, jurisdiction, and key clauses. Tags negotiation history and clauses won/conceded.

layers.name: Legal Research Repository
layers.description: Archives all legal research — opinions, memos, judgement summaries, regulatory guidance. Makes them searchable by topic, jurisdiction, and applicable statute.

layers.name: Playbook Maintenance
layers.description: Maintains all legal playbooks — contract negotiation, litigation strategy, regulatory response, and M&A checklist. Updates playbooks after each matter with lessons learned.

layers.name: AI-Powered Legal Search
layers.description: Provides natural language search across the full knowledge base — lawyers can ask 'what is our standard position on indemnity for SaaS contracts?' and get the exact precedent and playbook guidance.

layers.name: Knowledge Currency Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors the knowledge base for stale content — opinions based on overruled judgements, contracts with outdated clauses, and playbooks not updated for regulatory changes.

title: Legal Technology & Process Innovation
description:
Manages legal technology adoption — CLM, e-discovery, legal research AI, matter management, and process automation — identifying and implementing technology solutions that increase the legal team's capacity and quality.
  Legal technology investment typically delivers 3-5x return through productivity gains and error reduction.

layers.name: Technology Landscape Assessment
layers.description: Evaluates available legal technology solutions — CLM systems, e-billing, legal research AI, document automation, and matter management — against the team's needs and budget.

layers.name: Implementation Management
layers.description: Manages legal technology implementations — requirements definition, vendor selection, configuration, training, and adoption monitoring. Ensures legal team actually uses new tools.

layers.name: Process Automation Identification
layers.description: Identifies repetitive legal processes that can be automated — standard contract generation, regulatory filing reminders, board meeting pack assembly, and litigation deadline tracking.

layers.name: Innovation Pipeline
layers.description: Maintains a pipeline of legal process improvement initiatives — prioritised by impact and implementation complexity. Tracks delivery against the legal innovation roadmap.

layers.name: ROI Measurement
layers.description: Measures the return on legal technology investments — time savings per lawyer, error reduction, cycle time improvement, and cost avoidance. Reports to GC and CFO.

title: Legal Risk Management & Board Reporting
description:
Aggregates all legal risks — litigation, regulatory, contractual, IP, and compliance — into a single risk view for the GC, CFO, and board, with provisions, likelihood assessments, and strategic recommendations.
  Legal risk is often the largest unquantified risk on the balance sheet — systematic reporting brings it under management control.

layers.name: Legal Risk Register
layers.description: Maintains a comprehensive legal risk register — litigation (with Ind AS 37 classification), regulatory investigations, contract disputes, compliance gaps, and IP risks. Each risk has probability, impact, and mitigation.

layers.name: Financial Risk Quantification
layers.description: For each legal risk, computes the financial impact range — best case, expected case, and worst case. Aggregates to total legal risk exposure for CFO provisioning.

layers.name: Ind AS 37 Compliance
layers.description: Classifies all legal risks under Ind AS 37 — Provision (probable and estimable), Contingent Liability (possible but not probable), and Contingent Asset (possible favourable). Prepares disclosure notes.

layers.name: Risk Trend Analysis
layers.description: Tracks legal risk trends — is litigation increasing or decreasing? Are regulatory risks increasing? Which business units generate the most legal risk?

layers.name: Board Reporting
layers.description: Prepares the legal section of the board and audit committee report — material legal risks, new developments, provision requirements, and GC's overall legal health assessment.

title: Mergers, Acquisitions & Restructuring Legal Support
description:
Provides comprehensive legal support for M&A transactions — due diligence management, transaction documentation, regulatory approvals, and integration support — managing the full deal lifecycle.
  M&A transactions are high-value, high-risk legal events where errors can destroy deal value — systematic management is essential.

layers.name: Legal Due Diligence
layers.description: Manages the legal due diligence process — data room setup, DD questionnaire, review of target's contracts, litigation, IP, regulatory status, and corporate records. Prepares legal DD report with risk matrix.

layers.name: Transaction Documentation
layers.description: Drafts and negotiates the transaction documents — Term Sheet, NDA, SHA (Shareholders' Agreement), SPA (Share Purchase Agreement), and ancillary agreements.

layers.name: Regulatory Approvals
layers.description: Manages all regulatory approvals required — CCI (competition), NCLT (scheme of arrangement), RBI (FEMA approvals for cross-border), SEBI (for listed companies), and sector-specific approvals.

layers.name: Conditions Precedent Tracking
layers.description: Tracks all conditions precedent to closing — regulatory approvals, third-party consents, board and shareholder approvals, and lender consents. Manages the closing checklist.

layers.name: Post-Merger Integration
layers.description: Manages legal integration — entity rationalisation, contract novation, licence transfers, employment transfers (TUPE equivalent), and branding change documentation.

title: Legal Compliance Certification & Board Attestation
description:
Prepares the annual legal and statutory compliance certificate — confirming compliance with all applicable laws and regulations — for the board, annual report, and regulatory submissions.
  Board members are personally liable for compliance failures they failed to prevent — the compliance certificate provides confidence that systems are working.

layers.name: Compliance Universe Verification
layers.description: Verifies the compliance universe is complete — all applicable laws identified, obligations mapped, and owners assigned. Confirms no changes in the year that expanded the compliance universe without corresponding updates.

layers.name: Compliance Status Aggregation
layers.description: Aggregates compliance status from all function heads — Finance (Tax, FEMA), HR (Labour laws, POSH), Operations (EHS, Factories Act), IT (IT Act, DPDP), and Legal (all domains). Verifies each sign-off.

layers.name: Non-Compliance Investigation
layers.description: For any identified non-compliances, investigates — was it a one-time miss already rectified, an ongoing gap, or a systemic failure? Classifies and documents.

layers.name: External Secretarial Auditor
layers.description: Coordinates with the Secretarial Auditor (required for listed companies under SEBI LODR) — providing all compliance evidence, responding to queries, and reviewing the Secretarial Audit Report (Form MR-3).

layers.name: Board Attestation
layers.description: Prepares the compliance report for the board — list of applicable laws, compliance status (green/amber/red), non-compliances and remediation, and director responsibility statement for the annual report.

title: Spend Analysis & Category Classification
description:
Analyses all enterprise spend across ERP, P-cards, T&E, and invoices — classifying every transaction into a spend taxonomy, identifying savings opportunities and maverick spend.
  Provides the foundation for every sourcing decision with a live, accurate view of who is spending what, with which vendors, across which categories.

layers.name: Data Ingestion
layers.description: Pulls spend from all sources — SAP/Oracle AP module, corporate credit cards, expense management, and PO history. Normalises currencies and entity codes.

layers.name: Vendor Normalisation
layers.description: Resolves vendor name variants into canonical records using fuzzy matching and legal entity database (TCS vs. Tata Consultancy vs. Tata Consult.).

layers.name: Category Classification
layers.description: Maps each spend line to a standardised taxonomy using NLP on line item description + vendor category + cost centre. Handles ambiguous descriptions with confidence scoring.

layers.name: Maverick Spend Detection
layers.description: Identifies spend that bypassed procurement — no PO, unapproved vendor, off-contract pricing, or purchases from vendors not on the approved list.

layers.name: Opportunity Identification
layers.description: Analyses concentration, fragmentation, off-contract spend, and tail spend to surface consolidation and savings opportunities ranked by value.

title: Supplier Market Intelligence
description:
Continuously monitors the supplier market for each spend category — pricing benchmarks, new entrants, incumbent supplier financial health, geopolitical risks, and commodity movements.
  Provides procurement with real-time intelligence to time sourcing events and negotiate from strength.

layers.name: Category Market Monitoring
layers.description: Watches commodity indices, industry reports, and news feeds per category. Tracks input cost movements that affect supplier pricing.

layers.name: Supplier Financial Health
layers.description: Monitors incumbent and potential suppliers' financial health — credit ratings, annual reports, payment delay reports, and insolvency filings.

layers.name: New Supplier Discovery
layers.description: Scans databases, directories, and trade shows for new or alternative suppliers that could increase competition in each category.

layers.name: Pricing Benchmark
layers.description: Compares current contracted prices against market benchmarks — price indices, industry survey data, and anonymised peer comparisons.

layers.name: Risk & Opportunity Alert
layers.description: Generates timed alerts — commodity price drop = renegotiation opportunity; supplier distress = dual-source now; new entrant = leverage in renewal.

title: RFx Generation & Management
description:
Autonomously generates RFI, RFQ, and RFP documents from business requirements — managing the full sourcing event including Q&A, bid evaluation, and supplier shortlisting.
  Reduces sourcing event cycle time from weeks to days while improving consistency and fairness.

layers.name: Requirements Extraction
layers.description: Reads the procurement requisition, technical spec, SOW, and stakeholder input. Structures requirements into RFx format — mandatory, desired, and evaluation criteria.

layers.name: Template Selection & Population
layers.description: Selects the appropriate RFx template (RFI/RFQ/RFP) and populates with extracted requirements and standard commercial terms.

layers.name: Supplier Shortlist
layers.description: Identifies qualified suppliers from approved vendor list, spend analysis, and market intelligence — scored by category relevance and capacity.

layers.name: Q&A Management
layers.description: Reads supplier clarification questions, drafts answers (routing complex ones for SME input), and distributes responses to all bidders simultaneously and fairly.

layers.name: Bid Evaluation
layers.description: Scores submitted bids against evaluation criteria — price, technical compliance, quality certifications, delivery terms, and financial strength. Generates ranked shortlist.

title: Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Analysis
description:
Computes full TCO for sourcing decisions — beyond unit price to include quality costs, logistics, inventory, supplier risk premium, and ongoing support costs.
  Enables procurement to make true apples-to-apples supplier comparisons and justify decisions to stakeholders.

layers.name: Price Component Extraction
layers.description: Reads bid/quote to extract all price components — unit price, tooling, MOQ charges, volume tier pricing, freight, duties, and payment terms cost of capital.

layers.name: Quality Cost Modelling
layers.description: Applies historical supplier quality data — defect rates, rework costs, warranty claims, and inspection costs — to compute the quality cost premium per supplier.

layers.name: Logistics & Inventory
layers.description: Models landed cost — freight, insurance, customs duties, lead time-driven safety stock, and working capital in transit inventory.

layers.name: Risk Premium
layers.description: Applies a risk cost factor per supplier based on financial health, geographic risk, single-source dependency, and past disruption history.

layers.name: TCO Comparison
layers.description: Ranks suppliers by TCO, not just price. Highlights the gap between cheapest price and lowest TCO — often not the same supplier.

title: Savings Tracking & Realisation
description: Tracks every procurement savings initiative from identification through negotiation, contract signature, and actual spend realisation — ensuring savings committed to the CFO are verified and visible.

layers.name: Savings Identification
layers.description: Captures savings opportunities from spend analysis, market intelligence, and sourcing events. Classifies by type — price reduction, demand reduction, specification change, consolidation.

layers.name: Baseline Establishment
layers.description: Computes the savings baseline per category — prior year price, last contract rate, market benchmark, or budget rate per savings policy.

layers.name: Committed Savings Tracking
layers.description: Tracks savings at each milestone — identified, negotiated, contract signed, and realised in spend data.

layers.name: Realisation Monitoring
layers.description: Cross-checks contracted savings against actual spend. Flags where savings are contracted but not realised — demand change, compliance failure, or scope change.

layers.name: Finance Reconciliation
layers.description: Reconciles procurement-reported savings against P&L impact as recognised by Finance. Resolves hard vs. soft savings classification differences.

title: Supplier Onboarding & Qualification
description: Automates the full supplier onboarding journey — from registration request through document collection, verification, risk assessment, and approval — reducing onboarding time from weeks to days.

layers.name: Registration Intake
layers.description: Receives supplier registration request. Extracts supplier details — legal name, GSTIN/PAN, bank details, ownership structure, category, and certifications.

layers.name: Document Collection & Validation
layers.description: Sends automated document requests and validates authenticity using OCR + government database cross-check.

layers.name: Regulatory & Compliance Check
layers.description: Verifies GSTIN on GSTN portal, validates PAN with Income Tax database, checks debarment lists and sanctions screening.

layers.name: Financial Risk Assessment
layers.description: Analyses supplier financial health from submitted statements. Assigns financial risk grade (A/B/C/D).

layers.name: Category Qualification
layers.description: Validates supplier capability — ISO certifications, technical qualifications, capacity assessment, and reference checks.

title: Supplier Performance Management
description: Continuously measures supplier performance across quality, delivery, cost, and service — generating scorecards, flagging underperformance, and orchestrating improvement plans automatically.

layers.name: KPI Data Collection
layers.description: Pulls data from GRN records (delivery), quality inspections (defect rates), invoice processing (billing accuracy), and service desk (response times).

layers.name: Scorecard Computation
layers.description: Calculates weighted scores — on-time delivery (30%), quality (25%), price compliance (20%), invoice accuracy (15%), responsiveness (10%).

layers.name: Trend Analysis
layers.description: Tracks performance trend over 12 months. Identifies improving vs. deteriorating trajectories. Distinguishes one-off events from systemic issues.

layers.name: Root Cause Analysis
layers.description: For underperforming KPIs, retrieves underlying transactions — which deliveries were late, which batches failed, which invoices had errors.

layers.name: Action Triggering
layers.description: Based on performance level and trend, triggers formal improvement plan, strategic review, or preferred status recognition.

flow.description: KPI data pulled from all systems

title: Supplier Risk Monitoring
