description: Continuously monitors the risk landscape for every strategic supplier — financial distress, geopolitical events, ESG violations, cyber incidents, and capacity disruptions — to protect supply continuity.

layers.name: Financial Distress Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks credit rating changes, delayed filings, restructuring news, court filings, and GST non-compliance.

layers.name: Geopolitical & Supply Chain Risk
layers.description: Monitors country-level risk events — instability, natural disasters, port disruptions, and trade policy changes.

layers.name: ESG & Compliance Risk
layers.description: Tracks labour violations, environmental incidents, regulatory penalties, and ethical issues at suppliers or sub-suppliers.

layers.name: Operational Risk
layers.description: Monitors plant incidents, production capacity changes, key person departures, quality recalls, and cyber/ransomware events.

layers.name: Risk Scoring & Tiering
layers.description: Aggregates all risk signals into a composite risk score. Tiers suppliers by criticality and risk level. Triggers contingency planning.

title: Supplier Development & Collaboration
description: Orchestrates structured supplier development programmes — identifying strategic suppliers with growth potential, designing improvement roadmaps, and tracking joint value creation.

layers.name: Strategic Supplier Segmentation
layers.description: Classifies all suppliers into tiers — Strategic (top 5%), Preferred (next 20%), Approved, and Transactional — based on spend, criticality, and performance.

layers.name: Development Opportunity Identification
layers.description: For Strategic and Preferred suppliers, identifies development opportunities — cost engineering, quality improvement, capacity expansion, and innovation collaboration.

layers.name: Joint Business Plan
layers.description: Generates a structured JBP template with supplier-specific targets — cost reduction roadmap, quality milestones, volume commitments, and innovation agenda.

layers.name: Progress Tracking
layers.description: Monitors JBP milestone achievement. Computes joint value created. Tracks commitments by both parties.

layers.name: Innovation Pipeline
layers.description: Manages supplier innovation submissions and routes to internal R&D or engineering for evaluation.

title: Contract Compliance Monitoring
description: Monitors every active contract for compliance — pricing adherence, volume commitments, SLA performance, and contractual obligation milestones — and triggers action before breaches escalate.

layers.name: Contract Data Extraction
layers.description: Reads all active contracts using NLP — pricing tiers, volume thresholds, SLA terms, penalty clauses, review dates, indexation formulas, and notice periods.

layers.name: Invoice Price Validation
layers.description: Compares every supplier invoice price against the contracted price schedule. Flags overcharges, off-tier pricing, and incorrect surcharges.

layers.name: Volume Commitment Tracking
layers.description: Monitors purchase volumes against Minimum Purchase Commitments and volume-tier thresholds.

layers.name: SLA Compliance
layers.description: Cross-references actual performance against contracted SLA commitments. Computes service credits or penalties due.

layers.name: Contract Lifecycle Alerts
layers.description: Monitors contract expiry dates, auto-renewal triggers, notice period deadlines, indexation review dates, and renegotiation windows.

title: Automated Purchase Requisition to PO
description: Converts approved purchase requisitions into optimised purchase orders — selecting the right vendor, applying correct contract pricing, and routing for approval — with zero manual intervention for standard purchases.

layers.name: Requisition Reading
layers.description: Reads the approved PR — item description, quantity, delivery date, cost centre, budget code, and specification attachments. Validates completeness.

layers.name: Vendor Selection
layers.description: Identifies the preferred supplier — checks approved vendor list, existing contracts, preferred supplier by category, and any framework agreements.

layers.name: Price Determination
layers.description: Fetches the contracted price from the active price list or contract schedule. Applies volume tier pricing if quantity triggers a threshold.

layers.name: PO Optimisation
layers.description: Where multiple PRs exist for the same vendor, consolidates into a single PO. Splits large orders per delivery schedule if required.

layers.name: Approval Routing
layers.description: Routes PO for approval based on value, category, and cost centre rules. For pre-approved items within framework contracts, bypasses approval entirely.

title: GRN (Goods Receipt Note) Automation
description: Automates the goods receipt process — from delivery confirmation through quantity verification, quality inspection trigger, and GRN creation in ERP — closing the procure-to-pay loop accurately.

layers.name: Delivery Notification
layers.description: Captures delivery notifications — driver app, warehouse scan, email from logistics partner. Matches to open PO automatically.

layers.name: Quantity & Item Verification
layers.description: Compares delivered items and quantities against the PO. Identifies short deliveries, over-deliveries, wrong items, and substitutions.

layers.name: Quality Inspection Trigger
layers.description: Based on item category and inspection plan, triggers quality inspection workflow. Pre-approved items with clean history get auto-waived.

layers.name: GRN Creation
layers.description: On receipt confirmation (and inspection pass where required), creates the GRN in ERP — enabling AP to proceed with invoice matching.

layers.name: Discrepancy Management
layers.description: For shortages or quality failures, creates a GRDN — automatically communicates to vendor, holds payment on disputed quantity.

title: PO Change Order Management
description: Manages purchase order amendments — quantity changes, delivery date changes, price revisions, cancellations — with automated supplier communication, approval routing, and ERP update.

layers.name: Change Request Intake
layers.description: Receives change request from requester, operations, or supplier. Classifies change type — quantity, date, specification, price, cancellation.

layers.name: Impact Assessment
layers.description: Evaluates the impact — cost impact, delivery schedule impact, supplier commitment implications, and budget impact.

layers.name: Approval Routing
layers.description: Routes the change order for approval based on financial impact and change type.

layers.name: Supplier Communication
layers.description: Issues the change order to the supplier with clear documentation — what is changing, effective date, and commercial implications.

layers.name: ERP Update & Budget Reconciliation
layers.description: Updates the PO in ERP with approved changes. Reconciles budget commitment.

title: Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) Optimisation
description: Optimises inventory replenishment by analysing consumption patterns, lead times, and demand forecasts — automatically triggering POs before stockouts occur, without manual reorder point management.

layers.name: Consumption Monitoring
layers.description: Reads real-time inventory levels and daily consumption rates from the warehouse management system. Tracks actual vs. forecast variance.

layers.name: Demand Forecasting
layers.description: Applies time-series forecasting for the next 30/60/90 days — incorporating seasonality, production plan, sales orders, and historical patterns.

layers.name: Reorder Point Calculation
layers.description: Computes dynamic reorder point and order quantity — balancing holding cost, ordering cost, stockout cost, and lead time variability.

layers.name: Order Trigger
layers.description: When inventory drops below the dynamic ROP, automatically generates and issues a PO or VMI replenishment request to the supplier.

layers.name: Supplier Lead Time Tracking
layers.description: Monitors actual vs. contracted lead times. Feeds deviations back into safety stock calculations.

flow.description: Actual vs. contracted lead times compared

title: Contract Drafting & Clause Intelligence
description: Generates procurement contract drafts from standard templates, intelligently selecting and customising clauses based on supplier risk profile, category, deal value, and jurisdiction.

layers.name: Contract Parameter Extraction
layers.description: Reads the sourcing decision, supplier profile, deal value, product/service category, and delivery geography. Defines the contract type required.

layers.name: Clause Library Selection
layers.description: Selects appropriate clauses — liability caps, indemnification, IP ownership, data protection, force majeure, governing law — calibrated to risk level.

layers.name: Risk-Based Customisation
layers.description: Adjusts clause stringency based on deal risk — larger deals get stronger liability language; overseas suppliers get jurisdictional clauses; high-data-risk suppliers get DPDP compliance clauses.

layers.name: Supplier Redline Analysis
layers.description: When supplier returns redlined draft, classifies each change — pre-approved alternative, non-standard, or red-line (unacceptable).

layers.name: Negotiation Playbook
layers.description: For non-standard supplier positions, generates a playbook — what to accept, where to push back, concessions available, and walk-away positions.

title: Contract Obligation & Milestone Tracking
description: Extracts all obligations, milestones, and deliverables from executed contracts and tracks fulfilment by both the company and supplier — ensuring nothing falls through the cracks.

layers.name: Obligation Extraction
layers.description: Reads executed contracts using NLP to extract all obligations — supplier deliverables, company payment milestones, audit rights, reporting requirements, and review dates.

layers.name: Obligation Classification
layers.description: Classifies each obligation by owner (our obligation vs. supplier), type (deliverable, payment, reporting, audit), and time horizon.

layers.name: Calendar Population
layers.description: Creates a structured obligation calendar with owners assigned and advance notification windows configured.

layers.name: Fulfilment Tracking
layers.description: Monitors actual fulfilment against each obligation. Cross-references ERP, delivery systems, and email/portal.

layers.name: Breach & Remedy
layers.description: Identifies overdue obligations. Initiates cure notices for supplier breaches. Alerts internal teams for company-side risks.

title: Contract Renewal & Renegotiation Intelligence
description: Identifies contracts approaching expiry, assesses renewal strategy, prepares the category manager with market intelligence and negotiation strategies, and manages the renewal workflow.

layers.name: Expiry Calendar Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors all active contracts by expiry date. Identifies contracts approaching notice period windows (90-180 days). Triggers renewal planning workflow.

layers.name: Renewal Strategy Assessment
layers.description: Evaluates each expiring contract on market dynamics, supplier performance, satisfaction, and competitive alternatives — to recommend Renew / Renegotiate / Re-tender / Exit.

layers.name: Market Intelligence Briefing
layers.description: Assembles current market intelligence — benchmark pricing, market capacity, new entrants, and commodity trends — to inform negotiation position.

layers.name: Negotiation Position
layers.description: Computes the company's leverage — volume significance to supplier, switch cost, alternatives available — and recommends opening, target, and walk-away positions.

layers.name: Renewal Package
layers.description: Generates the complete renewal package — negotiation brief, BATNA analysis, draft term sheet, and approval request.

title: eAuction & Reverse Auction Management
description: Manages the full lifecycle of eAuctions and reverse auctions — from supplier invitation and rules configuration through live auction management, winner selection, and award documentation.

layers.name: Auction Design
layers.description: Configures auction parameters — type (English reverse, Dutch, sealed bid), opening price, minimum bid decrement, auction duration, lot structure, and qualification criteria.

layers.name: Supplier Preparation
layers.description: Sends personalised briefings to invited suppliers — item specs, rules, schedule, and technical support.

layers.name: Live Auction Management
layers.description: Monitors the live auction in real time — tracks bids, detects shill bidding, extends time on active bidding, and disqualifies rule violations.

layers.name: Post-Auction Analysis
layers.description: Analyses outcomes — savings vs. opening price, participation rate, competitive intensity, and price convergence. Flags low-competition auctions.

layers.name: Award & Documentation
layers.description: Generates award recommendation with full audit trail. Produces award letter, rejection notifications, and debrief for unsuccessful bidders.

title: Procurement Policy Compliance Monitoring
description: Monitors every procurement transaction for policy compliance — approval authority, vendor selection rules, competitive bidding requirements, and conflict of interest — generating real-time exception reports.

layers.name: Policy Rules Engine
layers.description: Loads procurement policy into a rules engine — approval thresholds, mandatory competitive bidding requirements, approved vendor rules, and single-source justification requirements.

layers.name: Transaction Screening
layers.description: Screens every PR, PO, and payment against policy rules in real time. Checks approval authority, vendor compliance, and competitive process adherence.

layers.name: Split Order Detection
layers.description: Identifies PO splitting — multiple smaller POs to the same vendor for the same purpose to avoid approval thresholds.

layers.name: Conflict of Interest Detection
layers.description: Screens approvers against vendor ownership databases. Flags where an approver may have a financial interest in the vendor they approved.

layers.name: Exception Reporting
layers.description: Generates a tiered exception report — minor deviations, moderate (policy bypasses), and critical (fraud indicators). Routes to appropriate authority.

flow.description: Every transaction checked in real time

title: ESG & Sustainability Compliance in Supply Chain
description: Monitors the supply chain for ESG compliance — environmental, labour practice, ethical sourcing, and sustainability criteria — ensuring procurement aligns with ESG commitments.

layers.name: ESG Framework Loading
layers.description: Loads the company's ESG procurement policy — minimum standards for carbon emissions, labour practices, conflict minerals, modern slavery, and diversity criteria.

layers.name: Supplier ESG Data Collection
layers.description: Collects ESG data from suppliers via questionnaires, third-party agencies (EcoVadis, Sustainalytics), and public reporting (CDP, GRI).

layers.name: Continuous Monitoring
layers.description: Scans news, NGO reports, and regulatory filings for ESG events at suppliers — labour violations, environmental penalties, bribery investigations.

layers.name: Carbon Footprint Tracking
layers.description: Computes Scope 3 emissions attributable to procurement spend using supplier-reported or spend-based emission factors.

layers.name: ESG Scorecard & Risk Rating
layers.description: Assigns an ESG score and risk rating to every supplier. Flags non-compliant suppliers and triggers improvement plans.

title: Anti-Bribery & Corruption (ABC) Compliance
description: Screens all procurement interactions — vendor gifts, hospitality, agent payments — for anti-bribery and corruption risk, ensuring compliance with the Prevention of Corruption Act and FCPA requirements.

layers.name: Transaction Screening
layers.description: Screens all vendor payments, agent commissions, and third-party payments for ABC red flags — unusual margins, shell company payments, geographic risk.

layers.name: Vendor Due Diligence
layers.description: Applies enhanced due diligence to vendors in high-risk jurisdictions, intermediaries, agents, and politically exposed person (PEP) connected entities.

layers.name: Gift & Hospitality Register
layers.description: Tracks all gifts and hospitality received or given in connection with procurement decisions. Flags where gifts approach policy thresholds.

layers.name: Tender Integrity Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors sourcing events for integrity risks — information leaks, bid manipulation, evaluation irregularities, and post-award relationship anomalies.

layers.name: Whistleblower Integration
layers.description: Processes anonymous whistleblower reports related to procurement corruption. Classifies, prioritises, and routes for investigation.

title: Import/Export Compliance & Trade Facilitation
description: Automates import/export documentation, customs classification, duty optimisation, and trade compliance screening — reducing customs delays, minimising duty costs, and ensuring regulatory compliance.

layers.name: HS Code Classification
layers.description: Reads product specifications and invoice descriptions to assign the correct Harmonised System code — critical for customs duty computation and trade restrictions.

layers.name: Duty Optimisation
layers.description: Identifies applicable duty exemptions, FTA benefits, EPCG scheme eligibility, and advance licence opportunities.

layers.name: Documentation Automation
layers.description: Generates and validates all import/export documents — Bill of Entry, Shipping Bill, Certificate of Origin, LC documents, and DGFT filings.

layers.name: Trade Sanctions Screening
layers.description: Screens all counterparties against OFAC, UN sanctions lists, and Indian denied entity lists.

layers.name: Customs Compliance Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks all imports and exports against ICEGATE records. Monitors duty demand notices, adjudications, and drawback claim status.

title: Invoice Matching & Exception Handling
description: Performs automated 2-way and 3-way invoice matching — intelligently handling exceptions, clearing 92%+ of invoices without human intervention.

layers.name: Invoice Extraction
layers.description: Reads incoming invoices from all channels. Extracts all fields with OCR + NLP: vendor, invoice number, line items, quantities, unit prices, taxes, and bank details.

layers.name: PO and GRN Matching
layers.description: Performs 3-way match — Invoice vs. PO vs. GRN — at line item level within configured tolerance.

layers.name: Exception Classification
layers.description: Classifies every match exception — price variance, quantity variance, PO not found, duplicate, or partial GRN.

layers.name: Intelligent Resolution
layers.description: Price variances within tolerance auto-approved; GRN shortfall triggers delivery confirmation; PO not found triggers PO lookup or non-PO workflow.

layers.name: Exception Escalation
layers.description: Exceptions that cannot be auto-resolved are routed to the appropriate resolver with full context.

title: Payment Terms Optimisation
description: Analyses supplier payment terms across the AP portfolio — identifying supply chain finance, dynamic discounting, extended terms, and payment timing opportunities to improve working capital.

layers.name: Payment Terms Mapping
layers.description: Reads all active contracts and POs to extract current payment terms for every supplier.

layers.name: Working Capital Impact Analysis
layers.description: Computes the working capital impact of each supplier's terms. Calculates DPO per supplier and total AP working capital.

layers.name: Discount Opportunity Analysis
layers.description: Identifies early payment discounts. Computes annualised return vs. cost of capital.

layers.name: Supply Chain Finance
layers.description: Identifies suppliers who would benefit from SCF — SMEs with higher cost of capital who would accept early payment at a discount.

layers.name: Terms Renegotiation Targets
layers.description: Identifies suppliers where payment terms are shorter than market standard — targets for extension in next renewal.

title: Supplier Portal Management
description: Manages all supplier interactions through a unified digital portal — self-service order acknowledgement, delivery scheduling, invoice submission, payment status, and dispute resolution.

layers.name: PO Notification & Acknowledgement
layers.description: Auto-notifies suppliers of new and amended POs via the portal. Tracks supplier acknowledgement and flags non-acknowledgements.

layers.name: Invoice Submission & Validation
layers.description: Supplier submits invoices through the portal. Agent validates completeness at point of entry — PO reference, GSTIN, correct amounts — before accepting.

layers.name: Delivery Scheduling
layers.description: Supplier confirms delivery dates and logistics details. Agent cross-checks against PO requirements and warehouse receiving capacity.

layers.name: Payment Status Transparency
layers.description: Provides suppliers real-time visibility of invoice status — received, matching, approved, scheduled, paid — eliminating payment enquiry calls.

layers.name: Dispute Management
layers.description: Supplier-raised disputes handled through portal workflow — structured submission, evidence attachment, and resolution tracking.

flow.description: New PO issued to supplier via portal

title: Three-Way Match at Scale
description: Handles high-volume, complex 3-way matching scenarios — blanket POs, partial deliveries, service entry sheets, and framework order matching — that standard ERP matching cannot handle.

layers.name: Complex PO Type Handling
layers.description: Manages blanket POs (match against cumulative releases), framework orders (match against call-off quantities), and consignment stock invoicing.

layers.name: Service Matching
layers.description: For service invoices, matches against Service Entry Sheets and time/material records. Validates hours logged, rates applied, and deliverable completion.

layers.name: Cumulative Tolerance Management
layers.description: Tracks cumulative matching across multiple partial invoices. Prevents tolerance gaming with multiple small invoices each within tolerance.

layers.name: Multi-Currency Matching
layers.description: Handles foreign currency invoices. Applies the correct exchange rate (contractual, booking, or FEMA rate). Flags forex gains/losses.

layers.name: Period-End Accrual Integration
layers.description: Identifies POs with GRNs but no invoices (accruals needed) and invoices without GRNs (early invoicing) — feeds directly to Finance accrual process.

title: Indirect Spend & Tail Spend Management
description: Automates management of indirect and tail spend — the thousands of small purchases that consume disproportionate procurement effort — through catalogues, virtual cards, and AI-powered spot buying.

layers.name: Spend Classification
layers.description: Identifies all tail spend transactions and classifies by category and frequency for treatment decision.

layers.name: Catalogue Development
layers.description: Identifies the top 80% of tail spend items by volume and creates digital catalogues with pre-negotiated pricing.

layers.name: Guided Buying
layers.description: Intercepts purchases and guides employees to the preferred buying channel — catalogue, approved vendor, or marketplace.

layers.name: Virtual Card Issuance
layers.description: For approved non-catalogue purchases below threshold, issues a single-use virtual card — spend-limited, merchant-category-limited, single-transaction.

layers.name: Tail Spend Analytics
layers.description: Tracks tail spend patterns — emerging categories worth cataloguing, vendors appearing repeatedly, and policy bypass hotspots.

title: Demand Aggregation & Consolidation
description: Aggregates procurement demand from all business units before orders are placed — identifying consolidation opportunities for better pricing, reduced overhead, and maximum leverage.

layers.name: Demand Signal Collection
layers.description: Reads demand signals from multiple sources — approved PRs, production plans, maintenance schedules, and project BOMs — across all BUs and geographies.

layers.name: Consolidation Identification
layers.description: Groups demand by commodity, specification, category, and time window. Identifies where multiple BUs buy the same or similar items from different vendors.

layers.name: Specification Harmonisation
layers.description: Identifies where similar but non-identical specs could be harmonised and quantifies the savings from standardisation.

layers.name: Volume Leverage Modelling
layers.description: Models the pricing improvement available from consolidated demand using current vendor price tiers or market benchmarks.

layers.name: Consolidated Sourcing Event
layers.description: Generates a consolidated sourcing plan — single RFQ or contract negotiation for the aggregated volume.

flow.description: All PRs and demand signals read

title: Specification Review & Standardisation
description: Reviews technical specifications in PRs for over-engineering, proprietary locking, and non-standard requirements — challenging specs that unnecessarily restrict competition or inflate cost.

layers.name: Specification Reading
layers.description: Reads technical specifications from PRs and BOMs. Identifies key parameters — materials, tolerances, performance requirements, brand specifications, and certifications.

layers.name: Over-Engineering Detection
layers.description: Compares specifications against industry standards and historical equivalents. Flags specs more demanding than functional requirements suggest.

layers.name: Proprietary Lock Detection
layers.description: Identifies brand-specific or sole-source specs that unnecessarily restrict competition. Flags 'or equivalent' opportunities.

layers.name: Standardisation Opportunity
layers.description: Identifies where multiple similar specs across the organisation could be harmonised to reduce variety and enable volume leverage.

layers.name: Value Engineering Recommendation
layers.description: Suggests specification changes that maintain functional performance while reducing cost — alternative materials, relaxed tolerances, standard components.

title: Budget & Commitment Management
description: Tracks procurement commitments against approved budgets in real time — ensuring POs do not exceed budget, flagging over-commitment risk, and providing finance-ready commitment reports.

layers.name: Budget Loading
layers.description: Reads approved operating and capital budgets by cost centre, GL account, and period from the Finance system. Maintains a live budget ledger.

layers.name: Commitment Tracking
layers.description: Logs every approved PR and issued PO as a commitment against the relevant budget line. Updates available balance in real time.

layers.name: Budget Breach Prevention
layers.description: Validates every PR against available budget before approval. If insufficient budget, blocks the PR and routes to budget owner.

layers.name: Commitment Liquidation
layers.description: Monitors POs for delivery and invoicing. Liquidates commitments when invoices are posted — ensuring commitment ledger always reflects actual exposure.

layers.name: Forecast & Alert
layers.description: Projects year-end commitment vs. budget based on current run rate and approved PRs in pipeline.

title: Catalogue & Preferred Supplier Management
description:
Manages the company's procurement catalogues and preferred supplier lists — keeping them current, guiding buyers to preferred choices, and measuring catalogue compliance and adoption rates.
  Ensures every buyer in the organisation has a clear, easy path to compliant purchasing without needing to know procurement policy.

layers.name: Catalogue Maintenance
layers.description: Monitors catalogue items for price expiry, product discontinuation, and specification changes. Automatically requests updated pricing from suppliers before catalogue items expire.

layers.name: New Item Addition
layers.description: Identifies frequently purchased non-catalogue items and initiates the process to add them to the catalogue — supplier negotiation, spec capture, and approval workflow.

layers.name: Preferred Supplier Guidance
layers.description: At point of purchase, surfaces the preferred supplier for the requested item/category. Shows price comparison, delivery time, and performance rating to guide the buyer to the best compliant choice.

layers.name: Compliance Measurement
layers.description: Tracks catalogue adoption rate — what percentage of eligible spend goes through catalogue vs. off-catalogue. Identifies cost centres with low compliance for targeted intervention.

layers.name: Catalogue Optimisation
layers.description: Analyses catalogue usage patterns — items never ordered, items always substituted, items ordered in non-standard quantities — and recommends catalogue rationalisation.

title: Procurement KPI Dashboard & Performance Reporting
description: Assembles and maintains a real-time procurement performance dashboard — tracking all key procurement KPIs across savings, compliance, supplier performance, cycle times, and sustainability — for CPO and board reporting.

layers.name: KPI Data Aggregation
layers.description: Pulls data from all procurement systems — ERP (PO cycle time, spend), supplier scorecard system (performance), savings tracker (savings), compliance log (policy adherence), and ESG system (sustainability).

layers.name: KPI Computation
layers.description: Calculates all standard procurement KPIs — Cost Savings %, Supplier On-Time Delivery, Invoice Processing Time, Catalogue Adoption, Contract Compliance Rate, Spend Under Management %, and ESG Score.

layers.name: Benchmark Comparison
layers.description: Compares KPIs against industry benchmarks (Hackett Group, APQC) and against prior period performance. Contextualises whether the organisation is best-in-class, average, or below par.

layers.name: Trend Analysis
layers.description: Tracks KPI movements over rolling 12 months. Identifies improving vs. deteriorating metrics. Flags KPIs that have crossed a threshold in either direction.

layers.name: Narrative Generation
layers.description: Drafts plain-English commentary for each KPI — what drove the result, what is improving, what needs attention — ready for CPO board pack without manual effort.

flow.description: KPIs extracted from all procurement systems

title: Category Management Intelligence
description: Provides deep category-level intelligence — spend trends, market dynamics, supplier concentration, savings pipeline, and risk exposure — giving category managers a single source of truth for strategic decisions.

layers.name: Category Spend Profile
layers.description: Aggregates all spend in the category — by supplier, sub-category, business unit, geography, and time period. Computes spend concentration and fragmentation metrics.

layers.name: Market Position Assessment
layers.description: Benchmarks the company's pricing position vs. market — are we paying above or below market? What is our volume significance to key suppliers in this category?

layers.name: Supplier Portfolio Analysis
layers.description: Evaluates the supplier portfolio for the category — number of suppliers, concentration risk, performance distribution, and geographic diversity.

layers.name: Opportunity Sizing
layers.description: Quantifies the total savings and value opportunity in the category — price renegotiation, demand reduction, specification optimisation, consolidation, and process efficiency.

layers.name: Category Strategy Recommendation
layers.description: Based on market attractiveness, supply risk, and savings potential, recommends the appropriate category strategy — leverage, strategic, bottleneck, or routine — with corresponding sourcing approach.

title: Supplier Diversity & Inclusion Tracking
description: Tracks spend with diverse suppliers — MSMEs, women-owned, SC/ST-owned, and local enterprises — ensuring compliance with diversity procurement mandates and supporting inclusive supply chain goals.

layers.name: Diversity Classification
layers.description: Classifies all vendors in the approved vendor list by diversity category — MSME (Micro/Small/Medium), women-owned, minority-owned, social enterprise, startup, local/regional.

layers.name: Spend Tracking
layers.description: Tags all procurement spend against diversity categories. Computes diversity spend as a percentage of total addressable spend — by category, BU, and entity.

layers.name: Mandate Compliance
layers.description: Checks compliance with applicable diversity procurement mandates — Government of India MSME procurement policy (25% minimum), PSU requirements, and customer-contractual diversity commitments.

layers.name: Pipeline Development
layers.description: Identifies categories where diversity supplier options exist but are not being used. Surfaces qualified diverse suppliers for upcoming sourcing events.

layers.name: Reporting
layers.description: Generates diversity spend reports for regulatory submission, ESG disclosure, and customer requirements. Tracks progress against diversity targets.

flow.description: All vendors classified by diversity attribute

title: Procurement Cost Avoidance Tracking
description: Tracks and quantifies cost avoidance achieved by procurement — price increases resisted, specification downgrades avoided, supply disruptions prevented — to capture the full value delivered beyond hard savings.

layers.name: Price Increase Resistance Tracking
layers.description: Monitors supplier price increase requests. For each request that is fully or partially resisted through negotiation, records the cost avoidance — the difference between requested and accepted increase.

layers.name: Market Inflation Benchmark
layers.description: Compares contracted prices against market inflation indices for each category. When inflation is rising and contracts hold prices flat, the delta is tracked as cost avoidance.

layers.name: Supply Disruption Avoidance
layers.description: Quantifies the cost of supply disruptions that were prevented through proactive risk monitoring and dual-sourcing actions — based on scenario modelling of what the disruption would have cost.

layers.name: Specification Optimisation
layers.description: Tracks savings from spec downgrades and standardisation that were resisted (over-spec challenge) — recording the cost if the original spec had been sourced.

layers.name: Finance Reconciliation
layers.description: Reconciles cost avoidance claims with Finance team — ensuring appropriate methodology is accepted and avoidance is included in procurement's value contribution to the P&L.

flow.description: Category inflation vs. contracted price compared

title: Procurement Digital Maturity & Continuous Improvement
description: Assesses the organisation's procurement digital maturity, benchmarks against best-in-class, identifies improvement priorities, and tracks the transformation journey — ensuring continuous capability development.

layers.name: Maturity Assessment
layers.description: Evaluates procurement capabilities across all dimensions — strategy, process, technology, data, talent, and governance — against a structured maturity model (e.g., Gartner Procurement Maturity Model).

layers.name: Benchmark Comparison
layers.description: Compares the organisation's maturity scores against industry peers and best-in-class organisations. Identifies specific capability gaps.

layers.name: Improvement Roadmap
layers.description: Prioritises improvement initiatives by impact vs. effort. Generates a phased transformation roadmap — Quick Wins (0-6 months), Medium Term (6-18 months), and Strategic (18-36 months).

layers.name: Initiative Tracking
layers.description: Tracks progress on each transformation initiative — completion status, KPI improvement achieved, adoption rate, and value realised vs. business case.

layers.name: AI Adoption Measurement
layers.description: Specifically tracks AI and automation adoption across procurement processes — percentage of transactions processed autonomously, human touchpoints eliminated, and decision quality improvement.

title: Competitive Intelligence & Market Monitoring
description:
Continuously monitors the competitive landscape — tracking competitor product launches, pricing changes, feature updates, job postings, patent filings, and investor activity — to inform strategic and product decisions in real time.
  Most R&D teams are surprised by competitor moves that were visible weeks or months in advance — continuous AI monitoring eliminates this blind spot.

layers.name: Competitor Signal Capture
layers.description: Monitors all competitor signal sources — product changelogs, press releases, app store updates, job postings (signalling R&D direction), patent filings, conference talks, and investor reports.

layers.name: Signal Classification
layers.description: Classifies each captured signal — Product Launch, Feature Update, Pricing Change, Market Entry, Partnership, Funding Event, or Key Hire. Rates strategic relevance to the company on a 1-5 scale.

layers.name: Competitive Position Mapping
layers.description: Maintains a live competitive feature matrix — for every key product capability, maps which competitors have it, at what depth, and how the company compares. Updates within 48 hours of any detected change.

layers.name: Trend Synthesis
layers.description: Aggregates signals across competitors to identify industry-wide trends — feature categories gaining investment, pricing model shifts, technology adoption patterns, and geography expansion patterns.

layers.name: Strategic Implication Analysis
layers.description: For high-relevance signals, generates a strategic implication brief — what this means for the company's roadmap, where the company must respond, and where the company has differentiation to defend.

flow.description: Strategic briefs generated for high-relevance signals

title: Customer Feedback & Unmet Needs Analysis
description:
Synthesises all customer feedback signals — support tickets, reviews, sales call notes, NPS verbatim, user research, and social media — into structured insight on unmet needs, pain points, and feature demand that drives product ideation.
  The richest source of product ideas is the customer feedback already flowing into the organisation — AI synthesis converts noise into signal.

layers.name: Multi-Source Feedback Aggregation
layers.description: Aggregates customer feedback from all sources — CS tickets (complaint themes), NPS verbatim, app store reviews, sales win/loss notes, user research reports, social media mentions, and community forum posts.

layers.name: Unmet Need Extraction
layers.description: Applies NLP to identify stated and unstated customer needs — direct feature requests, workarounds customers are using (indicating unmet needs), and problems that cause customers to churn or choose competitors.

layers.name: Need Quantification
layers.description: Quantifies each identified need by frequency (how many customers mention it), customer segment distribution (enterprise vs. SMB vs. consumer), revenue concentration (what % of ARR do customers with this need represent), and urgency.

layers.name: Opportunity Sizing
layers.description: For the top unmet needs, estimates the opportunity — what is the incremental revenue potential if this need is addressed, what is the churn risk if it is not, and what do comparable features cost to build vs. revenue potential.

layers.name: Insight Report
layers.description: Produces a monthly customer insight report — top 10 unmet needs ranked by opportunity, supporting verbatim evidence, customer segment breakdown, and recommended product response.

flow.description: Need frequency and revenue concentration computed
Need distribution by customer segment analysed

title: Technology & Innovation Scouting
description:
Scans the global technology landscape — academic papers, open-source projects, startup ecosystems, patent databases, and tech conferences — to identify emerging technologies relevant to the company's product roadmap before competitors adopt them.
  Technology surprises are as damaging as competitive surprises — systematic scouting ensures the R&D team is always ahead of technology curves.

layers.name: Academic & Research Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors arXiv, Google Scholar, IEEE, ACM, and domain-specific journals for papers relevant to the company's technology areas. Flags papers with high citation velocity and immediate applicability.

layers.name: Open Source Ecosystem Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors GitHub, Hugging Face, and relevant open-source communities for new libraries, models, and frameworks that could accelerate or threaten the company's technology stack.

layers.name: Startup & Venture Signal
layers.description: Monitors startup funding announcements, Y Combinator batches, and venture capital portfolio companies in the company's technology space. Identifies startups that could be acquisition targets or competitive threats.

layers.name: Technology Maturity Assessment
layers.description: For each identified technology, assesses maturity using the Technology Readiness Level (TRL) framework — experimental (TRL 1-3), development (TRL 4-6), or deployment-ready (TRL 7-9). Estimates time-to-relevance.

layers.name: Adoption Recommendation
layers.description: Recommends the company's response for each emerging technology — Monitor (early stage, watch), Experiment (worth a time-boxed spike), Adopt (ready for integration), or Partner/Acquire (strategic enough to build partnership or M&A thesis).

title: Ideation Management & Innovation Pipeline
description:
Manages the end-to-end ideation pipeline — capturing ideas from all sources (employees, customers, hackathons), structuring them for evaluation, scoring them against strategic criteria, and moving the best ideas into the product development process.
  Most companies have no shortage of ideas — they have a shortage of systematic processes to evaluate and develop them. This agent provides that process.

layers.name: Idea Capture
layers.description: Provides a structured intake for ideas from all sources — product managers, engineers, customer success, sales, customers, and external innovation challenges. Standardises idea submissions with a consistent format: problem, proposed solution, target customer, and estimated value.

layers.name: Deduplication & Clustering
layers.description: Deduplicates idea submissions — identifies when multiple submissions are addressing the same underlying problem. Clusters related ideas so reviewers see all perspectives on the same opportunity together.

layers.name: Initial Scoring
layers.description: Applies an initial multi-criteria scoring to each idea — strategic alignment, market size, customer pain severity, technical feasibility, competitive differentiation, and estimated time-to-value. Computes a composite opportunity score.

layers.name: Peer Review Facilitation
layers.description: Routes high-scoring ideas to a cross-functional review panel — PM, Engineering, UX, Finance, and domain experts. Collects structured feedback. Manages the review cycle to completion within 2 weeks.

layers.name: Pipeline Advancement
layers.description: Advances ideas through defined pipeline stages — Submitted, Under Review, Approved for Discovery, In Discovery, Approved for Development, and Released. Tracks the full pipeline with stage-gate criteria.

flow.description: High-score ideas routed to review panel

title: Market Sizing & Opportunity Validation
description:
Validates market opportunities before significant investment — estimating TAM/SAM/SOM, analysing demand signals, benchmarking against analogous markets, and providing a data-backed go/no-go recommendation for new product areas.
  Building products for markets too small or too crowded is a primary cause of R&D waste — market validation before investment prevents this.

layers.name: TAM/SAM/SOM Estimation
layers.description: Estimates the Total Addressable Market (TAM), Serviceable Addressable Market (SAM), and Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM) for the opportunity — using top-down (industry reports, market data) and bottom-up (customer count × ARPU) approaches.

layers.name: Demand Signal Analysis
layers.description: Analyses demand signals — search volume trends (Google Trends, keyword data), App Store category rankings, web traffic patterns, and buying intent signals — to validate that the market is growing and accessible.

layers.name: Competitive Density Assessment
layers.description: Maps the competitive landscape for the opportunity — how many players, what their market share, pricing, and go-to-market. Assesses whether there is a viable white space or the market is over-served.

layers.name: Analogous Market Benchmarking
layers.description: Identifies analogous markets where similar products were launched — what happened to revenue, adoption curve, and competitive response. Uses analogies to calibrate projections.

layers.name: Go/No-Go Recommendation
layers.description: Synthesises the analysis into a structured investment case — market size with confidence intervals, demand evidence, competitive white space, analogous benchmarks, and a go/no-go recommendation with conditions.

title: Product Requirements & Specification Management
description:
Manages the product requirements lifecycle — from raw input through structured PRD (Product Requirements Document) creation, stakeholder alignment, engineering handoff, and change management — ensuring every feature is built to specification.
  Poor requirements are the #1 cause of rework in software development — structured AI-assisted requirements management reduces this significantly.

layers.name: Requirements Intake
layers.description: Captures requirements from all sources — business stakeholders, customer requests, regulatory mandates, and technical constraints. Standardises into a structured format: user story, acceptance criteria, success metric, and priority.

layers.name: Ambiguity Detection
layers.description: Analyses each requirement for ambiguity, incompleteness, and untestability. Flags requirements that say 'fast', 'easy', 'better', or 'as needed' without quantitative definition. Routes for clarification before engineering begins.

layers.name: Dependency Mapping
layers.description: Identifies dependencies between requirements — which features require other features first, which share a common backend component, and which conflict with each other. Surfaces dependency risks before sprint planning.

layers.name: PRD Assembly
layers.description: Assembles the full Product Requirements Document — combining structured requirements, user journey maps, wireframe references, success metrics, edge cases, and non-functional requirements (performance, security, accessibility).

layers.name: Change Management
layers.description: When requirements change post-sign-off, assesses the impact — which components are affected, what is the development effort delta, and what is the timeline impact. Provides a structured change request with impact analysis.

title: Product Roadmap Planning & Prioritisation
description:
Builds and maintains the product roadmap — applying rigorous prioritisation frameworks, balancing short-term delivery against long-term strategy, and providing CPO and stakeholders with a defensible, data-driven view of what will be built and why.
  Roadmap decisions made by gut instinct or loudest stakeholder voice consistently misallocate engineering capacity — data-driven prioritisation maximises impact per engineering hour.

layers.name: Item Universe
layers.description: Maintains the full backlog of roadmap candidates — new features, technical debt, infrastructure, regulatory requirements, and platform improvements. Each item has an owner, estimated effort, and linked business case.

layers.name: Prioritisation Scoring
layers.description: Scores each backlog item using a multi-dimensional framework — RICE (Reach × Impact × Confidence ÷ Effort), strategic alignment score, customer demand signal (frequency, revenue concentration), and urgency (SLA or regulatory deadline).

layers.name: Constraint Modelling
layers.description: Models resource constraints — available engineering sprints per quarter, design capacity, QA capacity, and infrastructure budget. Determines what is feasible within the planning horizon.

layers.name: Roadmap Scenario Modelling
layers.description: Generates multiple roadmap scenarios — 'Customer Focus' (prioritise high-demand user features), 'Platform' (prioritise technical foundation), 'Revenue' (prioritise direct revenue drivers). Compares outcomes across scenarios.

layers.name: Roadmap Communication
layers.description: Produces the approved roadmap in formats for different audiences — strategic themes for board, initiative-level for leadership, feature-level for engineering, and public-facing roadmap for customers.

title: User Research & Discovery Management
description:
Designs, executes, and synthesises user research — interviews, usability tests, surveys, and ethnographic studies — providing the evidence base for product decisions and replacing assumption-driven development with insight-driven development.
  Products built without user research fail 40% more often at launch — systematic research de-risks every major product decision.

layers.name: Research Agenda Planning
layers.description: Determines which product questions most need research evidence — based on current roadmap decisions under uncertainty, hypothesis backlog, and areas with the highest decision risk. Prioritises research sprints.

layers.name: Research Method Selection
layers.description: For each research question, selects the most appropriate method — generative (interviews, ethnography) for exploring unknown needs, evaluative (usability testing, A/B) for validating specific designs, and quantitative (survey) for sizing and segmenting findings.

layers.name: Participant Recruitment
layers.description: Identifies and recruits research participants — matching the target customer segment, screening for relevant experience, and managing scheduling, incentives, and consent.

layers.name: Research Synthesis
layers.description: Synthesises research findings — identifies themes across sessions using affinity mapping, quantifies finding frequency, and creates user journey maps and personas updated with new evidence.

layers.name: Research Repository
layers.description: Maintains a searchable research repository — all research reports, session recordings, and synthesised insights tagged by product area and research question. Prevents repeat research on questions already answered.

flow.description: Research findings linked to product decisions

title: Design & Prototyping Workflow Management
description:
Manages the product design process — from design brief through wireframes, high-fidelity mockups, prototypes, and design handoff to engineering — ensuring design quality, consistency, and alignment with the product vision.
  Design debt and inconsistency accumulate quickly without systematic management — structured design workflows maintain quality at scale.

layers.name: Design Brief Generation
layers.description: Converts the approved PRD into a design brief — problem statement, user goals, constraints, success criteria, and competitive reference examples. Provides context for the design team without prescribing the solution.

layers.name: Design System Compliance
layers.description: Checks all design work against the company's design system — components, typography, colour palette, spacing, and accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1 AA). Flags deviations before engineering receives the spec.

layers.name: Prototype Management
layers.description: Manages the prototype lifecycle — low-fidelity (concept validation), medium-fidelity (usability testing), and high-fidelity (engineering handoff). Tracks which prototype version is current and links to research results.

layers.name: Design Review Facilitation
layers.description: Schedules and structures design reviews — critique sessions with PM, engineering lead, and stakeholders. Captures review feedback and tracks revisions to resolution.

layers.name: Engineering Handoff
layers.description: Manages the design-to-engineering handoff — ensuring specs are complete (measurements, states, interactions, edge cases), Figma links are final, and engineering has a single source of truth for all design assets.

title: Sprint Planning & Delivery Management
description:
Supports agile sprint planning — converting the roadmap into sprint-ready stories, managing sprint capacity, tracking delivery velocity, and identifying delivery risks before they become sprint failures.
  Sprint planning quality determines whether teams deliver what they planned — AI assistance improves accuracy and consistency.

layers.name: Story Decomposition
layers.description: Decomposes roadmap features into sprint-sized user stories — each story independently valuable, testable within a sprint, and estimated in story points. Flags stories that are too large or too vague for sprint inclusion.

layers.name: Sprint Capacity Modelling
layers.description: Computes sprint capacity — team velocity (rolling 6-sprint average), planned leave, ceremonies overhead, and any known capacity reduction. Ensures sprint commitment is realistic.

layers.name: Dependency Resolution
layers.description: Identifies cross-team or cross-sprint dependencies within the planned sprint. Flags stories that cannot start until another team or story completes. Surfaces blocking dependencies before the sprint begins.

layers.name: In-Sprint Risk Monitoring
layers.description: During the sprint, monitors progress against the sprint plan — story completion rate vs. time elapsed, blockers reported, and burndown trajectory. Predicts sprint shortfall 3+ days before sprint end.

layers.name: Sprint Retrospective Support
layers.description: After each sprint, synthesises retrospective data — velocity trend, blockers frequency, estimation accuracy, and quality metrics. Identifies systemic improvement areas for the engineering team.

title: Product Backlog Management & Grooming
description:
Maintains a healthy, prioritised, and always-ready product backlog — continuously grooming items, retiring stale ones, re-estimating aged items, and ensuring the backlog is a reliable planning tool rather than a dumping ground.
  An ungroomed backlog is a trust-eroding liability — teams that don't trust the backlog stop planning from it, creating even more waste.

layers.name: Staleness Detection
layers.description: Identifies backlog items that have not been touched in >90 days — flags for review, potential archival, or re-prioritisation. A stale item is likely either no longer relevant or needs a champion to refresh the case.

layers.name: Estimation Currency
layers.description: Detects backlog items whose estimates are stale — items estimated >6 months ago in a codebase that has changed significantly. Triggers re-estimation before sprint inclusion.

layers.name: Acceptance Criteria Completeness
layers.description: Checks all ready-for-sprint items for acceptance criteria completeness — items without clear acceptance criteria are flagged as not sprint-ready and routed back to PM.

layers.name: Grooming Meeting Support
layers.description: Prepares the backlog grooming agenda — selects the right items for grooming based on upcoming sprint priorities, staleness, and re-estimation need. Captures decisions and updates during the session.

layers.name: Backlog Health Metrics
layers.description: Tracks backlog health indicators — % of items with acceptance criteria, % with current estimates, backlog age distribution, and ready-for-sprint ratio. Provides PM with a backlog health score.

title: A/B Testing & Experimentation Platform Management
description:
Manages the company's experimentation programme — designing statistically rigorous A/B tests, tracking experiment health, analysing results, and converting winning experiments into product decisions with appropriate confidence.
  Companies with mature experimentation programmes make better product decisions at lower cost — but bad experiments waste engineering time and lead to wrong conclusions.

layers.name: Experiment Design
layers.description: Designs each A/B test with statistical rigour — defines the hypothesis, primary metric, guardrail metrics, sample size requirement (based on minimum detectable effect and statistical power), and target runtime to reach significance.

layers.name: Assignment & Consistency
layers.description: Manages experiment assignment — ensures consistent user experience (same user always in same variant), correct randomisation, and avoidance of experiment interaction effects (multi-experiment conflicts).

layers.name: Health Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors live experiments in real time — checks for Sample Ratio Mismatch (SRM), unexpected metric movements in guardrail metrics, and any technical issues with variant implementation. Pauses experiments if serious issues are detected.

layers.name: Results Analysis
layers.description: Analyses experiment results — computes statistical significance, effect size with confidence intervals, and segment breakdown (does the result differ by device, geography, or user cohort?). Flags p-hacking risk if multiple metrics are tested.

layers.name: Decision Support
layers.description: Translates results into a clear decision recommendation — ship (significant positive on primary metric, no guardrail degradation), iterate (mixed results), hold (needs more data), or kill (significant negative or no effect).

title: Applied Research & Proof-of-Concept Management
description:
Manages the applied research pipeline — coordinating PoC sprints, tracking hypothesis testing, managing research engineer time, and deciding which PoCs advance to production development vs. which are valuable learnings but not product-ready.
  Applied research without structure produces interesting demos but few shipped products — systematic PoC management converts research investment into product value.

layers.name: PoC Scoping
layers.description: Defines the scope of each proof of concept — the specific hypothesis being tested, the success criteria (what results would justify further investment), the time box (typically 2-6 weeks), and the resource allocation.

layers.name: Research Question Clarity
layers.description: Ensures every PoC has a clear, falsifiable research question — not 'can we build X?' but 'can we achieve Y accuracy at Z latency with N training data?'. Prevents open-ended research sprints.

layers.name: Progress Tracking
layers.description: Monitors PoC progress against the time box — are the experiments proceeding, what are the intermediate results, and is the team on track to answer the research question within the allocated time?

layers.name: Results Documentation
layers.description: Captures PoC results in a standardised format — hypothesis, methodology, results (with supporting data), conclusions, and recommendation (advance/pivot/shelve). Prevents knowledge loss when engineers move to other projects.

layers.name: Advancement Decision
layers.description: After each PoC, facilitates a structured go/no-go decision — do the results justify production investment? What are the remaining technical risks? What is the estimated production build effort?

title: Data Science & ML Model Lifecycle Management
description:
Manages the ML model lifecycle — from problem framing through data preparation, model training, evaluation, deployment, and monitoring — ensuring models are reliable, fair, explainable, and continuously improving.
  ML models degrade silently in production and can cause customer harm — systematic lifecycle management prevents this.

layers.name: Problem Framing & Feasibility
layers.description: Frames the ML problem precisely — prediction target, input features, evaluation metric, business success threshold, and constraints (latency, explainability, fairness). Assesses feasibility based on data availability.

layers.name: Data Quality & Preparation
layers.description: Analyses training data quality — missing values, distribution shifts, label quality, class imbalance, and data leakage risks. Prepares the data pipeline with versioning and reproducibility.

layers.name: Model Training & Evaluation
layers.description: Manages the model training process — experiment tracking (MLflow or equivalent), hyperparameter tuning, cross-validation, and evaluation against business-relevant metrics. Compares model versions.

layers.name: Fairness & Explainability
layers.description: Evaluates model fairness across demographic groups — detects disparate impact. Generates model explainability outputs (SHAP values, LIME) for regulated use cases and customer-facing decisions.

layers.name: Production Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors deployed models in production — data drift (input distribution changes), model drift (output distribution changes), and performance degradation against business metrics. Triggers retraining when degradation is detected.

title: Scientific Research & Literature Management
description:
Manages the company's scientific research activities — paper monitoring, internal research publication, conference participation, and academic collaboration — keeping the R&D team at the frontier of relevant scientific knowledge.
  R&D teams that don't systematically track scientific literature miss innovations that could accelerate their work by years.

layers.name: Literature Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors academic literature in the company's research domains — arXiv daily updates, journal alerts, and citation tracking. Reads abstracts and flags papers with high relevance scores for researcher review.

layers.name: Paper Digest
layers.description: For flagged papers, generates a structured digest — what problem the paper addresses, what method is used, key results with numbers, limitations, and a 1-paragraph implication for the company's research agenda.

layers.name: Research Replication Assessment
layers.description: For highly relevant papers, assesses replication feasibility — can the company replicate the experiment with available compute and data? Prepares a replication proposal for the research team.

layers.name: Internal Research Publication
layers.description: Manages the internal research publication process — technical report formatting, peer review, internal knowledge base publication, and where appropriate, external conference/journal submission.

layers.name: Academic Collaboration Management
layers.description: Manages relationships with university labs and research partners — track joint projects, grant applications, data sharing agreements, and co-publication timelines.

title: User Testing & Validation Management
description:
Manages structured user testing across the product development lifecycle — prototype tests, usability tests, beta programmes, and dogfooding — ensuring user feedback shapes the product before, not after, launch.
  User testing is the most cost-effective form of quality assurance — it costs 100x more to fix a usability problem after launch than during development.

layers.name: Testing Programme Planning
layers.description: Plans the testing programme for each product — which testing methods at which development stage, sample sizes, participant criteria, and what decisions each test will inform.

layers.name: Usability Test Management
layers.description: Manages moderated and unmoderated usability tests — recruitment, test environment setup, task scripting, session facilitation support, recording, and post-session analysis.

layers.name: Beta Programme Management
layers.description: Manages closed beta programmes — participant selection (matching target user profiles), onboarding, feedback collection (in-app, structured surveys, and interview recruitment), and bug reporting.

layers.name: Results Synthesis
layers.description: Synthesises testing results across participants — identifies consistent usability issues (appear in 3+ participants), quantifies task completion rates, and maps friction points in the user journey.

layers.name: Issue Tracking Integration
layers.description: Creates design and engineering tickets directly from usability issues — with severity (critical: prevents task completion; high: causes significant struggle; medium: minor friction), video clip evidence, and recommended fix.

title: Engineering Velocity & Technical Debt Management
description:
Tracks engineering productivity, identifies technical debt accumulation, and manages the balance between feature development and technical investment — ensuring the codebase remains healthy and engineering velocity is maintained.
  Technical debt compounds silently — by the time it becomes a crisis, the cost to fix is 5-10x what it would have been with early management.

layers.name: Velocity Tracking
layers.description: Measures engineering velocity per team — story points delivered vs. committed, cycle time (in-progress to done), lead time (backlog to done), and deployment frequency. Tracks trend over rolling 12 sprints.

layers.name: Technical Debt Identification
layers.description: Reads code quality metrics — cyclomatic complexity, code coverage, duplication ratio, code smell count (SonarQube or equivalent), and architectural debt flags. Trends this data to identify accumulation.

layers.name: Debt Cost Estimation
layers.description: Estimates the cost of technical debt — both the cost to pay it down (remediation effort) and the cost of leaving it (estimated drag on future velocity, incident risk, and onboarding difficulty).

layers.name: Debt Prioritisation
layers.description: Prioritises technical debt items — by cost-to-fix vs. cost-of-leaving, business risk of the affected component, and proximity to active development (debt in heavily-developed components is more urgent).

layers.name: Velocity/Debt Balance Management
layers.description: Recommends the optimal sprint allocation between feature work and debt reduction — typically the 20% rule for stable teams, more for teams experiencing velocity decline. Tracks impact of debt reduction on velocity.

title: Code Review & Quality Assurance Automation
description:
Augments the code review process — automated static analysis, security scanning, test coverage checks, and AI-assisted review comments — reducing review time while improving code quality and catching issues before they reach production.
  Manual code review is a bottleneck and quality floor, not a ceiling — AI augmentation speeds review and consistently catches a broader class of issues.

layers.name: Static Analysis
layers.description: Runs comprehensive static analysis on every pull request — code style, complexity, potential bugs, null pointer dereferences, resource leaks, and common antipatterns. Reports findings in line with the PR for immediate developer action.

layers.name: Security Scanning
layers.description: Scans for security vulnerabilities — OWASP Top 10, dependency vulnerabilities (CVE database), secrets in code (API keys, passwords), SQL injection risks, and insecure deserialization. Blocks PRs with Critical security findings.

layers.name: Test Coverage Analysis
layers.description: Checks test coverage for the changed code — flags PRs that reduce overall coverage below threshold, identifies untested code paths in high-risk components, and suggests specific test cases for edge conditions.

layers.name: AI Review Comments
layers.description: Generates contextual review suggestions — not just rule violations but logic improvements, performance optimisations, and architectural concerns. Trained on the company's own codebase patterns.

layers.name: Review Workflow
layers.description: Manages the review assignment (routes PRs to reviewers with relevant expertise), tracks review SLAs (no PR awaiting review for >24 hours), and detects PRs stuck in review.

title: Release Management & Deployment Orchestration
description:
Manages the software release process — from release planning through staging deployment, release gate checks, production deployment, rollback capability, and post-release monitoring — ensuring every release is controlled and reversible.
  Uncontrolled deployments are the #1 cause of production incidents — structured release management dramatically reduces deployment-related outages.

layers.name: Release Planning
layers.description: Builds the release plan — which features and bug fixes are included, deployment dependencies, required testing completion gates, stakeholder communication requirements, and rollback plan.

layers.name: Release Gate Checks
layers.description: Enforces release gates before production deployment — test coverage threshold, performance benchmark results, security scan clean, accessibility audit pass, and product manager sign-off. No deployment without all gates green.

layers.name: Staged Deployment
layers.description: Manages staged rollout — canary (1% of traffic), progressive (10%, 25%, 50%), and full rollout. Monitors key metrics at each stage before proceeding. Automatic rollback if error rate or latency exceeds threshold.

layers.name: Feature Flag Management
layers.description: Manages feature flags — enabling/disabling features without deployment, controlling rollout by user segment, and A/B test flag management. Prevents flag debt accumulation.

layers.name: Post-Release Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors post-release system health — error rate, latency, business metrics (conversion, engagement), and user-reported issues. Detects regressions within 15 minutes and triggers rollback if needed.

flow.description: Automatic rollback triggered on threshold breach

title: API & Integration Management
description:
Manages the company's API and integration ecosystem — API design standards, versioning, documentation, partner integration management, and API health monitoring — ensuring a reliable, well-documented API surface for internal and external consumers.
  Poor API management causes developer frustration, integration failures, and partner attrition — systematic management builds the ecosystem flywheel.

layers.name: API Design Review
layers.description: Reviews new API designs against the company's API standards — RESTful conventions, naming consistency, error response standardisation, pagination patterns, and authentication requirements. Flags non-compliant designs before implementation.

layers.name: API Documentation
layers.description: Generates and maintains API documentation — auto-generates OpenAPI specs from code annotations, produces human-readable API guides, and maintains sandbox environments for developer testing.

layers.name: API Versioning
layers.description: Manages API versioning — enforces deprecation policies (90-day notice minimum), tracks API consumer usage by version, and coordinates migration campaigns when old versions are retired.

layers.name: Integration Health Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors all external integrations in production — API response times, error rates, authentication failures, and SLA compliance. Alerts on-call when integration health degrades.

layers.name: Partner Developer Experience
layers.description: Manages the partner developer experience — onboarding documentation, API key provisioning, developer support (tickets), and partner usage analytics. Identifies high-value partners for deeper integration investment.

title: Security & Compliance by Design
description:
Embeds security and compliance requirements into the development process — threat modelling, OWASP testing, privacy impact assessments, and compliance validation — ensuring products are secure and compliant before they launch, not after.
  Security added after development is 10x more expensive and never as thorough — security by design is both better and cheaper.

layers.name: Threat Modelling
layers.description: Conducts threat modelling for new features and architecture changes — identifies attack vectors (STRIDE methodology), rates risk (CVSS scoring), and specifies security controls required before development begins.

layers.name: Security Requirements
layers.description: Translates threat model findings into specific security requirements — authentication, authorisation, input validation, encryption, session management, and audit logging. These are non-negotiable PRD additions.

layers.name: Privacy Impact Assessment
layers.description: For features that process personal data, conducts a DPDP/GDPR-aligned Privacy Impact Assessment — data minimisation, purpose limitation, retention schedule, consent mechanism, and third-party data flows.

layers.name: Pre-Launch Security Testing
layers.description: Conducts DAST (Dynamic Application Security Testing) and targeted penetration testing on new features before launch. Severity-gates deployment on Critical and High findings.

layers.name: Compliance Validation
layers.description: Validates compliance requirements are implemented correctly — regulatory technical controls (PCI DSS for payments, HIPAA for health, SOC2 for enterprise), and internal security policies.

title: Product Launch Planning & Execution
description:
Plans and orchestrates product and feature launches — coordinating all launch activities across product, engineering, marketing, sales, CS, and legal — from launch readiness assessment through post-launch monitoring.
  Launches without coordination fail even when the product is excellent — systematic launch management maximises the impact of every release.

layers.name: Launch Readiness Assessment
layers.description: Evaluates launch readiness across all dimensions — product completeness, engineering quality gates, documentation, CS training, marketing materials, legal review, and pricing approval. Produces a launch readiness score with gap analysis.

layers.name: Launch Timing Optimisation
layers.description: Analyses launch timing factors — competitor activity, marketing calendar, customer seasonality, sales cycle alignment, and technical readiness. Recommends the optimal launch window with supporting rationale.

layers.name: Launch Plan Coordination
layers.description: Builds and tracks the launch plan — cross-functional task list with owners, due dates, and dependencies. Manages the launch readiness review meeting with all stakeholders.

layers.name: Beta-to-GA Transition
layers.description: Manages the transition from closed beta to General Availability — beta customer graduation, pricing activation, public documentation release, and support team enablement.

layers.name: Launch Day Monitoring
layers.description: On launch day, monitors adoption metrics (sign-ups, activations, first use events), technical health (error rates, performance), and customer feedback signals (CS tickets, social mentions) in real time.

title: Pricing Strategy & Monetisation Management
description:
Manages product pricing decisions — analysing willingness-to-pay, competitive pricing, value-based pricing modelling, and pricing experiment design — ensuring pricing maximises revenue while supporting product adoption.
  Pricing is the highest-leverage go-to-market decision — a 1% improvement in pricing typically improves profit by 8-11% — yet most product companies under-invest in pricing rigour.

layers.name: Willingness-to-Pay Research
layers.description: Designs and analyses willingness-to-pay research — Van Westendorp price sensitivity meter, Conjoint analysis, and direct questioning surveys. Identifies the acceptable price range and optimal price point for each customer segment.

layers.name: Competitive Pricing Analysis
layers.description: Tracks competitor pricing — list prices, packaging, discount patterns, and promotional pricing. Maps where the company's pricing sits on the value-to-price matrix relative to each competitor.

layers.name: Value Metric Identification
layers.description: Identifies the right value metric for the pricing model — what unit of value scales with customer success (seats, usage, outcomes, revenue processed). Analyses customer data to find the metric most correlated with value delivered.

layers.name: Pricing Experiment Design
layers.description: Designs pricing A/B tests — different price points, packaging variations, or discount structures — with statistical rigour. Monitors conversion, ARPU, and churn impact at each variant.

layers.name: Pricing Decision Support
layers.description: Synthesises research, competitive data, and experiment results into a pricing recommendation brief for the CPO and CFO — with revenue model, sensitivity analysis, and implementation requirements.

title: Sales & CS Enablement
description:
Ensures sales teams and customer success have everything they need to sell and support new products — product training, competitive battlecards, demo environments, objection handling guides, and implementation playbooks — always current and accessible.
  Products fail in the market not because they are bad but because the sales team doesn't understand them — systematic enablement maximises commercial impact of every R&D investment.

layers.name: Enablement Content Creation
layers.description: Creates all required sales and CS enablement content — product overview, feature benefits (in customer language, not technical spec), competitive comparison, common objections, discovery questions, and implementation guide.

layers.name: Demo Environment Management
layers.description: Maintains up-to-date, scenario-specific demo environments — pre-loaded with compelling data, scenarios tailored to each industry vertical, and always reflecting the latest product version.

layers.name: Training Delivery
layers.description: Manages sales and CS training programmes — live sessions for major launches, asynchronous modules for incremental features, and certification tests to verify knowledge. Tracks completion and scores.

layers.name: Content Currency Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors all enablement content for staleness — detects when product updates make sales materials inaccurate. Triggers update workflow and ensures sales never uses outdated positioning.

layers.name: Enablement Effectiveness
layers.description: Tracks enablement effectiveness — training completion rates, sales win rates before and after training, deal size, and time-to-close for trained vs. untrained reps. Identifies enablement content that drives outcomes.

title: Partner & Ecosystem Integration Management
description:
Manages the product's partner ecosystem — integration partner management, marketplace listing, developer programme, and co-sell partnerships — growing the ecosystem to expand product reach and value.
  Partner ecosystems can multiply a product's market reach 5-10x — systematic management is required to realise this value.

layers.name: Partner Opportunity Identification
layers.description: Identifies integration and partnership opportunities — technology partners (complementary tools), distribution partners (channels with access to target customers), and OEM partners (embed the product in their offering).

layers.name: Integration Prioritisation
