layers.description: Prioritises integration builds based on customer demand (how often do customers request this integration), partner reciprocity (does the partner promote the integration), and strategic fit.

layers.name: Partner Onboarding
layers.description: Manages the technical and commercial partner onboarding process — API access, sandbox environment, integration documentation, and go-to-market agreement. Tracks progress to first integration live.

layers.name: Marketplace Listing Management
layers.description: Manages all marketplace listings — Salesforce AppExchange, Atlassian Marketplace, Microsoft AppSource, and company's own marketplace. Keeps listings current, rating management, and review response.

layers.name: Ecosystem Health Analytics
layers.description: Measures ecosystem health — active integrations, integration usage by customers, partner-sourced leads, co-sell revenue, and integration NPS. Identifies high-value partners for deeper investment.

title: Product Usage Analytics & Feature Adoption
description:
Instruments and analyses product usage data — feature adoption funnels, user engagement patterns, session analysis, and feature performance — giving PMs evidence on what users actually do vs. what they say they do.
  Usage analytics replace opinion with evidence in product decisions — the team that measures learns faster and builds better products.

layers.name: Instrumentation Management
layers.description: Manages product instrumentation — event taxonomy design, tracking implementation verification, data quality monitoring, and analytics SDK management. Ensures every feature is measurable before and after launch.

layers.name: Feature Adoption Measurement
layers.description: Measures adoption for every feature — Day 1, Day 7, Day 30 adoption rates by user segment and cohort. Compares to benchmarks and launch forecasts. Flags features with low adoption for investigation.

layers.name: Funnel Analysis
layers.description: Builds and monitors conversion funnels — onboarding funnel (sign-up to activation), feature discovery funnel (feature available to feature used), and value realisation funnel (first use to repeated use). Identifies high-drop-off steps.

layers.name: Engagement Segmentation
layers.description: Segments users by engagement level — Power Users, Regular Users, Occasional Users, and At-Risk (declining usage). For each segment, identifies what drives them up or down the engagement ladder.

layers.name: Product-Led Growth Signals
layers.description: Identifies product-led growth signals — viral loops (users inviting other users), expansion signals (approaching plan limits), and NPS-correlated usage patterns. Feeds into growth and sales strategies.

title: Customer Health Score & Retention Analytics
description:
Computes and monitors customer health scores — combining product usage, support activity, NPS, and commercial signals — to identify customers at risk of churn before they decide to leave.
  Customer churn is largely predictable from product usage signals — detecting it 90 days in advance allows intervention that retains 30-40% of at-risk customers.

layers.name: Health Score Model
layers.description: Builds the customer health score model — combining product usage depth (features used, frequency, breadth), support health (CS ticket volume and tone), satisfaction signals (NPS, CSAT), and commercial signals (contract utilisation, expansion activity).

layers.name: Health Signal Weighting
layers.description: Weights health signals based on their predictive power for churn — validates weights against historical churn data. Ensures the model gets better over time as more churn outcomes are observed.

layers.name: Churn Prediction
layers.description: Applies the health score model to predict churn probability at 30, 60, and 90 day horizons. Segments customers into health tiers — Green (healthy), Yellow (monitor), Orange (at-risk), Red (critical churn risk).

layers.name: Leading Indicator Monitoring
layers.description: Identifies the leading indicators that predict churn 90+ days out — login frequency decline, feature abandonment, team contraction, executive champion departure. These are the early warning signals.

layers.name: Retention Action Triggers
layers.description: For at-risk customers, triggers specific retention actions — CS outreach, PM champion call, feature adoption support, or commercial intervention. Tracks action completion and outcome.

title: Revenue & Business Metrics Dashboard
description:
Maintains the canonical product revenue and business metrics dashboard — ARR, MRR, NRR, churn, DAU/MAU, activation rate, and time-to-value — providing the CPO, CEO, and board with a consistent, trusted view of product performance.
  Metric inconsistency between teams destroys decision-making trust — a single, agreed, automated metrics layer solves this.

layers.name: Metric Definitions
layers.description: Maintains the canonical definitions for all product business metrics — how ARR is calculated, what counts as an activated user, how NRR is computed, and what the DAU/MAU ratio includes. Single source of truth.

layers.name: Data Pipeline
layers.description: Manages the data pipeline feeding the metrics dashboard — from raw product event data, billing data, and CRM data through transformation, validation, and loading. Monitors data freshness and quality.

layers.name: Metric Computation
layers.description: Computes all business metrics on the agreed cadence — daily for operational metrics, weekly for growth metrics, monthly for financial metrics. Validates computed values against expected ranges.

layers.name: Trend & Anomaly Analysis
layers.description: Tracks metric trends — week-over-week, month-over-month, and year-over-year. Detects anomalies (metrics that deviate significantly from trend) and alerts the CPO within 4 hours of detection.

layers.name: Board & Investor Reporting
layers.description: Produces the monthly board pack metrics section — formatted to board standards, with prior-period comparison, commentary on key movements, and forward-looking guidance update.

title: Product Quality & Reliability Engineering
description:
Manages product quality and reliability — SLAs, incident management, error budgets, performance monitoring, and reliability targets — ensuring the product delivers on its availability and performance promises to customers.
  A product that doesn't work reliably destroys trust faster than any feature can build it — systematic reliability engineering protects customer trust.

layers.name: SLA Definition & Monitoring
layers.description: Defines SLA commitments per product tier (e.g., 99.9% availability for Enterprise, 99.5% for Standard). Monitors real-time availability against SLA. Tracks monthly uptime and flags at-risk SLA months in advance.

layers.name: Error Budget Management
layers.description: Implements error budget tracking — available error budget per rolling 30 days, error budget burn rate, and error budget policy (freeze feature deployments when budget is 20% remaining).

layers.name: Performance Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors product performance — API response times by endpoint, database query performance, client-side load times, and mobile app performance. Sets performance budgets per endpoint.

layers.name: Incident Management
layers.description: Manages production incidents — detection, severity classification (P0/P1/P2), on-call escalation, incident bridge, customer communication, and post-incident review (PIR).

layers.name: Post-Incident Review
layers.description: After every P0 and P1 incident, manages the blameless post-incident review — timeline reconstruction, root cause analysis (5 Whys), contributing factors, and action items with owners and deadlines.

title: Product Localisation & Global Readiness
description:
Manages product localisation — translation, cultural adaptation, regional compliance, and market-specific features — ensuring the product is competitive in every market the company serves.
  Many companies lose 20-30% of potential international revenue because localisation is poor or inconsistently managed.

layers.name: Localisation Scope Management
layers.description: For each new release, identifies all content requiring localisation — UI strings, documentation, marketing materials, and in-product help. Generates the localisation work order with word counts, context, and deadline.

layers.name: Translation Quality
layers.description: Manages the translation workflow — vendor management or internal localisers, quality review by native speakers, terminology consistency (translation memory), and glossary management.

layers.name: Regional Compliance
layers.description: Identifies and manages region-specific compliance requirements — data residency, right-to-left language support, local payment methods, regional date/currency/number formats, and regulatory requirements (e.g., EU AI Act, India data localisation).

layers.name: Cultural Adaptation
layers.description: Reviews UI and content for cultural appropriateness — imagery, colours, metaphors, and examples that may not translate culturally. Involves regional advisors for key markets.

layers.name: Localisation Quality Assurance
layers.description: Tests localised builds — truncation, text overflow, RTL layout, regional format rendering, and functional testing with local data. Tracks localisation quality score per market.

title: R&D Portfolio Management & Investment Allocation
description:
Manages the R&D investment portfolio — balancing investment across innovation horizons (H1 core product, H2 adjacencies, H3 breakthrough), tracking ROI of R&D spending, and ensuring investment allocation is aligned to strategy.
  R&D portfolios without active management drift toward the comfortable and familiar — losing the breakthrough potential that justifies R&D investment.

layers.name: Portfolio Categorisation
layers.description: Categorises all R&D investment across three horizons — Horizon 1 (sustain and extend current products), Horizon 2 (build adjacent opportunities), and Horizon 3 (create breakthrough new capabilities). Tracks actual spend vs. target allocation.

layers.name: Investment Tracking
layers.description: Tracks all R&D investments at the project level — committed engineering time, third-party costs, infrastructure costs, and opportunity cost. Reconciles against the approved R&D budget monthly.

layers.name: ROI Measurement
layers.description: Measures the ROI of completed R&D investments — revenue generated or enabled, cost savings, competitive advantage preserved, and strategic optionality created. Connects investment to outcome.

layers.name: Portfolio Rebalancing
layers.description: Monitors allocation drift — when H1 crowd out H2 and H3 due to short-term commercial pressure. Flags rebalancing recommendations to CPO and CTO when allocation deviates more than 15% from target.

layers.name: Investment Gate Reviews
layers.description: Facilitates quarterly investment gate reviews — for each active R&D project, reviews progress vs. plan, updated ROI forecast, and strategic relevance. Recommends Continue, Accelerate, Pivot, or Kill.

title: Engineering Capacity Planning & Headcount Management
description:
Plans engineering headcount requirements — mapping roadmap ambition to capacity gaps, building the hiring plan, managing contractor and partner resourcing, and maintaining optimal team size and composition.
  Roadmaps that outrun capacity generate burnout and missed commitments — systematic capacity planning prevents both.

layers.name: Capacity Demand Forecasting
layers.description: Translates the product roadmap into engineering capacity demand — story point estimates, team composition requirements (frontend, backend, ML, DevOps), and timing. Produces a 12-month demand forecast by skill type.

layers.name: Current Capacity Assessment
layers.description: Assesses current engineering capacity — headcount by skill, average velocity per engineer, attrition risk (flight risk score), and current utilisation. Computes available capacity for the next 12 months.

layers.name: Gap Analysis
layers.description: Computes the gap between demand and supply — by skill, by quarter. Determines whether the gap should be closed by hiring, contractor augmentation, or roadmap scope reduction.

layers.name: Hiring Plan
layers.description: Builds the hiring plan — roles required, timing, expected time-to-fill, and budget. Prioritises hiring by skill criticality and lead time. Tracks hiring pipeline against the plan.

layers.name: Team Structure Optimisation
layers.description: Analyses engineering team structure — team sizes, domain ownership, cross-team dependency frequency, and Conway's Law alignment with the product architecture. Recommends team restructuring when needed.

title: Vendor & Technology Partner Management
description:
Manages R&D's vendor and technology partner relationships — SaaS tools, cloud providers, open-source dependencies, and technology licenses — optimising cost, managing risk, and ensuring the technology stack remains current.
  Technology vendor sprawl and unmanaged dependencies are a significant cost and risk for R&D teams — systematic management reduces both.

layers.name: Technology Inventory
layers.description: Maintains a complete inventory of all R&D tools and dependencies — SaaS subscriptions, cloud services, open-source libraries with licences, and commercial SDKs. Tracks cost, usage, and owner.

layers.name: Licence Compliance
layers.description: Monitors open-source licence compliance — identifies GPL, LGPL, and AGPL dependencies in commercial products. Flags licence conflicts. Manages CLA (Contributor Licence Agreement) for open-source contributions.

layers.name: Cost Optimisation
layers.description: Analyses cloud and SaaS spend — identifies unused resources, over-provisioned services, and opportunities for reserved instance or committed use discounts. Reports savings opportunities to CTO and CFO.

layers.name: Dependency Risk Management
layers.description: Monitors critical dependencies for risk — end-of-life dates, security vulnerability exposure (CVE tracking for all dependencies), and single-vendor concentration risk.

layers.name: Vendor Performance
layers.description: Tracks key vendor SLAs — cloud provider uptime, API availability, support response times. Manages vendor escalations and credits for SLA misses.

title: R&D Metrics, OKRs & Performance Management
description:
Manages R&D team OKRs, tracks key R&D metrics, facilitates performance management for engineering and product teams, and provides the CPO and CTO with data-driven visibility of R&D function health.
  R&D teams that are not measured cannot be improved — and cannot justify their investment to the business.

layers.name: OKR Management
layers.description: Facilitates quarterly OKR setting for R&D — company OKRs cascade to R&D objectives and then to team and individual key results. Tracks weekly progress updates. Flags off-track OKRs 4 weeks before quarter end.

layers.name: Engineering Metrics
layers.description: Tracks the DORA metrics (Deployment Frequency, Lead Time for Changes, MTTR, Change Failure Rate) and product quality metrics. Benchmarks against industry standards.

layers.name: Product Metrics
layers.description: Tracks product success metrics — feature adoption rate, product NPS, DAU/MAU ratio, activation rate, and time-to-value. Connects product metrics to business outcomes.

layers.name: Team Health
layers.description: Monitors engineering and product team health — eNPS, manager feedback scores, psychological safety indicators, meeting load, and on-call burden. Flags teams at risk of burnout.

layers.name: R&D Performance Review
layers.description: Facilitates quarterly R&D business reviews — preparing the review pack, presenting R&D contributions to business outcomes, and making the case for continued investment.

title: Intellectual Property & Innovation Capture
description:
Identifies and protects intellectual property created by the R&D team — patent-worthy innovations, trade secrets, copyrightable works, and open-source contribution policy — ensuring the company captures the full value of its innovation investment.
  Most R&D teams create IP without capturing it — systematic IP management converts innovation into defensible competitive advantage.

layers.name: Innovation Disclosure Process
layers.description: Maintains an easy-to-use innovation disclosure process — engineers and PMs can submit innovation disclosures when they create something novel. Structured format captures the innovation, how it works, and why it's novel.

layers.name: Patentability Assessment
layers.description: For each disclosure, conducts a preliminary patentability assessment — prior art search, novelty assessment, non-obviousness evaluation, and utility determination. Recommends patent filing, trade secret, or no-action.

layers.name: Patent Filing Pipeline
layers.description: Manages the patent filing process — invention brief to patent counsel, application drafting, prosecution management, and grant tracking. Tracks the cost per patent vs. strategic value.

layers.name: Trade Secret Classification
layers.description: For innovations better protected as trade secrets (faster, cheaper, no disclosure), manages the trade secret classification — access controls, documentation, and employee awareness.

layers.name: Open Source Policy
layers.description: Manages the company's open-source contribution policy — what engineers can open-source (must not include trade secrets or patentable innovations), contribution review and approval, and CLA management.

title: R&D Knowledge Management & Documentation
description:
Manages the R&D team's knowledge infrastructure — engineering documentation, architecture decision records, runbooks, onboarding materials, and institutional knowledge — preventing knowledge loss and accelerating team productivity.
  Poor documentation is the root cause of slow onboarding, repeated mistakes, and the 'key person dependency' problem — systematic knowledge management solves all three.

layers.name: Documentation Quality Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors the health of engineering and product documentation — identifies undocumented systems, outdated runbooks, missing architecture records, and knowledge silos. Prioritises gaps by risk (production systems without runbooks are critical).

layers.name: Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
layers.description: Manages the ADR process — every significant architectural decision is documented with context, decision, and consequences. Prevents revisiting decided questions and provides new engineers with decision rationale.

layers.name: Runbook Management
layers.description: Maintains operational runbooks — step-by-step procedures for all production operations, incident response, and disaster recovery. Tests runbooks regularly. Flags runbooks not updated in >90 days.

layers.name: Onboarding Knowledge Base
layers.description: Maintains a comprehensive engineering onboarding programme — environment setup, codebase orientation, architecture overview, key contacts, and first-sprint guidance. Updates with every new-hire feedback cycle.

layers.name: Knowledge Search & Discovery
layers.description: Provides AI-powered search across all R&D documentation — engineers can ask 'how does our payment processing work?' and get the relevant ADR, runbook, and code reference in one place.

title: Inbound Lead Capture & Qualification
description:
Captures leads from all inbound channels — web forms, email, chat, social, events — and instantly qualifies them against ICP (Ideal Customer Profile) criteria before any human touches the record.
  Assigns a lead score, routes to the right sales rep or nurture track, and logs the complete interaction history in CRM.

layers.name: Multi-Channel Ingestion
layers.description: Captures leads from website forms, chat transcripts, LinkedIn DMs, email enquiries, event registrations, and inbound calls. Normalises all into a standard lead record.

layers.name: ICP Matching
layers.description: Compares lead attributes (company size, industry, geography, role/title, technology stack) against the configured Ideal Customer Profile. Scores fit on each dimension.

layers.name: Behavioural Scoring
layers.description: Analyses engagement signals — pages visited, content downloaded, email opens, webinar attendance — and adds intent score to the fit score.

layers.name: Duplicate & Enrichment Check
layers.description: Cross-checks against existing CRM records for duplicates. Enriches lead with firmographic data (LinkedIn, Clearbit, ZoomInfo) to fill gaps.

layers.name: Routing Decision
layers.description: Combines fit score + intent score + lead source to determine routing — immediate sales assignment (hot), nurture sequence (warm), disqualify (poor fit), or partner referral.

flow.description: Lead attributes matched to ICP criteria
Composite score computed; routing decision made

title: Outbound Prospect Identification
description: Autonomously identifies high-fit prospects matching the ICP from external databases, news signals, and intent data — building targeted outbound lists without manual research.

layers.name: ICP Translation
layers.description: Converts the ICP definition into machine-readable filters — NAICS codes, employee range, revenue band, technology stack, geography, and funding stage.

layers.name: Database Prospecting
layers.description: Queries ZoomInfo, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Lusha, or Apollo against ICP filters. Returns candidate company and contact lists with confidence scores.

layers.name: Intent Signal Overlay
layers.description: Enriches candidates with third-party intent data — companies researching relevant topics, attending industry events, hiring for roles indicating budget and need.

layers.name: Trigger Event Detection
layers.description: Scans news, LinkedIn, and funding databases for trigger events — new funding round, leadership change, M&A, product launch, geographic expansion, regulatory change.

layers.name: List Scoring & Prioritisation
layers.description: Ranks all prospects by combined ICP fit + intent + trigger event recency. Outputs a prioritised, de-duplicated outbound list with contact details.

title: Lead Scoring & Prioritisation
description: Continuously scores and re-scores every lead in the CRM based on fit, behaviour, and engagement — ensuring sales reps always work the highest-probability leads first.

layers.name: Fit Scoring (Static)
layers.description: Evaluates firmographic and demographic fit against ICP — industry, company size, revenue, geography, technology stack, decision-maker seniority.

layers.name: Behavioural Scoring (Dynamic)
layers.description: Tracks every engagement action — email open (+5), link click (+10), page visit (+3), demo request (+40), pricing page view (+20), content download (+15).

layers.name: Score Decay
layers.description: Reduces score over time for inactive leads — if no engagement in 30 days, score decays 10% per week. Prevents stale leads appearing high-priority.

layers.name: Negative Scoring
layers.description: Subtracts points for disqualifying signals — wrong industry (-30), student email domain (-50), competitor domain (-40), unsubscribe (-30).

layers.name: MQL Threshold & Alert
layers.description: When score crosses the MQL threshold (configurable, e.g., 70), triggers immediate Sales alert and CRM status change from Lead to MQL.

flow.description: Lead record updated in real time
Sales rep and SDR notified on MQL trigger

title: Lead Nurture Sequence Automation
description: Enrols leads in personalised, multi-touch nurture sequences based on persona, industry, and stage — adapting content and cadence based on engagement responses in real time.

layers.name: Persona Classification
layers.description: Classifies lead by persona (CTO, CFO, COO, Procurement Head, etc.) and industry vertical. Selects the appropriate nurture track with relevant content.

layers.name: Content Selection
layers.description: Matches persona + stage + industry to the best available content assets — case studies, whitepapers, ROI calculators, webinars, product demos.

layers.name: Cadence Optimisation
layers.description: Determines send timing using AI — optimal days/times based on historical engagement data for this persona/industry. Avoids send fatigue.

layers.name: Engagement Adaptation
layers.description: If lead engages with email 1, next email goes deeper on the same topic. If lead ignores 3 emails, cadence slows and topic changes. If lead unsubscribes, sequence stops.

layers.name: Sequence Exit Triggers
layers.description: Exits lead from sequence on MQL conversion, direct sales contact, meeting booked, or account-level activity suggesting the deal is active.

flow.description: Best-fit content matched to lead profile
Exit condition checked after every step

title: Account-Based Marketing (ABM) Signal Detection
description: Monitors target accounts for buying signals — digital behaviour, news events, personnel changes, technology changes — and alerts the account team when a target is showing in-market intent.

layers.name: Target Account Monitoring
layers.description: Watches a defined list of high-priority target accounts across web, news, social, and job boards. Monitors employee activity, company news, and technology signals.

layers.name: Intent Signal Classification
layers.description: Classifies detected signals by type — Research Intent (visiting competitor/category pages), Hiring Intent (job postings for relevant roles), Funding Intent (new investment), Event Intent (conference registration).

layers.name: Signal Scoring
layers.description: Weights signals by intent strength — Demo request (100), Pricing research (80), Competitor comparison (70), Relevant job posting (40), Funding news (30).

layers.name: Account Spike Detection
layers.description: Identifies when signal volume for an account spikes above its rolling baseline — suggesting an active buying cycle has started.

layers.name: Account Team Alert
layers.description: Generates a personalised alert for the AE and CSM — what signals were detected, which contacts showed activity, recommended next action.

flow.description: Target account activity tracked across channels
Signal volume vs. rolling baseline compared

title: Personalised Outreach Generation
description: Generates highly personalised outreach messages for SDRs — email, LinkedIn, call scripts — using prospect research, company news, and pain-point mapping, eliminating generic templates.

layers.name: Prospect Research
layers.description: Reads the prospect's LinkedIn profile, company website, recent news, job postings, and CRM history. Builds a prospect intelligence brief.

layers.name: Pain Point Mapping
layers.description: Maps the prospect's role, industry, and company signals to known pain points in SequelString's ICP database — what problems this persona typically faces.

layers.name: Trigger Hook Identification
layers.description: Identifies the most relevant outreach hook — recent funding, new product launch, leadership change, hiring surge, a relevant news story.

layers.name: Message Drafting
layers.description: Generates a personalised first-line that references the specific hook, connects it to a relevant pain point, and delivers a crisp value proposition — not a generic pitch.

layers.name: Multi-Channel Variants
layers.description: Creates variants for email (subject line + body), LinkedIn connection request, LinkedIn InMail, and call opener — all from the same research base.

flow.description: Messages reviewed for tone and compliance
Draft presented to SDR for review

title: Outreach Sequence & Cadence Management
description: Manages multi-touch SDR outreach sequences — tracking touchpoint delivery, monitoring responses, adapting cadence based on engagement, and flagging hot prospects for immediate follow-up.

layers.name: Sequence Enrolment
layers.description: Enrols prospects in the appropriate outreach sequence based on persona, industry, and lead source. Sequences have 6-12 touchpoints across email, LinkedIn, and phone.

layers.name: Touchpoint Scheduling
layers.description: Calculates optimal timing for each touchpoint — day of week, time of day — based on historical reply rates for this persona/industry/geography.

layers.name: Response Detection
layers.description: Monitors all outbound channels for prospect responses — positive, neutral, objection, unsubscribe, or out-of-office. Classifies and routes each response type.

layers.name: Meeting Detection
layers.description: Detects when a prospect has booked a meeting (calendar link click, reply confirming meeting). Immediately removes from sequence; updates CRM; notifies AE.

layers.name: Sequence Analytics
layers.description: Tracks performance of each step — open rate, reply rate, meeting booked rate. Flags underperforming steps for SDR manager review.

title: Meeting Scheduling & Confirmation
description: Autonomously manages the back-and-forth of meeting scheduling — offering availability, confirming times, sending calendar invites, reminders, and pre-meeting prep packs to all parties.

layers.name: Availability Reading
layers.description: Reads AE/SDR calendar availability in real time. Identifies 3-5 optimal meeting slots that avoid conflicts, back-to-backs, and focus time blocks.

layers.name: Slot Presentation
layers.description: Presents availability to the prospect via a personalised scheduling link or embedded email options — single-click booking without email back-and-forth.

layers.name: Confirmation & Calendar
layers.description: On slot selection, automatically creates the meeting in all parties' calendars. Sends confirmation emails with agenda, dial-in/video link, and location.

layers.name: Reminder Sequence
layers.description: Sends automated reminders — 24 hours before (email), 1 hour before (email + SMS/WhatsApp). Detects if prospect opens reminder — flags if not, enabling human follow-up.

layers.name: Pre-Meeting Brief
layers.description: Assembles a pre-meeting intelligence brief for the AE — prospect background, company news, LinkedIn profile, previous interactions, and suggested talking points.

title: CRM Data Hygiene & Update Automation
description: Keeps CRM data accurate and current by automatically updating contact details, enriching records, detecting stale data, and capturing activity from email and calendar — without SDR manual entry.

layers.name: Email & Calendar Sync
layers.description: Reads all sales emails and calendar events. Automatically logs calls, emails, and meetings in CRM against the correct contact and deal record.

layers.name: Contact Enrichment
layers.description: Periodically re-enriches all CRM contacts against LinkedIn, Clearbit, and ZoomInfo. Updates job titles, phone numbers, email addresses, and company details automatically.

layers.name: Stale Data Detection
layers.description: Identifies contacts with no recent activity, bounced emails, departed employees, or mismatched job titles. Flags or auto-updates where confidence is high.

layers.name: Duplicate Detection
layers.description: Continuously scans for duplicate records — same person/company under different entries. Merges duplicates using confidence-scored matching.

layers.name: Deal Stage Consistency
layers.description: Validates that deal stages in CRM match the actual activity evidence — a deal cannot be in 'Proposal Sent' if no proposal document is attached.

title: Opportunity Scoring & Win Probability
description: Assigns a real-time win probability to every open opportunity using deal characteristics, engagement patterns, competitive signals, and historical win/loss data — replacing gut-feel forecasting.

layers.name: Deal Attribute Analysis
layers.description: Evaluates static deal factors — deal size, product fit, decision-maker seniority, procurement process complexity, competitive landscape, geographic fit.

layers.name: Engagement Velocity
layers.description: Measures how fast the deal is moving — frequency of meetings, email response speed, stakeholder expansion, document sharing, legal/procurement involvement.

layers.name: CRM Activity Analysis
layers.description: Reads all logged activities — notes, emails, calls — and uses NLP to detect positive signals (budget confirmed, timeline shared) and negative signals (ghosting, scope reduction).

layers.name: Historical Pattern Matching
layers.description: Matches current deal profile to historical won and lost deals with similar characteristics. Applies win rate from comparable deals.

layers.name: Risk Factor Detection
layers.description: Identifies specific risk flags — no executive sponsor, single-threaded relationship, procurement involved but no budget confirmed, competitor shortlisted.

title: Next Best Action Recommendation
description: For every open opportunity, recommends the highest-impact next action — who to contact, what to say, what content to share, what obstacle to address — based on deal stage and signals.

layers.name: Stage Assessment
layers.description: Reads current deal stage and maps it to the ideal sales motion at this stage — discovery, qualification, technical validation, commercial negotiation, procurement.

layers.name: Gap Analysis
layers.description: Identifies what is missing to progress to the next stage — economic buyer not engaged, security review not started, ROI business case not shared, legal terms not reviewed.

layers.name: Stakeholder Mapping
layers.description: Analyses who has been engaged and who has not. Identifies key stakeholders the AE has not yet met — champions, blockers, economic buyers.

layers.name: Content Matching
layers.description: Matches the deal stage and stakeholder to the most relevant content asset — customer case study (same industry), ROI calculator, technical documentation, security questionnaire.

layers.name: Action Prioritisation
layers.description: Ranks all possible next actions by expected impact on win probability. Presents top 3 recommended actions with rationale.

flow.description: Current stage and progression criteria reviewed
Best-fit content matched to stage and audience
All possible actions scored by impact

title: Proposal & Quote Generation
description: Generates customised proposals and quotes based on deal parameters — customer requirements, pricing rules, product configuration, and approved templates — in minutes instead of hours.

layers.name: Requirements Extraction
layers.description: Reads the opportunity record, discovery call notes, and RFP/RFI documents to extract the customer's specific requirements, use cases, and priorities.

layers.name: Product Configuration
layers.description: Maps requirements to the correct product/service configuration. Applies bundling rules, minimum quantities, and compatibility checks automatically.

layers.name: Pricing Engine
layers.description: Applies pricing rules — list price, volume discount tiers, contract term discounts, strategic account pricing, and any approved promotional rates. Validates against floor pricing.

layers.name: Template Population
layers.description: Populates the approved proposal template with customer name, requirements, proposed solution, pricing, ROI analysis, and relevant case studies.

layers.name: Approval Routing
layers.description: If pricing is outside standard parameters, routes quote for approval before release. Tracks approval status and notifies AE when approved.

flow.description: Proposal sent or shared via portal

title: Deal Risk & Stall Detection
description: Continuously monitors all open deals for stall signals and risk factors — alerting AEs and managers before a deal goes cold, and suggesting specific interventions.

layers.name: Activity Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks the time since last activity on every deal — email, call, meeting, document share. Flags deals with no activity beyond configurable thresholds.

layers.name: Stakeholder Engagement Decay
layers.description: Monitors whether key contacts are becoming less responsive — reply time increasing, opening emails but not responding, meeting attendance declining.

layers.name: Stage Duration Analysis
layers.description: Compares how long the deal has been in the current stage vs. the average time to progress. Flags deals stuck longer than 1.5x average stage duration.

layers.name: Competitor Risk Detection
layers.description: Scans meeting notes and emails for competitor mentions, RFP language suggesting competitive bids, or customer requests for feature comparison.

layers.name: Escalation Recommendation
layers.description: Based on risk profile, recommends the appropriate intervention — executive sponsor outreach, pricing review, technical proof of concept, competitive response.

flow.description: Time in stage vs. average computed
Notes and emails scanned for competitor signals

title: Competitive Intelligence Battlecard Delivery
description: Detects when a competitor is present in a deal and automatically surfaces the right battlecard, competitive positioning, and objection-handling guidance to the AE in real time.

layers.name: Competitor Detection
layers.description: Scans CRM notes, emails, and call transcripts for competitor mentions — direct names, euphemisms ('the other vendor'), RFP comparison requests.

layers.name: Competitor Identification
layers.description: Identifies which competitor(s) are present. Checks the competitive intelligence database for the latest battlecard and win/loss data.

layers.name: Positioning Selection
layers.description: Selects the relevant competitive positioning based on the customer's primary evaluation criteria — if they're focused on price, surfaces the value/TCO argument; if on features, surfaces the differentiation points.

layers.name: Objection Handling
layers.description: Retrieves the top anticipated objections this customer is likely to raise based on deal stage and competitor, along with proven responses from won deals.

layers.name: Reference Customer Matching
layers.description: Identifies a customer reference in the same industry who chose SequelString over this specific competitor — most powerful competitive proof point.

flow.description: CRM activity scanned for competitor signals
Customer evaluation criteria matched to positioning
Sales Engineer notified if technical competition

title: Pipeline Health & Forecast Generation
description: Generates a data-driven revenue forecast by analysing every deal in the pipeline — applying win probabilities, stage weights, and expected close dates to produce a bottom-up forecast with confidence intervals.

layers.name: Pipeline Aggregation
layers.description: Consolidates all open opportunities across all reps, territories, and segments. Validates deal stage, amount, and close date currency.

layers.name: Win Probability Application
layers.description: Applies AI-computed win probabilities (from UC10) to each deal — rather than using stage-based flat percentages, uses deal-specific, evidence-based probabilities.

layers.name: Close Date Reliability
layers.description: Assesses close date credibility — has the close date slipped before? Is there procurement/legal activity confirming the timeline? Adjusts expected timing accordingly.

layers.name: Forecast Category Override
layers.description: Compares AI forecast vs. rep-submitted forecast (Commit/Best Case/Pipeline). Flags significant discrepancies — rep over-committing or under-reporting.

layers.name: Confidence Interval
layers.description: Generates a forecast range — base case, upside, and downside scenarios — based on deal probability distribution and historical forecast accuracy.

flow.description: AI win probability applied per deal
AI forecast vs. rep commit compared

title: Quota Attainment & Performance Tracking
description: Tracks every rep's real-time quota attainment, pipeline coverage, and activity metrics — identifying who is on track, who is at risk, and what specific coaching actions will help.

layers.name: Quota Loading
layers.description: Loads each rep's quota by month, quarter, and year. Applies ramp factors for new hires. Handles territory and product-line quota splits.

layers.name: Attainment Calculation
layers.description: Computes real-time attainment — closed won deals / quota. Tracks against monthly, quarterly, and annual targets simultaneously.

layers.name: Pipeline Coverage Analysis
layers.description: Computes pipeline coverage ratio (total pipeline / remaining quota). Flags reps with < 3x coverage as at risk of missing target.

layers.name: Activity Benchmarking
layers.description: Compares each rep's activity metrics (calls, emails, meetings, demos) against team benchmarks and against their own historical best performance.

layers.name: Coaching Alert
layers.description: For reps below target with low pipeline coverage and below-benchmark activity, generates specific, data-backed coaching recommendations for the manager.

flow.description: Quota data loaded for all reps

title: Revenue Recognition Signal Detection
description: Monitors signed contracts and delivery milestones to identify when revenue recognition criteria are met — bridging the gap between Sales close and Finance revenue booking.

layers.name: Contract Analysis
layers.description: Reads signed contracts to extract revenue recognition terms — delivery milestones, acceptance criteria, subscription start dates, and multi-element arrangement allocation.

layers.name: Delivery Milestone Tracking
layers.description: Monitors project/delivery system for milestone completion — Go-Live, customer acceptance sign-off, module delivery, SLA commencement.

layers.name: Ind AS 115 / IFRS 15 Check
layers.description: Validates recognition against accounting standard criteria — performance obligation identified, transaction price allocated, control transferred.

layers.name: Finance Alert
layers.description: When recognition criteria are met, alerts Finance team to book revenue. Provides all supporting documentation — contract, delivery confirmation, customer acceptance.

layers.name: Deferred Revenue Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks unrecognised contract value as deferred revenue. Updates deferred revenue schedule on each recognition event.

title: Customer Health Scoring
description: Continuously computes a health score for every customer account — using product usage, support activity, engagement, sentiment, and commercial signals — identifying at-risk accounts before they churn.

layers.name: Product Usage Analysis
layers.description: Reads product usage data — login frequency, feature adoption, active users vs. licensed users, last login date. Low usage is the strongest early churn predictor.

layers.name: Support Ticket Analysis
layers.description: Monitors open tickets, resolution times, CSAT scores, and ticket volume trends. Spike in tickets or declining CSAT = health risk.

layers.name: Engagement Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks customer engagement with CSM — meeting frequency, QBR attendance, response rates, event participation, training completion.

layers.name: Commercial Signal Analysis
layers.description: Monitors renewal proximity, unpaid invoices, scope reduction requests, reduction in POs, and stakeholder changes (champion departure).

layers.name: NPS/CSAT Trend
layers.description: Analyses survey responses and sentiment from emails/calls using NLP — declining sentiment even before formal survey scores signal emerging risk.

title: Renewal Risk & Upsell Opportunity Detection
description: Identifies which accounts are at risk of non-renewal AND which are ready for upsell/cross-sell — prioritising CS team effort on the highest-impact accounts in both directions.

layers.name: Renewal Calendar
layers.description: Tracks all subscription and contract renewal dates. Alerts CSM team at 180, 90, 60, and 30 days before renewal with account health context.

layers.name: Churn Risk Assessment
layers.description: Applies health score, usage trends, and sentiment to assess churn probability. Distinguishes between at-risk-of-churn and likely-to-renew accounts.

layers.name: Upsell Signal Detection
layers.description: Identifies upsell readiness signals — high usage of current modules, requests for features in higher tiers, growth in headcount, new business units, geographic expansion.

layers.name: Revenue Impact Calculation
layers.description: Computes the ARR at risk (churn) and ARR opportunity (upsell) for each account — enabling CS team to prioritise effort by revenue impact.

layers.name: Action Plan Generation
layers.description: For each account, generates a recommended action plan — for at-risk accounts: retention actions; for upsell-ready: expansion conversation starter.

title: Churn Prediction & Intervention
description: Predicts which customers are likely to churn in the next 60-90 days — with enough lead time to intervene — and generates a specific, personalised retention playbook for each at-risk account.

layers.name: Churn Model
layers.description: Applies a machine learning churn prediction model trained on historical churned and retained accounts. Features include usage trends, ticket patterns, payment behaviour, and engagement decay.

layers.name: Churn Driver Identification
layers.description: For each predicted churn account, identifies the primary churn driver — product gaps, pricing, poor adoption, competitor, champion departure, business change.

layers.name: Intervention Playbook
layers.description: Matches churn driver to proven intervention playbooks — if product gap, trigger roadmap conversation; if adoption, trigger training programme; if pricing, trigger commercial review.

layers.name: Executive Escalation Threshold
layers.description: For accounts above ARR threshold or strategic importance, automatically escalates to VP CS or CEO for personal outreach.

layers.name: Intervention Tracking
layers.description: Tracks whether the recommended intervention was executed and whether health score improved. Closes the loop on retention effectiveness.

title: QBR (Quarterly Business Review) Preparation
description: Autonomously assembles the QBR deck for each account — pulling usage data, ROI metrics, support history, and roadmap relevance — so the CSM walks in prepared, not rushing.

layers.name: Account Data Aggregation
layers.description: Pulls all account data for the quarter — usage statistics, feature adoption, user counts, support tickets, and uptime/SLA performance.

layers.name: ROI & Value Realisation
layers.description: Computes the customer's ROI from SequelString — time saved, process efficiency gains, cost reduction — using agreed KPI metrics from the success plan.

layers.name: Goal Progress Review
layers.description: Checks progress against the goals set at the start of the engagement or last QBR. Summarises hits, misses, and adjusted targets.

layers.name: Product Roadmap Alignment
layers.description: Matches the customer's feature requests and pain points against the upcoming product roadmap. Surfaces upcoming features relevant to this customer.

layers.name: Next Quarter Planning
layers.description: Generates recommended next quarter goals, expansion opportunities, and success metrics — ready for the customer conversation.

flow.description: Upcoming features matched to customer needs

title: Customer Sentiment Analysis
description: Continuously analyses all customer communications — emails, support tickets, call transcripts, survey responses — to detect emerging sentiment changes before they manifest as formal complaints or churn.

layers.name: Communication Ingestion
layers.description: Reads all customer-facing communications — support tickets, email threads, chat transcripts, NPS survey verbatims, LinkedIn mentions, review platforms.

layers.name: Sentiment Classification
layers.description: Applies NLP sentiment analysis to each communication — positive, neutral, frustrated, urgent, or disappointed. Tracks trend over time per account.

layers.name: Topic Extraction
layers.description: Identifies the specific topics driving sentiment — product reliability, support quality, pricing, missing features, onboarding experience. Enables targeted response.

layers.name: Trend Detection
layers.description: Monitors for sentiment trend changes — account that was positive for 6 months going neutral is an early warning even before it goes negative.

layers.name: Alert & Escalation
layers.description: Negative sentiment from a key contact triggers CSM alert. Escalating frustration on multiple channels triggers management escalation.

title: Sales Territory & Account Assignment
description: Optimises territory design and account assignment — ensuring equitable quota distribution, minimising travel overlap, and assigning accounts to the best-fit rep based on industry expertise and relationships.

layers.name: Account Profiling
layers.description: Reads all accounts — ARR potential, industry, geography, relationship history, and existing rep relationships. Profiles each account for assignment optimisation.

layers.name: Rep Capability Matching
layers.description: Profiles each rep — industry experience, win rates by segment, existing relationships, language capabilities, and current capacity.

layers.name: Territory Balancing
layers.description: Runs optimisation algorithm to balance territories by total addressable revenue, account count, and geographic proximity — ensuring fair and achievable quota distribution.

layers.name: Conflict Detection
layers.description: Identifies territory conflicts — accounts that span multiple regions, existing relationships that cross territory boundaries, strategic accounts that need special handling.

layers.name: Change Impact Analysis
layers.description: When territory changes are proposed, models the revenue impact — accounts moving between reps, relationship disruption risk, and transition plan needs.

title: Sales Commission Calculation & Validation
description: Automatically calculates sales commissions for every rep based on closed deals, commission plan rules, accelerators, clawback conditions, and special SPIFs — with full audit trail.

layers.name: Deal Closure Confirmation
layers.description: Reads closed-won deals from CRM. Validates against Finance system — contract signed, invoice raised, payment terms confirmed.

layers.name: Commission Plan Application
layers.description: Applies each rep's commission plan — base rate, product mix rates, new logo vs. expansion rates, multi-year deal credits, and partner/co-sell splits.

layers.name: Accelerator Computation
layers.description: Calculates accelerators triggered by quota attainment levels — at 100% attainment, 1.5x multiplier; at 125%, 2x multiplier. Applied correctly per plan rules.

layers.name: Clawback Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks deals subject to clawback conditions — customer cancellation within warranty period, payment default. Auto-triggers clawback deduction from future commission.

layers.name: Dispute Resolution
layers.description: When reps dispute commission, agent retrieves full calculation evidence — deal record, plan document, approval history — for transparent resolution.

flow.description: Closed deal confirmed in CRM and Finance

title: Sales Methodology Adherence Monitoring
description: Monitors whether sales reps are following the defined sales methodology (MEDDIC, SPIN, Challenger, etc.) — coaching in real time where adherence is low and correlation with win rate is strong.

layers.name: Methodology Framework Loading
layers.description: Loads the sales methodology criteria — for MEDDIC: Metrics, Economic Buyer, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Identify Pain, Champion. Maps to CRM fields.

layers.name: CRM Completeness Check
layers.description: For each active deal, checks whether all methodology fields are populated with substantive data — not just placeholders or generic text.

layers.name: Quality Scoring
layers.description: Reads the content of completed fields using NLP. Scores quality — a MEDDIC 'Pain' field that says 'needs automation' scores lower than a specific, quantified pain statement.

layers.name: Win Rate Correlation
layers.description: Computes the correlation between methodology adherence scores and win rates across historical deals. Identifies which criteria most predict winning.

layers.name: Coaching Nudge
layers.description: For deals with low adherence on high-correlation criteria, surfaces a specific coaching question or task — 'Have you confirmed the economic buyer? Add to CRM.'

flow.description: Adherence gaps mapped to win rate impact

title: Sales Training & Onboarding Acceleration
description: Personalises the onboarding and ongoing training journey for each sales rep — identifying skill gaps, recommending specific learning content, and tracking competency development.

layers.name: Skill Gap Assessment
layers.description: Compares each rep's performance metrics, call quality scores, and CRM behaviour against the ideal competency profile. Identifies specific skill gaps.

layers.name: Learning Path Personalisation
layers.description: Recommends specific training modules, role-plays, and coaching sessions to address each rep's unique gaps — not a generic training curriculum.

layers.name: Call Recording Analysis
layers.description: Analyses sales call recordings for talk-to-listen ratio, filler word usage, question quality, objection handling, and close attempt frequency.

layers.name: Peer Benchmarking
layers.description: Identifies the team's top performers in each competency area. Recommends call shadowing or mentoring with specific top performers for each gap.

layers.name: Progress Tracking
layers.description: Tracks training completion, competency score progression, and links improvement back to deal outcomes — showing the ROI of training interventions.

flow.description: Rep performance and CRM behaviour analysed
Training completion and competency change tracked
Rep development plan summarised for manager
Training impact on deal metrics tracked

title: Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Automation
description: Manages the full contract lifecycle from initial draft through redline, legal review, approval, execution, and renewal — reducing contract cycle time and legal bottlenecks.

layers.name: Contract Generation
layers.description: Pulls deal parameters from CRM and auto-generates the contract from approved templates — MSA, SOW, SaaS subscription agreement — with correct terms for deal type and geography.

layers.name: Redline Analysis
layers.description: When the customer returns a redlined contract, the agent analyses changes — categorises each redline as Standard, Non-standard, or Rejected clause — and highlights for legal review.

layers.name: Pre-approved Clause Library
layers.description: Matches customer redlines against the pre-approved clause library. Auto-accepts standard alternative language; routes non-standard clauses to legal.

layers.name: Approval Routing
layers.description: Routes contract for internal approval — based on deal value, non-standard clauses, geography, and customer type — to the right approval authority.

layers.name: eSignature & Execution
layers.description: Sends for eSignature via DocuSign/Adobe Sign. Monitors signing status. On execution, extracts key terms and posts to CLM system and CRM.

title: Account Expansion Signal Detection
description: Identifies expansion signals within existing accounts — new use cases, new business units, new geographies, headcount growth — and alerts the AE to upsell and cross-sell opportunities before the customer asks.

layers.name: Account Growth Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks company growth signals in existing accounts — headcount growth on LinkedIn, new office openings, new product launches, M&A activity.

layers.name: Usage Ceiling Detection
layers.description: Identifies when customers are approaching licence or usage limits — high seat utilisation, frequent feature limit hits, requests for additional capacity.

layers.name: New Use Case Identification
layers.description: Scans support tickets, QBR notes, and email threads for mentions of new business challenges that SequelString can solve — unprompted expansion signals.

layers.name: Cross-Sell Mapping
layers.description: Matches the account's current product portfolio against SequelString's full catalogue. Identifies products/modules they don't have that are a natural fit.

layers.name: Opportunity Creation
layers.description: When signals cross a threshold, automatically creates an expansion opportunity in CRM and assigns to the AE with a recommended opening conversation.

flow.description: New pain points detected in communications
CSM looped in for warm intro

title: Executive Relationship Mapping
description: Maps the stakeholder landscape in every key account — identifying champions, blockers, economic buyers, and white-space contacts — and tracks relationship health at each level.

layers.name: Stakeholder Identification
layers.description: Reads LinkedIn, company website org charts, email headers, and meeting invites to identify all relevant stakeholders in the account — by function and seniority.

layers.name: Relationship Strength Assessment
layers.description: For each stakeholder, measures relationship strength — last interaction date, meeting frequency, response rate, engagement in communications.

layers.name: Stakeholder Role Classification
layers.description: Classifies each contact as Champion, Economic Buyer, User, Influencer, Blocker, or Coach — using interaction patterns and seniority.

layers.name: White Space Detection
layers.description: Identifies important stakeholders the AE has not yet engaged — heads of departments that use or could use the product, executives above the current point of contact.

layers.name: Relationship Risk Alert
layers.description: Flags relationship risks — champion departure, key contact going silent, economic buyer change, new executive joining who is unknown to SequelString.

title: Voice of Customer (VoC) Synthesis
description: Aggregates all customer feedback signals — NPS, CSAT, support tickets, call transcripts, QBR notes, and email sentiment — and synthesises actionable insights for product, sales, and leadership.

layers.name: Feedback Ingestion
layers.description: Collects all structured and unstructured customer feedback from NPS surveys, CSAT ratings, support transcripts, QBR notes, sales call recordings, and email threads.

layers.name: Theme Extraction
layers.description: Uses NLP topic modelling to extract recurring themes across all feedback — what customers love, what frustrates them, what they want that they don't have.

layers.name: Segmentation
layers.description: Breaks themes down by customer segment — industry, company size, product tier, geography — to identify whether issues are universal or segment-specific.

layers.name: Trend Analysis
layers.description: Tracks theme frequency over time — is a complaint getting more common? Is a positive theme growing? Distinguishes structural issues from one-off feedback.

layers.name: Action Routing
layers.description: Routes insights to the right team — product feedback to Product Management, pricing feedback to Sales Ops, support quality to CS Ops, feature requests to Product Roadmap.

flow.description: Themes broken down by customer segment

title: Account Plan Generation
description: Generates a data-driven, personalised account plan for each strategic account — covering growth strategy, relationship map, competitive threats, and a 12-month action plan.

layers.name: Account Intelligence Assembly
layers.description: Pulls together all account data — ARR, product usage, health score, relationship map, support history, decision-maker profiles, and strategic context.

layers.name: Growth Opportunity Mapping
layers.description: Identifies all expansion opportunities — white space products, new business units, geographic expansion — and quantifies the addressable ARR.

layers.name: Competitive Threat Assessment
layers.description: Scans for competitive threats in the account — competitor trials, alternative solutions in place, dissatisfaction signals that competitors could exploit.

layers.name: Relationship Gap Analysis
layers.description: Maps current relationship vs. ideal relationship — who should be engaged that isn't, which stakeholders need stronger connections.

layers.name: 12-Month Action Plan
layers.description: Generates a specific, time-bound action plan — milestones, owner, expected outcomes — covering relationship development, expansion plays, and retention activities.

flow.description: Plan routed to AE for personalisation

title: Win/Loss Analysis
description: Automatically analyses every closed deal — won and lost — to identify the patterns, differentiators, and risk factors that determine outcomes, feeding insights back to sales, product, and marketing.

layers.name: Deal Data Assembly
layers.description: Pulls all data from closed deals — deal attributes, activity history, competitive landscape, methodology adherence score, rep, segment, and close date.

layers.name: Win/Loss Survey Integration
layers.description: Reads post-sale customer surveys and automated win/loss interview data. NLP extracts key reasons stated by buyer.

layers.name: Pattern Recognition
layers.description: Runs statistical analysis to identify which factors most correlate with winning vs. losing — deal size, sales cycle length, number of stakeholders, demo to proposal ratio.

layers.name: Competitor Analysis
layers.description: Breaks down win/loss by competitor — win rate against each competitor, common loss reasons per competitor, most effective counter-strategies.

layers.name: Insight Distribution
layers.description: Routes insights to the right teams — pricing feedback to Finance, product gaps to Product, competitive analysis to Marketing, sales process issues to Sales Ops.

flow.description: Win/loss trend vs. prior period computed

title: Sales Pipeline Analytics
description: Provides real-time, multi-dimensional pipeline analytics — by stage, rep, segment, product, and region — with predictive insights on pipeline sufficiency, velocity, and conversion rates.

layers.name: Pipeline Snapshot
layers.description: Captures the full pipeline state — all open deals by stage, amount, expected close date, rep, and segment. Updated in real time from CRM.

layers.name: Velocity Metrics
layers.description: Computes pipeline velocity — average deal size x win rate x number of opportunities / average sales cycle length. Tracks trend over time.

layers.name: Stage Conversion Analysis
layers.description: Measures conversion rates between every pipeline stage — and identifies where deals are leaking most. High drop-off between stages indicates process or skill gap.

layers.name: Coverage Analysis
layers.description: Computes pipeline coverage ratio by rep, team, and company. Flags where coverage is insufficient to hit target — at the right specificity to take action.

layers.name: Comparative Benchmarking
layers.description: Benchmarks current pipeline metrics against same period last year, company target, and top-performer benchmarks — contextualising the data.

flow.description: Coverage ratio by rep and team computed
Current metrics vs. prior periods compared

title: Sales Activity & Productivity Tracking
description: Tracks sales rep activity against benchmarks — calls made, emails sent, meetings held, demos delivered, proposals submitted — and correlates activity level with pipeline and quota outcomes.

layers.name: Activity Capture
layers.description: Reads all sales activities from email, calendar, CRM, and dialler — logs every outbound email, call, meeting, demo, and proposal automatically, without rep manual entry.

layers.name: Benchmark Setting
layers.description: Computes the team's top-quartile activity benchmarks for each metric — calls/day, emails/day, meetings/week, demos/week. Updates benchmarks quarterly.

layers.name: Activity-Outcome Correlation
layers.description: Computes the correlation between activity types and revenue outcomes. Identifies which activities most drive pipeline creation and deal closing.

layers.name: Leading Indicator Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors leading indicators that predict quota attainment 4-6 weeks ahead — pipeline adds, demo completion rate, proposal submission rate.

layers.name: Productivity Insights
layers.description: Identifies time wasters — reps spending excessive time on administrative tasks, excessive back-and-forth on scheduling, time spent on low-score leads.

flow.description: Individual rep vs. benchmark gaps computed

title: Revenue Attribution & Campaign ROI
description: Tracks the contribution of every marketing campaign, channel, and content asset to pipeline and revenue — enabling data-driven marketing spend decisions and sales-marketing alignment.

layers.name: Touch Point Capture
layers.description: Logs every marketing and sales touch point on every deal — first touch (how the lead was generated), last touch (what drove the final conversion), and all intermediate touches.

layers.name: Attribution Modelling
layers.description: Applies multiple attribution models — first touch, last touch, linear, time-decay, and data-driven — to distribute revenue credit across touch points.

layers.name: Campaign Performance
layers.description: Computes pipeline generated, pipeline converted to revenue, and ROI for every marketing campaign, channel, and content asset.

layers.name: Channel Comparison
layers.description: Ranks all channels by cost-per-lead, cost-per-opportunity, and cost-per-closed-deal — enabling budget reallocation to highest-performing channels.

layers.name: Budget Recommendation
layers.description: Based on attribution data, recommends marketing budget reallocation — increase spend on high-ROI channels, reduce on low-ROI channels.

flow.description: All touch points logged per deal

title: Sales Compensation Plan Optimisation
description: Analyses the effectiveness of the current sales compensation plan — identifying whether it is driving desired behaviours, rewarding the right deals, and motivating the right reps — and recommends plan adjustments.

layers.name: Plan Performance Analysis
layers.description: Maps current commission plan rules against actual rep behaviour — are reps chasing the deals the plan incentivises? Are there unintended gaming behaviours?

layers.name: Behaviour Correlation
layers.description: Correlates commission plan changes (new rates, new SPIFs) with changes in rep behaviour — did a multi-year deal SPIF actually drive more multi-year deals?

layers.name: Equity Analysis
layers.description: Analyses whether the plan is equitable across territories — are reps in high-potential territories benefiting disproportionately? Are there windfall earners vs. under-earners for equal effort?

layers.name: Competitive Benchmarking
layers.description: Benchmarks compensation plan against market data for similar roles in similar companies — assessing whether the plan is competitive for retention and hiring.

layers.name: Plan Design Recommendations
layers.description: Generates specific, data-backed recommendations for the next plan period — rate adjustments, new accelerators, SPIF design, and cap removal suggestions.

flow.description: Current plan rules mapped to rep behaviour
Plan changes vs. behaviour changes measured
Proposed plan modelled on current deals

title: Market Strategy & Intelligence Agent
description: Provides comprehensive market intelligence including market sizing, growth trends, competitive landscape, regulatory changes, and strategic opportunities using AI-powered analysis of market data, industry reports, and competitive intelligence.

layers.name: Market Data Aggregation
layers.description: Collects market data from multiple sources including industry reports (Gartner, Forrester, IDC), financial databases, government statistics, trade associations, and news sources. Aggregates data on market size, growth rates, segmentation, and trends.

layers.name: Competitive Landscape Mapping
layers.description: Identifies key competitors, market share distribution, competitive positioning, and market dynamics. Analyzes competitor strategies, product offerings, pricing models, and go-to-market approaches. Creates competitive landscape visualization.

layers.name: Market Opportunity Analysis
layers.description: Identifies high-growth segments, underserved markets, emerging opportunities, and white space. Quantifies market opportunity size, accessibility, and strategic fit. Prioritizes opportunities by potential revenue and strategic importance.

layers.name: Regulatory & Compliance Intelligence
layers.description: Monitors regulatory changes, compliance requirements, industry standards, and policy developments impacting target markets. Assesses regulatory barriers, compliance costs, and policy risks.

layers.name: Strategic Insights Generation
layers.description: Synthesizes market intelligence into actionable strategic insights including market entry strategies, positioning recommendations, pricing strategies, and partnership opportunities. Generates executive briefings and market entry plans.

flow.description: Market data from multiple sources aggregated

title: Competitor Analysis & Battle Cards Agent
description: Creates comprehensive competitive battle cards with competitor strengths/weaknesses, product comparisons, pricing intelligence, win/loss themes, and proven competitive tactics enabling sales teams to effectively position against competitors.

layers.name: Competitor Intelligence Gathering
layers.description: Monitors competitor websites, product announcements, pricing changes, customer reviews, social media, job postings, and financial reports. Tracks competitor product features, roadmaps, partnerships, and strategic moves.

layers.name: Product Feature Comparison
layers.description: Compares product features, capabilities, technical specifications, and performance metrics across competitors. Identifies feature gaps, unique differentiators, and areas of parity. Creates detailed feature comparison matrices.

layers.name: Pricing & Packaging Intelligence
layers.description: Analyzes competitor pricing models, packaging options, discount structures, and total cost of ownership. Identifies pricing sweet spots, common objections, and value-based differentiation opportunities.

layers.name: Win/Loss Theme Analysis
layers.description: Analyzes historical win/loss data to identify patterns of why deals are won or lost against specific competitors. Identifies successful positioning strategies, common competitor weaknesses, and effective rebuttals.

layers.name: Battle Card Creation & Maintenance
layers.description: Generates dynamic battle cards with competitor overview, strengths/weaknesses, product comparison, pricing intelligence, common objections, and proven responses. Updates battle cards based on new intelligence and win/loss learnings.

title: Account Research & Profiling Agent
description: Performs deep account research creating comprehensive account profiles including company background, financial health, decision makers, organizational structure, technology stack, business priorities, and engagement history for targeted account planning.

layers.name: Company Intelligence Gathering
layers.description: Collects company information including corporate structure, subsidiaries, locations, employee count, revenue, growth trajectory, funding status, and ownership. Analyzes financial statements, annual reports, and earnings calls.

layers.name: Stakeholder & Decision Maker Identification
layers.description: Identifies key decision makers, influencers, champions, and economic buyers. Maps organizational structure, reporting relationships, and decision-making processes. Profiles stakeholder backgrounds, career history, and interests.

layers.name: Technology Stack & Digital Footprint
layers.description: Identifies technology stack using BuiltWith, Datanyze, and similar tools. Analyzes digital footprint including website technologies, cloud platforms, marketing tools, and IT infrastructure. Assesses technology maturity and modernization initiatives.

layers.name: Business Priority & Initiative Analysis
layers.description: Extracts business priorities from earnings calls, press releases, job postings, and strategic announcements. Identifies key initiatives, transformation programs, pain points, and investment areas. Aligns solution value to business priorities.

layers.name: Relationship Mapping & Engagement History
layers.description: Maps existing relationships, past interactions, deal history, and engagement patterns. Identifies warm introductions, mutual connections, and relationship leverage opportunities. Tracks account health and satisfaction indicators.

title: RFP Response Automation Agent
description: Automates RFP/RFI response process including requirement extraction, content matching, response generation, compliance checking, and assembly of complete RFP responses using AI and content libraries reducing response time by 70%.

layers.name: RFP Intake & Parsing
layers.description: Automatically extracts RFP requirements from various formats (PDF, Word, Excel) using AI-powered document parsing. Identifies all questions, requirements, evaluation criteria, submission guidelines, and deadlines. Structures requirements into categorized, actionable items.

layers.name: Content Library & Knowledge Base
layers.description: Maintains comprehensive content library including standard responses, technical descriptions, company information, certifications, case studies, and past proposals. Uses semantic search and AI matching to find relevant content for each requirement.

layers.name: Automated Response Generation
layers.description: Generates draft responses by matching requirements to content library using NLP and semantic similarity. Customizes responses based on customer industry, size, and specific requirements. Ensures response consistency and message alignment across sections.

layers.name: Compliance & Completeness Checking
layers.description: Validates response completeness ensuring all mandatory requirements addressed, page limits respected, and formatting guidelines followed. Checks for compliance with submission requirements, file formats, and naming conventions.

layers.name: Collaboration & Review Workflow
layers.description: Orchestrates collaboration across stakeholders (sales, technical, legal, finance) with task assignment, progress tracking, and automated reminders. Maintains version control and consolidates inputs into final response.

title: Solution Document Generator Agent
description: Generates comprehensive solution documents including solution overview, architecture diagrams, technical specifications, implementation approach, and delivery methodology customized to customer requirements and industry best practices.

layers.name: Requirement Analysis & Solution Design
layers.description: Analyzes customer requirements, technical constraints, integration needs, and success criteria. Designs solution architecture addressing functional and non-functional requirements. Selects appropriate components, technologies, and deployment models.

layers.name: Architecture Diagram Generation
layers.description: Creates solution architecture diagrams including high-level architecture, component diagrams, data flow diagrams, and integration architecture. Uses standard notation (UML, C4 model) and generates diagrams programmatically.

layers.name: Technical Specification Authoring
layers.description: Generates detailed technical specifications including system requirements, interface specifications, data models, security architecture, and performance characteristics. Ensures specifications align with architecture and requirements.

layers.name: Implementation Approach Definition
layers.description: Defines implementation methodology, phasing strategy, migration approach, testing strategy, and deployment plan. Identifies risks, dependencies, and critical success factors. Creates implementation timeline and resource plan.

layers.name: Document Assembly & Formatting
layers.description: Assembles solution document with professional formatting including executive summary, solution overview, architecture, specifications, implementation plan, and appendices. Ensures consistency, readability, and professional presentation.

title: Proposal Completeness Checker Agent
description: Validates proposal completeness by checking all RFP requirements addressed, mandatory sections included, compliance criteria met, formatting correct, and supporting materials attached before submission.

layers.name: Requirement Coverage Analysis
layers.description: Cross-references proposal content against RFP requirements ensuring every question answered, every requirement addressed, and all mandatory sections included. Identifies gaps and missing responses.

layers.name: Compliance Validation
layers.description: Validates compliance with RFP submission requirements including page limits, font specifications, file formats, naming conventions, and submission portal requirements. Checks for disqualifying omissions or errors.

layers.name: Supporting Documentation Verification
layers.description: Verifies all required supporting documents attached including financial statements, certifications, insurance certificates, references, case studies, and technical documentation. Ensures documents are current and complete.

layers.name: Formatting & Presentation Quality
layers.description: Checks formatting consistency including fonts, headers, page numbers, table of contents, and professional presentation. Validates diagram quality, readability, and proper labeling.

layers.name: Pre-Submission Checklist
layers.description: Generates submission checklist including all items requiring verification before submission. Tracks completion status and sends reminders for pending items. Provides final go/no-go recommendation.

title: GSTR-1 Outward Supply Return Preparation
description:
Aggregates all outward supply data for the month — B2B invoices, B2C transactions, exports, credit notes, and debit notes — and auto-prepares the GSTR-1 return with accurate HSN summary, document totals, and amendment schedules.
  Eliminates the manual compilation effort that typically consumes 3-5 days of the tax team every month.

layers.name: Sales Register Extraction
layers.description: Reads all outward supply transactions from ERP — tax invoices, credit notes, debit notes, export invoices, bill of supply, and receipt vouchers — for the return period.

layers.name: Supply Classification
layers.description: Classifies each transaction by supply type — B2B (with GSTIN), B2C large (> ₹2.5 lakh), B2C small, exports (with/without payment of tax), exempt/nil-rated, and non-GST supplies.

layers.name: GSTIN Validation
layers.description: Validates counterparty GSTINs against the GSTN database in real time. Flags invalid, cancelled, or mismatched GSTINs before return filing to prevent ITC mismatch for counterparties.

layers.name: HSN Summary Computation
layers.description: Aggregates turnover and tax by HSN/SAC code. Validates that HSN detail is complete for all applicable transactions (mandatory for annual turnover > ₹5 Cr).

layers.name: Amendment Detection
