layers.description: Identifies transactions from prior periods that require amendment in the current GSTR-1 (Tables 9A, 9B, 9C). Flags the delta and generates the amendment schedule.

title: GSTR-3B Computation & Self-Assessment
description: Computes the GSTR-3B monthly summary return — output tax liability, eligible ITC, ITC reversal, and net tax payable — with full reconciliation against GSTR-1 and GSTR-2B, identifying any discrepancies before filing.

layers.name: Output Tax Liability
layers.description: Aggregates GSTR-1 data to compute Table 3.1 output tax liability — taxable supplies (IGST/CGST/SGST/Cess), zero-rated, exempt, and non-GST supplies.

layers.name: ITC Computation
layers.description: Reads GSTR-2B auto-populated ITC. Applies eligibility filters — business use test, Section 17(5) blocked credit exclusion, RCM credits, and import IGST credits.

layers.name: ITC Reversal Computation
layers.description: Computes mandatory ITC reversals — Rule 42 (exempt/non-business use proportion), Rule 43 (capital goods for exempt use), and Rule 37 (non-payment to supplier within 180 days).

layers.name: Net Tax Payable
layers.description: Computes net GST payable — Output Tax minus Eligible ITC minus ITC reversals. Applies credit ledger utilisation in correct order (IGST first, then CGST/SGST).

layers.name: GSTR-1 vs. GSTR-3B Reconciliation
layers.description: Reconciles GSTR-3B liability against GSTR-1 declared supplies. Flags any variance — must be within CBIC tolerance limits to avoid SCN.

flow.description: Late payment interest computed if applicable

title: GSTR-2B Reconciliation & ITC Optimisation
description: Reconciles the auto-generated GSTR-2B (supplier-filed ITC) against the company's purchase register — identifying missing ITC, supplier non-filers, and ITC at risk — maximising eligible credit claims.

layers.name: GSTR-2B Data Download
layers.description: Fetches the GSTR-2B statement from the GST portal for the period. Extracts all ITC available — B2B invoices, import IGST, RCM inward supplies, and ISD credits.

layers.name: Purchase Register Matching
layers.description: Matches GSTR-2B entries against the company's purchase register line by line — GSTIN + Invoice No + Date + Amount. Classifies each as: Matched, Invoice Date Mismatch, Amount Mismatch, or Book entry not in GSTR-2B.

layers.name: Mismatch Classification
layers.description: For each mismatch, determines the root cause — supplier has not yet filed GSTR-1, supplier filed with wrong invoice number, supplier filed with wrong amount, or genuine dispute.

layers.name: Supplier Communication
layers.description: For invoices in books but not in GSTR-2B, generates supplier communication — requesting the supplier to file GSTR-1 correctly or amend the filed return.

layers.name: ITC Risk Quantification
layers.description: Computes ITC at risk — amounts where supplier has not filed and ITC cannot be claimed beyond the cap (currently 105% of GSTR-2B ITC). Flags for CFO provisioning decision.

title: GST Annual Return (GSTR-9) & Reconciliation Statement (GSTR-9C)
description: Prepares the annual GST return GSTR-9 and, where applicable, the reconciliation statement GSTR-9C — reconciling the annual audited financials against GST returns filed during the year.

layers.name: Annual Data Compilation
layers.description: Aggregates all 12 months of GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B data. Builds the annual supply and ITC summary. Validates that monthly returns are consistent with each other.

layers.name: Books vs. Returns Reconciliation
layers.description: Reconciles annual turnover and tax in GST returns against audited financial statements. Identifies differences — timing differences (advance receipts), classification differences, and genuine omissions.

layers.name: ITC Annual Reconciliation
layers.description: Reconciles ITC claimed in GSTR-3B across the year against GSTR-2A/2B and books. Identifies excess ITC claimed (reversal needed) and unclaimed eligible ITC (claim opportunity).

layers.name: HSN Turnover Summary
layers.description: Aggregates annual turnover and tax by HSN code across all monthly returns. Validates against books and resolves classification inconsistencies.

layers.name: Auditor Collaboration
layers.description: For GSTR-9C, prepares the reconciliation statement with difference explanations, tax liability on unreported/under-reported supplies, and auditor sign-off package.

flow.description: Additional tax liability computed if any

title: GST Refund Application Management
description: Identifies and quantifies GST refund entitlements — export refunds, inverted duty structure refunds, and excess ITC refunds — and manages the application, tracking, and deficiency memo response.

layers.name: Refund Entitlement Identification
layers.description: Scans the tax position for refund scenarios — exports with accumulated ITC (LUT route), inverted duty structure where input tax rate exceeds output rate, and excess cash in electronic ledger.

layers.name: Net ITC Computation
layers.description: For inverted duty refunds, computes the eligible refund using the prescribed formula — (Turnover of inverted supplies / Total turnover) × Net ITC for the period.

layers.name: Export Refund Validation
layers.description: For export refunds, matches shipping bills against GSTR-1 export invoices. Validates FIRC/BRC receipt for export proceeds. Flags discrepancies.

layers.name: Application Preparation
layers.description: Prepares Form RFD-01 with all required annexures — statement of invoices, computation of refund, bank details, undertaking, and supporting documents.

layers.name: Deficiency Memo Handling
layers.description: When the GST officer issues a deficiency memo (Form RFD-03), reads the objections, prepares responses with additional documents, and refiles within the prescribed window.

flow.description: Deficiency memo read and responded to

title: E-Invoice Compliance & IRN Management
description: Automates the e-invoice generation, IRN issuance, QR code embedding, and real-time validation process — ensuring 100% compliance with the mandatory e-invoicing regime for applicable taxpayers.

layers.name: Invoice Eligibility Check
layers.description: Determines which invoices require e-invoicing — based on taxpayer turnover threshold, supply type (B2B/exports/reverse charge), and transaction nature. Excludes SEZ, banking, insurance, and other exempt categories.

layers.name: Schema Validation
layers.description: Validates invoice data against the IRP (Invoice Registration Portal) schema before transmission — mandatory fields, format compliance, GSTIN validity, and HSN code correctness.

layers.name: IRN Generation
layers.description: Transmits validated invoice data to the IRP via API. Receives and stores the IRN (Invoice Reference Number), signed e-invoice JSON, and QR code.

layers.name: PDF Embedding & Distribution
layers.description: Embeds the QR code and IRN on the PDF invoice. Routes the e-invoice to the customer via the correct channel — email, portal, EDI.

layers.name: Cancellation & Amendment Management
layers.description: For invoices that need to be cancelled (within 24-hour window), manages the IRP cancellation API call. For post-24-hour corrections, issues credit/debit notes with reference to original IRN.

flow.description: Each invoice assessed for e-invoice applicability
QR code and IRN embedded in invoice PDF

title: GST Notice Management & Response
description: Reads, classifies, and manages all GST notices — ASMT-10, DRC-01, SCN, audit notices — computing tax liabilities, preparing detailed responses with evidence, and tracking proceedings through closure.

layers.name: Notice Classification
layers.description: Reads the notice from the GST portal or email. Classifies by type — scrutiny (ASMT-10), DRC-01 (demand after audit/investigation), SCN (Show Cause Notice), and audit/inspection notices.

layers.name: Issue Identification
layers.description: Extracts the specific issues raised — ITC mismatch, turnover mismatch, classification dispute, rate dispute, reverse charge non-compliance, or place of supply error.

layers.name: Evidence Compilation
layers.description: Retrieves all relevant evidence from ERP and document management — invoices, GRNs, contracts, payment proofs, GSTR filings, and reconciliations — to support or challenge the demand.

layers.name: Tax Liability Computation
layers.description: Computes the correct tax liability per the company's legal position. Calculates tax, interest (Section 50), and penalty (Section 73/74) amounts if any portion of demand is conceded.

layers.name: Response Drafting
layers.description: Prepares a detailed reply — legal grounds, supporting evidence, reconciliation schedules, and computation of admitted vs. disputed liability. Routes for review and filing within the response window.

title: Advance Tax Computation & Payment
description: Computes quarterly advance tax liability based on year-to-date income, expenses, deductions, and tax credits — ensuring instalments are accurate and paid by due dates to avoid interest under Sections 234B and 234C.

layers.name: Year-to-Date P&L Extraction
layers.description: Reads the YTD profit & loss account from the accounting system. Extracts revenue, expenses, depreciation, and other income components relevant to taxable income computation.

layers.name: Taxable Income Projection
layers.description: Projects the full-year taxable income — adjusting YTD actuals for: remaining period projections, disallowable expenses (Section 40a, 40A), deductions (80IC, 80JJAA, 80M), and losses brought forward.

layers.name: Tax Liability Computation
layers.description: Applies the correct tax rate (base rate + surcharge + cess) to projected taxable income. Computes MAT liability (Section 115JB) and compares with normal tax — higher of the two applies.

layers.name: Instalment Computation
layers.description: Computes the advance tax instalment due — based on the percentage schedule (15%/45%/75%/100% by June/September/December/March) less TDS already deducted and previous instalments paid.

layers.name: Payment Authorisation & Challan
layers.description: Prepares Challan ITNS 280 with correct assessment year, tax type, and amount. Routes for CFO authorisation and initiates bank payment. Archives paid challan.

flow.description: YTD P&L data read from accounting system
Applicable tax rate and surcharge applied
TDS deducted and deposited so far read

title: Income Tax Return (ITR) Preparation
description: Prepares the corporate income tax return (ITR-6) — computing total income, tax liability, Schedule AL, Schedule BP, and all required disclosures — from the audited financial statements and supporting tax workings.

layers.name: Financial Statements Reading
layers.description: Reads audited P&L, balance sheet, and notes. Extracts all income and expenditure line items and maps them to the relevant ITR schedule heads.

layers.name: Tax Computation
layers.description: Applies income tax adjustments to book profit — adds back disallowances (Section 40, 40A, 43B), reduces exempt income (Section 10), applies deductions (Chapter VIA), and computes taxable income under each head.

layers.name: Schedule BP (Business Profit)
layers.description: Completes Schedule BP — book profit as per P&L, adjustments for depreciation under Section 32, provisions disallowed under Section 43B, and 80-IC/J/JJA deductions.

layers.name: MAT / AMT Computation
layers.description: Computes book profit under Section 115JB, applies MAT rate, and claims MAT credit (Section 115JAA) from prior years. Computes AMT where applicable.

layers.name: Tax Credit & Self-Assessment
layers.description: Aggregates TDS credits (Form 26AS / AIS), advance tax paid, and MAT credit. Computes self-assessment tax payable / refund due. Prepares all schedules and XML/JSON for e-filing.

flow.description: Audited P&L and balance sheet read

title: TDS Compliance — Deduction, Deposit & Returns
description: Manages the complete TDS compliance cycle — determining the correct TDS section and rate for every payment, verifying PAN, computing threshold accumulation, deducting, depositing, and filing returns (26Q/27Q/27EQ).

layers.name: Payment Nature Classification
layers.description: Reads each vendor payment from the accounts payable system. Classifies the nature of payment — professional fees (194J), contract (194C), rent (194I), interest (194A), commission (194H), salary (192), etc.

layers.name: TDS Section & Rate Determination
layers.description: Maps payment nature to the correct TDS section. Applies the applicable rate — standard rate, reduced rate (lower deduction certificate), or higher rate (Section 206AA for PAN-less payees).

layers.name: Threshold Accumulation Tracking
layers.description: Tracks cumulative payments to each vendor under each TDS section across the financial year. Triggers TDS deduction only when the aggregate crosses the statutory threshold.

layers.name: PAN & TRACES Validation
layers.description: Validates vendor PAN against the Income Tax PAN database. Checks TRACES for lower deduction certificates (Form 13). Applies 20% TDS for invalid/missing PAN (Section 206AA).

layers.name: Challan, Return & Certificate
layers.description: Generates Challan ITNS 281 for TDS deposit. Compiles data for quarterly return (26Q/27Q/27EQ) using RPU. Generates TDS certificates (Form 16A/16/27D) for deductees.

title: Form 26AS / AIS Reconciliation
description: Reconciles the company's books of account against Form 26AS (TDS/TCS credit statement) and AIS (Annual Information Statement) — ensuring all tax credits are captured and no transactions are unreported.

layers.name: 26AS Data Download
layers.description: Downloads the current Form 26AS and AIS from the TRACES / Compliance Portal. Extracts all TDS credits (by deductor), TCS collected, advance tax paid, and refunds received.

layers.name: Books Matching — TDS Credits
layers.description: Matches each TDS credit in 26AS against the corresponding income entry in the books. Identifies income that was received (and TDS deducted) but not reflected in books — risk of omission.

layers.name: Books Matching — Payments
layers.description: Matches advance tax and self-assessment tax challan entries in 26AS against the company's tax payment records. Ensures no challan is unmatched or misallocated.

layers.name: AIS Verification
layers.description: Reviews the AIS for transactions flagged by the Income Tax Department — high-value transactions, SFT (Statement of Financial Transaction) data from banks, MCA filings. Validates each against books.

layers.name: Feedback Submission
layers.description: For AIS entries that are incorrect (wrong amount, wrong entity, or already reported elsewhere), submits online feedback on the Compliance Portal to correct the AIS before it impacts assessment.

title: Transfer Pricing Documentation & Compliance
description: Monitors all international and domestic transactions between related parties, computes arm's length pricing, prepares TP documentation (Form 3CEB), and manages TP audit responses.

layers.name: Related Party Transaction Identification
layers.description: Reads all intercompany transactions from the ERP — loans, service fees, royalties, goods sales, guarantees, and cost allocations — across all group entities and geographies.

layers.name: Materiality & Reporting Threshold
layers.description: Checks whether transactions cross the reporting thresholds — international transactions (any amount), specified domestic transactions (> ₹20 Cr). Determines which need TP study.

layers.name: Method Selection & Benchmarking
layers.description: Selects the most appropriate TP method — CUP, CPM, TNMM, PSM, or RPM — based on transaction type and comparability analysis. Runs benchmarking database search for comparable uncontrolled transactions.

layers.name: Arm's Length Range Computation
layers.description: Computes the arm's length price (ALP) and acceptable range. Validates whether the actual transaction price falls within the ALP range. Flags transactions outside the range.

layers.name: Form 3CEB & TP Report
layers.description: Prepares Form 3CEB (Accountant's Report) and the comprehensive TP documentation report — benchmarking analysis, functional analysis, economic analysis, and year-end adjustment workings.

title: Income Tax Assessment & Litigation Management
description: Manages income tax assessment proceedings from the intimation/notice stage through scrutiny, CIT(A), ITAT, and higher court stages — tracking demands, deposits, and stay applications across all litigation matters.

layers.name: Assessment Notice Management
layers.description: Reads all assessment notices from the Compliance Portal — Section 143(1) intimations, Section 143(2) scrutiny notices, Section 148 reopening notices, and Section 263/264 revision notices.

layers.name: Assessment Order Analysis
layers.description: Reads assessment orders, computes additions/disallowances made by the assessing officer, and identifies each issue — facts, legal grounds, and quantum.

layers.name: Demand Computation & Verification
layers.description: Verifies the demand computation in the assessment order. Identifies computational errors. Computes the correct demand including interest under Section 234A/234B/234C.

layers.name: Appeal Strategy
layers.description: For each addition, evaluates the legal strength of the company's position — favourable judicial precedents, CBDT circulars, and case-specific facts. Recommends appeal or acceptance per issue.

layers.name: Litigation Tracking
layers.description: Maintains a complete litigation register — all open assessment years, issues in dispute, amounts, current stage (CIT(A)/ITAT/HC/SC), expected hearing dates, and provisions made in books.

title: TCS (Tax Collected at Source) Compliance
description: Manages TCS compliance for all applicable transactions — sale of goods (Section 206C(1H)), foreign remittances, overseas tour packages, alcohol, timber, tendu leaves, and scrap — with auto-collection, deposit, and return filing.

layers.name: TCS Applicability Determination
layers.description: Reads each sale/collection transaction. Determines TCS applicability — Section 206C(1H) for goods sales exceeding ₹50 lakh threshold per buyer, foreign remittances > ₹7 lakh (LRS), and specified goods.

layers.name: Threshold Tracking
layers.description: Tracks cumulative receipts from each buyer under Section 206C(1H). Triggers TCS collection only after the ₹50 lakh threshold is crossed for that buyer in the financial year.

layers.name: TCS Rate Application
layers.description: Applies the correct TCS rate — 0.1% for Section 206C(1H), higher rates for foreign remittances (5%/0.5%), specified goods (various). Applies 5% higher rate (206CC) if PAN not provided.

layers.name: Receipt Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors all receipts (not invoices) from buyer. TCS under Section 206C(1H) is triggered on receipt — so actual collection triggers the liability, not the invoice date.

layers.name: Deposit & Return
layers.description: Generates Challan ITNS 281 for TCS deposit. Compiles quarterly TCS return (27EQ) using RPU. Generates TCS certificate (Form 27D) for buyers.

title: Salary TDS (Section 192) & Form 16 Compliance
description: Computes monthly salary TDS for every employee — accounting for all income sources, deductions, exemptions, and regime choice — generating Form 16 Parts A and B at year end.

layers.name: Salary Structure Reading
layers.description: Reads employee salary structures from HRMS — basic, HRA, special allowance, LTA, reimbursements, bonuses, and ESOP perquisites. Applies tax exemptions (Section 10) — HRA, LTA, gratuity, leave encashment.

layers.name: Regime Selection
layers.description: Determines the employee's tax regime choice — old (with deductions) or new (without deductions, lower rates). Applies the chosen regime throughout the year and flags if it can be changed.

layers.name: Deduction Computation (Old Regime)
layers.description: For employees on old regime, computes all eligible deductions — Section 80C (PF, ELSS, LIC, PPF), 80D (medical insurance), 80E (education loan), 80G, HRA, and professional tax.

layers.name: Monthly TDS Computation
layers.description: Distributes annual tax liability across remaining months. Adjusts each month for changes in salary, regime switches, new declarations, and year-end reconciliation.

layers.name: Form 16 Generation
layers.description: At year end, generates Form 16 Part A (from TRACES — TDS deposited) and Part B (salary computation, exemptions, deductions, and net taxable income). Provides digital delivery to employees.

title: TRACES Reconciliation & TDS Credit Management
description: Reconciles TDS deposited, TDS returns filed, and TDS credits reflected on TRACES — ensuring that all deducted and deposited amounts are correctly credited to deductees and all discrepancies are corrected.

layers.name: TRACES Data Download
layers.description: Downloads TDS liability, challan details, and Form 26AS data from the TRACES portal. Extracts the status of all TDS returns filed (processed / defective / corrections pending).

layers.name: Challan-to-Return Matching
layers.description: Matches each TDS deposit challan against the corresponding entries in the quarterly TDS return. Identifies challan consumption mismatches — challan not consumed, over-consumed, or wrong AY applied.

layers.name: Deductee Credit Verification
layers.description: Checks whether TDS deducted from deductees is appearing in their Form 26AS. Identifies credits not appearing — due to TDS return errors, PAN mismatches, or unprocessed returns.

layers.name: Correction Return Management
layers.description: For errors identified, prepares correction statements (C1/C2/C3/C4/C5 corrections) using RPU. Validates the correction before submission to TRACES.

layers.name: Demand Resolution
layers.description: For TRACES demands (short deduction, short deposit, interest), prepares response — either correction return, payment, or legal response as appropriate.

title: DTAA (Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement) Benefit Determination
description: Analyses cross-border payments and determines eligibility for DTAA treaty benefits — reduced withholding tax rates, exemptions, and Permanent Establishment exposure — to optimise international tax costs.

layers.name: Cross-Border Payment Identification
layers.description: Reads all outbound payments to non-residents from the treasury and accounts payable system — royalties, technical service fees, dividends, interest, software payments, and FTS charges.

layers.name: Treaty Identification
layers.description: Identifies the applicable DTAA between India and the recipient's country of residence. Maps the payment type to the relevant treaty article — Article 12 (Royalties), Article 13 (Capital Gains), Article 7 (Business Profits), etc.

layers.name: Tax Residency Certificate Validation
layers.description: Validates that the non-resident recipient has provided a valid Tax Residency Certificate (TRC) — prerequisite for treaty benefit under Section 90(4). Checks TRC validity period and other mandatory Form 10F information.

layers.name: Beneficial Ownership & LOB
layers.description: Analyses whether the recipient qualifies as the beneficial owner of the income (required for most treaties). Applies Limitation of Benefits (LOB) clause analysis where the treaty contains one.

layers.name: Withholding Tax Computation
layers.description: Computes withholding tax at the beneficial treaty rate vs. the domestic TDS rate. Generates the Form 15CA/15CB package for FEMA compliance along with the Treaty benefit claim.

flow.description: Treaty rate vs. domestic rate compared

title: Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) Compliance
description: Prepares the Country-by-Country Report (CbCR) — Form 3CEAA — for multinational enterprises with consolidated revenue above ₹5,500 Cr, detailing revenue, profit, tax, and employee data by jurisdiction.

layers.name: Applicability Check
layers.description: Determines CbCR obligation — is the Indian entity the Ultimate Parent Entity (UPE), surrogate parent, or a constituent entity? Checks the ₹5,500 Cr consolidated revenue threshold and applicable filing jurisdiction.

layers.name: Data Aggregation by Jurisdiction
layers.description: Aggregates revenue, profit before tax, income tax paid, income tax accrued, stated capital, accumulated earnings, employees, and tangible assets by jurisdiction from consolidated group data.

layers.name: Constituent Entity List
layers.description: Compiles the complete list of constituent entities — legal name, jurisdiction of tax residence, jurisdiction of incorporation, and principal business activities.

layers.name: Data Validation
layers.description: Validates CbCR data for internal consistency — revenue aggregation, profit reconciliation against consolidated P&L, and data completeness per jurisdiction.

layers.name: Form 3CEAA Filing
layers.description: Prepares Form 3CEAA (Table 1 — jurisdiction summary, Table 2 — constituent entity list, Table 3 — additional notes) and files on the income tax portal by the due date (on or before filing of ITR).

flow.description: Group financial data by jurisdiction compiled
Form filed on income tax portal

title: Equalization Levy Compliance
description: Manages Equalization Levy compliance — on digital advertising services (6%) and e-commerce operator transactions (2%) — computing, depositing, and filing the annual statement of Equalization Levy.

layers.name: Transaction Identification
layers.description: Identifies transactions subject to Equalization Levy — payments to non-resident digital advertising platforms (Google, LinkedIn, Meta) and purchases from non-resident e-commerce operators (Amazon marketplace, etc.).

layers.name: Rate Determination
layers.description: Applies the correct Equalization Levy rate — 6% on specified services (digital advertising) paid to non-residents without PE in India, and 2% on e-commerce operator transactions (where levy applicable).

layers.name: DTAA / Income Tax Interaction
layers.description: Checks whether the transaction is also subject to income tax (TDS) — where income tax applies, Equalization Levy does not. Ensures no double taxation between EL and TDS on the same transaction.

layers.name: Levy Deposit
layers.description: Computes the Equalization Levy amount. Generates Challan for quarterly deposit (June, September, December, March). Ensures deposit by the 7th of the month following the quarter.

layers.name: Annual Statement
layers.description: Prepares the Annual Statement of Equalization Levy (Form 1) — summary of all transactions and levy paid — and files on the income tax portal by June 30.

title: FEMA Compliance for Outbound Payments
description: Ensures all outbound cross-border payments comply with FEMA — correct purpose codes, Form 15CA/15CB filings, RBI reporting, and LRS (Liberalised Remittance Scheme) compliance for applicable transactions.

layers.name: Payment Classification
layers.description: Reads each outbound foreign payment. Classifies by FEMA purpose code — current account (trade payments, services, royalties) vs. capital account (investments, loans, equity). Determines applicable regulatory framework.

layers.name: Form 15CA/15CB Determination
layers.description: Determines which Form 15CA category applies — Part A (payment ≤ ₹5 lakh, non-taxable), Part B (CA certificate), Part C (large payments, CA certificate), or Part D (exempt from tax).

layers.name: CA Certificate Coordination
layers.description: For Form 15CB (required for Part C payments), prepares the supporting package for the CA — payment details, tax analysis, TRC, DTAA analysis, and TDS computation — to enable efficient CA certification.

layers.name: RBI Reporting
layers.description: Identifies transactions requiring RBI reporting — ODI (Overseas Direct Investment), FLA (Foreign Liabilities and Assets), and ECB (External Commercial Borrowings) filings. Prepares and files the reports within prescribed timelines.

layers.name: Compliance Audit Trail
layers.description: Maintains a complete FEMA compliance register for every cross-border payment — purpose code, Form 15CA/15CB reference, bank remittance details, and RBI acknowledgement where applicable.

title: Customs Duty Classification & Valuation
description: Classifies imported and exported goods under the correct Customs Tariff Heading, determines assessable value, computes customs duty, and identifies applicable exemption notifications — minimising duty costs and avoiding classification disputes.

layers.name: HS Code Classification
layers.description: Reads the product description, technical specification, and import invoice. Applies the Customs Tariff (based on WCO Harmonised System) to assign the correct 8-digit HS code. Validates against DGFT Product Schedule.

layers.name: Duty Rate Determination
layers.description: Reads the Basic Customs Duty (BCD), IGST, Compensation Cess, and Anti-Dumping Duty rates applicable to the HS code. Applies MFN rate vs. FTA preferential rate where applicable.

layers.name: Assessable Value Computation
layers.description: Computes customs assessable value per Customs Valuation Rules — CIF value (cost + insurance + freight), additions for royalties/licence fees/commissions, and deductions for post-importation costs.

layers.name: Exemption Notification Identification
layers.description: Searches the current Customs Notification database for applicable exemptions — project imports (9801), EOU/SEZ benefits, EPCG scheme, advance authorisation, and end-use exemptions.

layers.name: ICEGATE Filing & Bill of Entry
layers.description: Prepares the Bill of Entry data for ICEGATE e-filing. Computes total duty outgo. Identifies classification risk and prepares advance ruling application if high-value/high-risk classification.

flow.description: HS code determined from product specification
All duty components read from tariff

title: Professional Tax Compliance
description: Manages Professional Tax compliance across all states of operation — registration, monthly/annual deduction from salaries, employer PT payment, and return filing — for all applicable employees and establishments.

layers.name: Applicability Assessment
layers.description: Determines Professional Tax applicability per state — different states have different rates, thresholds, and filing frequencies. Checks which states the company has establishments in and the applicable PT Act.

layers.name: Employee Slab Application
layers.description: For each state, applies the monthly salary slab to determine the PT liability per employee — typically ₹200/month for salaries above the threshold (varies by state).

layers.name: Employer PT Computation
layers.description: Computes the employer's own PT liability (Profession Tax on the entity) where applicable — different from the employee deduction obligation. Varies by state.

layers.name: Deduction & Deposit
layers.description: Deducts PT from employee salaries in the correct month (most states: monthly, Karnataka: annual). Initiates deposit to the respective state PT authority within the due date.

layers.name: Return Filing
layers.description: Files the periodic PT return in each state — monthly (Maharashtra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu), annual (others) — with the correct enrolment certificate details and payment challan.

flow.description: Employee salary vs. state PT slab applied

title: Stamp Duty Compliance on Transactions
description: Identifies stamp duty obligations on contracts, agreements, and instruments — computing correct duty per applicable state Act, managing e-stamping/physical stamping, and ensuring adjudication where required.

layers.name: Instrument Classification
layers.description: Reads contracts, agreements, and instruments being executed — loan agreements, lease deeds, share transfers, mortgage deeds, partnership deeds, powers of attorney, indemnity bonds. Determines instrument type under applicable Stamp Act.

layers.name: State Act Identification
layers.description: Determines which state Stamp Act applies — based on where the instrument is executed or where the property is situated. Different states have different stamp duty rates for the same instrument.

layers.name: Duty Computation
layers.description: Computes stamp duty per the applicable schedule — ad valorem duty on the instrument value / consideration, or a fixed amount, applying any caps or reductions for group transactions.

layers.name: E-Stamping / Physical Stamping
layers.description: Initiates e-stamping through the SHCIL e-stamp portal for applicable states. For states not yet on e-stamping, identifies the correct physical stamp paper requirement.

layers.name: Adjudication Management
layers.description: For high-value instruments or ambiguous instruments, manages the adjudication process — filing the instrument with the Collector of Stamps, responding to queries, and obtaining the adjudication order.

title: Tax on Real Estate Transactions
description: Manages all tax compliance for real estate transactions — GST on under-construction property purchases, TDS under Section 194IA on property sales, stamp duty, and income tax implications of property transactions.

layers.name: Transaction Classification
layers.description: Reads the real estate transaction — purchase, sale, lease, or development. Determines the nature of transaction — under-construction (GST applicable), ready-to-move (no GST), or commercial lease (GST applicable).

layers.name: GST on Real Estate
layers.description: For under-construction purchases, computes GST — 1% for affordable housing, 5% for other residential (without ITC), 12% for commercial (with ITC). Validates whether the developer has opted for the old scheme.

layers.name: TDS under Section 194IA
layers.description: For immovable property purchases exceeding ₹50 lakh, deducts TDS at 1% of the consideration (or stamp duty value, whichever higher). Deposits via Form 26QB and issues Form 16B to seller.

layers.name: Capital Gains Analysis
layers.description: For property sales, computes capital gains — LTCG (> 2 years holding) at 20% with indexation (pre-July 2024) or 12.5% without indexation (post-July 2024 amendment), STCG at slab rate. Identifies Section 54/54F reinvestment exemptions.

layers.name: Stamp Duty & Registration
layers.description: Identifies state-specific stamp duty on the property transaction. Flags circle rate vs. consideration comparison — the higher of the two is used for stamp duty and TDS base under Section 50C/43CA.

flow.description: State stamp duty on property computed

title: Tax on Employee Benefits & Perquisites
description: Computes the taxable value of all employee perquisites — company car, rent-free accommodation, ESOP, meal vouchers, club memberships, and other benefits — ensuring correct TDS under Section 192.

layers.name: Perquisite Identification
layers.description: Reads the company's HR benefits database and expense records. Identifies all perquisites provided — accommodation, car, ESOP, meal coupons, club memberships, gifts, interest-free loans.

layers.name: Taxable Value Computation
layers.description: Applies Rule 3 of Income Tax Rules to compute the taxable perquisite value — accommodation (15%/10%/7.5% of salary depending on city), car (per month flat rate), and prescribed formula for others.

layers.name: ESOP Perquisite
layers.description: For ESOPs, computes the perquisite at the time of exercise — difference between FMV of shares on exercise date and exercise price paid. Applies capital gains on subsequent sale.

layers.name: Exempt Perquisites
layers.description: Identifies perquisites specifically exempt from tax — meal coupons up to ₹50/meal, medical reimbursement (pre-Budget limits), uniform allowance, and laptop/computer provided for business use.

layers.name: Perquisite TDS Integration
layers.description: Adds the monthly perquisite value to the employee's salary for TDS computation. Ensures perquisites are included in Form 16 Part B and reflected in the employee's ITR.

flow.description: Perquisite value added to salary for TDS
Perquisite details in Form 16 Part B
ESOP share sale capital gains tracked

title: Deferred Tax Computation (Ind AS 12 / AS 22)
description: Computes deferred tax assets and liabilities arising from temporary differences between book and tax carrying values — ensuring correct DTA/DTL recognition, MAT credit entitlement computation, and adequate disclosure under Ind AS 12.

layers.name: Temporary Difference Identification
layers.description: Compares book carrying values and tax base of every asset and liability. Identifies taxable temporary differences (future tax liability — DTL) and deductible temporary differences (future tax benefit — DTA).

layers.name: DTA Recoverability Assessment
layers.description: For each DTA, assesses recoverability — is it probable that sufficient future taxable profit will be available? Applies the 'probable' criterion conservatively for loss-making entities.

layers.name: Key Timing Differences
layers.description: Computes deferred tax for the most common items — depreciation differences (book vs. tax), Section 43B disallowances (deductible on payment), provisions (deductible on crystallisation), and ESOP cost (deductible on exercise).

layers.name: MAT Credit Entitlement
layers.description: Computes MAT credit — where MAT is higher than normal tax, the difference is recognised as DTA (MAT credit entitlement) to be carried forward and set off in years where normal tax exceeds MAT.

layers.name: Balance Sheet Recognition & Disclosure
layers.description: Computes the movement in DTA/DTL for the period. Prepares the tax expense note — current tax, deferred tax, reconciliation of effective tax rate to statutory rate (ETR reconciliation).

flow.description: Book vs. tax carrying values compared

title: Tax Provision & Effective Tax Rate Analysis
description: Computes the quarterly and annual tax provision — current tax, deferred tax, and uncertain tax positions — and analyses the effective tax rate with a full book-to-tax reconciliation for CFO and board reporting.

layers.name: Current Tax Provision
layers.description: Computes current income tax provision — applying the enacted tax rate to estimated taxable income for the period. Includes surcharge, cess, and MAT where applicable.

layers.name: Deferred Tax Provision
layers.description: Applies the period's deferred tax movement (from UC26) to compute total tax expense. Validates that deferred tax rate equals the enacted rate expected to apply when timing differences reverse.

layers.name: Uncertain Tax Position (UTP) Reserve
layers.description: Identifies uncertain tax positions — matters in litigation, aggressive positions taken, untested interpretations. Applies recognition threshold (more-likely-than-not under Ind AS 12 App C) and measures the reserve.

layers.name: Effective Tax Rate (ETR) Computation
layers.description: Computes the ETR — total tax expense / pre-tax profit. Builds the ETR reconciliation — starting from statutory rate (25.17%), adjusted for permanent differences, exempt income, non-deductible expenses, deductions, and UTP movements.

layers.name: CFO & Board Reporting
layers.description: Prepares the tax provision summary for quarterly board packs — current provision, deferred tax movement, UTP reserve changes, cash tax payments, and year-to-date ETR vs. budget.

flow.description: Tax section of board pack assembled

title: Tax Risk Register & Control Framework
description: Maintains a live tax risk register — identifying, assessing, and tracking all tax risks across GST, income tax, customs, and international tax — with controls testing and board-level disclosure recommendations.

layers.name: Tax Risk Identification
layers.description: Continuously scans for tax risks — new legislation, GST notifications, CBDT circulars, court judgements, pending notices, open assessment years, aggressive positions, and cross-border exposures.

layers.name: Risk Assessment
layers.description: For each identified risk, computes a risk score — likelihood (1-5) × impact (1-5). Classifies risks as Critical (>15), High (10-15), Medium (5-10), or Low (<5).

layers.name: Control Mapping
layers.description: Maps existing controls against each risk — reconciliation processes, return review controls, authority matrix for sign-off, and system-generated validations. Identifies control gaps.

layers.name: Action Tracking
layers.description: Tracks remediation actions — who is responsible, due date, and completion status. Escalates overdue actions to the CFO.

layers.name: Board Disclosure
layers.description: Prepares the tax risk disclosure for the board / audit committee — material tax uncertainties, contingent liabilities, open litigation exposure, and tax provision adequacy assessment.

flow.description: Tax landscape and internal positions scanned
Tax risk section of board pack prepared

title: Tax Technology & Data Management
description: Manages the quality, completeness, and readiness of tax data across ERP and tax systems — ensuring that master data, transaction coding, and system configurations support accurate and timely tax compliance across all tax types.

layers.name: Tax Master Data Governance
layers.description: Monitors key tax master data — vendor GSTIN, customer GSTIN, HSN/SAC codes, TDS sections per vendor, exemption categories, and cost centre tax treatment. Flags outdated or missing data.

layers.name: Tax Configuration Validation
layers.description: Periodically validates ERP tax configuration — GST rate determination logic, TDS rate tables, place of supply rules, reverse charge applicability, and SEZ/export settings — against current law.

layers.name: Transaction Coding Audit
layers.description: Samples completed transactions and reviews tax coding applied — correct GST rate, correct TDS section, correct place of supply, and correct exemption applied. Identifies systematic miscoding.

layers.name: Data Quality Reporting
layers.description: Computes data quality metrics — % of vendor records with valid GSTIN, % of invoices with HSN codes, % of payments with correct TDS section. Reports to tax and finance teams.

layers.name: Legislative Change Management
layers.description: Monitors GST Council decisions, CBDT notifications, and budget amendments. Assesses impact on ERP configuration and compliance processes. Coordinates system updates before effective dates.

title: Tax-Efficient Business Structure Planning
description: Analyses the company's legal structure, inter-entity transactions, and operational footprint to identify tax planning opportunities — deductions, holidays, regime changes, and restructuring options — and quantifies the tax saving potential.

layers.name: Entity Structure Analysis
layers.description: Maps all legal entities, their tax status (Section 10A/10B/10AA SEZ units, 80-IC hill area units, newly incorporated companies, startups), and the tax holiday periods applicable.

layers.name: Deduction Optimisation
layers.description: Identifies and maximises all available deductions — 80JJAA (additional employee cost), 80M (dividend income deduction), 35(1)(ii) (scientific research), 32AC/32AD (investment allowance), and 36(1)(ix) for employee welfare.

layers.name: Regime Analysis
layers.description: Evaluates whether the company should opt for the concessional tax regime (Section 115BAA at 22% + surcharge + cess) vs. the old regime with deductions. Computes break-even point.

layers.name: Loss Utilisation
layers.description: Tracks brought-forward business losses, unabsorbed depreciation, and capital losses. Plans income recognition timing and business restructuring to maximise loss utilisation within the 8-year carry-forward window.

layers.name: Restructuring Options
layers.description: Evaluates tax implications of business restructuring — mergers, demergers, slump sales, asset transfers — under Sections 47, 47A, 72A, and the tax neutrality conditions.

flow.description: Entity map and tax status compiled

title: Tax Dispute Prevention & Advance Rulings
description: Proactively identifies high-risk tax positions, structures advance ruling applications, and manages Advance Pricing Agreements (APAs) — preventing disputes before they arise rather than fighting them after.

layers.name: High-Risk Position Identification
layers.description: Scans tax positions for potential dispute triggers — novel transactions, positions contrary to recent judgements, industry-specific CBDT focus areas, and transactions flagged in previous assessments.

layers.name: Advance Ruling Eligibility
layers.description: Assesses whether an Advance Ruling application to the AAR/AAAR (under GST) or Board for Advance Rulings (under Income Tax) is feasible and beneficial for the identified issue.

layers.name: APA Application
layers.description: For high-volume transfer pricing transactions, evaluates the feasibility of an Advance Pricing Agreement — bilateral APA with foreign tax authority through CBDT — to provide certainty on TP pricing for 5 years.

layers.name: Ruling Application Preparation
layers.description: Prepares the advance ruling application — statement of facts, legal questions, taxpayer's view, and supporting analysis. Manages the application process and hearing representation.

layers.name: Ruling Outcome Management
layers.description: Monitors ruling decisions. Assesses impact on current year and future year tax positions. For adverse rulings, evaluates appeal options or position restructuring.

flow.description: Ruling outcome added to tax knowledge base

title: Tax Cash Flow Planning
description: Plans the company's tax cash outflows across the year — advance tax instalments, TDS deposits, GST payments, and custom duty — optimising payment timing within legal limits to improve working capital.

layers.name: Tax Calendar Assembly
layers.description: Compiles all tax payment due dates for the year — advance tax instalments (June, September, December, March), monthly GST, monthly TDS deposits, customs duty on imports, and professional tax.

layers.name: Cash Outflow Projection
layers.description: Projects the tax cash outflow for each period — estimated advance tax (based on current year forecast), recurring TDS deposits (based on AP pipeline), and GST net payable (output minus ITC).

layers.name: Working Capital Impact
layers.description: Models the working capital impact of tax outflows — GST paid on purchases before input credit recovery, advance tax paid before year-end reconciliation, and TDS deposited before credit recovery.

layers.name: Legal Optimisation
layers.description: Identifies legal optimisation opportunities — using ITC credit before cash payment for GST, timing of purchases to maximise input credit in the period, and utilisation of existing credit ledger balance.

layers.name: Treasury Integration
layers.description: Integrates the tax cash flow plan with the treasury cash flow forecast — ensuring sufficient liquidity on all due dates and no surprise demands on cash.

flow.description: Tax cash outflows projected per period
Working capital impact of tax outflows computed
Tax cash flow merged with treasury forecast

title: New Tax Law Impact Assessment
description: Monitors Union Budget announcements, Finance Acts, GST Council decisions, CBDT/CBIC circulars, and significant court judgements — and assesses their impact on the company's tax position and compliance obligations within 48 hours.

layers.name: Legislative Monitoring
layers.description: Continuously monitors the Ministry of Finance, CBDT, CBIC, GST Council, and SEBI for new legislation, notifications, circulars, and press releases. Also monitors High Court and Supreme Court tax judgements.

layers.name: Relevance Filtering
layers.description: Filters legislative changes for relevance to the company — based on business sectors, transaction types, entity structure, and tax positions. Avoids alerting for changes that don't apply.

layers.name: Impact Quantification
layers.description: For relevant changes, quantifies the financial impact — change in tax liability, new compliance obligations, cost of new reporting requirements, and system change requirements.

layers.name: Action Plan
layers.description: Identifies the actions required — ERP configuration changes, return preparation process changes, new registrations, revised vendor/customer communications, and training needs.

layers.name: CFO Brief
layers.description: Prepares a concise CFO impact brief within 48 hours of significant announcements — what changed, financial impact, action required, and timeline.

flow.description: Change logged in tax knowledge base

title: Tax Audit Report (Form 3CD) Preparation
description: Prepares the comprehensive tax audit report in Form 3CD — covering all 44 clauses including depreciation, MSME payments, SFT reporting, related party transactions, and TDS compliance — reducing audit time and ensuring completeness.

layers.name: Clause-Wise Data Extraction
layers.description: For each of the 44 clauses of Form 3CD, extracts the relevant data from the ERP and accounting records — depreciation schedules, loan and advance details, payments to MSMEs, and TDS deduction data.

layers.name: Depreciation Schedule (Clause 18)
layers.description: Computes the opening WDV, additions, deletions, and closing WDV for each block of assets per Section 32. Reconciles with the fixed asset register and tax computation.

layers.name: MSME Payment Clause (Clause 22)
layers.description: Identifies all payments to registered MSMEs delayed beyond 45 days. Disallows such amounts under Section 43B(h) and provides the schedule for Form 3CD disclosure.

layers.name: TDS Non-Deduction / Short Deduction (Clause 34)
layers.description: Compiles all instances of TDS non-deduction or short-deduction across all vendor payments. Assesses the Section 40(a)(ia) disallowance impact.

layers.name: Auditor Package
layers.description: Assembles the complete Form 3CD data package for the tax auditor — clause-wise workings, supporting schedules, reconciliations, and explanatory notes — enabling the auditor to efficiently certify and file.

flow.description: All 44 clauses mapped to data sources

title: Statement of Financial Transactions (SFT) Filing
description: Identifies, compiles, and files the Statement of Financial Transactions (Form 61A) for all specified high-value transactions — property purchases, cash deposits, credit card payments, mutual fund transactions, and more.

layers.name: SFT Transaction Identification
layers.description: Scans all transactions for those meeting SFT reporting thresholds — property purchases above ₹30 lakh, cash deposits above ₹10 lakh, credit card payments above ₹1 lakh (cash) / ₹10 lakh (other), foreign exchange above ₹10 lakh.

layers.name: Obligor Identification
layers.description: Determines which entity is the SFT filer — the company as a buyer/seller of specified assets, or the company's bank/broker on the company's behalf. Ensures no duplication with financial institution SFT.

layers.name: PAN Collection & Validation
layers.description: For each SFT transaction, ensures the counterparty PAN is collected, validated, and linked. SFT filing with missing or invalid PAN attracts penalty.

layers.name: Form 61A Data Compilation
layers.description: Aggregates qualifying transactions into Form 61A format — transaction code, PAN of counterparty, amount, and date. Validates the data file against the schema.

layers.name: Filing & Correction
layers.description: Files Form 61A on the Income Tax Reporting Portal (ITDREIN) by May 31. For corrections, files correction statements. Reconciles filed SFT against AIS data.

flow.description: All transactions screened against SFT thresholds
Cumulative transaction value tracked per counterparty

title: Pillar Two (Global Minimum Tax) Compliance
description: Monitors and computes the OECD Pillar Two Global Minimum Tax (GloBE) exposure — assessing top-up tax liability by jurisdiction, GloBE safe harbours eligibility, and preparing for the STTR/domestic minimum tax where applicable.

layers.name: Applicability Check
layers.description: Assesses Pillar Two applicability — consolidated group revenue threshold (€750 million), jurisdictions covered, and the company's role (UPE, IIR, UTPR). Tracks India's implementation of the domestic minimum top-up tax (DMTT).

layers.name: GloBE Income Computation
layers.description: Computes GloBE income by jurisdiction — starting from financial accounting profit, applying GloBE adjustments (excluded dividends, excluded equity gains, specific deductions), and excluding jurisdictions below the de minimis threshold.

layers.name: Covered Tax Computation
layers.description: Computes covered taxes by jurisdiction — income taxes paid and deferred taxes, adjusted for GloBE timing difference elections and deferred tax recapture.

layers.name: Effective Tax Rate (ETR) by Jurisdiction
layers.description: Computes the GloBE ETR per jurisdiction — Covered Taxes / GloBE Income. Identifies jurisdictions with ETR below 15% — subject to top-up tax.

layers.name: Safe Harbour Assessment & Top-Up Tax
layers.description: Evaluates Transitional Safe Harbours (CbCR-based ETR, Substance-Based Income Exclusion) to reduce compliance burden. For non-safe-harbour jurisdictions, computes Qualified Domestic Top-up Tax (QDTT) and IIR top-up tax.

flow.description: Group revenue threshold and jurisdictions checked
Top-up tax computed for non-safe-harbour jurisdictions

title: Spend Analysis & Category Intelligence
description: Analyzes organizational spend across categories, suppliers, business units, and geographies to identify sourcing opportunities, consolidation potential, maverick spending, and cost savings targets using AI-powered spend classification and analytics.

layers.name: Spend Data Aggregation & Cleansing
layers.description: Aggregates spend data from multiple sources including AP systems, procurement systems, corporate cards, and P-Cards. Cleanses vendor names using fuzzy matching and entity resolution. Normalizes transaction data across different systems and formats. Handles multi-currency spend conversion.

layers.name: AI-Powered Spend Classification
layers.description: Classifies spend into standardized taxonomy (UNSPSC, custom categories) using machine learning trained on historical classifications. Automatically categorizes unclassified spend with confidence scores. Handles multi-classification for complex items. Continuously learns from user corrections.

layers.name: Supplier Consolidation Analysis
layers.description: Identifies suppliers providing similar goods/services across the organization. Detects duplicate suppliers with different names or entity structures. Calculates consolidation opportunity value. Analyzes tail spend concentration and rationalization potential.

layers.name: Maverick Spend Detection
layers.description: Identifies off-contract purchases and unauthorized suppliers. Compares actual purchasing patterns against negotiated contracts. Flags purchases bypassing preferred suppliers. Quantifies contract leakage and maverick spend volume. Analyzes root causes of non-compliance.

layers.name: Savings Opportunity Identification
layers.description: Identifies cost savings opportunities including volume consolidation, supplier rationalization, contract renegotiation, payment term optimization, and alternative sourcing. Quantifies potential savings by category and opportunity type. Prioritizes opportunities by impact and feasibility.

flow.description: Spend data from all sources collected

title: Supplier Discovery & Market Intelligence
description: Discovers and evaluates potential suppliers through market research, supplier databases, industry networks, and AI-powered supplier matching, providing comprehensive supplier intelligence including capabilities, certifications, financial health, and competitive positioning.

layers.name: Multi-Source Supplier Database
layers.description: Accesses multiple supplier databases (D&B, ThomasNet, industry-specific databases, public registries). Aggregates supplier information including company profiles, capabilities, certifications, customer references, and financial data. Maintains proprietary supplier database with enriched information.

layers.name: Capability-Based Supplier Matching
layers.description: Matches sourcing requirements to supplier capabilities using semantic search and AI matching algorithms. Analyzes supplier product/service offerings, manufacturing capabilities, geographic coverage, capacity, and specializations. Ranks suppliers by fit score.

layers.name: Diversity & Certification Verification
layers.description: Identifies diverse suppliers (minority-owned, women-owned, veteran-owned, small business). Verifies diversity certifications through official certification bodies. Tracks sustainability certifications (ISO 14001, B-Corp), quality certifications (ISO 9001), and industry-specific certifications.

layers.name: Financial Health Assessment
layers.description: Evaluates supplier financial stability using credit ratings, financial statements, payment behavior, and predictive bankruptcy models. Assesses financial risk scores. Identifies financially distressed suppliers requiring monitoring or alternative sourcing.

layers.name: Competitive Intelligence Gathering
layers.description: Analyzes supplier market positioning, competitive landscape, pricing trends, and innovation capabilities. Monitors supplier news, product launches, acquisitions, and strategic initiatives. Provides market intelligence for negotiation leverage.

title: RFx Creation & Management (RFI/RFP/RFQ)
description: Automates creation, distribution, and management of Request for Information (RFI), Request for Proposal (RFP), and Request for Quotation (RFQ) documents with AI-powered requirement extraction, template management, supplier communication, and response collection.

layers.name: RFx Template Library & Automation
layers.description: Maintains library of RFx templates by category, spend type, and complexity. Auto-generates RFx documents from templates with AI-populated requirements. Includes standard sections (scope, terms, pricing, qualifications). Customizes templates based on project-specific needs.

layers.name: Requirement Extraction & Specification
layers.description: Extracts requirements from requisitions, specifications, and stakeholder inputs using NLP. Converts unstructured requirements into structured RFx sections. Identifies must-have vs. nice-to-have requirements. Generates technical specification tables and scoring criteria.

layers.name: Supplier Selection & Invitation
layers.description: Recommends suppliers for RFx based on capability matching, past performance, and category expertise. Manages supplier eligibility and qualification criteria. Sends RFx invitations with customized messaging. Tracks invitation acceptance and supplier opt-ins.

layers.name: Clarification Management
layers.description: Manages supplier questions and clarification requests. Consolidates similar questions. Provides responses to all suppliers simultaneously to maintain fairness. Maintains Q&A log. Issues amendments to RFx based on clarifications.

layers.name: Response Collection & Organization
layers.description: Provides supplier portal for response submission. Validates response completeness against required sections. Organizes responses for comparative evaluation. Handles late submissions and extensions. Maintains confidentiality and prevents early disclosure.

title: Bid Evaluation & Scoring Automation
description: Automates bid evaluation using configurable scoring models, performs quantitative and qualitative analysis, conducts should-cost modeling, identifies best value suppliers, and generates recommendation reports with AI-assisted comparative analysis.

layers.name: Multi-Criteria Scoring Model
layers.description: Defines weighted scoring criteria including price (cost, TCO), quality (certifications, process capability), delivery (lead time, on-time delivery), service (responsiveness, support), innovation, and sustainability. Allows customization by category. Supports mandatory pass/fail criteria.

layers.name: Quantitative Analysis Automation
layers.description: Automatically extracts and normalizes pricing from supplier responses. Calculates total cost of ownership including shipping, duties, payment terms discount, warranty costs, and lifecycle costs. Performs price-per-unit normalization for comparison.

layers.name: Qualitative Scoring with AI
layers.description: Uses AI to evaluate qualitative responses including technical approach, project methodology, risk mitigation, and innovation. Scores narrative responses based on completeness, relevance, and quality. Identifies strengths and concerns in each response.

layers.name: Should-Cost Modeling
layers.description: Performs should-cost analysis estimating fair market price based on material costs, labor costs, overhead, and reasonable margin. Compares supplier bids to should-cost baseline. Identifies bids significantly above or below should-cost requiring investigation.

layers.name: Scenario Analysis & Sensitivity
layers.description: Performs what-if scenarios with different scoring weights, price changes, or requirement modifications. Conducts sensitivity analysis showing impact of weight changes on supplier ranking. Identifies split award opportunities and multi-sourcing scenarios.

title: E-Auction & Reverse Auction Management
description: Manages electronic auctions and reverse auctions enabling competitive bidding, real-time price discovery, dynamic lot management, proxy bidding, and market-based pricing optimization for maximum cost savings.

layers.name: Auction Strategy & Design
layers.description: Determines optimal auction format (reverse, forward, Dutch, Japanese) based on category and market conditions. Defines lot structure (single lot, multiple lots, combinatorial). Sets auction parameters including duration, bid decrements, extensions, and reserve prices.

layers.name: Supplier Qualification & Training
layers.description: Pre-qualifies suppliers for auction participation based on capability and commercial criteria. Provides auction platform training and practice sessions. Issues auction credentials and access. Validates supplier technical readiness.

layers.name: Real-Time Auction Execution
layers.description: Manages live auction event with real-time bid submission and ranking display. Implements automatic bid extensions to prevent last-second sniping. Handles proxy/maximum bidding. Monitors auction integrity and prevents manipulation. Provides real-time analytics during auction.

layers.name: Lot Optimization & Bundling
layers.description: Optimizes lot bundling based on bidder capabilities and prices. Handles split awards across multiple suppliers. Evaluates combinatorial bids for lot combinations. Maximizes savings through optimal lot allocation.

layers.name: Post-Auction Award & Negotiation
layers.description: Performs post-auction analysis comparing results to pre-auction estimates. Manages award decisions and notifications. Handles post-auction negotiations for minor clarifications. Generates savings reports and executive summaries.

title: Balance Sheet Account Reconciliation Automation
description: Automates the complete balance sheet reconciliation process including data extraction, automated matching, reconciling item identification, aging analysis, and certification workflows, ensuring all balance sheet accounts are reconciled accurately and timely.

layers.name: Reconciliation Universe Management
layers.description: Maintains complete inventory of all balance sheet accounts requiring reconciliation with defined frequency (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly), preparer and reviewer assignments, and account-specific reconciliation methodologies

layers.name: Automated Data Collection
layers.description: Automatically extracts GL balances, sub-ledger details, and external source data (bank statements, third-party confirmations) for each reconciliation. Formats data for comparison and identifies data quality issues

layers.name: Intelligent Matching Engine
layers.description: Applies multi-dimensional matching algorithms to automatically match transactions between GL and supporting systems using exact matching, tolerance-based matching, and fuzzy logic for partial matches across amount, date, reference, and description fields

layers.name: Reconciling Item Analysis
layers.description: Identifies unmatched items on both GL and source sides, categorizes items by type (timing difference, permanent difference, error), ages items by transaction date, and calculates net reconciling balance

layers.name: Certification & Sign-Off Workflow
layers.description: Routes completed reconciliations through preparer review, independent reviewer approval, and management certification. Tracks certification status, maintains certification evidence, and ensures compliance with certification requirements

title: Advanced Transaction Matching Engine
description: Employs sophisticated matching algorithms including exact matching, fuzzy matching, many-to-many matching, and AI-driven pattern recognition to automatically match transactions across systems, minimizing manual matching effort and improving matching accuracy by 95%+.

layers.name: Multi-Method Matching Framework
layers.description: Applies sequential matching methods starting with exact matches (amount, date, reference), then rule-based matching (tolerance ranges, date windows), followed by fuzzy matching (similar amounts, approximate dates), and finally AI pattern matching for complex scenarios

layers.name: Configurable Matching Rules
layers.description: Maintains library of matching rules customizable by account type including matching criteria (amount precision, date tolerance, reference field weights), auto-match thresholds (confidence scores), and exception routing rules

layers.name: Many-to-Many Matching
layers.description: Handles complex matching scenarios where multiple transactions on one side match to multiple on the other (e.g., batch payments, consolidated invoices). Identifies optimal matching combinations that minimize unmatched balances

layers.name: Historical Pattern Learning
layers.description: Analyzes historical matching patterns to improve matching accuracy over time. Learns user matching decisions, identifies recurring transaction patterns, and suggests matches based on historical behavior

layers.name: Match Confidence Scoring
layers.description: Assigns confidence scores to each match based on matching criteria satisfaction. Auto-matches high-confidence matches, routes medium-confidence for review, and flags low-confidence for manual intervention

title: Bank Reconciliation & Cash Management Integration
description: Automates bank reconciliation by integrating directly with bank feeds, matching transactions, identifying outstanding items, calculating book-to-bank differences, and tracking cash position in real-time across all bank accounts globally.

layers.name: Multi-Bank Integration
layers.description: Connects to bank accounts across multiple banks and countries via secure APIs, SWIFT messaging, or file feeds. Retrieves transaction data, balances, and bank statements automatically on scheduled basis or real-time

layers.name: Automated Bank Matching
layers.description: Matches bank transactions to cash book entries using check numbers, wire references, amounts, dates, and payee information. Handles different transaction types (checks, wires, ACH, card payments) with type-specific matching logic

layers.name: Outstanding Item Management
layers.description: Identifies and tracks outstanding checks, deposits in transit, outstanding wires, and bank errors. Ages outstanding items, calculates days outstanding, and flags items exceeding aging thresholds for investigation

layers.name: Book-to-Bank Reconciliation
layers.description: Calculates book-to-bank difference by adding outstanding deposits and subtracting outstanding checks from bank balance. Validates reconciled balance equals cash book balance and investigates variances

layers.name: Cash Position Forecasting
layers.description: Provides real-time cash position across all accounts, forecasts cash availability based on outstanding items clearing patterns, identifies potential overdrafts, and optimizes cash deployment across accounts

title: Credit Card & Corporate Card Reconciliation
description: Reconciles corporate credit card transactions to expense reports and general ledger, matches card transactions to receipts, identifies unsubmitted expenses, and ensures complete expense reporting and proper GL coding.

layers.name: Card Feed Integration
layers.description: Integrates with corporate card providers (Amex, Visa, MasterCard) to receive daily transaction feeds including merchant name, transaction amount, date, cardholder, merchant category code, and foreign exchange details

layers.name: Expense Report Matching
layers.description: Matches card transactions to submitted expense reports, identifies submitted expenses with receipts, flags card charges without expense reports, and tracks time lag between transaction date and expense submission

layers.name: Merchant Category Intelligence
layers.description: Uses merchant category codes and names to suggest expense categories, learns from user coding patterns, pre-populates expense categories, and identifies potential personal expenses based on merchant type

layers.name: Multi-Currency Handling
layers.description: Handles foreign currency card transactions, compares card provider exchange rates to accounting rates, calculates FX differences, and posts appropriate GL entries for FX variances

layers.name: Unsubmitted Expense Management
layers.description: Identifies card transactions not yet submitted as expenses, sends reminders to cardholders, tracks aging of unsubmitted expenses, and escalates overdue expense reports to management

title: Exception Handling & Resolution Workflows
description: Manages reconciliation exceptions through intelligent routing, investigation workflows, resolution tracking, and automated escalation, ensuring all reconciliation differences are investigated and resolved timely.

layers.name: Exception Detection & Categorization
layers.description: Identifies reconciliation exceptions including unmatched transactions, balance differences, duplicate entries, and data quality issues. Categorizes by type (timing, permanent, error), severity (based on amount and age), and complexity

layers.name: Intelligent Exception Routing
layers.description: Routes exceptions to appropriate resolvers based on exception type, account ownership, dollar amount, and resolver capacity. Balances workload across team and ensures high-value items get priority attention

layers.name: Investigation Workflow
layers.description: Provides investigation workspace with access to transaction details, supporting documents, communication history, and similar historical exceptions. Guides investigator through resolution steps and documentation requirements

layers.name: Resolution Tracking & Documentation
layers.description: Tracks resolution status from identification through closure, maintains investigation notes and supporting evidence, requires management approval for significant adjustments, and preserves complete audit trail

layers.name: Automated Escalation
layers.description: Escalates unresolved exceptions based on age, amount, and complexity thresholds. Sends escalation notifications to management, tracks escalation response time, and ensures timely resolution of high-risk items

title: Manual Journal Entry Creation & Validation
description: Manages manual journal entry lifecycle including entry creation with validation, supporting documentation attachment, approval workflows, posting to GL, and complete audit trail maintenance.

layers.name: Entry Form Validation
layers.description: Validates journal entry data including balanced debits and credits, valid account codes, mandatory fields completion, appropriate posting period, and account combination validation. Prevents posting of unbalanced or invalid entries

layers.name: Supporting Documentation Management
layers.description: Requires supporting documentation for all manual entries, validates document relevance and completeness, maintains document repository with entry linkage, and provides retrieval for audit and review

layers.name: Account Authorization Control
layers.description: Validates that users have authorization to post to specific accounts, prevents posting to restricted accounts (e.g., statutory accounts, consolidated accounts), and enforces segregation of duties between entry preparation and approval

layers.name: Entry Description Standards
layers.description: Enforces entry description standards ensuring clear business purpose, appropriate level of detail, consistent naming conventions, and inclusion of key reference information for audit trail

layers.name: Duplicate Detection
layers.description: Identifies potential duplicate journal entries by comparing entry characteristics (accounts, amounts, descriptions, dates) to recent postings, flags suspected duplicates for review, and prevents unintentional duplicate posting

title: Recurring Journal Entry Automation
description: Automates recurring journal entries including depreciation, amortization, accruals, and allocations through template-based processing, scheduled execution, and automated posting workflows.

layers.name: Template Library Management
layers.description: Maintains comprehensive library of journal entry templates for recurring transactions including depreciation, amortization, intercompany charges, allocations, accruals, and standard adjustments with account codes, formulas, and posting logic

layers.name: Template Parameterization
layers.description: Enables parameterized templates where amounts, dates, or other variables can be input at posting time. Supports formula-based calculations (e.g., percentage allocations, proration), lookup values from other systems, and conditional logic

layers.name: Automated Recurring Postings
layers.description: Schedules recurring journal entries for automatic posting on defined frequencies (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly). Validates template calculations, applies current period data, and posts automatically without manual intervention

layers.name: Template Version Control
layers.description: Maintains version history for templates, tracks template modifications with effective dates, manages approval for template changes, and ensures historical postings remain auditable with original template version

layers.name: Template Performance Analytics
layers.description: Tracks template usage statistics, identifies frequently used templates, flags templates with high error rates, and provides recommendations for template improvements or additional templates needed

title: Compliance & Regulatory Journal Entries
description: Manages compliance-related journal entries including tax adjustments, statutory reporting entries, regulatory compliance journals, and audit adjustments, ensuring proper documentation and approval for compliance entries.

layers.name: Compliance Entry Classification
layers.description: Identifies and tags journal entries related to compliance including tax provision entries, statutory adjustments, regulatory filing entries, audit adjustments, and SOX-required entries. Applies special controls and documentation requirements

layers.name: Tax Adjustment Management
layers.description: Manages tax-related journal entries including income tax provision, deferred tax adjustments, tax rate changes, valuation allowance changes, and uncertain tax positions. Validates tax calculations and maintains tax-specific supporting documentation

layers.name: Statutory Reporting Adjustments
layers.description: Handles entries required for statutory reporting in different jurisdictions including local GAAP adjustments, statutory reserve calculations, regulatory capital adjustments, and mandatory disclosure entries

layers.name: Audit Adjustment Tracking
layers.description: Tracks all audit adjustments proposed by internal or external auditors including passed adjustments, posted adjustments, and disagreements. Maintains audit adjustment register and links to audit findings

layers.name: Enhanced Documentation Requirements
layers.description: Enforces stricter documentation requirements for compliance entries including technical accounting memos, management approval evidence, auditor communication, and regulatory filing references

title: Accruals & Deferrals Management
description: Automates accrual and deferral calculations, manages prepayment amortization, handles deferred revenue recognition, processes recurring accruals, and ensures accurate period-end adjustments.

layers.name: Accrual Identification & Calculation
layers.description: Identifies items requiring accrual including GRNI (goods received not invoiced), unbilled services, accrued interest, accrued payroll, and accrued expenses. Calculates accrual amounts based on contracts, POs, service agreements, or estimates

layers.name: Prepayment & Deferred Cost Management
layers.description: Tracks prepaid expenses and deferred costs, calculates monthly amortization based on benefit period, generates amortization journal entries automatically, and maintains prepayment/deferral schedules

layers.name: Deferred Revenue Recognition
layers.description: Manages deferred revenue for advance payments, subscription revenue, and milestone-based contracts. Calculates revenue recognition based on performance obligations, time elapsed, or milestone completion per ASC 606/IFRS 15

layers.name: Recurring Accrual Automation
layers.description: Automates recurring accruals such as rent, utilities, insurance, and subscriptions. Validates accrual amounts against contracts, adjusts for contract changes, and reverses accruals in subsequent period

layers.name: Accrual Reversal Management
layers.description: Automatically reverses period-end accruals in the subsequent period, tracks invoice receipt against accruals, identifies accrual-to-invoice variances, and adjusts or writes off accruals without corresponding invoices

title: Journal Entry Risk & Fraud Detection
description: Employs AI-powered risk scoring, anomaly detection, and fraud prevention to identify high-risk journal entries, unusual patterns, and potential fraudulent activities in the journal entry process.

layers.name: AI-Powered Risk Scoring
layers.description: Assigns risk scores to journal entries based on multiple factors including entry type, amount, accounts used, timing, preparer profile, and historical patterns. Flags high-risk entries for enhanced review

layers.name: Anomaly Detection Algorithms
layers.description: Identifies unusual journal entry patterns including round-dollar amounts, unusual account combinations, entries just below approval thresholds, weekend/holiday postings, and entries reversing prior period adjustments

layers.name: Segregation of Duties Monitoring
layers.description: Validates segregation of duties compliance ensuring preparers don't approve their own entries, same person doesn't create and post entries, and authority limits are respected. Detects SOD violations in real-time

layers.name: Revenue Recognition Scrutiny
