layers.description: Applies enhanced scrutiny to revenue recognition journal entries, monitors for aggressive revenue recognition patterns, flags unusual revenue timing, and validates revenue entries against contracts and policies

layers.name: Predictive Fraud Risk Modeling
layers.description: Uses machine learning to identify fraud patterns based on historical fraud cases and industry patterns. Scores entries for fraud likelihood and generates alerts for investigation of highest-risk entries

title: Multi-Entity Consolidation & Legal Entity Rollup
description: Consolidates financial results from multiple legal entities into group consolidated financials, manages entity hierarchies, applies consolidation methods, and produces consolidated trial balance and financial statements.

layers.name: Entity Hierarchy Management
layers.description: Maintains complete organizational hierarchy including parent-subsidiary relationships, ownership percentages, consolidation method by entity (full consolidation, proportionate, equity method), effective dates of ownership, and changes in structure

layers.name: Trial Balance Collection & Validation
layers.description: Collects trial balances from all entities in consolidation scope, validates data completeness and quality, ensures consistent chart of accounts mapping across entities, and identifies missing or late entity submissions

layers.name: Consolidation Method Application
layers.description: Applies appropriate consolidation method based on ownership percentage and control

layers.name: Elimination & Adjustment Processing
layers.description: Processes all consolidation eliminations including intercompany transactions, intercompany balances, intercompany profits, and investment elimination. Applies consolidation adjustments for purchase accounting, goodwill, and minority interests

layers.name: Consolidated Output Generation
layers.description: Aggregates entity trial balances, applies eliminations and adjustments, calculates minority interest, produces consolidated trial balance, and generates consolidated financial statements (balance sheet, income statement, cash flow, equity)

title: Automated Intercompany Elimination
description: Automatically eliminates intercompany transactions and balances including IC sales/COGS, IC receivables/payables, IC profits in inventory, IC loans/interest, and IC dividends to produce accurate consolidated financials.

layers.name: Intercompany Transaction Identification
layers.description: Identifies all intercompany transactions across entities including IC sales and purchases, IC services, IC interest and royalties, IC dividends, and IC asset transfers. Tags transactions with IC codes for tracking and elimination

layers.name: Automatic Elimination Entry Generation
layers.description: Generates consolidation elimination entries automatically by matching IC transactions across entities, creating offsetting entries to remove IC revenue/expense, IC assets/liabilities, and IC equity transactions

layers.name: IC Profit Elimination
layers.description: Identifies intercompany profits in inventory, fixed assets, or other balance sheet items. Calculates unrealized profit to eliminate based on markup percentage and remaining balance. Reverses profit elimination when assets sold to third parties

layers.name: IC Loan & Investment Elimination
layers.description: Eliminates intercompany loans by offsetting IC receivable in lending entity against IC payable in borrowing entity. Eliminates IC interest income/expense. Eliminates investment in subsidiary against subsidiary equity (acquisition method)

layers.name: Minority Interest Calculation
layers.description: Calculates minority (non-controlling) interest in consolidated subsidiaries based on ownership percentage less than 100%. Allocates subsidiary net income and equity to controlling and non-controlling interests

title: Intercompany Reconciliation & Netting
description: Reconciles intercompany balances across entities to ensure IC receivables equal IC payables, identifies and resolves IC differences, manages IC netting agreements, and validates IC balance elimination.

layers.name: IC Balance Extraction
layers.description: Extracts intercompany receivables and payables from all entities, identifies IC balances by counterparty entity, categorizes IC balances by type (trade, loans, other), and summarizes IC balances by entity pair

layers.name: IC Balance Matching
layers.description: Matches IC receivables in one entity to IC payables in counterparty entity, identifies matched balances that offset, flags unmatched balances requiring investigation, and calculates net IC difference by entity pair

layers.name: IC Difference Investigation
layers.description: Investigates IC balance differences due to timing (transaction recorded in only one entity), FX translation (entities use different functional currencies), errors (incorrect recording), or disputes (entities disagree on amount)

layers.name: IC Difference Resolution
layers.description: Resolves IC differences through entity communication, adjusting entries to correct errors, timing difference documentation for legitimate delays, and FX difference calculation and allocation

layers.name: IC Netting Application
layers.description: Applies intercompany netting agreements where entities agree to settle net position rather than gross balances. Calculates net IC position by entity pair, generates netting entries, and maintains netting documentation

title: Top-Side Consolidation Adjustments & Corporate Entries
description: Manages consolidation-level adjustments not recorded in legal entity books including fair value adjustments, goodwill, impairment, equity method income, and other corporate-level entries required for consolidated reporting.

layers.name: Purchase Accounting Adjustments
layers.description: Maintains purchase accounting adjustments for acquisitions including fair value step-ups to assets acquired, recognition of intangible assets identified, goodwill calculation, and deferred tax impacts of fair value adjustments

layers.name: Goodwill Impairment Testing
layers.description: Performs goodwill impairment testing at reporting unit level, compares carrying value to fair value or value-in-use, recognizes impairment losses if carrying value exceeds recoverable amount, and allocates impairment to goodwill

layers.name: Equity Method Investment Accounting
layers.description: Records equity method income for associates (20-50% ownership), applies proportionate share of investee income, adjusts for basis differences (fair value vs. book value), eliminates upstream profits, and tracks cumulative equity method adjustments

layers.name: Corporate Overhead Allocations
layers.description: Allocates corporate-level costs not recorded in operating entities including parent company overhead, shared services costs, corporate function expenses, and management fees. Applies allocation methodology consistently

layers.name: Other Consolidation Adjustments
layers.description: Records other consolidation-only entries including elimination of treasury stock, consolidation of VIEs (variable interest entities), recognition of stock-based compensation, and FX translation of non-functional currency balances

title: Management Reporting Hierarchies & Segment Reporting
description: Creates management reporting views separate from legal entity structure, enables segment reporting by business unit/product/geography, supports matrix reporting, and provides flexible reporting hierarchies for management decision-making.

layers.name: Multi-Dimensional Hierarchy Management
layers.description: Maintains multiple reporting hierarchies including legal entity structure, management reporting structure, geographic regions, business segments, product lines, and customer channels. Enables cross-dimensional reporting and analysis

layers.name: Segment Identification & Mapping
layers.description: Identifies operating segments per ASC 280/IFRS 8 based on how management views the business, maps legal entities and cost centers to operating segments, and aggregates segment financial information

layers.name: Transfer Pricing & IC Allocations
layers.description: Applies transfer pricing between segments for internal sales, allocates shared costs to segments using allocation drivers, calculates segment profitability including allocated costs, and maintains segment P&L

layers.name: Geographic Reporting
layers.description: Aggregates financial data by geographic region for disclosure requirements, classifies revenue by customer location and by entity location, allocates assets to geographic regions, and provides country-level reporting

layers.name: Matrix Reporting Capability
layers.description: Enables multi-dimensional reporting cutting data by multiple hierarchies simultaneously (e.g., business segment by geography, product by customer type). Provides drill-down from summary to detail across dimensions

title: Multi-Currency Consolidation & Translation
description: Translates foreign subsidiary financial statements to group reporting currency using appropriate translation methods, tracks cumulative translation adjustments, and ensures compliance with ASC 830/IAS 21.

layers.name: Functional Currency Determination
layers.description: Determines functional currency for each entity based on primary economic environment, currency of cash flows, currency of financing, and currency of operating expenses. Documents functional currency assessment and monitors for changes

layers.name: Translation Method Selection
layers.description: Applies current rate method for translating foreign functional currency entities (assets/liabilities at current rate, income/expense at average rate, equity at historical rate) or temporal method for foreign entities using parent currency as functional

layers.name: Exchange Rate Application
layers.description: Applies appropriate exchange rates including spot rates for balance sheet (current rate), average rates for income statement, historical rates for equity, and acquisition-date rates for goodwill and other acquisition-related items

layers.name: CTA Calculation & Tracking
layers.description: Calculates cumulative translation adjustment (CTA) as the balancing figure in translation process, tracks CTA by entity in OCI (other comprehensive income), and recycles CTA to income upon disposal of foreign operation

layers.name: Hyperinflationary Economy Adjustment
layers.description: Identifies entities operating in hyperinflationary economies (3-year cumulative inflation >100%), applies IAS 29 adjustments restating historical cost to current purchasing power before translation

title: Automated Exchange Rate Updates & Management
description: Automatically retrieves foreign exchange rates from external sources, maintains rate history, validates rate reasonableness, manages rate effective dates, and ensures timely rate availability for period-end close.

layers.name: Automated Rate Feeds
layers.description: Integrates with external FX rate providers (Bloomberg, Reuters, Central Banks, OFX) to automatically retrieve daily spot rates, month-end rates, and period average rates. Supports multiple rate sources for validation and backup

layers.name: Rate Type Management
layers.description: Maintains different rate types including spot rates (for balance sheet translation), average rates (for income statement), budget rates (for planning), and custom rates (for specific contracts or hedges)

layers.name: Rate Validation & Reasonability
layers.description: Validates retrieved rates against expected ranges, compares rates across multiple sources to identify anomalies, flags significant rate movements requiring investigation, and alerts finance team to rate issues before close

layers.name: Historical Rate Repository
layers.description: Maintains complete history of exchange rates by currency pair, rate type, and effective date. Provides rate retrieval for any historical date, supports audit requirements for rate evidence, and enables rate variance analysis

layers.name: Rate Effective Date Management
layers.description: Manages rate effective dates ensuring period-end rates are available timely, allows manual rate override with approval for exceptions, tracks rate approval workflow, and maintains rate change audit trail

title: Foreign Exchange Gain/Loss Calculation & Recognition
description: Calculates realized and unrealized foreign exchange gains and losses on transactions and balances, posts FX G/L to income statement, and provides FX impact analysis by currency and entity.

layers.name: Transaction FX Gain/Loss (Realized)
layers.description: Calculates realized FX gains/losses on settled transactions including difference between transaction date rate and settlement date rate for AR/AP, FX impact on intercompany settlements, and FX on foreign currency cash transactions

layers.name: Revaluation FX Gain/Loss (Unrealized)
layers.description: Calculates unrealized FX gains/losses on unsettled monetary assets and liabilities by comparing carrying amount at historical rate to current value at period-end rate. Revalues foreign currency cash, AR, AP, loans, and other monetary items

layers.name: FX on Intercompany Balances
layers.description: Calculates FX gains/losses on intercompany balances including IC receivables/payables, IC loans, and IC investments. Handles differences when entities have different functional currencies and record IC balance at different rates

layers.name: Hedge Accounting FX Treatment
layers.description: Applies hedge accounting treatment for designated FX hedges including fair value hedges (FX on hedged item and hedging instrument both to P&L), cash flow hedges (effective portion to OCI), and net investment hedges (to CTA)

layers.name: FX Impact Analysis & Attribution
layers.description: Analyzes total FX impact by currency pair, entity, transaction type, and realized vs. unrealized. Attributes FX movements to currency rate changes, volume changes, and new transactions. Provides management reporting on FX exposure and impact

title: Cumulative Translation Adjustment (CTA) Management
description: Tracks cumulative translation adjustments in accumulated other comprehensive income, manages CTA by entity and currency, recycles CTA upon disposal of foreign operations, and provides CTA roll-forward reporting.

layers.name: CTA Calculation & Accumulation
layers.description: Calculates period CTA for each foreign entity as balancing figure in translation process, accumulates CTA in OCI by entity and currency, segregates CTA from other OCI components (pension, hedges), and tracks controlling vs. non-controlling CTA

layers.name: CTA Attribution & Analysis
layers.description: Analyzes CTA movements by driver including currency rate changes, changes in net asset position, changes in functional currency, and acquisition/disposal of foreign operations. Provides variance analysis of CTA period-over-period

layers.name: CTA Recycling on Disposal
layers.description: Recycles accumulated CTA to income statement upon complete or partial disposal of foreign operation, calculates proportionate CTA to recycle for partial disposals, validates disposal triggers CTA recycling (loss of control, significant reduction), and posts CTA recycling entry

layers.name: CTA Tax Impact
layers.description: Calculates tax impact of CTA recycling upon disposal, determines tax basis of foreign investment including accumulated CTA, recognizes deferred tax on CTA if tax basis differs from book basis, and discloses tax impact of CTA in income tax footnote

layers.name: CTA Disclosure & Reporting
layers.description: Provides CTA roll-forward for financial statement disclosure showing beginning balance, period translation adjustment, disposals/recycling, and ending balance. Discloses CTA by entity or geographic region. Maintains audit support for CTA calculations

title: Multi-Currency Transaction Processing & Settlement
description: Processes transactions in multiple currencies, converts to functional currency at transaction date, manages multi-currency AR/AP, handles currency settlements, and tracks currency positions across entities.

layers.name: Transaction Currency Capture
layers.description: Captures original transaction currency for all foreign currency transactions including sales invoices, purchase invoices, receipts, payments, and journal entries. Validates currency codes and maintains transaction currency amount alongside functional currency

layers.name: Transaction Date Conversion
layers.description: Converts transaction currency to functional currency at transaction date using spot rate or approved rate. Maintains both original currency and functional currency amounts. Locks transaction rate for AR/AP until settlement

layers.name: Multi-Currency AR/AP Management
layers.description: Manages accounts receivable and payable in multiple currencies, tracks open items by currency, calculates FX exposure by currency, and provides aging reports in both transaction currency and functional currency

layers.name: Currency Settlement Processing
layers.description: Processes foreign currency receipts and payments, calculates realized FX gain/loss as difference between transaction rate and settlement rate, handles partial settlements with proportionate FX calculation, and settles multi-currency transactions

layers.name: Currency Position Reporting
layers.description: Provides real-time currency position reporting showing net exposure by currency across all entities, monitors currency limits and hedging requirements, alerts to excess currency exposure, and supports treasury FX risk management

title: Automated Period-End Close Orchestration
description: Orchestrates the complete financial close process from pre-close activities through final reporting, automates task sequencing, monitors progress, identifies bottlenecks, and ensures timely close completion.

layers.name: Close Process Definition
layers.description: Defines complete close process including pre-close tasks (data validation, rate updates), close tasks (reconciliations, journal entries, consolidation), post-close tasks (reporting, review), and task sequencing with dependencies

layers.name: Automated Task Triggering
layers.description: Triggers close tasks automatically based on schedule (calendar-based, business day-based) or completion of prerequisite tasks. Initiates parallel tasks where dependencies allow. Manages human and system tasks in integrated workflow

layers.name: Close Timeline Management
layers.description: Manages close calendar with planned vs. actual dates for each task, tracks close cycle time, calculates days to close, monitors progress against timeline, and provides early warning for at-risk tasks

layers.name: Bottleneck Detection & Resolution
layers.description: Identifies close bottlenecks where tasks are waiting, analyzes root causes (resource constraints, data issues, system problems), suggests resolution actions, and escalates persistent bottlenecks to management

layers.name: Close Completion Validation

title: 01 | AI-Driven Demand Forecasting
description:
Predicts demand using seasonality, weather patterns, economic indicators, competitor activities to adjust forecasts in real-time.
  Runs thousands of "what-if" scenarios autonomously based on changing market conditions.
  Learns from past campaigns and predicts demand spikes/dips for new promotions with confidence intervals.
  Uses similarity matching with existing products to forecast demand for SKUs with no historical data.
  Incorporates geopolitical disruptions, industry trends, and macroeconomic variables.
  Automatically detects and adjusts for seasonal patterns and emerging trends.

layers.name: Data Aggregation
layers.description: Collects historical sales data, point-of-sale (POS) data across channels, weather patterns, economic indicators, competitor pricing, promotional calendars, and market dynamics.

layers.name: Pattern Recognition
layers.description: Identifies seasonality, trends, cyclical patterns, and correlation with external factors using machine learning models. Detects emerging trends and shifting demand patterns.

layers.name: Predictive Modeling
layers.description: Generates demand forecasts at SKU-location-time level with confidence intervals. Produces multiple scenarios with probability distributions.

layers.name: Alert Generation
layers.description: Flags significant deviations from forecast and triggers procurement/production alerts. Calculates urgency and priority based on impact.

layers.name: Continuous Learning
layers.description: Updates models based on forecast accuracy and incorporates new data patterns. Retrains automatically when accuracy drops below threshold.

id: SUP-Outcome:  10-25% inventory reduction. Lower stock-outs by 15-30%. Better working capital management. Forecast accuracy improved 15-20%. Agile response to market changes.
title: 02 | Real-Time Demand Sensing
description:
Analyzes real-time point-of-sale (POS) data across all channels (retail, e-commerce, distributor).
  Monitors distributor and retailer data feeds continuously for early demand signals.
  Tracks social media trends and sentiment analysis for viral product trends.
  Detects sudden demand spikes from viral social media trends or influencer endorsements.
  Monitors ongoing promotions and their real-time impact on demand patterns.
  Adjusts short-term forecasts dynamically (weekly/daily) based on live signals.

layers.name: Multi-Channel Data Monitoring
layers.description: Monitors POS data from retail stores, e-commerce platforms, distributor networks, social media platforms, and competitor activities in real-time.

layers.name: Signal Detection
layers.description: Identifies anomalies, trending patterns, and early warning indicators using advanced analytics. Distinguishes noise from genuine demand signals.

layers.name: Short-Term Forecast Adjustment
layers.description: Updates weekly and daily forecasts dynamically based on confirmed signals. Calculates revised demand with updated confidence intervals.

layers.name: Cross-Functional Alert System
layers.description: Notifies production planning, inventory management, and procurement teams immediately with specific action recommendations and urgency levels.

layers.name: Response Tracking & Learning
layers.description: Monitors effectiveness of demand sensing adjustments. Tracks actual vs. predicted demand post-adjustment and learns from outcomes to improve future signal interpretation.

id: SUP-Outcome:  Response time reduced from weeks to hours. Lost sales prevented through early detection. Markdown costs from excess inventory minimized. Inventory turns improved 20-30%.
title: 03 | Scenario Simulation & What-If Analysis
description:
Simulates "what-if" scenarios such as: "If oil prices increase 15%, what happens to supply chain costs?"
  Models impact of demand spikes, supply disruptions, or major supplier changes on network.
  Calculates financial impact across the entire P&L (revenue, costs, margins).
  Suggests mitigation strategies for each simulated scenario with trade-off analysis.
  Runs sensitivity analysis on key variables to identify critical risk factors.
  Supports strategic decision-making with quantified risk assessments and recommendations.

layers.name: Scenario Definition
layers.description: Captures variables and parameters to simulate - price changes, demand shifts, supplier failures, capacity constraints, policy changes, natural disasters, geopolitical events.

layers.name: Model Execution
layers.description: Runs simulation across entire supply chain network including suppliers, manufacturing, warehouses, distribution centers, and transportation. Uses digital twin of supply chain.

layers.name: Impact Calculation
layers.description: Computes cost impact (materials, freight, labor), revenue impact (lost sales, market share), and service level impacts (OTIF, stockouts). Calculates net P&L effect.

layers.name: Mitigation Options Identification
layers.description: Identifies and evaluates alternative response strategies - alternate suppliers, expedited shipping, inventory pre-builds, capacity adjustments, pricing changes.

layers.name: Decision Support & Recommendations
layers.description: Presents trade-offs between cost, service, and risk. Prioritizes actions by ROI and feasibility. Provides executive dashboard with clear recommendations.

id: SUP-Dynamic Inventory Management, Multi-Echelon Optimization & Smart Replenishment
title: 04 | Dynamic Inventory Optimization
description:
Makes autonomous replenishment decisions considering lead times, supplier reliability, demand volatility, working capital costs, and storage constraints simultaneously.
  Optimizes stock across warehouses, distribution centers, and retail stores in real-time.
  Determines optimal safety stock, reorder points, and cycle stock considering demand variability and lead times.
  Identifies slow-moving inventory early and triggers markdown/liquidation strategies.
  Continuously recalculates safety stock based on service level targets and supply variability.
  Balances inventory investment with service level requirements to optimize working capital.

layers.name: Lead Time Analysis
layers.description: Analyzes supplier lead times, lead time variability, and reliability. Considers transportation time, customs clearance, and receiving/QC time. Factors in supplier production schedules.

layers.name: Demand Variability Assessment
layers.description: Calculates demand volatility at SKU-location level. Identifies patterns - seasonal, promotional, trend-based. Computes coefficient of variation for each SKU.

layers.name: Multi-Objective Optimization
layers.description: Balances competing objectives - minimize inventory investment, maximize service levels, minimize stockouts, optimize warehouse space utilization. Uses constraint-based optimization.

layers.name: Safety Stock Calculation
layers.description: Dynamically calculates safety stock using demand variability, lead time variability, and target service level. Adjusts for demand spikes and supply disruptions.

layers.name: Replenishment Decision
layers.description: Determines optimal order quantity, reorder point, and order frequency. Considers EOQ, volume discounts, MOQ constraints, and cash flow impact.

id: SUP-Outcome:  15-20% reduction in excess inventory. 20% reduction in holding costs. Working capital improved 15-25%. Service levels maintained or improved. Stockout rates reduced 30%.
title: 05 | Multi-Echelon Inventory Balancing
description:
Optimizes inventory across the entire network - suppliers, manufacturing plants, distribution centers, warehouses, and retail stores.
  Balances inventory positioning between central and regional locations based on demand patterns.
  Coordinates replenishment across echelons to minimize total inventory while maintaining service.
  Identifies opportunities for inventory pooling and postponement strategies.
  Manages inter-warehouse transfers to balance supply and demand dynamically.
  Optimizes inventory placement considering transportation costs and lead times.

layers.name: Network Topology Mapping
layers.description: Maps entire supply chain network with all nodes (suppliers, plants, DCs, warehouses, stores) and connections (transportation lanes, lead times, costs).

layers.name: Demand Allocation
layers.description: Allocates demand to optimal fulfillment locations based on proximity, inventory availability, and cost. Considers demand correlation across locations.

layers.name: Inventory Positioning
layers.description: Determines optimal inventory levels at each echelon. Balances push vs pull strategies. Calculates echelon safety stocks.

layers.name: Transfer Optimization
layers.description: Identifies opportunities for inter-warehouse transfers to avoid stockouts while reducing excess elsewhere. Optimizes transfer timing and quantity.

layers.name: Cost-Service Trade-off
layers.description: Balances total supply chain cost (inventory holding, transportation, stockouts) against service level targets. Runs optimization to find Pareto optimal solutions.

id: SUP-Outcome:  Total network inventory reduced 12-18%. Improved inventory turns by 20-25%. Stockout incidents reduced 35%. Transportation costs optimized. Better working capital efficiency.
title: 06 | Smart Replenishment Automation
description:
Auto-triggers replenishment based on predictive demand signals and inventory positions.

id: SUP-Outcome:  Replenishment cycle time reduced from 5 days to 24 hours. Manual purchase requisitions eliminated 80%. Supplier coordination improved. Optimal order quantities achieved. Discount capture improved.
title: 13 | Predictive Quality & Defect Prevention Agent
description:

  Monitors Statistical Process Control (SPC) and capability (Cpk) triggering alerts when process drifts before producing defects.

layers.name: Process Optimization

id: SUP-Smart Receiving, Put-Away, Pick Optimization, Cycle Counting & Automation Orchestration

layers.name: Variance Analysis

layers.name: Root Cause Investigation
layers.description: Analyzes variance patterns identifying systemic issues. Categorizes causes (picking error, putaway error, theft, damage, system error).

id: SUP-Outcome:  Inventory accuracy improved from 95% to 99.2%. Stockouts from inventory errors reduced 60%. Physical inventory eliminated replaced by perpetual counting. Cycle counting productivity doubled through optimization. Root cause identification enables targeted improvements. Annual physical inventory costs eliminated saving $200K. Inventory adjustments reduced 70% through better accuracy.

id: SUP-Order Promising, Intelligent Allocation & Fulfillment Orchestration

description:

id: SUP-Outcome:  Promise accuracy improved from 85% to 95%. Customer satisfaction increased through reliable delivery dates. Lost sales reduced 40% through better ATP visibility. Inventory reserves optimized reducing excess safety stock 20%. Backorder management improved customer retention. Multi-channel allocation prevents channel conflicts.

id: SUP-Outcome:  Crisis response time reduced from 48 hours to 4 hours through automated detection and activation. Customer impact minimized through proactive communication maintaining 90% satisfaction during crises. Recovery time improved 50% through coordinated response. Revenue protected through prioritization and alternates. Lessons learned captured improving future resilience.
title: 31 | Business Continuity & Recovery Planning Agent
description:
Maintains comprehensive business continuity plans for all critical supply chain processes with clear roles, procedures, alternatives.
  Tests BCP annually through simulations and tabletop exercises identifying gaps and improving preparedness.
  Identifies single points of failure across supply chain including sole-source suppliers, single DC for region, key equipment.
  Develops mitigation strategies for each SPOF including backup suppliers, redundant capacity, inventory buffers, alternate routing.
  Manages recovery prioritization determining which customers, products, processes to restore first based on business criticality.
  Tracks BCP maturity and readiness providing confidence to leadership, customers, and auditors in resilience capabilities.

layers.name: BCP Development
layers.description: Documents critical processes, dependencies, recovery time objectives, recovery point objectives. Identifies roles and responsibilities.

layers.name: SPOF Identification
layers.description: Analyzes supply chain for single points of failure. Assesses risk and impact of each SPOF.

layers.name: Mitigation Planning
layers.description: Develops specific mitigation strategies for each SPOF. Calculates investment required and risk reduction achieved.

layers.name: Testing
layers.description: Conducts annual simulations and tabletops. Identifies gaps in plans, training, or capabilities. Updates plans.

layers.name: Recovery Prioritization
layers.description: Ranks customers, products, processes by criticality. Defines sequence for restoration during major disruption.

id: SUP-Trade Compliance, Regulatory Management & Contract Analytics
title: 32 | Trade Compliance & Export Control Agent
description:
Screens 100% of international shipments automatically against restricted party lists, export controls, sanctions ensuring compliance.
  Classifies products with correct HS codes and determines licensing requirements preventing shipment delays and penalties.
  Validates trade documentation (commercial invoice, packing list, certificate of origin) ensuring accuracy and completeness.
  Calculates and validates duties, taxes, and trade agreement benefits (USMCA, FTA) optimizing costs while ensuring compliance.
  Manages preferential trade certificates and documentation maintaining eligibility for duty savings programs.
  Provides audit-ready compliance documentation with complete transaction history preventing penalties during customs audits.

layers.name: Restricted Party Screening
layers.description: Screens all parties (customers, consignees, freight forwarders) against OFAC, BIS Denied Parties, EU sanctions lists.

layers.name: Product Classification
layers.description: Determines HS code and ECCN for each product. Identifies export license requirements. Flags controlled items.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Generates compliant commercial invoices, packing lists, certificates of origin. Validates completeness and accuracy.

layers.name: Duty Calculation
layers.description: Calculates duties based on HS code, origin, destination. Applies FTA benefits when eligible. Optimizes duty costs.

layers.name: Audit Trail
layers.description: Maintains complete documentation of all screenings, classifications, licenses for 7+ years. Provides reports for audits.

id: SUP-Outcome:  Zero compliance penalties through comprehensive screening and classification. Duty savings $500K annually through FTA optimization. Shipment delays reduced 80% through proactive compliance checks. Audit readiness ensured through complete documentation. Staff compliance awareness improved through training. Export license management streamlined.
title: 33 | Regulatory Compliance & Reporting Agent
description:
Monitors changing regulations across all jurisdictions ensuring supply chain operations remain compliant with evolving requirements.
  Manages regulatory reporting including TSCA, REACH, RoHS, conflict minerals, Prop 65 ensuring timely accurate submissions.
  Tracks product certifications and testing maintaining validity and renewals preventing market access issues.
  Validates regulatory compliance of suppliers through audits, certifications, and questionnaires ensuring supply base meets standards.
  Manages regulatory documentation (SDS, compliance certs, test reports) ensuring accessibility and currency for authorities and customers.
  Provides compliance dashboard showing regulatory status, gaps, actions required enabling proactive management and audit readiness.

layers.name: Regulation Monitoring
layers.description: Tracks regulatory changes across FDA, EPA, OSHA, DOT, EU, country-specific. Assesses impact on operations.

layers.name: Reporting
layers.description: Generates required reports for conflict minerals, REACH, RoHS, TSCA. Submits on schedule. Maintains submission records.

layers.name: Certification Tracking
layers.description: Tracks product certifications (UL, CE, FCC) and testing. Monitors expiration dates. Initiates renewals.

layers.name: Supplier Validation
layers.description: Audits suppliers for regulatory compliance. Collects certificates and test reports. Validates authenticity.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Maintains SDS library, compliance certificates, test reports. Ensures accessibility to authorities, customers, internal teams.

id: SUP-Outcome:  Zero regulatory violations through proactive compliance management. Certifications maintained current preventing market access disruptions. Regulatory reporting 100% on-time. Supplier compliance verified reducing supply risk. Documentation audit-ready at all times. Regulatory change impact assessed proactively enabling smooth transitions.
title: 34 | Contract Analytics & Performance Management Agent
description:
Analyzes 1000+ supplier and customer contracts extracting key performance metrics, obligations, and commitments.
  Tracks contract performance comparing actual results vs committed terms identifying underperformance requiring action.
  Manages contract amendments and renegotiations documenting changes and ensuring proper approvals before execution.
  Provides contract analytics showing total contract value, performance trends, risk exposures, savings opportunities.
  Ensures contract compliance across organization through monitoring, alerts, and training preventing violations.
  Generates contract performance scorecards enabling data-driven relationship management and renewal negotiations.

layers.name: Performance Extraction
layers.description: Parses contracts extracting KPIs (OTIF, quality, pricing, volume commitments). Structures data for tracking.

layers.name: Performance Monitoring
layers.description: Compares actual performance to contracted terms. Calculates compliance rates. Identifies gaps.

layers.name: Amendment Management
layers.description: Routes contract changes through proper approval workflow. Tracks versions. Maintains audit trail.

layers.name: Analytics
layers.description: Aggregates contract data providing insights on portfolio performance, risk concentration, optimization opportunities.

layers.name: Compliance Monitoring
layers.description: Ensures teams operate within contract terms. Alerts to potential violations. Provides training on key clauses.

id: SUP-Sales & Operations Planning and Supplier Collaboration
title: 35 | Integrated S&OP Agent
description:
Orchestrates monthly S&OP coordinating demand, supply, financial planning achieving consensus across sales, operations, finance.
  Identifies demand-supply gaps quantifying revenue risk, excess costs, service impacts enabling data-driven trade-off decisions.
  Runs multiple scenarios (optimistic, pessimistic, constrained) showing P&L, cash flow, service implications for each.
  Facilitates cross-functional collaboration ensuring aligned objectives and commitments tracked through execution.
  Measures plan performance tracking forecast accuracy, supply attainment, financial variance driving continuous improvement.
  Generates executive dashboard showing metrics, gaps, decisions required enabling effective governance.

layers.name: Data Integration
layers.description: Collects demand forecasts, supply plans, inventory, financials, constraints from all systems into unified view.

layers.name: Gap Analysis
layers.description: Compares demand vs supply identifying mismatches. Quantifies impacts on revenue, costs, service, inventory.

layers.name: Scenario Planning
layers.description: Generates planning scenarios with different assumptions. Simulates outcomes showing financial and operational impacts.

layers.name: Collaboration
layers.description: Facilitates S&OP meetings with data, analyses, recommendations. Captures decisions and actions. Tracks follow-through.

layers.name: Performance
layers.description: Monitors execution vs approved plan. Calculates forecast bias, supply attainment, variance. Identifies improvements.

id: SUP-Outcome:  S&OP cycle 4 weeks to 2 weeks. Forecast accuracy 75% to 85%. Supply attainment 85% to 92%. Financial variance 8% to 3%. Fire-drills reduced 60%. Data-driven decisions. Executive confidence increased.
title: 36 | Supplier Collaboration & VMI Agent
description:
Enables real-time collaboration sharing forecasts, inventory, POS data with strategic suppliers for better planning.
  Implements VMI where supplier monitors inventory and auto-replenishes based on agreed min/max parameters.
  Facilitates CPFR with joint demand planning and exception management improving forecast accuracy.
  Shares NPI and promotion plans early enabling supplier capacity planning and material preparation.
  Tracks collaboration performance measuring forecast sharing, VMI accuracy, CPFR effectiveness.
  Provides secure portal for data exchange, documents, performance dashboards with access controls.

layers.name: Data Sharing
layers.description: Shares forecasts, inventory, POS with authorized suppliers via secure portal. Configures access by supplier.

layers.name: VMI
layers.description: Supplier monitors customer inventory and usage. Generates replenishment within parameters. Customer receives and pays.

layers.name: CPFR
layers.description: Joint planning sessions. Exception management for forecast variances. Collaborative resolution.

layers.name: Launch Planning
layers.description: Shares NPI and promotions early. Facilitates capacity planning, material sourcing, production scheduling.

layers.name: Performance
layers.description: Tracks forecast sharing timeliness, VMI stock-outs, CPFR accuracy. Generates scorecards. Drives improvement.

title: 37 | Intelligent Returns Management Agent
description:
Automates returns authorization evaluating return requests against policy, warranty, purchase history approving/rejecting instantly.
  Optimizes returns routing directing to nearest processing center or store minimizing transportation costs.
  Processes returns efficiently through inspection, grading, disposition decisions (resell, refurbish, recycle, dispose).
  Manages refunds and replacements automatically updating inventory, financials, customer accounts in real-time.
  Analyzes return root causes identifying product quality issues, shipping damage, incorrect descriptions driving improvements.
  Tracks returns metrics including return rate, processing time, recovery value, customer satisfaction enabling optimization.

layers.name: Authorization
layers.description: Evaluates return request against policy (timeframe, condition, reason). Checks warranty status. Approves or rejects automatically.

layers.name: Routing
layers.description: Determines optimal return destination based on customer location, item type, processing capabilities. Generates return label.

layers.name: Inspection
layers.description: Upon receipt, inspects item against expected condition. Grades as A (resell), B (refurbish), C (parts), D (dispose).

layers.name: Disposition
layers.description: Routes to appropriate channel based on grade. A to resale inventory, B to refurbishment, C to parts recovery, D to recycling.

layers.name: Financial
layers.description: Processes refund or replacement. Updates inventory, GL, customer account. Tracks recovery value vs original cost.

id: SUP-Outcome:  Returns processing time reduced from 7 to 3 days. Auto-approval 80% vs 100% manual. Recovery value improved from 50% to 60% of cost. Root cause analysis drives quality improvements reducing returns 25%. Customer satisfaction 85% despite returns. Fraud detected and prevented saving $500K.
title: 38 | Refurbishment & Repair Optimization Agent
description:
Evaluates returned items determining repair vs refurbish vs scrap decisions optimizing recovery value.
  Manages refurbishment workflow routing items through cleaning, testing, repair, repackaging with quality checks.
  Optimizes spare parts inventory for repair supporting refurbishment operations while minimizing carrying costs.
  Prices refurbished products dynamically based on condition, demand, costs maximizing revenue and turnover.
  Tracks refurbishment economics comparing costs vs recovery value identifying profitable vs unprofitable items.
  Manages certified refurbished programs ensuring quality standards, warranties, customer satisfaction.

layers.name: Evaluation
layers.description: Assesses returned items determining repair feasibility and cost. Compares to market value of refurbished item.

layers.name: Workflow
layers.description: Routes items through refurbishment process with work orders, quality checks, testing at each stage.

layers.name: Parts Management
layers.description: Maintains spare parts inventory for repairs. Forecasts needs based on return patterns. Optimizes stock levels.

layers.name: Pricing
layers.description: Prices refurbished items based on condition grade, market demand, refurbishment cost, target margin.

layers.name: Economics
layers.description: Tracks refurbishment costs (labor, parts, overhead) vs revenue. Identifies profitable items to prioritize.

id: SUP-Outcome:  Refurbishment program generates $5M revenue annually. Recovery value improved from 40% to 65% of original. Refurbishment costs optimized reducing labor 30%. Parts inventory right-sized reducing carrying costs $200K. Certified refurb customer satisfaction 90%. Environmental impact reduced through reuse vs disposal.
title: 39 | Sustainable Disposal & Recycling Agent
description:
Routes end-of-life products to appropriate disposal channels (recycling, donation, energy recovery, landfill) maximizing sustainability.
  Ensures compliance with disposal regulations (WEEE, battery, hazmat) preventing environmental violations and penalties.
  Partners with recycling vendors extracting value from materials (metals, plastics, electronics) recovering costs.
  Manages hazardous waste disposal ensuring proper handling, documentation, tracking meeting regulatory requirements.
  Tracks disposal metrics including recycling rate, landfill diversion, value recovery, compliance enabling sustainability reporting.
  Optimizes disposal economics balancing environmental goals with cost minimization finding sustainable solutions.

layers.name: Classification
layers.description: Classifies items by disposal requirements (WEEE electronics, batteries, hazmat, general waste).

layers.name: Routing
layers.description: Routes to appropriate disposal channel based on material type, regulations, vendor capabilities, economics.

layers.name: Compliance
layers.description: Ensures regulatory compliance for hazardous materials. Generates required documentation. Maintains records.

layers.name: Recycling
layers.description: Partners with recyclers extracting value from materials. Negotiates revenue share. Tracks recovery.

layers.name: Reporting
layers.description: Tracks disposal metrics for sustainability reporting. Calculates recycling rates, landfill diversion, carbon impact.
